The appellation is situated in the Álava province, with vineyards often located on slopes that facilitate good drainage and sun exposure.
geo:es:arabako-txakolina.style: Crisp, fresh white wines with high acidity

geo:fr:arbois.name: Arbois
geo:fr:arbois.description: Arbois is an AOC located in the Jura region of France, recognized for its diverse wine production, particularly white wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, including clay-limestone and marl soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. Arbois is notable for its historical significance as one of the oldest wine-producing areas in France, with a tradition dating back to the Roman era. The wines from this appellation often reflect the unique microclimates and local grape varieties.
geo:fr:arbois.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and minerality

geo:es:arlanza.name: Arlanza
geo:es:arlanza.description: The Arlanza DO is located in the Castilla y León region of Spain, characterized by its diverse terroir and elevation. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass various municipalities in the province of Burgos. The climate is continental, with significant temperature variations between day and night, contributing to the development of the grapes. The primary focus of production is red wine, with a notable emphasis on traditional Spanish grape varieties.
geo:es:arlanza.style: Red wines with a focus on Tempranillo and Garnacha

geo:es:arribes.name: Arribes
geo:es:arribes.description: Arribes is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla y León region of Spain. It is characterized by its rugged terrain and the presence of the Duero River, which influences the local climate. The appellation is known for its traditional winemaking practices and the use of indigenous grape varieties. The wines produced here are primarily red, showcasing the unique terroir of the area.
geo:es:arribes.style: Red wines with a focus on indigenous grape varieties

geo:us:arroyo-grande-valley.name: Arroyo Grande Valley
geo:us:arroyo-grande-valley.description: Arroyo Grande Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within the Central Coast region of California. The appellation is characterized by its cool coastal climate, which is influenced by marine fog and breezes from the Pacific Ocean. The valley's diverse soils and topography contribute to the cultivation of a variety of grape types, with a focus on both red and white varieties. The AVA is known for producing high-quality wines, particularly from Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.
geo:us:arroyo-grande-valley.style: Notable for cool-climate varietals, especially whites and Pinot Noir

geo:pt:arruda.name: Arruda
geo:pt:arruda.description: Arruda is a designated appellation (DOC) located within the Lisboa wine region of Portugal. It is characterized by its production of red wines primarily made from local grape varieties. The appellation's boundaries encompass specific areas that are recognized for their unique terroir and viticultural practices.
geo:pt:arruda.style: Red wines with regional varietal characteristics

geo:it:asolo-prosecco-superiore-docg.name: Asolo Prosecco Superiore
geo:it:asolo-prosecco-superiore-docg.description: Asolo Prosecco Superiore is a designated appellation within the Veneto region of Italy, classified as a DOCG. It is situated in the hilly area surrounding the town of Asolo, which provides a unique terroir characterized by well-drained soils and a favorable microclimate. The appellation is known for producing high-quality sparkling wines primarily made from the Glera grape. The legal boundaries of Asolo Prosecco Superiore encompass specific municipalities, ensuring strict adherence to production standards.
geo:it:asolo-prosecco-superiore-docg.style: Sparkling wine with a focus on freshness and floral notes

geo:it:asti.name: Asti
geo:it:asti.description: Asti is a DOCG appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, primarily known for its production of sparkling wines made from the Moscato grape. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is conducive to the cultivation of aromatic varieties. Asti wines are typically produced using the Charmat method, which preserves the fresh and fruity characteristics of the Moscato grape. The legal boundaries of the appellation include several municipalities within the province of Asti, and it is recognized for its sweet and effervescent style.
geo:it:asti.style: Sweet sparkling wines made from Moscato grapes

geo:us:atlas-peak.name: Atlas Peak
geo:us:atlas-peak.description: Atlas Peak is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. Established in 1992, it is situated in the eastern part of Napa Valley, characterized by its mountainous terrain and elevation, which ranges from 1,400 to 2,600 feet. The region benefits from a cooler climate compared to the valley floor, allowing for a longer growing season that contributes to the development of complex flavors in the grapes. The AVA is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grapes.
geo:us:atlas-peak.style: Red wines with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot

geo:fr:auxey-duresses.name: Auxey-Duresses
geo:fr:auxey-duresses.description: Auxey-Duresses is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated to the west of the village of Meursault and is characterized by its sloping vineyards that benefit from a favorable microclimate. The wines from Auxey-Duresses often exhibit a balance of fruit and earthiness, reflecting the region's terroir.
geo:fr:auxey-duresses.style: Red wines with fruit-forward character and earthy undertones

geo:de:baden.name: Baden
geo:de:baden.description: Germany's warmest and third-largest wine region, stretching along the Rhine from Heidelberg to Basel. The Kaiserstuhl's volcanic soils produce Germany's best Spätburgunder (Pinot Noir). Grauburgunder (Pinot Gris) and Weissburgunder (Pinot Blanc) also excel here. Baden is Germany's only region in EU wine zone B.

geo:de:baden-qba.name: Baden
geo:de:baden-qba.description: Baden is an appellation located in southwestern Germany, characterized by its diverse microclimates and varied soils. It is the third-largest wine region in Germany, stretching along the Rhine River from the French border to the north of Lake Constance. The region is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on Pinot varieties and other international grapes.
geo:de:baden-qba.style: Diverse wines, primarily focused on Pinot Noir and Riesling

geo:pt:bairrada.name: Bairrada
geo:pt:bairrada.description: Home of Baga, Portugal's most tannic grape. Clay soils near the Atlantic produce powerful reds that demand aging but reward patience with complexity. Sparkling wines, made traditionally, are an underrated specialty. Luís Pato leads the quality revolution.

geo:pt:bairrada-doc.name: Bairrada
geo:pt:bairrada-doc.description: Bairrada is an appellation located in central Portugal, defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the municipalities of Aveiro and Coimbra. The region is characterized by a maritime climate, influenced by the Atlantic Ocean, which contributes to its unique terroir. Bairrada is known for its limestone and clay soils, which are conducive to viticulture. The appellation primarily produces red wines from the Baga grape, as well as white wines from varieties such as Bical and Maria Gomes.
geo:pt:bairrada-doc.style: Red wines with high acidity and aging potential

geo:fr:bandol-aoc.name: Bandol
geo:fr:bandol-aoc.description: Provence's most serious red wine appellation, where Mourvèdre rules. The terraced hillsides above the Mediterranean produce wines of remarkable depth — meaty, herbal, built to age. Minimum 18 months in barrel is required. The rosés are exceptional too. Bandol rewards patience.

geo:za:banghoek-wo.name: Banghoek
geo:za:banghoek-wo.description: Banghoek is a wine appellation located within the Stellenbosch district of South Africa, classified under the Wine of Origin (WO) system. It is situated in a sheltered mountain valley between the Helshoogte Pass and Simonsberg, which provides a unique microclimate conducive to viticulture. The region is known for its notable estates, including Delaire Graff, and produces a range of wines, primarily red. The terroir is characterized by diverse soils and varying altitudes, contributing to the complexity of the wines.
geo:za:banghoek-wo.style: Primarily red wines with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon.

geo:fr:banyuls.name: Banyuls
geo:fr:banyuls.description: Banyuls is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, known for its fortified wines produced primarily from Grenache grapes. The appellation is situated along the Mediterranean coast, characterized by steep terraced vineyards that benefit from a warm, sunny climate and the influence of the sea. Banyuls wines are typically made using a mutage process, where fermentation is halted by the addition of grape spirit, resulting in a rich, sweet profile. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific communes, ensuring the preservation of traditional production methods and local terroir.
geo:fr:banyuls.style: Rich, sweet fortified wines with complex flavors

geo:it:barbaresco.name: Barbaresco
geo:it:barbaresco.description: Barolo's elegant sibling. The slightly lower altitude and different soils produce Nebbiolo of less power but greater early charm. Gaja made Barbaresco famous; Angelo Gaja remains its ambassador. The best examples rival Barolo in complexity while offering earlier accessibility.

geo:it:barbera-dalba.name: Barbera d'Alba
geo:it:barbera-dalba.description: Barbera d'Alba is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Barbera grape variety, characterized by their vibrant acidity and fruit-forward profiles. The appellation encompasses several communes around the town of Alba, which are recognized for their unique terroir and favorable climate for viticulture.
geo:it:barbera-dalba.style: Red wines with high acidity and rich fruit flavors

geo:it:barbera-dasti.name: Barbera d'Asti
geo:it:barbera-dasti.description: Barbera d'Asti is a DOCG appellation located in the Asti province of Piedmont, Italy. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass several communes, including Asti, Nizza Monferrato, and Costigliole d'Asti. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Barbera, which thrives in the region's clay and limestone soils. The wines produced here are characterized by their vibrant acidity and fruit-forward profiles, often showcasing flavors of red berries and cherries. The appellation also allows for the production of Barbera d'Asti Superiore, which requires a minimum aging period and typically exhibits greater complexity.
geo:it:barbera-dasti.style: Fruity red wines with high acidity and moderate tannins

geo:it:bardolino.name: Bardolino
geo:it:bardolino.description: Bardolino is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. It is situated along the eastern shore of Lake Garda, characterized by its hilly terrain and moderate climate. The appellation primarily produces red wines, which are typically light and fruity, with a focus on early consumption. The legal boundaries of Bardolino encompass several communes, including Bardolino, Garda, and Lazise.
geo:it:bardolino.style: Light-bodied red wines with fruity and floral notes

geo:it:barolo.name: Barolo
geo:it:barolo.description: The wine of kings, the king of wines. Nebbiolo in the Langhe hills produces wines of pale color and immense structure — tar and roses, iron and cherry. Traditionalists age in large oak; modernists use barriques. Both can be profound. Barolo demands patience and rewards it.

geo:au:barossa-valley-gi.name: Barossa Valley
geo:au:barossa-valley-gi.description: Barossa Valley is a designated Geographical Indication (GI) located in South Australia, for its rich viticultural heritage. The region is characterized by a warm Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the production of full-bodied red wines. Barossa Valley is particularly noted for its Shiraz, which thrives in the area's diverse soils and climate conditions, resulting in wines with concentrated flavors and aging potential.
geo:au:barossa-valley-gi.style: Full-bodied red wines, primarily based on Shiraz

geo:fr:barsac.name: Barsac
geo:fr:barsac.description: Barsac is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, primarily known for its sweet white wines. The appellation is situated on the left bank of the Garonne River, adjacent to the Sauternes AOC, and benefits from a microclimate conducive to the development of noble rot, or Botrytis cinerea. Barsac wines are characterized by their rich, honeyed flavors and complexity, resulting from the late harvest of grapes. The appellation has a long history of wine production, with its wines being recognized since the 17th century.
geo:fr:barsac.style: Sweet white wines with rich flavors and complexity

geo:fr:beaujolais-aoc.name: Beaujolais
geo:fr:beaujolais-aoc.description: Beaujolais is an appellation located in the Burgundy region of France, primarily known for producing red wines made from the Gamay grape. The appellation encompasses several distinct areas, including Beaujolais Nouveau, which is released shortly after harvest. The region's granite and clay soils, along with a temperate climate, contribute to the characteristic fruit-forward style of its wines.
geo:fr:beaujolais-aoc.style: Light-bodied red wines, often fruity and low in tannins

geo:fr:beaujolais-villages.name: Beaujolais-Villages
geo:fr:beaujolais-villages.description: Beaujolais-Villages is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, encompassing a designated area for the production of red wines primarily made from the Gamay grape. The appellation includes several communes that are situated outside the ten crus of Beaujolais, allowing for a broader range of terroirs while still adhering to the quality standards set by the AOC regulations. The wines produced here are typically characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and are often consumed young.
geo:fr:beaujolais-villages.style: Light-bodied red wines with fruity characteristics

geo:fr:beaumes-de-venise.name: Beaumes-de-Venise
geo:fr:beaumes-de-venise.description: Beaumes-de-Venise is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Beaumes-de-Venise and is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes limestone and clay soils. The climate is Mediterranean, contributing to the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are often blends, showcasing the region's unique characteristics.
geo:fr:beaumes-de-venise.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice notes

geo:fr:beaune.name: Beaune
geo:fr:beaune.description: Beaune is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for its red wines made from Pinot Noir, which thrive in the region's varied terroir. The appellation encompasses the town of Beaune and surrounding vineyards, which benefit from a continental climate and diverse soil types. Beaune wines are characterized by their elegance and complexity, often reflecting the specific sites from which they originate.
geo:fr:beaune.style: Red wines known for elegance and complexity, primarily from Pinot Noir

geo:au:beechworth-gi.name: Beechworth
geo:au:beechworth-gi.description: Beechworth is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia. The region is characterized by its high altitude and diverse microclimates, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. Beechworth is particularly noted for its Chardonnay, Shiraz, and Pinot Noir, with a focus on both varietal and blended wines that reflect the unique terroir of the area.
geo:au:beechworth-gi.style: White wines with notable Chardonnay and Shiraz production

geo:fr:bellet.name: Bellet
geo:fr:bellet.description: Bellet is an AOC located in the Provence region of France, characterized by its unique hillside vineyards overlooking Nice. The appellation is known for its diverse microclimates and varied soils, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The legal boundaries of Bellet encompass approximately 150 hectares, with a focus on small-scale production. The region primarily produces white wines, often blending local and international grape varieties.
geo:fr:bellet.style: White wines with floral and citrus characteristics

geo:us:bennett-valley.name: Bennett Valley
geo:us:bennett-valley.description: Bennett Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 2003, it is characterized by its cooler climate, influenced by nearby San Pablo Bay, which allows for a longer growing season. The AVA is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from Merlot and Syrah, as well as Chardonnay. The diverse soils and elevation variations contribute to the complexity of the wines produced here.
geo:us:bennett-valley.style: Red wines with notable Merlot and Syrah expressions

geo:fr:bergerac.name: Bergerac
geo:fr:bergerac.description: Bergerac is an AOC (Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée) located in the South West France region. It encompasses a variety of vineyards situated along the Dordogne River, characterized by a diverse range of soils and microclimates. The appellation primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Malbec. Bergerac has a historical significance in French viticulture, being one of the oldest wine-producing areas in the country, with records dating back to the Roman era.
geo:fr:bergerac.style: Red wines, typically blends with fruit-forward profiles

geo:de:bernkastel.name: Bernkastel
geo:de:bernkastel.description: Bernkastel is a designated wine appellation within the Mosel region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is situated along the Mosel River, known for its steep vineyards and slate soil, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The appellation is particularly for its production of high-quality white wines, primarily from the Riesling grape, which thrives in the region's cool climate. Bernkastel encompasses several notable villages and is recognized for its distinct wine styles that reflect the local geology and microclimates.
geo:de:bernkastel.style: White wines with a focus on Riesling

geo:it:bianco-di-pitigliano.name: Bianco di Pitigliano
geo:it:bianco-di-pitigliano.description: Bianco di Pitigliano is a white wine appellation located within the Maremma region of Italy, classified as a DOC. The appellation is known for its production of white wines primarily from the Trebbiano Toscano, Greco, and Malvasia Bianca Lunga grape varieties. The legal boundaries of Bianco di Pitigliano encompass specific areas around the town of Pitigliano, characterized by volcanic soils and a Mediterranean climate that contribute to the wine's distinctive profile.
geo:it:bianco-di-pitigliano.style: Crisp white wines with floral and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:bienvenues-batard-montrachet.name: Bienvenues-Batard-Montrachet
geo:fr:bienvenues-batard-montrachet.description: Bienvenues-Batard-Montrachet is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is situated adjacent to the more Batard-Montrachet and is part of the Montrachet grand cru vineyard area. The appellation is exclusively dedicated to the production of white wines made from Chardonnay grapes. The terroir is characterized by limestone soils, which contribute to the wines' mineral complexity and aging potential. Bienvenues-Batard-Montrachet wines are typically noted for their elegance and balance.
geo:fr:bienvenues-batard-montrachet.style: Elegant white wines with mineral complexity and aging potential

geo:es:bierzo-do.name: Bierzo
geo:es:bierzo-do.description: Bierzo is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the northwest of Spain, within the autonomous community of Castilla y León. The region is characterized by its diverse geography, including mountainous terrain and river valleys, which contribute to a unique microclimate suitable for viticulture. The predominant grape variety in Bierzo is Mencía, known for producing red wines with distinct aromatic profiles and moderate acidity. The DO was officially established in 1989, reflecting a growing recognition of its wine quality and heritage.
geo:es:bierzo-do.style: Red wines, primarily from Mencía, with aromatic complexity

geo:it:biferno.name: Biferno
geo:it:biferno.description: Biferno is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Molise region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass specific areas within Molise. Biferno primarily produces red wines, with a focus on Montepulciano and Aglianico grape varieties, alongside white wines made from Trebbiano d'Abruzzo. The terroir is characterized by a mix of hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate, contributing to the distinctiveness of the wines.
geo:it:biferno.style: Red wines with a focus on Montepulciano and Aglianico

geo:es:bizkaiko-txakolina.name: Bizkaiko Txakolina
geo:es:bizkaiko-txakolina.description: Bizkaiko Txakolina is a designated wine appellation (DO) located in the Basque Country of Spain. It is primarily known for producing white wines, which are characterized by their freshness and acidity. The region benefits from a maritime climate, influenced by the nearby Atlantic Ocean, and features a mix of hilly terrain and fertile valleys.
geo:es:bizkaiko-txakolina.style: Crisp, refreshing white wines with high acidity

geo:fr:blanquette-de-limoux.name: Blanquette de Limoux
geo:fr:blanquette-de-limoux.description: Blanquette de Limoux is an AOC sparkling wine produced in the Limoux region of Languedoc, France. The appellation is notable for its historical significance as one of the earliest regions to produce sparkling wine, with records dating back to the 16th century. The production method typically involves secondary fermentation in the bottle, known as méthode traditionnelle, which contributes to its effervescence.
geo:fr:blanquette-de-limoux.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with a focus on freshness and complexity

geo:fr:blaye-cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:blaye-cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, situated on the right bank of the Gironde estuary. The appellation is characterized by its rolling hills and clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the production of red wines primarily from Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, with a focus on producing wines that reflect the local terroir and climate. The wines are typically approachable and fruit-forward, with a balance of acidity and tannin.
geo:fr:blaye-cotes-de-bordeaux.style: Fruit-forward red wines with balanced acidity and tannin

geo:it:boca.name: Boca
geo:it:boca.description: Boca is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is situated in the northern part of the region, characterized by hilly terrain and a continental climate that influences the viticulture. The appellation is known for its red wines, primarily produced from the Nebbiolo grape, often blended with other local varieties. Boca's wines are noted for their structure and aging potential, reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:it:boca.style: Structured red wines with aging potential

geo:it:bolgheri-doc.name: Bolgheri
geo:it:bolgheri-doc.description: Tuscany's coastal revolution. When Sassicaia proved that Cabernet could thrive here, an appellation was born. The maritime influence — sea breezes, sandy soils — produces wines different from inland Tuscany. International varieties dominate, but the results are distinctly Italian.

geo:it:bolgheri-sassicaia.name: Bolgheri Sassicaia
geo:it:bolgheri-sassicaia.description: Bolgheri Sassicaia is a prestigious red wine appellation located within the Bolgheri region of Tuscany, Italy. Established as a DOC in 1994, it is known for its strict production regulations that emphasize quality. The appellation is renowned for its unique terroir, characterized by a combination of maritime influence and well-drained soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. Bolgheri Sassicaia is primarily associated with the Bordeaux grape varieties Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, which are cultivated in this area to produce wines of notable structure and aging potential.
geo:it:bolgheri-sassicaia.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and aging potential.

geo:fr:bonnes-mares.name: Bonnes-Mares
geo:fr:bonnes-mares.description: Bonnes-Mares is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits region of Burgundy, France. It is classified as a Grand Cru vineyard and is known exclusively for producing red wine from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation encompasses a small area that benefits from a favorable microclimate and diverse soil types, contributing to the complexity of the wines. Bonnes-Mares is situated between the villages of Chambolle-Musigny and Morey-Saint-Denis, with its vineyards primarily facing east and southeast, allowing for optimal sun exposure.
geo:fr:bonnes-mares.style: Red wine with depth and complexity, showcasing Burgundy's terroir

geo:fr:bonnezeaux.name: Bonnezeaux
geo:fr:bonnezeaux.description: Bonnezeaux is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, specifically within the Anjou-Saumur region. It is for producing sweet dessert wines primarily from the Chenin Blanc grape, which benefits from the region's unique microclimate and botrytis cinerea. The appellation is situated on the banks of the Loire River, characterized by its schist and clay soils that contribute to the complexity of the wines. Bonnezeaux wines are typically harvested late to achieve optimal ripeness and are known for their rich, honeyed flavors and balancing acidity.
geo:fr:bonnezeaux.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich flavors and good acidity

geo:fr:bordeaux-aoc.name: Bordeaux
geo:fr:bordeaux-aoc.description: Bordeaux is a prominent wine appellation located in southwestern France, known for its diverse range of red, white, and sweet wines. The region is divided into several subregions, including the Left Bank, Right Bank, and Entre-Deux-Mers, each with distinct terroirs and grape varieties. Bordeaux wines are primarily blends, with Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Cabernet Franc being the most significant red varieties. The appellation has a long history of viticulture, dating back to Roman times, and is recognized for its influence on global wine production.
geo:fr:bordeaux-aoc.style: Diverse blends, known for structure and aging potential

geo:fr:bordeaux-superieur.name: Bordeaux Supérieur
geo:fr:bordeaux-superieur.description: Bordeaux Supérieur is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, designated for red wines. It encompasses a broader area than the more specific Bordeaux AOC, allowing for a greater range of vineyard sites and conditions. The appellation's regulations permit the use of several grape varieties, primarily focusing on Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, which thrive in the region's diverse terroirs. Bordeaux Supérieur wines are typically characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate aging potential.
geo:fr:bordeaux-superieur.style: Fruit-forward red wines with moderate aging potential

geo:fr:bourgogne.name: Bourgogne
geo:fr:bourgogne.description: Bourgogne is an AOC appellation located in the Bourgogne Régional region of France. It encompasses a broad area and is primarily known for producing red wines made from Pinot Noir. The appellation's legal boundaries include various communes within the Bourgogne region, allowing for a diverse range of terroirs and expressions of the grape.
geo:fr:bourgogne.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with varying complexity

geo:fr:bourgogne-aligote.name: Bourgogne Aligoté
geo:fr:bourgogne-aligote.description: Bourgogne Aligoté is an AOC located within the Bourgogne Régional region of France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Aligoté grape variety. The appellation encompasses a range of vineyards across the Bourgogne region, characterized by diverse terroirs that influence the wine's profile. Bourgogne Aligoté wines are typically light and crisp, often consumed as an aperitif or paired with food.
geo:fr:bourgogne-aligote.style: Light, crisp white wines primarily from Aligoté grapes

geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-beaune.name: Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Beaune
geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-beaune.description: Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Beaune is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is situated on the elevated slopes to the west of the main Côte d'Or vineyards. The appellation primarily produces red wines from Pinot Noir, characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins. The vineyards benefit from a continental climate, with a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the wine's structure and complexity.
geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-beaune.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-nuits.name: Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Nuits
geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-nuits.description: Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Nuits is an appellation located in the Côte de Nuits region of Burgundy, France. It is classified as an AOC and encompasses vineyards situated on the slopes above the main Côte de Nuits vineyards. The appellation primarily produces red wines made from Pinot Noir, which benefit from the region's diverse terroir and varying altitudes. The wines are characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins, reflecting the cooler climate of the higher elevations.
geo:fr:bourgogne-hautes-cotes-de-nuits.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:bourgueil.name: Bourgueil
geo:fr:bourgueil.description: Bourgueil is an AOC located in the Touraine region of France, primarily known for its red wines made from Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Loire River, benefiting from a continental climate that influences the ripening of grapes. Bourgueil encompasses several communes, with a diverse range of soils including clay, limestone, and gravel, contributing to the complexity of its wines. The wines of Bourgueil are characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins, often exhibiting a balance of freshness and structure.
geo:fr:bourgueil.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:boutenac.name: Boutenac
geo:fr:boutenac.description: Boutenac is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, primarily known for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Boutenac, within the Corbières subregion, and is characterized by its Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The legal boundaries of Boutenac encompass specific vineyards that are recognized for their quality and potential. The permitted grape varieties include Carignan, Grenache, and Syrah, among others, which are often blended to create wines that reflect the terroir's unique characteristics.
geo:fr:boutenac.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and earthy notes.

geo:fr:bouzeron.name: Bouzeron
geo:fr:bouzeron.description: Bouzeron is an AOC located in the Côte Chalonnaise region of France, recognized for its production of white wines primarily made from the Aligoté grape. The appellation is situated near the village of Bouzeron, which is at the northern end of the Côte Chalonnaise. The terroir features clay-limestone soils and a continental climate, contributing to the unique characteristics of the wines produced here. Bouzeron is notable for being one of the few appellations in Burgundy to focus on Aligoté, which is often overshadowed by Chardonnay in the region.
geo:fr:bouzeron.style: Fresh, crisp white wines with mineral notes

geo:it:brachetto-dacqui.name: Brachetto d'Acqui
geo:it:brachetto-dacqui.description: Brachetto d'Acqui is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing sweet, sparkling red wines made from the Brachetto grape. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that supports the cultivation of aromatic varieties, contributing to the distinctive flavor profile of the wines.
geo:it:brachetto-dacqui.style: Sweet, sparkling red wines with aromatic profiles

geo:it:bramaterra.name: Bramaterra
geo:it:bramaterra.description: Bramaterra is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is situated in the northern part of the region, primarily within the province of Biella. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The wines produced here are predominantly red, with a focus on traditional grape varieties that reflect the local viticultural heritage.
geo:it:bramaterra.style: Red wines with structure and complexity, often with aging potential

geo:it:breganze.name: Breganze
geo:it:breganze.description: Breganze is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes hilly terrain and a climate conducive to viticulture. Breganze is known for producing a range of wines, with a focus on white varieties, particularly Vespaiola. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several municipalities, ensuring the preservation of local winemaking traditions.
geo:it:breganze.style: White wines with a focus on Vespaiola

geo:de:breisgau.name: Breisgau
geo:de:breisgau.description: Breisgau is a designated Bereich within the Baden wine region of Germany. It is characterized by a diverse range of microclimates and soils, contributing to the production of various wine styles. The area is primarily known for its red wines, particularly those made from Spätburgunder, as well as white wines from Müller-Thurgau and Grauburgunder. The region benefits from the warm climate influenced by the Rhine River, which aids in the ripening of grapes.
geo:de:breisgau.style: Red wines with notable white varieties

geo:fr:brouilly.name: Brouilly
geo:fr:brouilly.description: Brouilly is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known for producing red wines primarily from the Gamay grape. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, including granite and clay soils, which contribute to the wine's characteristics. Brouilly is situated on the southern slopes of Mont Brouilly, benefiting from a temperate climate that enhances grape ripening. The wines are typically light to medium-bodied with fruit-forward profiles and are intended for early consumption.
geo:fr:brouilly.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with fruit-forward profiles

geo:it:brunello-di-montalcino.name: Brunello di Montalcino
geo:it:brunello-di-montalcino.description: Tuscany's most powerful expression of Sangiovese. The warm slopes around Montalcino produce wines of deep color and firm tannin — rare qualities in a grape often light and tart. Minimum five years aging is required. The best examples age for decades, developing haunting complexity.

geo:pt:bucelas.name: Bucelas
geo:pt:bucelas.description: Bucelas is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located within the Lisboa region of Portugal. It is primarily known for its white wines, which are produced under specific legal boundaries that emphasize quality and regional characteristics. The appellation benefits from a Mediterranean climate, with influences from the Atlantic Ocean, contributing to the unique terroir of the area.
geo:pt:bucelas.style: Crisp and aromatic white wines with notable acidity

geo:fr:bugey.name: Bugey
geo:fr:bugey.description: Bugey is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, known for its diverse wine production. The appellation encompasses several communes and is characterized by a mix of hilly terrain and valleys, which contribute to its varied microclimates. Bugey produces both still and sparkling wines, with a focus on traditional methods for the latter. The region's historical significance includes a long-standing viticultural tradition dating back to the Roman era.
geo:fr:bugey.style: Sparkling wines with some still varieties

geo:es:bullas.name: Bullas
geo:es:bullas.description: Bullas is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Murcia region of Spain. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which features hot summers and mild winters, conducive to grape cultivation. The legal boundaries of Bullas encompass several municipalities, with a focus on producing red wines primarily from Monastrell and Tempranillo grapes. The region's diverse soil types, including clay and limestone, contribute to the complexity of its wines.
geo:es:bullas.style: Predominantly red wines with a focus on Monastrell

geo:at:burgenland.name: Burgenland
geo:at:burgenland.description: Austria's warmest region, where Pannonian heat meets the moderating Neusiedlersee. Blaufränkisch produces serious reds; the humid lakeshores create conditions for noble-rot Ausbruch. Increasingly recognized for both dry and sweet wines of character.

geo:at:burgenland-dac.name: Burgenland
geo:at:burgenland-dac.description: Burgenland is an Austrian appellation located in the eastern part of the country, characterized by its diverse terroir and favorable climate for viticulture. The region is known for its wide range of wine styles, including red, white, and dessert wines. Notable areas within Burgenland include Neusiedlersee, known for its sweet wines, and Eisenstadt, which produces high-quality red wines.
geo:at:burgenland-dac.style: Diverse wines including red, white, and dessert styles

geo:fr:buzet.name: Buzet
geo:fr:buzet.description: Buzet is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the Garonne River and benefits from a temperate climate that influences grape ripening. Buzet's vineyards are characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines. The AOC was established in 1973 and emphasizes the use of local grape varieties.
geo:fr:buzet.style: Red wines with a blend of fruit and earthy characteristics

geo:fr:batard-montrachet.name: Bâtard-Montrachet
geo:fr:batard-montrachet.description: Bâtard-Montrachet is an AOC Grand Cru appellation located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality white wines exclusively from the Chardonnay grape. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Mont Rachet hill, benefiting from a favorable microclimate and limestone-rich soils that contribute to the complexity and richness of the wines. Bâtard-Montrachet is adjacent to other Grand Cru vineyards, including Le Montrachet and Bienvenues-Bâtard-Montrachet, and is characterized by its distinctive terroir that imparts unique qualities to the wines.
geo:fr:batard-montrachet.style: Rich, full-bodied white wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:cabardes.name: Cabardès
geo:fr:cabardes.description: Cabardès is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, known for its red wines. The appellation is situated near the city of Carcassonne and benefits from a unique climate influenced by both the Atlantic and Mediterranean. The legal boundaries of Cabardès encompass a diverse range of soils, including limestone and clay, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The AOC regulations permit a blend of grape varieties, allowing for a range of styles within the red wine category.
geo:fr:cabardes.style: Red wines with a blend of Mediterranean and Atlantic influences

geo:fr:cabernet-danjou.name: Cabernet d'Anjou
geo:fr:cabernet-danjou.description: Cabernet d'Anjou is an appellation located within the Anjou-Saumur region of France, specifically designated for the production of rosé wines. The appellation is characterized by its use of Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc grapes, which are blended to create wines that are typically off-dry to semi-sweet. The legal boundaries of Cabernet d'Anjou encompass specific areas within the Loire Valley, where the climate and soil conditions contribute to the unique profile of the wines produced here.

geo:cl:cachapoal-valley.name: Cachapoal Valley
geo:cl:cachapoal-valley.description: Cachapoal Valley is a designated wine region within the Rapel Valley in Chile, classified as a Denominación de Origen (DO). It is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from the Cabernet Sauvignon and Carmenere grape varieties. The valley benefits from a Mediterranean climate, characterized by warm days and cool nights, which contributes to the development of flavor and balance in the wines.
geo:cl:cachapoal-valley.style: Red wines with rich fruit and structured tannins.

geo:fr:cadillac.name: Cadillac
geo:fr:cadillac.description: Cadillac is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, specifically known for its white wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Garonne River, near the town of Cadillac. It benefits from a temperate maritime climate, which is conducive to the cultivation of white grape varieties. Cadillac is recognized for its sweet white wines, often produced through late harvesting methods that enhance the concentration of sugars and flavors.
geo:fr:cadillac.style: Sweet white wines with rich flavors and balanced acidity

geo:fr:cadillac-cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Cadillac Cotes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:cadillac-cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux is an AOC located within the Bordeaux wine region of France. It is situated on the right bank of the Garonne River, adjacent to the Entre-Deux-Mers region. The appellation is known for its red wines, which are primarily produced from Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The terroir features clay and limestone soils, contributing to the character of the wines. Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux is recognized for its approachable and fruit-forward style, often consumed young.
geo:fr:cadillac-cotes-de-bordeaux.style: Red wines, typically fruit-forward and approachable

geo:ar:cafayate-ig.name: Cafayate
geo:ar:cafayate-ig.description: Cafayate is an Indicación Geográfica (IG) located within the Salta province of Argentina. It is characterized by high-altitude vineyards that benefit from a unique microclimate, resulting in distinctively aromatic wines. The region is particularly noted for its production of Torrontés, a white grape variety that thrives in the area's conditions, alongside red varieties such as Malbec and Tannat.
geo:ar:cafayate-ig.style: Aromatic white wines, with some notable reds

geo:fr:cahors.name: Cahors
geo:fr:cahors.description: Cahors is an AOC located in the South West France region, known primarily for its red wines. The appellation is characterized by its limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the distinctive profile of its wines. The legal boundaries of Cahors encompass several communes, with a focus on the Lot River valley. The region has a historical significance dating back to Roman times, and it is often associated with the Malbec grape, which is the dominant variety used in its production.
geo:fr:cahors.style: Full-bodied red wines, primarily from Malbec, with dark fruit flavors

geo:fr:cairanne.name: Cairanne
geo:fr:cairanne.description: Cairanne is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. The appellation is known for its red wines, primarily produced from Grenache and Syrah grapes. The legal boundaries of Cairanne encompass a specific area within the Vaucluse department, characterized by a diverse terroir that includes clay, limestone, and sandy soils. The climate is Mediterranean, contributing to the ripening of grapes and the development of the wine's character. Cairanne wines often exhibit a balance of fruit and structure, reflecting the unique conditions of the region.
geo:fr:cairanne.style: Red wines with balanced fruit and structure

geo:es:calatayud-do.name: Calatayud
geo:es:calatayud-do.description: Calatayud is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Aragón region of Spain, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is characterized by its high altitude vineyards, which benefit from a continental climate with significant temperature variations between day and night. The region's soils are predominantly clay and limestone, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced. Calatayud has a historical significance in Spanish viticulture, with evidence of winemaking dating back to Roman times.
geo:es:calatayud-do.style: Predominantly red wines with fruit-forward profiles and good acidity

geo:ar:calchaqui-valley.name: Calchaquí Valley
geo:ar:calchaqui-valley.description: The Calchaquí Valley is a Geographical Indication (GI) located within the Salta region of Argentina. It is characterized by high-altitude vineyards that benefit from a unique climate, which contributes to the development of distinctive wine styles. The valley is known for its diverse terroir, which includes a range of soil types and microclimates, allowing for the cultivation of various grape varieties. The primary focus of production in this appellation includes both red and white wines, with a notable emphasis on Malbec and Torrontés.
geo:ar:calchaqui-valley.style: Primarily red wines with notable freshness and aromatic complexity.

geo:us:calistoga-ava.name: Calistoga
geo:us:calistoga-ava.description: Calistoga is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in the northern part of Napa Valley, California. The appellation is characterized by its warm climate and diverse soil types, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. It is known primarily for red wine production, with a focus on robust varieties that thrive in the region's conditions.
geo:us:calistoga-ava.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich flavors and firm tannins

geo:it:campi-flegrei.name: Campi Flegrei
geo:it:campi-flegrei.description: Campi Flegrei is a designated DOC appellation located in the Campania region of Italy. It encompasses a volcanic landscape near Naples, characterized by its unique geological features and Mediterranean climate. The appellation primarily produces white wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties that reflect the local terroir.
geo:it:campi-flegrei.style: White wines with distinct mineral and citrus characteristics

geo:es:campo-de-borja-do.name: Campo de Borja
geo:es:campo-de-borja-do.description: Campo de Borja is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Aragón region of Spain. It is situated to the southwest of Zaragoza and is characterized by a continental climate with significant temperature variations between day and night. The appellation is known primarily for its red wines, which are predominantly made from the Garnacha grape variety, along with other permitted varieties. The region's diverse soils, including clay and limestone, contribute to the unique expression of its wines.
geo:es:campo-de-borja-do.style: Predominantly red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:au:canberra-district.name: Canberra District
geo:au:canberra-district.description: Canberra District is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in New South Wales, Australia. It encompasses a range of vineyards situated in a cool climate, which contributes to the production of high-quality wines. The region is known for its diverse soil types and elevation, leading to a variety of wine styles, particularly red and white wines. The principal grape varieties cultivated in this appellation include Shiraz, Riesling, and Viognier, which thrive in the region's conditions.
geo:au:canberra-district.style: Known for balanced reds and aromatic whites.

geo:it:cannonau-di-sardegna.name: Cannonau di Sardegna
geo:it:cannonau-di-sardegna.description: Cannonau di Sardegna is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) appellation located in Sardinia, Italy. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Cannonau grape, which is genetically related to Grenache. The appellation encompasses various zones across the island, benefiting from a Mediterranean climate and diverse soils that contribute to the wine's character.
geo:it:cannonau-di-sardegna.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and herbal notes

geo:fr:canon-fronsac.name: Canon-Fronsac
geo:fr:canon-fronsac.description: Canon-Fronsac is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, known primarily for its red wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Dordogne River, adjacent to the Fronsac appellation. It encompasses a variety of terroirs, characterized by clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. The climate is maritime, influenced by the nearby river, which helps to moderate temperature extremes. Canon-Fronsac wines typically exhibit a blend of fruitiness and structure, often benefiting from aging potential.
geo:fr:canon-fronsac.style: Structured red wines with fruit-forward profiles and aging potential

geo:pt:carcavelos.name: Carcavelos
geo:pt:carcavelos.description: Carcavelos is a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located in the Lisboa region of Portugal. It is situated near the coastal town of Carcavelos, characterized by its Mediterranean climate and proximity to the Atlantic Ocean. The appellation is known for producing fortified wines, primarily from indigenous grape varieties. The legal boundaries of Carcavelos encompass specific vineyard areas that benefit from unique terroir conditions, including sandy soils and maritime influences.
geo:pt:carcavelos.style: Fortified wines with a distinctive character and aging potential

geo:it:carignano-del-sulcis.name: Carignano del Sulcis
geo:it:carignano-del-sulcis.description: Carignano del Sulcis is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Sulcis region of Sardinia, Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its red wines made from the Carignano grape variety, which thrives in the sandy soils and Mediterranean climate of the area. The legal boundaries of the DOC encompass several municipalities, and the wines are characterized by their deep color and robust structure, often exhibiting ripe fruit and herbal notes.
geo:it:carignano-del-sulcis.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and herbal characteristics

geo:es:carinena.name: Cariñena
geo:es:carinena.description: Cariñena is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Aragón region of Spain. Established in 1932, it is one of the oldest wine regions in the country. The appellation is characterized by a continental climate with significant temperature variations between day and night, which influences the ripening of grapes. The region's diverse soil types, including clay, limestone, and sandy soils, contribute to the complexity of its wines.
geo:es:carinena.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:it:carmignano.name: Carmignano
geo:it:carmignano.description: Carmignano is a DOCG appellation located in Tuscany, Italy, recognized for its production of red wines. The appellation has historical significance, with winemaking in the area dating back to the Etruscan period. It is situated near the town of Carmignano, characterized by hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate that contributes to the quality of the grapes grown here.
geo:it:carmignano.style: Red wines primarily based on Sangiovese, often blended with Canaiolo and Cabernet varieties

geo:us:carneros-ava.name: Carneros
geo:us:carneros-ava.description: Carneros is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, straddling the counties of Napa and Sonoma. It is characterized by its cool climate, influenced by the nearby San Pablo Bay, which allows for a longer growing season. The region is known for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, benefiting from the marine breezes that moderate temperatures. Carneros is recognized for its distinct terroir, which includes clay and volcanic soils, contributing to the unique characteristics of the wines produced here.
geo:us:carneros-ava.style: Red and white wines with a focus on Pinot Noir and Chardonnay

geo:at:carnuntum.name: Carnuntum
geo:at:carnuntum.description: Carnuntum is a designated Austrian wine appellation located within the Niederösterreich region. It is classified as a DAC (Districtus Austriae Controllatus), which emphasizes the production of high-quality wines that reflect the local terroir. The appellation is known primarily for its red wines, particularly those made from the Zweigelt and Blaufränkisch grape varieties, which thrive in the region's favorable climate and diverse soils.
geo:at:carnuntum.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit and structure

geo:it:carso-doc.name: Carso
geo:it:carso-doc.description: Carso is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its unique karst limestone soil and a climate influenced by both the Adriatic Sea and the nearby Alps. The wines produced here are primarily red, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties that reflect the local terroir.
geo:it:carso-doc.style: Red wines with depth and complexity, often with earthy notes

geo:fr:cassis-aoc.name: Cassis
geo:fr:cassis-aoc.description: Cassis is an AOC located in the Provence region of France, known primarily for its white wines. The appellation is situated near the Mediterranean coast, characterized by its limestone cliffs and a warm, sunny climate that influences the viticulture. The vineyards benefit from the proximity to the sea, which moderates temperatures and contributes to the unique terroir. Cassis is recognized for its production of dry white wines, often blended from local grape varieties.
geo:fr:cassis-aoc.style: Dry white wines with mineral and citrus characteristics

geo:it:castel-del-monte.name: Castel del Monte
geo:it:castel-del-monte.description: Castel del Monte is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Puglia region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. The legal boundaries of Castel del Monte encompass several municipalities, with a focus on the area surrounding the historic castle from which it derives its name. The wines produced here are known for their structure and aging potential, often showcasing a balance of fruit and acidity.
geo:it:castel-del-monte.style: Structured red wines with aging potential and balanced acidity

geo:it:castel-del-monte-bombino-nero.name: Castel del Monte Bombino Nero
geo:it:castel-del-monte-bombino-nero.description: Castel del Monte Bombino Nero is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Puglia region of Italy. This appellation is primarily known for producing rosé wines made from the Bombino Nero grape variety. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas around the Castel del Monte, a UNESCO World Heritage site, which influences the terroir and viticultural practices in the region. The climate is characterized by hot summers and mild winters, contributing to the ripening of the grapes. The wines typically exhibit fresh acidity and fruit-forward characteristics.
geo:it:castel-del-monte-bombino-nero.style: Rosé wines with fresh acidity and fruit-forward characteristics

geo:fr:castillon-cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:castillon-cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux is an AOC located on the right bank of the Bordeaux region, near the town of Castillon-la-Bataille. The appellation is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a temperate climate influenced by the nearby Dordogne River. It primarily produces red wines, which are known for their fruit-forward profiles and approachable tannins. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, and it is recognized for its emphasis on Merlot and Cabernet Franc varieties.
geo:fr:castillon-cotes-de-bordeaux.style: Fruity red wines with soft tannins and moderate acidity

geo:es:catalunya-do.name: Catalunya
geo:es:catalunya-do.description: Catalunya is a designated wine appellation in the Catalonia region of Spain, encompassing a diverse range of wine styles and production methods. The appellation includes various subregions and municipalities, allowing for a wide variety of grape cultivation and winemaking practices. It is governed by regulations that permit both traditional and modern techniques, reflecting the region's rich viticultural heritage.
geo:es:catalunya-do.style: Diverse styles including red, white, and sparkling wines.

geo:es:cava.name: Cava
geo:es:cava.description: Spain's méthode traditionnelle sparkling wine, mostly from Penedès in Catalonia. Indigenous grapes — Macabeo, Xarel-lo, Parellada — create a style distinct from Champagne. Reserva and Gran Reserva categories indicate extended aging. Remarkable value for the quality.

geo:us:central-coast.name: Central Coast
geo:us:central-coast.description: Central Coast is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, encompassing a diverse range of climates and geographies. It is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a significant focus on varietals such as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. The region includes several notable subregions and appellations, contributing to its reputation for high-quality wine production.
geo:us:central-coast.style: Diverse styles with emphasis on red varietals, especially Pinot Noir

geo:nz:central-otago-gi.name: Central Otago
geo:nz:central-otago-gi.description: The world's southernmost Pinot Noir region. Schist soils, continental climate, and extreme altitude create conditions found nowhere else. The wines are vivid, fruit-pure, almost crystalline. Bannockburn and Gibbston Valley lead; new sub-regions emerge yearly.

geo:it:cerasuolo-dabruzzo.name: Cerasuolo d'Abruzzo
geo:it:cerasuolo-dabruzzo.description: Cerasuolo d'Abruzzo is a red wine appellation located in the Abruzzo region of Italy. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass specific communes within the province of Teramo and Pescara. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Montepulciano, which must constitute at least 85% of the blend. The wines are characterized by their vibrant cherry color and are typically medium-bodied with moderate tannins. The appellation is known for producing wines that are approachable and versatile, often enjoyed young.
geo:it:cerasuolo-dabruzzo.style: Medium-bodied red with cherry flavors and moderate tannins.

geo:it:cerasuolo-di-vittoria.name: Cerasuolo di Vittoria
geo:it:cerasuolo-di-vittoria.description: Cerasuolo di Vittoria is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in Sicily, Italy. It is characterized by a blend of Nero d'Avola and Frappato grapes, which are cultivated in the region's warm Mediterranean climate. The appellation is known for its rich history, being the first Sicilian wine to receive DOCG status in 2005, reflecting its quality and tradition.
geo:it:cerasuolo-di-vittoria.style: Medium-bodied red wine with fruity and floral notes

geo:fr:chablis.name: Chablis
geo:fr:chablis.description: The purest expression of Chardonnay in the world. Isolated from the rest of Burgundy, Chablis' Kimmeridgian clay — packed with fossilized oyster shells — gives its wines a steely minerality found nowhere else. Unoaked, austere, and age-worthy, Chablis is Burgundy stripped to its essence.

geo:fr:chablis-aoc.name: Chablis
geo:fr:chablis-aoc.description: Chablis is an appellation located in the northern part of Burgundy, France, known for its production of white wine exclusively from the Chardonnay grape. The region is characterized by its unique Kimmeridgian limestone soils, which contribute to the wine's mineral qualities. Chablis is divided into several classifications, including Chablis, Chablis Premier Cru, and Chablis Grand Cru, each reflecting different terroirs and quality levels. The climate is continental, with cool temperatures that influence the ripening of grapes, leading to wines with high acidity and distinctive flavors.
geo:fr:chablis-aoc.style: Dry white wines with high acidity and mineral notes

geo:fr:chablis-grand-cru.name: Chablis Grand Cru
geo:fr:chablis-grand-cru.description: Chablis Grand Cru is an AOC located within the Chablis region of France, known for its production of high-quality white wines exclusively from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation encompasses seven climats, including Les Clos, Vaudésir, and Bougros, which are characterized by their unique terroirs and microclimates. The wines are typically marked by a pronounced minerality, high acidity, and complex flavor profiles, reflecting the region's Kimmeridgian limestone soils. Chablis Grand Cru wines are subject to strict regulations regarding vineyard practices and winemaking techniques to ensure quality and authenticity.
geo:fr:chablis-grand-cru.style: Mineral-driven white wines with high acidity and complexity

geo:fr:chablis-premier-cru.name: Chablis Premier Cru
geo:fr:chablis-premier-cru.description: Chablis Premier Cru is an appellation within the Chablis region of France, classified as AOC. It encompasses several vineyards known for producing high-quality white wines primarily from the Chardonnay grape. The Premier Cru designation indicates a higher quality level than the basic Chablis AOC, with specific vineyard sites recognized for their superior terroir. The wines typically exhibit characteristics of minerality and acidity, influenced by the region's Kimmeridgian limestone soils.
geo:fr:chablis-premier-cru.style: High-quality white wines with minerality and acidity

geo:us:chalk-hill-ava.name: Chalk Hill
geo:us:chalk-hill-ava.description: Chalk Hill is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 1983, it is characterized by its unique volcanic soil and a cool climate influenced by nearby coastal winds. The AVA is primarily known for its production of white wines, particularly Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc, which thrive in the region's diverse microclimates and well-drained soils.
geo:us:chalk-hill-ava.style: White wines with a focus on Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc

geo:fr:chambertin.name: Chambertin
geo:fr:chambertin.description: Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation is situated on the mid-slope of the Côte d'Or, benefiting from a favorable microclimate and well-drained limestone soils. Chambertin's wines are characterized by their depth, complexity, and aging potential, often reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:chambertin.style: Full-bodied red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:chambertin-clos-de-beze.name: Chambertin-Clos de Beze
geo:fr:chambertin-clos-de-beze.description: Chambertin-Clos de Bèze is an AOC located within the Côte de Nuits region of Burgundy, France. It is recognized for its red wines made exclusively from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated adjacent to the Chambertin vineyard and benefits from a similar terroir, characterized by limestone-rich soils and a favorable microclimate that enhances grape ripening.
geo:fr:chambertin-clos-de-beze.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and aging potential

geo:fr:chambolle-musigny.name: Chambolle-Musigny
geo:fr:chambolle-musigny.description: Burgundy at its most delicate. The limestone soils here produce Pinot Noir of ethereal lightness — floral, silky, almost weightless on the palate. Musigny and Bonnes-Mares, the Grand Crus, are among France's most sought-after vineyards. Chambolle rewards those who listen closely.

geo:fr:champagne.name: Champagne
geo:fr:champagne.description: Not just a wine, a method and a place. The chalky soils and cool climate of this northern region create base wines of high acidity, perfect for the méthode champenoise. Pinot Noir and Meunier add body; Chardonnay brings finesse. The result is the world's benchmark for sparkling wine.

geo:fr:champagne-aoc.name: Champagne
geo:fr:champagne-aoc.description: Champagne is a designated wine region in France, known for its production of sparkling wines under strict regulations. The appellation is located in the northeastern part of the country, primarily within the Marne department. It encompasses several key areas, including the Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, and Côte des Blancs. The wines produced here are typically made using the traditional méthode champenoise, which involves secondary fermentation in the bottle. The region's unique chalky soil and cool climate contribute to the distinctive characteristics of its wines.
geo:fr:champagne-aoc.style: Sparkling wine, primarily produced using méthode champenoise

geo:fr:chapelle-chambertin.name: Chapelle-Chambertin
geo:fr:chapelle-chambertin.description: Chapelle-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated adjacent to the Grand Cru vineyard Chambertin, contributing to its esteemed reputation. The terroir features clay-limestone soils, which are conducive to the cultivation of high-quality Pinot Noir grapes. The wines from this appellation are characterized by their elegance and complexity.
geo:fr:chapelle-chambertin.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and finesse

geo:fr:charmes-chambertin.name: Charmes-Chambertin
geo:fr:charmes-chambertin.description: Charmes-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation encompasses a portion of the larger Chambertin vineyard, characterized by its rich, clay-limestone soils and favorable microclimate. Charmes-Chambertin wines are noted for their elegance and complexity, often exhibiting a balance of fruit and earthy characteristics.
geo:fr:charmes-chambertin.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and balance

geo:fr:chassagne-montrachet.name: Chassagne-Montrachet
geo:fr:chassagne-montrachet.description: Chassagne-Montrachet is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for its white wines, primarily made from Chardonnay, and is part of the Montrachet vineyard area. The appellation encompasses several premier cru sites and is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes limestone soils and varying microclimates that contribute to the complexity of its wines.
geo:fr:chassagne-montrachet.style: Rich, full-bodied white wines with mineral and fruit characteristics

geo:fr:chatillon-en-diois.name: Chatillon-en-Diois
geo:fr:chatillon-en-diois.description: Chatillon-en-Diois is an AOC located in the Rhône Valley of France, recognized for its production of red wines primarily from Gamay and Pinot Noir, as well as Aligoté. The appellation is situated in the Diois region, which features a mix of limestone and clay soils, contributing to the character of the wines. The climate is influenced by the nearby Alps, resulting in a continental climate with significant temperature variations between day and night. The wines from Chatillon-en-Diois are known for their fruit-forward profiles and freshness.
geo:fr:chatillon-en-diois.style: Fruit-forward red wines with freshness and balance

geo:fr:chevalier-montrachet.name: Chevalier-Montrachet
geo:fr:chevalier-montrachet.description: Chevalier-Montrachet is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality white wines exclusively from the Chardonnay grape. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Mont Rachet, benefiting from a unique microclimate and well-drained limestone soils that contribute to the complexity and elegance of its wines.
geo:fr:chevalier-montrachet.style: Rich, complex white wines with notable aging potential

geo:fr:cheverny.name: Cheverny
geo:fr:cheverny.description: Cheverny is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, known primarily for its white wines. The appellation encompasses a defined area around the town of Cheverny, characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils. The climate is influenced by the nearby Loire River, contributing to the freshness and balance of the wines produced here. Cheverny white wines are typically blends, with a focus on Sauvignon Blanc and Chardonnay, reflecting the region's diverse viticultural heritage.
geo:fr:cheverny.style: Fresh and aromatic white wines, often blended

geo:it:chianti.name: Chianti
geo:it:chianti.description: Chianti is a red wine appellation located in Tuscany, Italy, classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG). The appellation encompasses several subzones and is primarily known for its use of the Sangiovese grape, which must constitute at least 70% of the blend. Chianti wines are characterized by their bright acidity and moderate tannins, often exhibiting flavors of red fruits and earthy notes.
geo:it:chianti.style: Red wines with bright acidity and moderate tannins

geo:it:chianti-classico.name: Chianti Classico
geo:it:chianti-classico.description: The historic heart of Chianti, between Florence and Siena. The Gallo Nero (black rooster) marks wines from this original zone. Sangiovese here is more structured than in outer Chianti — firmer tannins, greater aging potential. The best Gran Selezione wines rival Brunello.

geo:it:chianti-colli-senesi.name: Chianti Colli Senesi
geo:it:chianti-colli-senesi.description: Chianti Colli Senesi is a designated appellation within the Chianti region of Tuscany, Italy, classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG). It encompasses the hilly areas surrounding the city of Siena, characterized by a mix of clay, limestone, and galestro soils. The climate is typically Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, contributing to the production of red wines primarily based on Sangiovese. The appellation emphasizes traditional winemaking practices and regional identity.
geo:it:chianti-colli-senesi.style: Medium-bodied red wines with bright acidity and red fruit flavors

geo:it:chianti-rufina.name: Chianti Rufina
geo:it:chianti-rufina.description: Chianti Rufina is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located within the Tuscany region of Italy. It is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Apennine Mountains and the proximity to the Arno River. The appellation is known for producing red wines primarily from the Sangiovese grape, which must constitute at least 70% of the blend, along with permitted varieties such as Canaiolo and Colorino. The wines of Chianti Rufina are noted for their balance, acidity, and aging potential.
geo:it:chianti-rufina.style: Medium-bodied red wines with good acidity and aging potential

geo:us:chiles-valley.name: Chiles Valley
geo:us:chiles-valley.description: Chiles Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain and diverse microclimates, which contribute to the production of a range of wine styles. The AVA is primarily known for its red wines, particularly those made from Zinfandel and Cabernet Sauvignon, as well as some white wines, including Chenin Blanc. The region's elevation and soil composition play a significant role in the viticulture of the area.
geo:us:chiles-valley.style: Red wines with notable Zinfandel and Cabernet Sauvignon

geo:fr:chinon.name: Chinon
geo:fr:chinon.description: Chinon is an AOC located in the Touraine region of France, primarily known for its red wines made from Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Vienne River, which influences its microclimate and terroir. Chinon encompasses several communes, with the town of Chinon being the central hub. The region's diverse soils, including clay, limestone, and gravel, contribute to the complexity of its wines. The style of Chinon wines can range from light and fruity to more structured and age-worthy.
geo:fr:chinon.style: Red wines, primarily from Cabernet Franc, varying in structure and complexity

geo:fr:chiroubles.name: Chiroubles
geo:fr:chiroubles.description: Chiroubles is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known for producing red wines primarily from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Monts du Beaujolais, characterized by its granite soils and a climate that favors the cultivation of this varietal. The wines from Chiroubles are typically noted for their light body and fruit-forward character, reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:chiroubles.style: Light-bodied red wines with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:chorey-les-beaune.name: Chorey-lès-Beaune
geo:fr:chorey-les-beaune.description: Chorey-lès-Beaune is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. The appellation is situated to the north of Beaune and is characterized by its clay and limestone soils. It primarily produces red wines made from Pinot Noir, with a focus on fruit-forward expressions. The appellation is known for its relatively approachable wines that can be enjoyed at a younger age compared to other Burgundy wines.
geo:fr:chorey-les-beaune.style: Red wines, typically fruit-forward and approachable

geo:fr:chateau-chalon.name: Château-Chalon
geo:fr:chateau-chalon.description: Château-Chalon is an AOC located in the Jura region of France, known for its distinctive white wines made primarily from the Savagnin grape. The appellation is situated on a limestone plateau, characterized by its unique terroir that includes a mix of clay and marl soils. The wines produced here are often aged under a veil of yeast, similar to the production of Vin Jaune, which contributes to their complex flavor profile. The legal boundaries of Château-Chalon encompass a small area, emphasizing the production of high-quality, traditional Jura wines.
geo:fr:chateau-chalon.style: Oxidative white wines with nutty and complex flavors

geo:fr:chateau-grillet.name: Château-Grillet
geo:fr:chateau-grillet.description: Château-Grillet is an AOC located in the Northern Rhône region of France, recognized for its production of white wine exclusively from the Viognier grape variety. The appellation is situated on a small, steep hillside overlooking the Rhône River, which contributes to its unique microclimate and terroir. Château-Grillet is one of the smallest AOCs in France, with a history dating back to the 18th century, and it is known for producing rich, aromatic wines that reflect the characteristics of the Viognier grape.
geo:fr:chateau-grillet.style: Rich, aromatic white wines from Viognier

geo:fr:chateauneuf-du-pape.name: Châteauneuf-du-Pape
geo:fr:chateauneuf-du-pape.description: The Southern Rhône's flagship, where 13 grape varieties are permitted. The famous galets roulés — large rounded stones — retain heat and produce wines of remarkable warmth and generosity. Grenache dominates the blend, creating wines that are powerful yet silky. The whites are increasingly celebrated.

geo:fr:chateauneuf-du-pape-blanc.name: Châteauneuf-du-Pape Blanc
geo:fr:chateauneuf-du-pape-blanc.description: Châteauneuf-du-Pape Blanc is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, known for its white wine production. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs characterized by a mix of clay, limestone, and galets roulés (round stones). It is situated around the village of Châteauneuf-du-Pape, which has historical significance as a papal residence in the 14th century. The wines are primarily produced from several permitted grape varieties, contributing to a diverse range of styles.
geo:fr:chateauneuf-du-pape-blanc.style: Full-bodied white wines with complexity and richness

geo:fr:chenas.name: Chénas
geo:fr:chenas.description: Chénas is an AOC located within the Beaujolais region of France, known for its production of red wines primarily made from the Gamay grape. The appellation covers a small area characterized by its granite soils and hilly terrain, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. Chénas is one of the ten Beaujolais Crus, recognized for producing wines that can exhibit both fruitiness and complexity, often with floral and mineral notes.
geo:fr:chenas.style: Red wines with fruitiness and complexity, often floral and mineral notes

geo:es:cigales-do.name: Cigales
geo:es:cigales-do.description: Cigales is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla y León region of Spain, primarily known for its production of rosé wines. The appellation is situated to the north of the Duero River and benefits from a continental climate, characterized by hot summers and cold winters. The vineyards are predominantly planted on sandy and clay soils, which contribute to the unique characteristics of the wines produced here. Established as a DO in 1991, Cigales has a historical tradition of winemaking that dates back to the Middle Ages.
geo:es:cigales-do.style: Rosé wines with fresh acidity and fruit-forward profiles

geo:it:cinque-terre.name: Cinque Terre
geo:it:cinque-terre.description: Cinque Terre is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Liguria region of Italy. The appellation encompasses a small coastal area characterized by steep terraces and a Mediterranean climate. It is primarily known for producing white wines, which are often made from local grape varieties. The unique terroir, influenced by the proximity to the sea and the rugged landscape, contributes to the distinct character of the wines.
geo:it:cinque-terre.style: Light-bodied white wines with mineral and citrus notes.

geo:it:ciro.name: Cirò
geo:it:ciro.description: Cirò is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Calabria, Italy. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly produced from the Gaglioppo grape variety. The appellation encompasses a specific geographic area characterized by its Mediterranean climate and hilly terrain, which contribute to the distinctiveness of the wines. Cirò wines are often noted for their structure and aging potential, reflecting the traditional winemaking practices of the region.
geo:it:ciro.style: Structured red wines with aging potential, often featuring dark fruit and earthy notes

geo:fr:clairette-de-bellegarde.name: Clairette de Bellegarde
geo:fr:clairette-de-bellegarde.description: Clairette de Bellegarde is an AOC located in the Languedoc-Roussillon region of France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Clairette grape, along with Grenache Blanc and Roussanne.
