The appellation is defined by its specific legal boundaries and regulations that govern the permitted grape varieties and production methods, ensuring the preservation of its unique terroir and wine style.
geo:fr:clairette-de-bellegarde.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and aromatic complexity

geo:fr:clairette-de-die.name: Clairette de Die
geo:fr:clairette-de-die.description: Clairette de Die is an AOC located in the Rhône Valley of France, known for its sparkling wines. The appellation is characterized by its unique production method, which involves the use of the ancestral method, resulting in naturally sparkling wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes in the Drôme department, with a focus on the village of Die. The terroir is influenced by the surrounding mountains and the Rhône River, contributing to the distinct characteristics of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:clairette-de-die.style: Sparkling wines made using the ancestral method

geo:au:clare-valley-gi.name: Clare Valley
geo:au:clare-valley-gi.description: Clare Valley is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in South Australia, recognized for its distinct terroir and climate that contribute to the production of high-quality wines. The region is characterized by its cool climate, which is influenced by elevation and proximity to the Mount Lofty Ranges. Clare Valley is particularly noted for its Riesling, which thrives in the region's diverse soil types and diurnal temperature variations. The appellation encompasses several subregions, each with unique conditions that affect grape growing and wine production.
geo:au:clare-valley-gi.style: White wines, especially dry and aromatic Rieslings

geo:fr:clos-de-la-roche.name: Clos de la Roche
geo:fr:clos-de-la-roche.description: Clos de la Roche is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is classified as a Grand Cru vineyard, known exclusively for producing red wines from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses a specific area with defined legal boundaries, ensuring the quality and characteristics of its wines. The terroir features a mix of limestone and clay soils, contributing to the complexity and aging potential of the wines. Clos de la Roche is recognized for its ability to produce wines with depth, structure, and a distinctive expression of the Burgundy region.
geo:fr:clos-de-la-roche.style: Red wines with depth and aging potential

geo:fr:clos-de-tart.name: Clos de Tart
geo:fr:clos-de-tart.description: Clos de Tart is a Grand Cru appellation located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is one of the few monopole vineyards, meaning it is owned entirely by a single producer. The vineyard covers approximately 7.53 hectares and is situated on a slope with a predominantly clay-limestone soil composition, contributing to the unique terroir. The appellation is for producing high-quality red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes, characterized by their complexity and aging potential.
geo:fr:clos-de-tart.style: Red wine known for complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:clos-de-vougeot.name: Clos de Vougeot
geo:fr:clos-de-vougeot.description: Clos de Vougeot is a Grand Cru appellation located within the Côte de Nuits region of Burgundy, France. It encompasses a single vineyard of approximately 50 hectares, surrounded by stone walls, and is known for its historical significance, having been established by Cistercian monks in the 12th century. The vineyard's diverse terroir includes varying soil types and microclimates, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced. The appellation is exclusively dedicated to red wine made from Pinot Noir grapes, which are characterized by their depth and aging potential.
geo:fr:clos-de-vougeot.style: Red wines known for depth, complexity, and aging potential

geo:fr:clos-saint-denis.name: Clos Saint-Denis
geo:fr:clos-saint-denis.description: Clos Saint-Denis is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. The appellation is known for its production of red wines primarily from the Pinot Noir grape. It is situated between the more famous Clos de la Roche and Clos de Tart, benefiting from a similar terroir that includes limestone-rich soils and a favorable microclimate. The wines from Clos Saint-Denis are characterized by their elegance and complexity, often reflecting the nuances of the specific vineyard site.
geo:fr:clos-saint-denis.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and depth

geo:pt:colares.name: Colares
geo:pt:colares.description: Colares is a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located within the Lisboa region of Portugal. It is characterized by its unique coastal terroir, with vineyards planted in sandy soils that are resistant to phylloxera. The appellation is known for its production of red wines primarily from the Ramisco grape, as well as white wines from Malvasia. The region's proximity to the Atlantic Ocean influences its climate, contributing to the distinctiveness of its wines.
geo:pt:colares.style: Red wines with a focus on Ramisco, known for their structure and complexity

geo:it:colli-berici.name: Colli Berici
geo:it:colli-berici.description: Colli Berici is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the nearby Po River and the surrounding mountains. It is known for producing a range of red wines, primarily from the Tai Rosso, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon grape varieties, as well as white wines from Garganega. The wines from this region often reflect the unique terroir, which includes clay and limestone soils.
geo:it:colli-berici.style: Red wines with varied profiles, often fruit-forward and approachable

geo:it:colli-bolognesi.name: Colli Bolognesi
geo:it:colli-bolognesi.description: Colli Bolognesi is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by hilly terrain and a temperate climate, which contribute to the cultivation of various grape varieties. The primary focus is on white wines, particularly those made from Pignoletto, with Barbera also being significant. The wines produced here are known for their freshness and acidity, reflecting the region's unique terroir.
geo:it:colli-bolognesi.style: Primarily white wines with fresh acidity and moderate body.

geo:it:colli-bolognesi-pignoletto.name: Colli Bolognesi Pignoletto
geo:it:colli-bolognesi-pignoletto.description: Colli Bolognesi Pignoletto is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Pignoletto grape, which is a local variant of the Grechetto grape. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific hilly areas around Bologna, characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils. The wines typically exhibit fresh acidity and floral notes, reflecting the region's climate and terroir.
geo:it:colli-bolognesi-pignoletto.style: Fresh white wines with floral and fruity characteristics.

geo:it:colli-di-luni.name: Colli di Luni
geo:it:colli-di-luni.description: Colli di Luni is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Liguria region of Italy. It encompasses the hills surrounding the town of Luni, which is situated near the border with Tuscany. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, with influences from the nearby Ligurian Sea, and its diverse soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. The primary focus of the appellation is on white wines, particularly those made from the Vermentino grape, although red wines from Sangiovese are also permitted.
geo:it:colli-di-luni.style: White wines with a focus on Vermentino

geo:it:colli-euganei.name: Colli Euganei
geo:it:colli-euganei.description: Colli Euganei is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by hilly terrain and volcanic soil, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, allowing for a variety of grape cultivation. The region is known for producing predominantly red wines, with some white varieties also permitted. Key producers in the area focus on both traditional and modern winemaking techniques.
geo:it:colli-euganei.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit-forward profiles

geo:it:colli-orientali-del-friuli.name: Colli Orientali del Friuli
geo:it:colli-orientali-del-friuli.description: Colli Orientali del Friuli is an Italian appellation located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region. Established in 1970, it encompasses a hilly area characterized by diverse soils and microclimates, which contribute to the production of high-quality white wines. The appellation is known for its strict regulations regarding grape varieties and winemaking practices, ensuring a focus on quality and expression of terroir.
geo:it:colli-orientali-del-friuli.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and complexity

geo:it:colli-tortonesi.name: Colli Tortonesi
geo:it:colli-tortonesi.description: Colli Tortonesi is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Apennines and the Po River. It is known for producing a range of red wines, primarily from the Barbera grape, along with white wines from Timorasso and Cortese. The wines of Colli Tortonesi often reflect the unique terroir of the area, which includes clay and limestone soils.
geo:it:colli-tortonesi.style: Red wines with some white varieties, showcasing regional terroir

geo:it:collio-doc.name: Collio
geo:it:collio-doc.description: Collio is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. It is primarily known for its white wines, which are produced from a variety of grape types grown in the area's distinctive hilly terrain. The region benefits from a unique microclimate influenced by the nearby Adriatic Sea and the Julian Alps, contributing to the complexity and character of its wines.
geo:it:collio-doc.style: White wines with aromatic complexity and freshness

geo:fr:collioure.name: Collioure
geo:fr:collioure.description: Collioure is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, known for its production of red wines primarily from the Mediterranean coastal area. The appellation is situated near the town of Collioure, bordered by the sea and the foothills of the Pyrenees, which contribute to its unique terroir. The climate is characterized by hot summers and mild winters, with a significant influence from the Mediterranean Sea. Collioure wines are typically blends that showcase the region's diverse grape varieties and are noted for their depth and complexity.
geo:fr:collioure.style: Rich, full-bodied red wines with complexity and depth

geo:us:columbia-valley-ava.name: Columbia Valley
geo:us:columbia-valley-ava.description: Columbia Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Washington State, encompassing a diverse range of microclimates and soils. It is the largest AVA in Washington, covering approximately 11 million acres, with a significant portion dedicated to viticulture. The region is characterized by its arid climate, with warm summers and cold winters, which contributes to the ripening of grapes and the development of concentrated flavors in the wines produced.
geo:us:columbia-valley-ava.style: Red wines with notable varietals and balanced acidity

geo:es:conca-de-barbera.name: Conca de Barberà
geo:es:conca-de-barbera.description: Conca de Barberà is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Catalonia region of Spain. It is characterized by its Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. The appellation is known for its focus on indigenous grape varieties, particularly Trepat, which is often used for both still and sparkling wines. The legal boundaries of Conca de Barberà encompass several municipalities, with a strong emphasis on sustainable viticulture practices.
geo:es:conca-de-barbera.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit and freshness

geo:es:conca-del-riu-anoia.name: Conca del Riu Anoia
geo:es:conca-del-riu-anoia.description: Conca del Riu Anoia is an appellation located in the Catalonia region of Spain, recognized for its production of sparkling wines. The appellation is defined by its geographical boundaries along the Anoia River, which influences the local climate and terroir. The area is characterized by a Mediterranean climate with a mix of continental influences, contributing to the unique growing conditions for its grape varieties. The primary grapes used in the production of sparkling wines from this appellation include Xarel·lo, Macabeo, and Parellada, which are traditional varieties in the region.
geo:es:conca-del-riu-anoia.style: Sparkling wines with a focus on traditional Catalan varieties.

geo:es:condado-de-huelva.name: Condado de Huelva
geo:es:condado-de-huelva.description: Condado de Huelva is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Andalusia region of Spain. It is characterized by its unique terroir, which includes a mix of sandy and clay soils, and a Mediterranean climate that influences the viticulture. The appellation is primarily known for producing white wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The region has historical significance in the production of wine, dating back to ancient times.
geo:es:condado-de-huelva.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and acidity

geo:fr:condrieu.name: Condrieu
geo:fr:condrieu.description: The world's greatest expression of Viognier. The steep granite terraces here produce wines of intoxicating perfume — apricot, peach blossom, honeysuckle — with a richness that approaches decadence. Once nearly extinct, Condrieu has been revived by a new generation of growers. Best drunk young.

geo:it:conero-docg.name: Conero
geo:it:conero-docg.description: Conero is a DOCG (Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita) located in the Marche region of Italy. It encompasses the area around Monte Conero, characterized by its hilly terrain and proximity to the Adriatic Sea. The appellation is known for its red wines primarily made from Montepulciano and Sangiovese grapes, reflecting the local terroir and climate.
geo:it:conero-docg.style: Red wines with structure and depth, often with dark fruit flavors

geo:it:contea-di-sclafani.name: Contea di Sclafani
geo:it:contea-di-sclafani.description: Contea di Sclafani is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Sicily, Italy. It is primarily known for its production of red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs that contribute to the distinct characteristics of its wines.
geo:it:contea-di-sclafani.style: Red wines with a focus on indigenous Sicilian varieties

geo:it:contessa-entellina.name: Contessa Entellina
geo:it:contessa-entellina.description: Contessa Entellina is a designated DOC appellation located in Sicily, Italy. It encompasses a specific area known for its diverse terroir and favorable climate for viticulture. The region allows for the production of both red and white wines, with a focus on indigenous and international grape varieties. The appellation's regulations govern the permitted grape varieties and winemaking practices to ensure quality and authenticity.
geo:it:contessa-entellina.style: White wines with a range of varietal expressions

geo:us:coombsville.name: Coombsville
geo:us:coombsville.description: Coombsville is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its cooler climate compared to other Napa subregions, which allows for a longer growing season. The AVA is defined by its unique topography, including volcanic soils and rolling hills, contributing to the distinct terroir. Coombsville primarily produces red wines, with a focus on varietals such as Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, as well as some Chardonnay. The region's wines are noted for their balance and complexity.
geo:us:coombsville.style: Balanced red wines with complexity and depth.

geo:au:coonawarra-gi.name: Coonawarra
geo:au:coonawarra-gi.description: Coonawarra is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in South Australia, for its distinctive terra rossa soil, which is a rich red clay over limestone. The region's climate is characterized by a moderate maritime influence, providing ideal conditions for grape growing. Coonawarra is particularly famous for its Cabernet Sauvignon, which thrives in the unique terroir, leading to wines with concentrated flavors and structure. Other varieties are also cultivated, but Cabernet Sauvignon remains the hallmark of the region.
geo:au:coonawarra-gi.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and aging potential

geo:fr:corbieres.name: Corbières
geo:fr:corbieres.description: Corbières is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 1985. It encompasses a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and scrubland, situated between the Mediterranean Sea and the Montagne Noire. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, with warm summers and mild winters, which is conducive to grape cultivation. Corbières is known primarily for its red wines, which are often blends of several grape varieties, reflecting the region's rich viticultural heritage.
geo:fr:corbieres.style: Full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and herbal notes

geo:fr:cornas.name: Cornas
geo:fr:cornas.description: The Northern Rhône's most brooding appellation. Pure Syrah from decomposed granite, no Viognier softening here. Cornas is dense, tannic, sometimes savage in youth — but give it time and it reveals depths that rival Hermitage. A cult favorite among those who like their wine uncompromising.

geo:it:cortese-dellalto-monferrato.name: Cortese dell'Alto Monferrato
geo:it:cortese-dellalto-monferrato.description: Cortese dell'Alto Monferrato is an Italian wine appellation located in the Monferrato region of Piedmont. It is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is conducive to viticulture. The appellation primarily produces white wines from the Cortese grape variety, which is known for its crisp acidity and floral notes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific communes within the Alto Monferrato area, ensuring the preservation of its unique terroir.
geo:it:cortese-dellalto-monferrato.style: Light, crisp white wines with floral and citrus notes

geo:fr:corton.name: Corton
geo:fr:corton.description: Corton is an AOC Grand Cru appellation located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for its red wines made from Pinot Noir, which are produced on the slopes of the Corton hill. The appellation encompasses several climats, each contributing to the complexity and character of the wines. Corton is recognized for its ability to produce wines that can age well, reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:corton.style: Red wines with depth, structure, and aging potential

geo:fr:corton-charlemagne.name: Corton-Charlemagne
geo:fr:corton-charlemagne.description: Corton-Charlemagne is an AOC Grand Cru located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is exclusively dedicated to the production of white wine made from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Corton hill, benefiting from a unique microclimate and diverse soil types that contribute to the complexity of its wines. The legal boundaries of Corton-Charlemagne encompass several communes, including Aloxe-Corton and Pernand-Vergelesses. The wines are characterized by their richness, minerality, and aging potential.
geo:fr:corton-charlemagne.style: Rich, mineral-driven white wines with aging potential

geo:it:cortona.name: Cortona
geo:it:cortona.description: Cortona is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Tuscany, Italy. It is known for producing primarily red wines, with a focus on varietals such as Syrah, Merlot, and Sangiovese. The appellation benefits from a favorable climate and diverse soils, contributing to the complexity of its wines.
geo:it:cortona.style: Red wines with a focus on Syrah and blends

geo:it:costa-damalfi.name: Costa d'Amalfi
geo:it:costa-damalfi.description: Costa d'Amalfi is a DOC located along the Amalfi Coast in Campania, Italy. It encompasses several municipalities, including Amalfi, Ravello, and Minori. The appellation is characterized by steep terraced vineyards that benefit from a Mediterranean climate, with a mix of coastal and mountainous influences. The region is known for its production of white wines, often made from indigenous grape varieties, as well as some red and rosé wines.
geo:it:costa-damalfi.style: White wines with fresh acidity and mineral notes

geo:es:costers-del-segre.name: Costers del Segre
geo:es:costers-del-segre.description: Costers del Segre is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Catalonia region of Spain. Established in 1986, it encompasses a diverse range of microclimates and soils across its various subzones. The DO is characterized by its focus on both traditional and international grape varieties, reflecting the region's adaptability and innovation in viticulture.
geo:es:costers-del-segre.style: Red wines with a blend of local and international varieties

geo:fr:costieres-de-nimes-aoc.name: Costières de Nîmes
geo:fr:costieres-de-nimes-aoc.description: Costières de Nîmes is an AOC located in the southern Rhône Valley of France, characterized by its proximity to the Mediterranean Sea. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, including clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the diversity of its wines. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, facilitating the ripening of grapes. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre.
geo:fr:costieres-de-nimes-aoc.style: Red blends with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:coteaux-champenois.name: Coteaux Champenois
geo:fr:coteaux-champenois.description: Coteaux Champenois is an AOC located within the Champagne region of France, recognized for its production of still wines. The appellation encompasses specific areas where the terroir allows for the cultivation of grapes used in non-sparkling wine production. It is characterized by a focus on traditional Champagne grape varieties, primarily Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot Meunier.
geo:fr:coteaux-champenois.style: Still wines made from traditional Champagne grape varieties.

geo:fr:coteaux-daix-en-provence.name: Coteaux d'Aix-en-Provence
geo:fr:coteaux-daix-en-provence.description: Coteaux d'Aix-en-Provence is an AOC located in the Provence region of France, established in 1985. It encompasses a diverse range of terroirs characterized by limestone and clay soils, with a Mediterranean climate that influences the viticulture. The appellation is primarily known for its rosé wines, but also produces red and white wines. The legal boundaries include parts of the Bouches-du-Rhône department, with specific regulations governing grape varieties and production methods.
geo:fr:coteaux-daix-en-provence.style: Rosé, with some red and white wines

geo:fr:coteaux-de-laubance.name: Coteaux de l'Aubance
geo:fr:coteaux-de-laubance.description: Coteaux de l'Aubance is an appellation located within the Anjou-Saumur region of France, primarily known for its sweet white wines made from late-harvest grapes. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Aubance River, benefiting from a microclimate that allows for the development of noble rot, essential for producing its characteristic sweet wines. The legal boundaries of Coteaux de l'Aubance encompass specific vineyard sites that are recognized for their unique terroir, which contributes to the complexity and richness of the wines. The primary grape variety permitted in this appellation is Chenin Blanc, which thrives in the region's diverse soils and climatic conditions.
geo:fr:coteaux-de-laubance.style: Sweet white wines, often with botrytis influence

geo:fr:coteaux-du-giennois.name: Coteaux du Giennois
geo:fr:coteaux-du-giennois.description: Coteaux du Giennois is an AOC located in the Centre-Loire region of France. It is known for producing primarily white wines, with a focus on Sauvignon Blanc, along with red wines made from Pinot Noir and Gamay. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs characterized by clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the distinctive profiles of the wines.
geo:fr:coteaux-du-giennois.style: White wines with fresh acidity and floral notes

geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon.name: Coteaux du Layon
geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon.description: Coteaux du Layon is an AOC located in the Loire Valley, specifically in the Maine-et-Loire department. It is for producing sweet dessert wines primarily from the Chenin Blanc grape, which benefits from the region's unique microclimate and the presence of noble rot. The appellation encompasses several communes along the Layon River, where the combination of riverbanks and slopes creates ideal conditions for the development of botrytis cinerea.
geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, honeyed flavors

geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon-premier-cru-chau.name: Coteaux du Layon Premier Cru Chaume
geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon-premier-cru-chau.description: Coteaux du Layon Premier Cru Chaume is an AOC located within the Anjou-Saumur region of France, recognized for its production of high-quality dessert wines. The appellation is situated on the banks of the Layon River, benefiting from a microclimate that promotes the development of noble rot in grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific hillside vineyards known for their optimal drainage and exposure to sunlight, which contribute to the concentration of sugars and flavors in the grapes. The primary grape variety permitted in this appellation is Chenin Blanc, which is cultivated to produce sweet wines characterized by their richness and complexity.
geo:fr:coteaux-du-layon-premier-cru-chau.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich flavors and high acidity

geo:fr:coteaux-du-loir.name: Coteaux du Loir
geo:fr:coteaux-du-loir.description: Coteaux du Loir is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, recognized for its production of primarily white wines. The appellation is situated along the Loir River, which contributes to the region's unique terroir. The climate is characterized by a continental influence, with warm summers and cold winters, facilitating the growth of specific grape varieties. The legal boundaries of Coteaux du Loir encompass several communes, with a focus on the production of wines that reflect the local soil and climate conditions.
geo:fr:coteaux-du-loir.style: White wines with a focus on Chenin Blanc

geo:fr:coteaux-du-vendomois.name: Coteaux du Vendomois
geo:fr:coteaux-du-vendomois.description: Coteaux du Vendomois is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, recognized for its production of red wines primarily from the Pineau d'Aunis grape, along with white wines made from Chenin Blanc and Chardonnay. The appellation is situated around the town of Vendôme, characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The climate is influenced by the nearby Loire River, providing a temperate environment suitable for viticulture.
geo:fr:coteaux-du-vendomois.style: Red wines with some white varieties

geo:fr:coteaux-varois-en-provence.name: Coteaux Varois en Provence
geo:fr:coteaux-varois-en-provence.description: Coteaux Varois en Provence is an AOC located in the Provence region of France. It encompasses a variety of communes in the Var department, characterized by a Mediterranean climate and diverse soils. The appellation primarily produces rosé wines, which are known for their freshness and fruit-forward profiles, reflecting the local terroir.
geo:fr:coteaux-varois-en-provence.style: Rosé wines with fresh and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-de-bergerac.name: Cotes de Bergerac
geo:fr:cotes-de-bergerac.description: Côtes de Bergerac is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines primarily from Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, characterized by a mix of clay, limestone, and gravel soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines. Côtes de Bergerac is situated near the Dordogne River, benefiting from a temperate climate that supports the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are often noted for their fruit-forward character and approachable style.
geo:fr:cotes-de-bergerac.style: Fruit-forward red wines with approachable character

geo:fr:cotes-de-bordeaux-saint-macaire.name: Cotes de Bordeaux Saint-Macaire
geo:fr:cotes-de-bordeaux-saint-macaire.description: Cotes de Bordeaux Saint-Macaire is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, specifically known for its white wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Garonne River and benefits from a temperate maritime climate, which is conducive to the cultivation of white grape varieties. The area is characterized by clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the distinctive qualities of the wines produced here. The appellation primarily focuses on the production of dry and sweet white wines, with a history that reflects the broader viticultural practices of Bordeaux.

geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-frejus.name: Cotes de Provence Frejus
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-frejus.description: Côtes de Provence Fréjus is an AOC located within the Provence region of France, recognized for its production of rosé wines. The appellation is situated near the Mediterranean coast, benefiting from a warm climate and diverse soils that contribute to the character of its wines. Established to highlight the unique terroir of the Fréjus area, it emphasizes the use of local grape varieties.
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-frejus.style: Produces dry rosé wines with fresh acidity

geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-la-londe.name: Cotes de Provence La Londe
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-la-londe.description: Côtes de Provence La Londe is an AOC located within the Provence region of France, specifically designated for the production of rosé wines. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the cultivation of grape varieties suited for rosé production. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas around La Londe-les-Maures, ensuring a focus on quality and regional expression.
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-la-londe.style: Produces dry rosé wines with fresh acidity

geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-sainte-victoire.name: Cotes de Provence Sainte-Victoire
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-sainte-victoire.description: Côtes de Provence Sainte-Victoire is an AOC located in the Provence region of France, specifically in the foothills of the Sainte-Victoire Mountain. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which is influenced by the nearby mountain range, providing a unique terroir for viticulture. The vineyards are primarily situated on slopes and terraces, benefiting from excellent sun exposure and well-drained soils. The AOC is known for producing primarily rosé wines that reflect the region's distinct characteristics.
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence-sainte-victoire.style: Rosé wines with fresh and fruity profiles

geo:fr:cotes-de-saint-mont.name: Cotes de Saint-Mont
geo:fr:cotes-de-saint-mont.description: Côtes de Saint-Mont is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Saint-Mont and encompasses a variety of terroirs that contribute to the complexity of its wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation include specific areas where the climate and soil conditions favor the cultivation of certain grape varieties. The wines produced here are often characterized by their structure and aging potential.
geo:fr:cotes-de-saint-mont.style: Structured red wines with aging potential

geo:fr:cotes-du-marmandais.name: Cotes du Marmandais
geo:fr:cotes-du-marmandais.description: Côtes du Marmandais is an AOC located in the South West France region, characterized by its production of red wines primarily from Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Cabernet Sauvignon. The appellation is situated near the town of Marmande, benefiting from a climate that is influenced by both the Atlantic and Mediterranean, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, with a focus on producing wines that reflect the local terroir and varietal characteristics.
geo:fr:cotes-du-marmandais.style: Red wines with a blend of fruit and structure

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-chusclan.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Chusclan
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-chusclan.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Chusclan is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries which encompass the village of Chusclan and surrounding areas. The appellation primarily produces red wines characterized by a blend of Grenache and Syrah, reflecting the terroir of the region. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and spice, influenced by the Mediterranean climate and the diverse soils of the area.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-chusclan.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and spicy notes

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-laudun.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Laudun
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-laudun.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Laudun is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass several communes, with a focus on producing red wines. The appellation benefits from the Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines. The wines are typically characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and balanced structure.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-laudun.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and balanced structure

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-massif-du.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Massif d'Uchaux
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-massif-du.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Massif d'Uchaux is an appellation located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the village of Uchaux and surrounding areas. The terroir is characterized by clay and limestone soils, with a Mediterranean climate that influences the grape growing conditions. The appellation permits a variety of grape types, primarily focusing on blends that reflect the regional characteristics.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-massif-du.style: Red and white blends with a focus on fruit and spice characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-plan-de-d.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Plan de Dieu
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-plan-de-d.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Plan de Dieu is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is known for producing red wines primarily from Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre grapes. The appellation is situated near the village of Plan de Dieu, characterized by its clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the wines' structure and complexity.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-plan-de-d.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-puymeras.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Puymeras
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-puymeras.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Puymeras is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass specific communes, allowing for the production of higher-quality wines than standard Côtes du Rhône. The terroir is characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines. The appellation primarily produces red wines, which are known for their fruit-forward character and balanced structure.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-puymeras.style: Red wines with fruit-forward character and balanced structure

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-roaix.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Roaix
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-roaix.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Roaix is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the village of Roaix and surrounding areas. The appellation is primarily known for producing red wines, which are typically blends dominated by Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. The terroir features a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the character of the wines.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-roaix.style: Red wines, often blends with fruit-forward and spicy characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sablet.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Sablet
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sablet.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Sablet is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries which encompass the village of Sablet and surrounding areas. The appellation primarily produces red wines, characterized by a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre grapes. The terroir features a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, facilitating optimal grape ripening.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sablet.style: Red wines with a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-saint-ger.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Saint-Gervais
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-saint-ger.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Saint-Gervais is an AOC located within the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries which encompass specific communes that adhere to stricter production standards than the broader Côtes du Rhône AOC. The appellation primarily produces red wines characterized by a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre, reflecting the typical varietal composition of the region. The terroir includes a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the wines' structure and complexity.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-saint-ger.style: Red wines with a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sainte-ce.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Sainte-Cecile
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sainte-ce.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Sainte-Cécile is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass specific villages, allowing for a higher quality of wine production than standard Côtes du Rhône. The appellation primarily produces red wines characterized by a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre, reflecting the typical varietal composition of the Southern Rhône. The terroir consists of clay-limestone soils and a Mediterranean climate, which contribute to the distinctive profiles of the wines.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-sainte-ce.style: Red wines with a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-seguret.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Seguret
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-seguret.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Séguret is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the village of Séguret and surrounding areas. The appellation primarily produces red wines, characterized by a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. The terroir features clay-limestone soils and a Mediterranean climate, contributing to the wines' structure and complexity.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-seguret.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-signargue.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Signargues
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-signargue.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Signargues is an appellation located in the Southern Rhône region of France, recognized for its red wines. The appellation is part of the broader Côtes du Rhône Villages designation, which allows for higher quality standards than the general Côtes du Rhône AOC. It encompasses several communes, with specific regulations governing vineyard practices and wine production to ensure quality. The terroir is characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-signargue.style: Red wines, typically robust and fruit-forward

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-visan.name: Cotes du Rhone Villages Visan
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-visan.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages Visan is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It encompasses the village of Visan and its surrounding areas, characterized by a Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, including clay, limestone, and gravel. The appellation is known for producing red wines that reflect the traditional Rhône blend, primarily using Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined by the INAO, ensuring adherence to specific viticultural practices and quality standards.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages-visan.style: Red wines with a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre

geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon-villages.name: Cotes du Roussillon Villages
geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon-villages.description: Côtes du Roussillon Villages is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, recognized for its red wines. The appellation encompasses several communes within the Roussillon, characterized by a Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, including clay, limestone, and schist. The wines produced here are typically blends, showcasing the region's key grape varieties and reflecting the terroir's influence. The legal boundaries of the appellation allow for specific vineyard practices and grape selections to ensure quality.
geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon-villages.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice characteristics

geo:fr:cotes-du-vivarais.name: Cotes du Vivarais
geo:fr:cotes-du-vivarais.description: Côtes du Vivarais is an AOC located in the Rhône Valley of France, established in 2015. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs characterized by limestone and clay soils, with a Mediterranean climate that influences the grape growing conditions. It primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that showcase the region's key grape varieties. The wines are known for their fruit-forward profiles and balanced structure.
geo:fr:cotes-du-vivarais.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and balanced structure

geo:fr:cour-cheverny.name: Cour-Cheverny
geo:fr:cour-cheverny.description: Cour-Cheverny is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, established in 1993. It is known for producing white wines primarily from the Romorantin grape variety, which is indigenous to the region. The appellation's vineyards benefit from a continental climate, with a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the character of the wines.
geo:fr:cour-cheverny.style: Fresh, mineral-driven white wines with citrus and floral notes

geo:fr:cremant-de-bordeaux.name: Cremant de Bordeaux
geo:fr:cremant-de-bordeaux.description: Crémant de Bordeaux is an appellation for sparkling wine produced in the Bordeaux region of France. It is classified as an AOC and is characterized by traditional method production, where secondary fermentation occurs in the bottle. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, contributing to the diversity of styles within the sparkling wines.
geo:fr:cremant-de-bordeaux.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with diverse grape varieties

geo:fr:cremant-de-die.name: Cremant de Die
geo:fr:cremant-de-die.description: Crémant de Die is an AOC sparkling wine produced in the Rhône Valley region of France. The appellation is situated around the town of Die and is known for its unique terroir, characterized by limestone soils and a continental climate. The sparkling wines are primarily made using the traditional method, which involves secondary fermentation in the bottle. Crémant de Die is recognized for its fresh and fruity profile, often showcasing the region's distinctive grape varieties.
geo:fr:cremant-de-die.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with fresh and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:cremant-de-limoux.name: Cremant de Limoux
geo:fr:cremant-de-limoux.description: Cremant de Limoux is an AOC designated sparkling wine produced in the Limoux region of Languedoc, France. The appellation is known for its traditional method of production, which includes secondary fermentation in the bottle. It is recognized for its historical significance as one of the earliest regions in France to produce sparkling wine, with records dating back to the 16th century.
geo:fr:cremant-de-limoux.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with diverse grape blends

geo:fr:criots-batard-montrachet.name: Criots-Batard-Montrachet
geo:fr:criots-batard-montrachet.description: Criots-Batard-Montrachet is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is for its production of white wines exclusively from the Chardonnay grape. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Mont Rachet, benefiting from a unique microclimate and limestone-rich soils that contribute to the complexity of the wines. The legal boundaries of Criots-Batard-Montrachet are defined, and it is part of the Montrachet grand cru area, known for producing some of the white wines in the world.
geo:fr:criots-batard-montrachet.style: Rich, complex white wines with aging potential

geo:fr:crozes-hermitage.name: Crozes-Hermitage
geo:fr:crozes-hermitage.description: Hermitage's sprawling neighbor, offering Northern Rhône character at accessible prices. The flatter vineyards produce wines that are fruitier and more approachable than the hill above, though the best sites — Gervans, Les Chassis — approach Hermitage in quality. An excellent entry point.

geo:fr:crozes-hermitage-blanc.name: Crozes-Hermitage Blanc
geo:fr:crozes-hermitage-blanc.description: Crozes-Hermitage Blanc is an AOC located in the Northern Rhône region of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, with soils composed of clay, limestone, and alluvial deposits. The climate is characterized by a continental influence, with warm summers and cold winters, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are typically made from Marsanne and Roussanne grapes, showcasing the region's unique characteristics.
geo:fr:crozes-hermitage-blanc.style: Rich, full-bodied white wines with floral and stone fruit notes

geo:fr:cremant-dalsace.name: Crémant d'Alsace
geo:fr:cremant-dalsace.description: Crémant d'Alsace is a sparkling wine appellation located in the Alsace region of France. Established in 1976, it is known for producing traditional method sparkling wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of communes within Alsace, characterized by a diverse range of soils and microclimates that contribute to the wine's complexity.
geo:fr:cremant-dalsace.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with diverse grape varieties

geo:fr:cremant-de-bourgogne.name: Crémant de Bourgogne
geo:fr:cremant-de-bourgogne.description: Crémant de Bourgogne is an AOC designation for sparkling wines produced in the Burgundy region of France. The appellation encompasses various communes across the region, allowing for a diverse range of terroirs. The wines are typically made using the traditional method, which involves a secondary fermentation in the bottle. Crémant de Bourgogne can be produced in both white and rosé styles, showcasing the region's characteristic freshness and complexity.
geo:fr:cremant-de-bourgogne.style: Sparkling wine made using the traditional method

geo:fr:cremant-de-loire.name: Crémant de Loire
geo:fr:cremant-de-loire.description: Crémant de Loire is an AOC designation for sparkling wines produced in the Loire Valley, specifically within the Touraine region. The appellation allows for traditional method sparkling wine production, utilizing secondary fermentation in the bottle. It encompasses a variety of terroirs and microclimates, contributing to the diversity of styles within the wines.
geo:fr:cremant-de-loire.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with diverse grape varieties

geo:fr:cremant-du-jura.name: Crémant du Jura
geo:fr:cremant-du-jura.description: Crémant du Jura is an AOC-designated sparkling wine produced in the Jura region of France. The appellation is known for its traditional method of production, which involves secondary fermentation in the bottle. It encompasses several communes within the Jura, characterized by unique geological formations and a continental climate that influences the wine's characteristics.
geo:fr:cremant-du-jura.style: Traditional method sparkling wine with diverse grape blends

geo:fr:crepy.name: Crépy
geo:fr:crepy.description: Crépy is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, specifically in the Haute-Savoie department. The appellation is known for its production of white wines primarily from the Jacquère grape variety. The vineyards are situated on the slopes of the Mont Blanc massif, benefiting from a unique microclimate that influences the character of the wines. The legal boundaries of Crépy encompass several communes, with a focus on maintaining traditional viticultural practices.
geo:fr:crepy.style: Light, crisp white wines with mineral notes

geo:it:custoza.name: Custoza
geo:it:custoza.description: Custoza is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is situated near Lake Garda, benefiting from a mild climate and diverse soils that contribute to the character of its wines. Custoza primarily produces white wines, often characterized by a blend of local grape varieties. The area has a historical significance in Italian viticulture, with winemaking traditions dating back centuries.
geo:it:custoza.style: Fresh, light-bodied white wines with floral and fruity notes

geo:fr:cerons.name: Cérons
geo:fr:cerons.description: Cérons is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, primarily known for its sweet dessert wines. The appellation is situated near the Garonne River, benefiting from a microclimate that promotes the development of noble rot (Botrytis cinerea) on the grapes. Cérons wines are typically produced from Sémillon, Sauvignon Blanc, and Muscadelle, which are harvested late to achieve high sugar levels. The legal boundaries of Cérons encompass specific communes, ensuring the preservation of its unique terroir and wine style.
geo:fr:cerons.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, complex flavors

geo:fr:cote-de-brouilly.name: Côte de Brouilly
geo:fr:cote-de-brouilly.description: Côte de Brouilly is an AOC located within the Beaujolais region of France, known for its production of red wines primarily from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Brouilly hill, characterized by volcanic soil and a unique microclimate that influences the ripening of the grapes. The legal boundaries of Côte de Brouilly encompass specific vineyards that benefit from optimal sun exposure and drainage, contributing to the distinctiveness of the wines produced here. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruitiness and acidity, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:cote-de-brouilly.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and balanced acidity

geo:fr:cote-de-nuits-villages.name: Côte de Nuits-Villages
geo:fr:cote-de-nuits-villages.description: Côte de Nuits-Villages is an AOC located in the northern and southern parts of the Côte de Nuits region in Burgundy, France. It encompasses wines produced from select communes including Fixin, Brochon, Premeaux-Prissey, Comblanchien, and Corgoloin. The appellation primarily focuses on red wines made from Pinot Noir, with some production of Chardonnay. The wines are characterized by their expression of the local terroir, which includes clay-limestone soils and a continental climate.
geo:fr:cote-de-nuits-villages.style: Primarily red wines with some white; reflects local terroir.

geo:fr:cote-des-bar.name: Côte des Bar
geo:fr:cote-des-bar.description: Côte des Bar is an appellation located in the southern part of the Champagne region of France. It is characterized by its predominantly chalky and clay soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The appellation is known for producing primarily Pinot Noir, which is often blended with other varieties to create traditional method sparkling wines. The legal boundaries of Côte des Bar encompass several villages, with Bar-sur-Aube being a notable center of production. The wines from this appellation are recognized for their fruit-forward profiles and are often crafted by smaller, independent producers.
geo:fr:cote-des-bar.style: Sparkling wines with a focus on fruit-forward characteristics

geo:fr:cote-des-blancs.name: Côte des Blancs
geo:fr:cote-des-blancs.description: Côte des Blancs is an appellation located within the Champagne region of France, primarily known for its production of high-quality sparkling wines. It is situated on the eastern side of the Montagne de Reims and is characterized by its chalky soils, which are conducive to the cultivation of Chardonnay grapes. The appellation encompasses several villages, including Avize, Cramant, and Oger, which are recognized for their exceptional terroir and contribution to the production of Blanc de Blancs Champagne. The wines from this area are typically noted for their elegance and finesse.
geo:fr:cote-des-blancs.style: Elegant sparkling wines, predominantly made from Chardonnay

geo:fr:cote-roannaise.name: Côte Roannaise
geo:fr:cote-roannaise.description: Côte Roannaise is an appellation located in the Loire Valley of France, known primarily for its red wines made from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated on the western slopes of the Monts de la Madeleine, characterized by granite soils and a continental climate that contributes to the unique expression of its wines. Established as an AOC in 1994, Côte Roannaise encompasses several communes and is recognized for its focus on quality and terroir-driven production.
geo:fr:cote-roannaise.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:cote-rotie.name: Côte-Rôtie
geo:fr:cote-rotie.description: The Northern Rhône's most prestigious appellation, where Syrah reaches its aromatic peak. The 'roasted slope' — terraces carved into granite and schist — produces wines of haunting complexity. A splash of Viognier is permitted, adding floral lift. The best examples evolve for decades.

geo:fr:cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Côtes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Côtes de Bordeaux is an AOC located within the Bordeaux wine region of France. It encompasses several sub-regions, including Côtes de Bordeaux Castillon, Côtes de Bordeaux Blaye, and Côtes de Bordeaux Francs. The appellation is characterized by its diverse terroirs, which include clay, limestone, and gravel soils, contributing to a range of wine styles. The climate is maritime, influenced by the nearby Atlantic Ocean, which helps in the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are primarily red, with a focus on fruit-forward profiles and approachable tannins.
geo:fr:cotes-de-bordeaux.style: Fruity red wines with approachable tannins

geo:fr:cotes-de-bourg.name: Côtes de Bourg
geo:fr:cotes-de-bourg.description: Côtes de Bourg is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, known primarily for its red wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Dordogne River, characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a temperate maritime climate. Côtes de Bourg is recognized for producing wines that are typically blends, with a focus on Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The region has a long viticultural history, dating back to Roman times, and has gained recognition for its quality and value.
geo:fr:cotes-de-bourg.style: Red wines, often fruit-forward with moderate tannins

geo:fr:cotes-de-duras.name: Côtes de Duras
geo:fr:cotes-de-duras.description: Côtes de Duras is an AOC located in the South West France region, characterized by its diverse terroir and varied microclimates. The appellation is situated near the town of Duras, encompassing a mix of hills and valleys that influence grape cultivation. The region primarily produces red wines, often blended from several grape varieties, and has a history of winemaking that dates back to the Roman era.
geo:fr:cotes-de-duras.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:cotes-de-provence.name: Côtes de Provence
geo:fr:cotes-de-provence.description: The appellation that made pale rosé a global phenomenon. Stretching from Nice to Aix-en-Provence, the region's limestone and schist soils produce dry, elegant pink wines that define summer drinking. Reds and whites exist but rosé dominates — over 80% of production.

geo:fr:cotes-du-jura.name: Côtes du Jura
geo:fr:cotes-du-jura.description: Côtes du Jura is an AOC located within the Jura region of France, known primarily for its white wines. The appellation encompasses several communes and is characterized by its unique geological formations and microclimates, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The region's winemaking traditions date back centuries, with a focus on both traditional and innovative techniques.
geo:fr:cotes-du-jura.style: White wines with a focus on Chardonnay and Savagnin

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone.name: Côtes du Rhône
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone.description: Côtes du Rhône is an AOC located in the Rhône Valley of France, established to regulate the production of red, white, and rosé wines. The appellation encompasses a large area, extending from Vienne in the north to Avignon in the south, and includes a diverse range of terroirs. Red wines are predominantly produced, characterized by a blend of grape varieties, with Grenache being the most significant. The region's climate is Mediterranean, contributing to the ripening of grapes and the development of distinct wine styles.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone.style: Red wines, often blended, with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages.name: Côtes du Rhône Villages
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages.description: Côtes du Rhône Villages is an AOC located within the Rhône Valley, designated for red wines produced from specific villages. The appellation encompasses a selection of communes that meet stricter quality criteria than the broader Côtes du Rhône designation. It is characterized by a diverse range of terroirs, contributing to the complexity of the wines. The regulations permit a blend of several grape varieties, with an emphasis on traditional Rhône varietals.
geo:fr:cotes-du-rhone-villages.style: Red wines, typically full-bodied with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon.name: Côtes du Roussillon
geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon.description: Côtes du Roussillon is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, known for its diverse terroirs and Mediterranean climate. The appellation encompasses a variety of landscapes, including hills, valleys, and coastal areas, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. It allows for a wide range of grape varieties, with a focus on red wines, and is characterized by its blend of local and international varietals.
geo:fr:cotes-du-roussillon.style: Predominantly red wines, often blended, with rich fruit flavors

geo:fr:cotes-du-ventoux.name: Côtes du Ventoux
geo:fr:cotes-du-ventoux.description: Côtes du Ventoux is an appellation located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is situated on the slopes of Mont Ventoux, characterized by a diverse range of soils and a Mediterranean climate that influences the viticulture. The appellation is primarily known for red wines, which are produced under the regulations set forth by the AOC designation. The area encompasses several communes, and the wines often reflect the unique terroir of the region.
geo:fr:cotes-du-ventoux.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:es:dehesa-del-carrizal.name: Dehesa del Carrizal
geo:es:dehesa-del-carrizal.description: Dehesa del Carrizal is a wine appellation located within the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is classified as a Vino de Pago, which denotes a single estate with specific quality standards. The appellation is characterized by its unique terroir, influenced by a combination of climate and soil types that support the cultivation of various grape varieties. The wines produced here are primarily red, showcasing the region's ability to produce complex and structured wines.
geo:es:dehesa-del-carrizal.style: Red wines with complexity and structure

geo:it:delle-venezie.name: Delle Venezie
geo:it:delle-venezie.description: Delle Venezie is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) that spans three regions: Veneto, Friuli-Venezia Giulia, and Trentino-Alto Adige. Established as a DOC in 2017, it is recognized as Italy's largest DOC by volume. The appellation primarily produces white wines, with a focus on varietals such as Pinot Grigio, Chardonnay, and Garganega.
geo:it:delle-venezie.style: Primarily white wines, notably Pinot Grigio.

geo:us:diamond-mountain-district.name: Diamond Mountain District
geo:us:diamond-mountain-district.description: Diamond Mountain District is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Napa Valley, California. Established in 2001, it is characterized by its mountainous terrain and elevation, which contributes to unique microclimates. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc. The volcanic soils and varying elevations influence the flavor profiles of the wines produced here.
geo:us:diamond-mountain-district.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and complexity

geo:it:dogliani.name: Dogliani
geo:it:dogliani.description: Dogliani is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Dolcetto grape variety. The appellation encompasses the communes of Dogliani and parts of surrounding areas, characterized by hilly terrain and a climate conducive to viticulture. The wines are typically characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins, reflecting the unique terroir of the region.
geo:it:dogliani.style: Fruity red wines with moderate tannins and acidity

geo:it:dolcetto-dalba.name: Dolcetto d'Alba
geo:it:dolcetto-dalba.description: Dolcetto d'Alba is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It encompasses various communes around the town of Alba, known for its hilly terrain and favorable climate for grape cultivation. The appellation is primarily associated with the Dolcetto grape variety, which produces red wines characterized by their fruit-forward profile and moderate acidity. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined by specific communes, ensuring the preservation of local viticultural practices.
geo:it:dolcetto-dalba.style: Fruity red wines with low acidity and soft tannins

geo:it:dolcetto-di-ovada.name: Dolcetto di Ovada
geo:it:dolcetto-di-ovada.description: Dolcetto di Ovada is a red wine appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, classified as a DOC. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries around the town of Ovada and includes specific communes within the province of Alessandria. Dolcetto is the primary grape variety permitted for production, known for its low acidity and fruit-forward profile. The terroir features clay and limestone soils, contributing to the wine's character. The wines from this appellation are typically characterized by their deep color and approachable tannins.
geo:it:dolcetto-di-ovada.style: Red wine with low acidity and fruit-forward characteristics

geo:es:dominio-de-valdepusa.name: Dominio de Valdepusa
geo:es:dominio-de-valdepusa.description: Dominio de Valdepusa is a wine appellation located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is recognized as a Vino de Pago, a classification that denotes high-quality wines produced in a specific estate. The appellation is known for its unique terroir, characterized by a combination of clay and limestone soils, which influence the characteristics of the wines produced. The climate is continental, with hot summers and cold winters, contributing to the ripening of the grapes.
geo:es:dominio-de-valdepusa.style: Red wines with a focus on complexity and structure

geo:pt:douro.name: Douro
geo:pt:douro.description: Once known only for Port, now Portugal's most exciting table wine region. The same terraces, same grapes, same extreme conditions — but fermented dry. Wines of power and originality, distinctly Portuguese. Niepoort and Quinta do Vale Meão lead the way.

geo:pt:douro-doc.name: Douro
geo:pt:douro-doc.description: The Douro appellation is located in northern Portugal, defined by the Douro River valley. It is for its steep terraced vineyards and is one of the oldest demarcated wine regions in the world, established in 1756. The region's unique schist soil and Mediterranean climate contribute to the production of both Port and dry table wines. The appellation encompasses several subregions and is characterized by its diverse microclimates and elevations, influencing the style and quality of the wines produced.
geo:pt:douro-doc.style: Known for Port wine and robust red table wines

geo:us:dry-creek-valley-ava.name: Dry Creek Valley
geo:us:dry-creek-valley-ava.description: Dry Creek Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. The region is characterized by its diverse microclimates and well-drained soils, which contribute to the cultivation of a variety of grape types. The AVA is particularly noted for its production of Zinfandel, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Sauvignon Blanc, reflecting the area's rich viticultural heritage and favorable growing conditions.
geo:us:dry-creek-valley-ava.style: Red wines with a focus on Zinfandel and Cabernet Sauvignon

geo:fr:duche-duzes.name: Duche d'Uzes
geo:fr:duche-duzes.description: The Duché d'Uzès is an AOC located in the southern Rhône Valley of France, established in 2013. It encompasses a range of communes around the town of Uzès, characterized by a diverse terroir that includes clay, limestone, and sandy soils. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which influences the ripening of grapes. The appellation allows for both red and white wines, showcasing a variety of styles that reflect the local conditions.
geo:fr:duche-duzes.style: Red and white wines with varied styles reflecting local terroir

geo:us:dundee-hills-ava.name: Dundee Hills
geo:us:dundee-hills-ava.description: Dundee Hills is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within the Willamette Valley in Oregon. Established in 2001, it is characterized by its volcanic soils and hilly terrain, which contribute to the unique terroir of the region. The climate is influenced by the Pacific Ocean, resulting in a temperate maritime climate that is conducive to viticulture. The AVA is known for producing high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, reflecting the characteristics of the region's geology and climate.
geo:us:dundee-hills-ava.style: Red wines with notable Pinot Noir and some Chardonnay

geo:it:durello.name: Durello
geo:it:durello.description: Durello is a DOC appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy, primarily known for its production of sparkling wines. The appellation encompasses specific legal boundaries that define the area where Durello grapes can be cultivated and processed. The terroir is characterized by volcanic soils and a climate that supports the cultivation of high-acidity grapes, which are essential for sparkling wine production. The wines produced under this appellation often exhibit a distinct minerality and freshness.
geo:it:durello.style: Sparkling wines with high acidity and mineral character

geo:pt:dao.name: Dão
geo:pt:dao.description: Mountain-ringed region in central Portugal, producing elegant reds from Touriga Nacional and fragrant whites from Encruzado. Granite soils and altitude create wines of restraint and minerality — Portugal's answer to Burgundy. Quality revolution ongoing.

geo:pt:dao-doc.name: Dão
geo:pt:dao-doc.description: Dão is an appellation located in the Dão region of Portugal, characterized by its mountainous terrain and granite soils. The legal boundaries of Dão encompass several municipalities, including Viseu and Nelas. The region is known for its moderate climate, which is influenced by the nearby Serra da Estrela mountains, allowing for the production of both red and white wines. Dão wines often exhibit a balance of acidity and fruit, with aging potential.
geo:pt:dao-doc.style: Balanced red and white wines with good aging potential

geo:fr:echezeaux.name: Echezeaux
geo:fr:echezeaux.description: Échezeaux is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation encompasses several climats, contributing to a diversity of expressions within the wines. Échezeaux is adjacent to the more famous Grand Cru vineyards, which influences its reputation and market presence.
geo:fr:echezeaux.style: Red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:au:eden-valley-gi.name: Eden Valley
geo:au:eden-valley-gi.description: The Barossa's elevated cousin, where Riesling finds its Australian benchmark. The cooler altitude and ancient soils produce wines of citrus purity and remarkable longevity. Henschke's Hill of Grace, though Shiraz, is Australia's most celebrated single vineyard.

geo:us:edna-valley.name: Edna Valley
geo:us:edna-valley.description: Edna Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within the Central Coast region of California. It is characterized by a cool climate influenced by the nearby Pacific Ocean, which allows for a long growing season. The AVA is known for its production of high-quality Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, with a focus on both still and sparkling wines. The valley's unique topography and soil composition contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines.

geo:at:eisenberg.name: Eisenberg
geo:at:eisenberg.description: Eisenberg is a designated Austrian wine appellation located within the Burgenland region, classified as a DAC (Districtus Austriae Controllatus). It is primarily known for its production of red wines, with Blaufränkisch being the dominant grape variety. The appellation features a unique terroir characterized by steep slopes and mineral-rich soils, which contribute to the distinctive quality of the wines produced here.
geo:at:eisenberg.style: Red wines, primarily from Blaufränkisch, known for their depth and structure

geo:it:elba.name: Elba
geo:it:elba.description: Elba is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located on the island of Elba in Tuscany, Italy. The region is characterized by its Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of a range of wines. The appellation primarily focuses on red wines, with a notable emphasis on indigenous grape varieties. Elba's winemaking history dates back to ancient times, reflecting the island's long-standing agricultural traditions.
geo:it:elba.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit and moderate tannins

geo:es:emporda-do.name: Empordà
geo:es:emporda-do.description: Empordà is a designated wine appellation located in the northeastern part of Spain, specifically in the province of Girona, Catalonia. It is situated near the Mediterranean coast, characterized by a diverse landscape that includes hills and plains. The region has a Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes and the production of both red and white wines. Empordà is known for its use of indigenous grape varieties as well as international ones, reflecting its historical and cultural influences.
geo:es:emporda-do.style: Wines range from fresh whites to robust reds, often with Mediterranean influences

geo:pt:encostas-de-aire.name: Encostas de Aire
geo:pt:encostas-de-aire.description: Encostas de Aire is a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located within the Lisboa region of Portugal. The appellation is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes a mix of clay and sandy soils, contributing to the unique profiles of its wines. Encostas de Aire primarily produces red wines, with a focus on traditional Portuguese grape varieties. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which influences the ripening of the grapes and the overall style of the wines.
geo:pt:encostas-de-aire.style: Red wines with regional grape varieties

geo:fr:entraygues-le-fel.name: Entraygues-Le Fel
geo:fr:entraygues-le-fel.description: Entraygues-Le Fel is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines. The appellation is characterized by its unique terroir, which is influenced by the confluence of the Lot and Truyère rivers. The climate is continental, with warm summers and cool winters, contributing to the ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific communes, ensuring the preservation of its distinct wine styles.
geo:fr:entraygues-le-fel.style: Red wines with a focus on freshness and fruit expression

geo:fr:entre-deux-mers.name: Entre-Deux-Mers
geo:fr:entre-deux-mers.description: Entre-Deux-Mers is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, defined by its geographical position between the Garonne and Dordogne rivers. The appellation is primarily known for its white wines, which are typically blends of Sauvignon Blanc, Sémillon, and Muscadelle. The region's climate is influenced by the nearby rivers, contributing to the freshness and acidity of the wines. The legal boundaries of Entre-Deux-Mers encompass a variety of terroirs, including clay, limestone, and gravel soils, which affect the character of the wines produced.
geo:fr:entre-deux-mers.style: Crisp, aromatic white wines with good acidity

geo:us:eola-amity-hills-ava.name: Eola-Amity Hills
geo:us:eola-amity-hills-ava.description: Eola-Amity Hills is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within the Willamette Valley in Oregon. The appellation is characterized by its rolling hills and volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the region. The climate is influenced by the Pacific Ocean, providing a temperate environment suitable for grape growing. Eola-Amity Hills is known for producing high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, with a focus on expressing the characteristics of the local terroir.
geo:us:eola-amity-hills-ava.style: Red wines with a focus on Pinot Noir

geo:it:erbaluce-di-caluso.name: Erbaluce di Caluso
geo:it:erbaluce-di-caluso.description: Erbaluce di Caluso is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass specific communes within the province of Turin. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Erbaluce grape, which is indigenous to the area. The wines are characterized by their crisp acidity and aromatic profile, often showcasing floral and citrus notes.
geo:it:erbaluce-di-caluso.style: Crisp white wines with floral and citrus characteristics

geo:fr:estaing.name: Estaing
geo:fr:estaing.description: Estaing is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines primarily from the Fer Servadou grape. The appellation is characterized by its unique terroir, which includes a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the distinctiveness of its wines. Estaing is situated near the Lot River, benefiting from a climate that supports the cultivation of its key grape varieties. The wines produced here often reflect the local traditions and are typically crafted to showcase the varietal characteristics of the grapes used.
geo:fr:estaing.style: Red wines with a focus on Fer Servadou and local varietals

geo:it:etna-doc.name: Etna
geo:it:etna-doc.description: Etna is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located on the slopes of Mount Etna in Sicily, Italy. The appellation is characterized by its volcanic soils and high altitudes, which contribute to the unique terroir. It primarily produces red wines, with Nerello Mascalese being the dominant grape variety. The region's climate is influenced by its elevation and proximity to the Mediterranean Sea, leading to a distinct flavor profile in its wines.
geo:it:etna-doc.style: Red wines with mineral notes and bright acidity

geo:it:falerno-del-massico.name: Falerno del Massico
geo:it:falerno-del-massico.description: Falerno del Massico is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Campania region of Italy. The appellation is situated in the province of Caserta, near the ancient Roman site of Falernum, known for its historical significance in wine production. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters, which contributes to the ripening of its grape varieties.
geo:it:falerno-del-massico.style: Red wines with structured profiles and aging potential

geo:it:faro.name: Faro
geo:it:faro.description: Faro is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Sicily, Italy. Established in 1971, it encompasses a specific area around the town of Faro Superiore, near Messina. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and Mediterranean climate, which contribute to the cultivation of high-quality red wines. Faro wines are primarily made from indigenous grape varieties, reflecting the region's viticultural heritage.
geo:it:faro.style: Red wines with structure and complexity, often with aging potential

geo:fr:faugeres.name: Faugères
geo:fr:faugeres.description: Faugères is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 1982. The appellation is situated on the slopes of the Cévennes mountains, characterized by schist soils that contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. Faugères primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. The climate is Mediterranean, with hot summers and mild winters, which supports the ripening of grapes.
geo:fr:faugeres.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and complexity

geo:it:fiano-di-avellino.name: Fiano di Avellino
geo:it:fiano-di-avellino.description: Fiano di Avellino is a white wine appellation located in the Campania region of Italy, classified as DOCG. The appellation is known for its production of wines primarily from the Fiano grape variety, which is indigenous to the area. The legal boundaries encompass several municipalities within the province of Avellino, characterized by hilly terrain and volcanic soils that contribute to the wine's distinct profile. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, allowing for optimal grape ripening.
geo:it:fiano-di-avellino.style: Dry white wines with floral and nutty characteristics

geo:fr:fitou.name: Fitou
