geo:fr:fitou.description: Fitou is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 1948. It is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes both coastal and inland influences. The appellation is known for its red wines, primarily produced from a blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Carignan. Fitou's vineyards are situated on hillsides and plateaus, benefiting from a Mediterranean climate that contributes to the ripening of grapes. The wines typically exhibit a robust character with a focus on fruit and spice.
geo:fr:fitou.style: Robust red wines with fruit and spice characteristics

geo:fr:fixin.name: Fixin
geo:fr:fixin.description: The northernmost village appellation of the Côte de Nuits, just south of Dijon. Fixin produces robust, structured Pinot Noir that rewards patience. Often overlooked in favor of famous neighbors like Gevrey-Chambertin, Fixin offers serious Burgundy at more accessible prices. The village has five Premier Cru vineyards, including Clos de la Perrière.

geo:fr:fleurie.name: Fleurie
geo:fr:fleurie.description: Fleurie is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known primarily for its production of red wines made from the Gamay grape. The appellation is characterized by its granitic soils and a relatively warm climate, which contribute to the aromatic and fruit-forward style of its wines. Fleurie is one of the ten Beaujolais Crus, recognized for producing wines that are often more refined and elegant compared to other Beaujolais wines.
geo:fr:fleurie.style: Light-bodied red wines with floral and fruity characteristics

geo:us:fort-ross-seaview-ava.name: Fort Ross-Seaview
geo:us:fort-ross-seaview-ava.description: Fort Ross-Seaview is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 2011, it is situated on the rugged coastal hills of the Sonoma Coast, characterized by its elevation and proximity to the Pacific Ocean. The region benefits from a cool maritime climate, which is conducive to the production of high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. The unique terroir, with its diverse soils and microclimates, contributes to the distinctive character of the wines produced here.
geo:us:fort-ross-seaview-ava.style: Red wines with a focus on Pinot Noir

geo:it:franciacorta-docg.name: Franciacorta
geo:it:franciacorta-docg.description: Italy's answer to Champagne, made by the same method from the same grapes. The morainic soils of Lombardy produce wines of finesse and complexity. Less famous than its French counterpart, often equally good. Satèn, the blanc de blancs, is particularly elegant.

geo:fr:francs-cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Francs Côtes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:francs-cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Francs Côtes de Bordeaux is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France. It is situated on the right bank of the Dordogne River, adjacent to the more prominent Côtes de Bordeaux appellations. The appellation covers a variety of terroirs, characterized by clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the production of primarily red wines. The climate is influenced by the Atlantic Ocean, providing a temperate environment suitable for viticulture. The wines produced here are often blends, showcasing the region's diverse grape varieties.
geo:fr:francs-cotes-de-bordeaux.style: Red wines, typically blends, with fruit-forward characteristics

geo:de:franken.name: Franken
geo:de:franken.description: Distinctive for its Bocksbeutel flask and dry Silvaner wines, Franken (Franconia) centers on the Main River around Würzburg. The continental climate and shell-limestone soils produce earthy, full-bodied whites quite unlike typical German Riesling. Silvaner here achieves a depth rarely found elsewhere.

geo:de:franken-qba.name: Franken
geo:de:franken-qba.description: Franken is a wine appellation located in the northern part of Bavaria, Germany. It is characterized by its distinct limestone soils and a continental climate, which contribute to the unique terroir of the region. The appellation is known for its traditional wine production methods and the use of Bocksbeutel bottles, a flat, round-shaped container. Key grape varieties include Silvaner, Riesling, and Müller-Thurgau, with Silvaner being particularly emblematic of the region. The wines from Franken often exhibit a balance of acidity and minerality, reflecting the terroir.
geo:de:franken-qba.style: Wines are typically dry, with a focus on minerality and freshness

geo:it:freisa-dasti.name: Freisa d'Asti
geo:it:freisa-dasti.description: Freisa d'Asti is a DOC appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, specifically around the town of Asti. It is known for producing red wines primarily from the Freisa grape variety, which is indigenous to the area. The appellation has a long history of viticulture, with records dating back to the Middle Ages. The wines are characterized by their bright acidity and moderate tannins, often exhibiting floral and berry notes.
geo:it:freisa-dasti.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with acidity and berry flavors

geo:it:friuli-colli-orientali.name: Friuli Colli Orientali
geo:it:friuli-colli-orientali.description: Friuli Colli Orientali is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. It encompasses hilly terrain characterized by a diverse range of soils, including marl and sandstone, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. The appellation is known primarily for its white wines, which are often crafted from a variety of indigenous and international grape varieties. The region benefits from a continental climate with influences from the Adriatic Sea, allowing for a long growing season and the development of aromatic profiles in the wines.
geo:it:friuli-colli-orientali.style: White wines with aromatic complexity and freshness

geo:it:friuli-grave.name: Friuli Grave
geo:it:friuli-grave.description: Friuli Grave is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its flat terrain and alluvial soils, which contribute to the cultivation of various grape varieties. It primarily produces white wines, with a focus on fresh and aromatic styles. The region benefits from a continental climate, influenced by the nearby Adriatic Sea and the Julian Alps.
geo:it:friuli-grave.style: Fresh and aromatic white wines with good acidity

geo:it:friuli.name: Friuli
geo:it:friuli.description: Regional umbrella DOC covering all four provinces of Friuli-Venezia Giulia — Udine, Pordenone, Gorizia, and Trieste — across approximately 20,000 hectares. Established by Italian ministerial decree in 2016 and registered as an EU PDO in 2020, the Friuli DOC sits alongside — not above — the historic sub-zone DOCs including Collio, Friuli Colli Orientali, Friuli Grave, Isonzo, and Carso. The signature style is crisp, aromatic varietal whites led by Pinot Grigio, alongside Friulano, Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay, and Ribolla Gialla. Structured reds — principally Merlot, Refosco, and Cabernet — are also permitted across the regional designation.
geo:it:friuli.style: Crisp aromatic varietal whites; structured reds across the regional umbrella DOC

geo:fr:fronsac.name: Fronsac
geo:fr:fronsac.description: Fronsac is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, recognized for its red wines primarily made from Merlot. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Dordogne River, adjacent to the more famous Pomerol and Saint-Émilion regions. The terroir consists of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the character of the wines produced. Fronsac has a historical significance dating back to the 18th century, with a reputation for producing quality wines that often offer good value relative to their more neighbors.
geo:fr:fronsac.style: Red wines, predominantly Merlot-based, with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:fronton.name: Fronton
geo:fr:fronton.description: Fronton is an AOC located in the South West France region, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is situated near the city of Toulouse and is characterized by its clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the unique terroir. The legal boundaries of Fronton encompass several communes, with a focus on the production of wines that reflect the local climate and geography. The wines are typically blends, showcasing the region's diverse grape varieties.
geo:fr:fronton.style: Red wines, often blends, with fruit-forward characteristics

geo:fr:gaillac.name: Gaillac
geo:fr:gaillac.description: Gaillac is an AOC located in the South West France region, characterized by its diverse terroir and a long history of winemaking dating back to Roman times. The appellation encompasses a variety of microclimates and soils, which contribute to the production of both red and white wines. The legal boundaries of Gaillac include several communes, with a focus on traditional grape varieties and innovative winemaking techniques.
geo:fr:gaillac.style: White wines with a range of styles from dry to sweet

geo:it:gallura.name: Gallura
geo:it:gallura.description: Gallura is a designated wine appellation located in the northeastern part of Sardinia, Italy. It is primarily known for its white wines, which are predominantly produced from the Vermentino grape variety. The appellation is characterized by its unique granite soils and a Mediterranean climate that contributes to the aromatic profile of its wines. Gallura is recognized for its commitment to quality and traditional winemaking practices, with a focus on expressing the local terroir.
geo:it:gallura.style: Crisp and aromatic white wines with mineral notes

geo:it:gambellara.name: Gambellara
geo:it:gambellara.description: Gambellara is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its white wines, which are predominantly produced from the Garganega grape, along with Trebbiano and Chardonnay. The region's volcanic soil and favorable climate contribute to the distinct characteristics of the wines.
geo:it:gambellara.style: White wines with fresh acidity and mineral notes

geo:it:gattinara.name: Gattinara
geo:it:gattinara.description: Gattinara is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Nebbiolo grape, which thrives in the region's unique terroir characterized by volcanic and glacial soils. Gattinara wines are noted for their structure and aging potential, often exhibiting complex aromas and flavors. The legal boundaries of Gattinara encompass several communes, with strict regulations governing vineyard practices and wine production.
geo:it:gattinara.style: Structured red wines with aging potential, primarily from Nebbiolo

geo:it:gavi.name: Gavi
geo:it:gavi.description: Gavi is a designated Controlled and Guaranteed Designation of Origin (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Cortese grape variety. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that benefits from both the Alps and the Mediterranean, contributing to the freshness and acidity of the wines.
geo:it:gavi.style: Crisp, dry white wines with high acidity and floral notes

geo:es:getariako-txakolina.name: Getariako Txakolina
geo:es:getariako-txakolina.description: Getariako Txakolina is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Basque Country of Spain, specifically along the northern coast. This appellation is characterized by its unique microclimate, influenced by the Atlantic Ocean, which contributes to the freshness and acidity of the wines produced. The region primarily focuses on white wines, with a tradition of producing Txakoli, a slightly effervescent wine that is often enjoyed young. The legal boundaries of the DO encompass several municipalities, including Getaria, Zarautz, and Aia, which are known for their steep vineyards and coastal landscapes.
geo:es:getariako-txakolina.style: Crisp, slightly effervescent white wines with high acidity

geo:fr:gevrey-chambertin.name: Gevrey-Chambertin
geo:fr:gevrey-chambertin.description: Home to more Grand Crus than any other Burgundy commune. Gevrey's wines combine power with finesse in a way few appellations can match. Le Chambertin itself — Napoleon's favorite — anchors a constellation of legendary vineyards. The best examples are profound, long-lived, and unmistakably great.

geo:it:ghemme.name: Ghemme
geo:it:ghemme.description: Ghemme is a DOCG appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, recognized for its production of red wines primarily from the Nebbiolo grape. The appellation is situated in the northern part of Piedmont, near the Sesia River, and is characterized by its hilly terrain and diverse microclimates that contribute to the complexity of its wines. Ghemme wines must adhere to strict regulations regarding grape composition, aging, and production methods, ensuring a high standard of quality.
geo:it:ghemme.style: Structured red wines with aging potential and floral, fruity notes

geo:fr:gigondas.name: Gigondas
geo:fr:gigondas.description: Châteauneuf's less famous neighbor, often offering similar quality at lower prices. The Dentelles de Montmirail mountains shelter vineyards that produce wines of structure and depth. More Syrah and Mourvèdre here than in Châteauneuf, giving wines a firmer spine. Age-worthy and undervalued.

geo:fr:givry.name: Givry
geo:fr:givry.description: Givry is an AOC located in the Côte Chalonnaise region of France, primarily known for its red wines made from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated in the Saône-et-Loire department and encompasses several villages, with Givry itself being the most notable. The vineyards benefit from a continental climate, with warm summers and cold winters, contributing to the ripening of the grapes. Givry wines are characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:givry.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:au:grampians-gi.name: Grampians
geo:au:grampians-gi.description: The Grampians is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia. It is characterized by a cool climate and diverse soil types, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. The region is known for its elevation, which influences the ripening of grapes and the development of flavor profiles. The principal grape varieties cultivated in the Grampians include Shiraz and Riesling, with a focus on red wine production.
geo:au:grampians-gi.style: Red wines with a focus on Shiraz

geo:fr:grands-echezeaux.name: Grands-Echezeaux
geo:fr:grands-echezeaux.description: Grands-Echezeaux is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated adjacent to the more famous Echezeaux, and it benefits from a similar terroir characterized by limestone and clay soils. The wines from Grands-Echezeaux are known for their complexity and aging potential, reflecting the unique microclimate of the area.
geo:fr:grands-echezeaux.style: Complex red wines with aging potential, primarily from Pinot Noir

geo:fr:graves-aoc.name: Graves
geo:fr:graves-aoc.description: Graves is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, known for its gravelly soils that influence the character of the wines produced. The appellation encompasses both red and white wine production, with a focus on blends. It is historically significant as the site of some of the earliest classified growths in Bordeaux, including Château Haut-Brion. The region benefits from a temperate maritime climate, which aids in the ripening of grapes.
geo:fr:graves-aoc.style: Balanced red and white wines with a focus on terroir expression

geo:fr:graves-de-vayres.name: Graves de Vayres
geo:fr:graves-de-vayres.description: Graves de Vayres is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France. It is situated on the right bank of the Dordogne River, characterized by its gravelly soils which are conducive to viticulture. The appellation primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that include Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, as well as white wines made from Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc. The climate is maritime, influenced by the nearby river, which contributes to the ripening of grapes.
geo:fr:graves-de-vayres.style: Red wines with some white blends, showcasing fruit and structure

geo:fr:graves-superieures.name: Graves Superieures
geo:fr:graves-superieures.description: Graves Supérieures is an appellation d'origine contrôlée (AOC) located within the Graves region of Bordeaux, France. It is specifically designated for sweet white wines produced primarily from Sémillon, Sauvignon Blanc, and Muscadelle grapes. The appellation benefits from the region's gravelly soils and a climate conducive to the development of noble rot, which enhances the sweetness and complexity of the wines.
geo:fr:graves-superieures.style: Sweet white wines with complexity and richness

geo:au:great-southern-gi.name: Great Southern
geo:au:great-southern-gi.description: Great Southern is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Western Australia, known for its diverse climate and varied terroir. It encompasses several subregions, each contributing unique characteristics to the wines produced. The region is recognized for its high-quality Riesling, Shiraz, and Cabernet Sauvignon, benefiting from a cooler climate that allows for extended ripening periods. Great Southern's wines are often noted for their balance and expression of terroir.
geo:au:great-southern-gi.style: Diverse wines with a focus on Riesling and Shiraz

geo:it:greco-di-tufo.name: Greco di Tufo
geo:it:greco-di-tufo.description: Greco di Tufo is a white wine appellation located in the Campania region of Italy, classified as DOCG. It is primarily produced from the Greco grape variety, which is known for its aromatic qualities and ability to express the local terroir. The appellation is situated in the province of Avellino, characterized by hilly terrain and volcanic soils, which contribute to the wine's distinctive profile.
geo:it:greco-di-tufo.style: Aromatic white wines with mineral notes and good acidity

geo:us:green-valley-of-russian-river-val.name: Green Valley of Russian River Valley
geo:us:green-valley-of-russian-river-val.description: The Green Valley of Russian River Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 1983, it is characterized by its cool climate, influenced by coastal fog and proximity to the Pacific Ocean. The region is primarily known for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, benefiting from the unique terroir that includes well-drained soils and a diverse microclimate. The AVA is recognized for its focus on producing elegant and balanced wines that reflect the distinct characteristics of the area.
geo:us:green-valley-of-russian-river-val.style: Produces elegant Pinot Noir and Chardonnay

geo:fr:grignan-les-adhemar.name: Grignan-les-Adhemar
geo:fr:grignan-les-adhemar.description: Grignan-les-Adhemar is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate with warm summers and mild winters, which is conducive to grape growing. The appellation is situated near the town of Grignan and is known for its diverse terroir, including clay-limestone soils. The primary focus is on red wines, which are typically blends dominated by Grenache and Syrah. The wines often exhibit fruit-forward characteristics and moderate tannins.
geo:fr:grignan-les-adhemar.style: Red wines with fruit-forward characteristics and moderate tannins

geo:it:grignolino-dasti.name: Grignolino d'Asti
geo:it:grignolino-dasti.description: Grignolino d'Asti is an Italian DOCG appellation located in the Asti province of Piedmont. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Grignolino grape, which is characterized by its light color and high acidity. The appellation has specific regulations regarding vineyard practices and winemaking techniques to ensure quality and authenticity.
geo:it:grignolino-dasti.style: Light-bodied red wines with high acidity and subtle tannins

geo:it:grignolino-del-monferrato-casales.name: Grignolino del Monferrato Casalese
geo:it:grignolino-del-monferrato-casales.description: Grignolino del Monferrato Casalese is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Monferrato region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Grignolino grape variety. The geographical boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas within the Monferrato region, characterized by hilly terrain and a climate conducive to viticulture. The wines typically exhibit a light body and moderate tannins, reflecting the unique terroir of the region.
geo:it:grignolino-del-monferrato-casales.style: Light-bodied red wines with moderate tannins

geo:fr:griotte-chambertin.name: Griotte-Chambertin
geo:fr:griotte-chambertin.description: Griotte-Chambertin is an AOC located within the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is known for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation encompasses a small area, characterized by its unique terroir, which includes limestone soils and a favorable microclimate that contribute to the quality of the wines. Griotte-Chambertin is adjacent to the more prominent Chambertin and is recognized for its elegant and refined style.
geo:fr:griotte-chambertin.style: Elegant red wines with finesse and complexity

geo:fr:gros-plant-du-pays-nantais.name: Gros Plant du Pays Nantais
geo:fr:gros-plant-du-pays-nantais.description: Gros Plant du Pays Nantais is an AOC located in the Pays Nantais region of France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Folle Blanche grape variety. The appellation is characterized by its proximity to the Loire River, which influences the local climate and soil composition, contributing to the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:gros-plant-du-pays-nantais.style: Dry white wines with high acidity and mineral notes

geo:ar:gualtallary.name: Gualtallary
geo:ar:gualtallary.description: Gualtallary is a geographical indication (GI) located within the Uco Valley region of Argentina. It is characterized by its high altitude, which contributes to a unique microclimate suitable for viticulture. The region is known for producing red wines, particularly from Malbec and Cabernet Franc, which thrive in the area's well-drained soils and significant temperature variations between day and night.
geo:ar:gualtallary.style: Red wines with depth and complexity, often showcasing fruit and floral notes.

geo:fr:haut-medoc-aoc.name: Haut-Médoc
geo:fr:haut-medoc-aoc.description: Haut-Médoc is an AOC located in the Médoc region of Bordeaux, France. It encompasses a variety of communes and is known for producing red wines primarily from Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The appellation is situated on the left bank of the Gironde estuary, benefiting from a maritime climate that influences its viticulture. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and tannin, with aging potential due to the presence of gravelly soils that enhance drainage.
geo:fr:haut-medoc-aoc.style: Red wines with structure and aging potential

geo:fr:haut-poitou.name: Haut-Poitou
geo:fr:haut-poitou.description: Haut-Poitou is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, characterized by its production of primarily white wines. The appellation is situated to the east of the city of Poitiers and encompasses a variety of terroirs that contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The climate is influenced by both the Atlantic Ocean and the continental climate, leading to a diversity of styles within the region. The AOC was established in 2011, reflecting a growing recognition of the area's viticultural potential.
geo:fr:haut-poitou.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and acidity

geo:au:heathcote-gi.name: Heathcote
geo:au:heathcote-gi.description: Heathcote is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia, known primarily for its red wines, particularly Shiraz. The region features a diverse range of soils, including ancient volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique character of its wines. The climate is characterized by warm days and cool nights, allowing for extended ripening periods and the development of complex flavors in the grapes. Heathcote has gained recognition for producing high-quality Shiraz that reflects the region's terroir.

geo:za:hemel-en-aarde-wo.name: Hemel-en-Aarde
geo:za:hemel-en-aarde-wo.description: Hemel-en-Aarde is an appellation located within the Walker Bay region of South Africa, recognized for its cool climate and proximity to the Atlantic Ocean. The area is characterized by rolling hills and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. Hemel-en-Aarde is particularly noted for its focus on varietals such as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, which thrive in the region's conditions. The appellation is classified as a Wine of Origin (WO) area, indicating its commitment to quality and authenticity.
geo:za:hemel-en-aarde-wo.style: Red wines with a focus on elegance and balance

geo:fr:hermitage.name: Hermitage
geo:fr:hermitage.description: A single hill overlooking the Rhône, producing wines of legendary power and longevity. Hermitage Syrah is among the world's greatest red wines — dense, concentrated, immortal. The whites, from Marsanne and Roussanne, age magnificently as well. This is Syrah at its most monumental.

geo:fr:hermitage-blanc.name: Hermitage Blanc
geo:fr:hermitage-blanc.description: Hermitage Blanc is an AOC located in the Northern Rhône region of France, known for its white wines primarily made from the Marsanne and Roussanne grape varieties. The appellation encompasses the Hermitage hill, which benefits from a diverse microclimate and varied soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced. The wines are characterized by their richness, depth, and potential for aging, often displaying flavors of stone fruits, nuts, and floral notes.
geo:fr:hermitage-blanc.style: Rich, full-bodied white wines with aging potential

geo:us:howell-mountain-ava.name: Howell Mountain
geo:us:howell-mountain-ava.description: Howell Mountain is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain, with elevations ranging from 1,400 to 2,200 feet. The region benefits from a unique microclimate that contributes to the production of full-bodied red wines, primarily from Bordeaux varietals. Howell Mountain is known for its volcanic soil, which enhances the complexity and structure of the wines produced here.
geo:us:howell-mountain-ava.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich flavors and strong tannins

geo:au:hunter-valley-gi.name: Hunter Valley
geo:au:hunter-valley-gi.description: Hunter Valley is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in New South Wales, Australia. It is one of the country's oldest wine regions, established in the early 19th century. The region is characterized by a warm climate and fertile soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines, particularly white varieties. The Hunter Valley is for its Semillon, which is often produced in both dry and sweet styles.
geo:au:hunter-valley-gi.style: Known for dry and sweet Semillon wines

geo:fr:irancy.name: Irancy
geo:fr:irancy.description: Irancy is an AOC located in the Burgundy region of France, known for its production of red wines primarily from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated near the town of Irancy, approximately 10 kilometers southwest of Auxerre. It is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a continental climate, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The wines of Irancy are often noted for their structure and complexity, reflecting the influence of both the local geology and climate. The AOC was officially recognized in 1999.
geo:fr:irancy.style: Structured red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:irouleguy.name: Irouléguy
geo:fr:irouleguy.description: Irouléguy is an AOC located in the South West France region, specifically in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department. The appellation is characterized by its mountainous terrain and Atlantic climate, which contribute to the unique terroir suitable for viticulture. Irouléguy primarily produces red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, and the wines are known for their structure and aging potential.
geo:fr:irouleguy.style: Structured red wines with aging potential

geo:it:irpinia-doc.name: Irpinia
geo:it:irpinia-doc.description: Irpinia is an Italian appellation located in the Campania region, known for its hilly terrain and volcanic soils. It is recognized for the production of both red and white wines, primarily from indigenous grape varieties. The appellation encompasses several municipalities and is characterized by a continental climate, which contributes to the complexity of its wines. Irpinia is particularly noted for its Aglianico and Fiano di Avellino, showcasing the region's unique terroir.
geo:it:irpinia-doc.style: Diverse wines with a focus on indigenous varieties and terroir expression

geo:it:ischia.name: Ischia
geo:it:ischia.description: Ischia is a designated DOC appellation located on the volcanic island of Ischia in the Campania region of Italy. The appellation is known for its white wines, primarily produced from indigenous grape varieties. The volcanic soil and Mediterranean climate contribute to the unique characteristics of the wines produced in this region.
geo:it:ischia.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and minerality

geo:it:isonzo.name: Isonzo
geo:it:isonzo.description: Isonzo is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its proximity to the Isonzo River, which influences the local climate and soil composition. The region primarily produces white wines, with a focus on varietals that thrive in its unique terroir. The wines from Isonzo are known for their freshness and aromatic complexity, reflecting the diverse microclimates present within the area.
geo:it:isonzo.style: Fresh and aromatic white wines with good acidity

geo:fr:jasnieres.name: Jasnières
geo:fr:jasnieres.description: Jasnières is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is situated near the village of the same name, within the larger Touraine region. The vineyards benefit from a continental climate, characterized by warm summers and cold winters, which influences the grape growing conditions. Jasnières is recognized for its unique terroir, which consists of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the distinct character of its wines.
geo:fr:jasnieres.style: Dry to off-dry white wines with high acidity

geo:es:jerez.name: Jerez
geo:es:jerez.description: Jerez is a fortified wine appellation located in the province of Cádiz, Andalusia, Spain. It is known for its unique production methods, which involve the use of a solera system for aging. The region's climate is characterized by hot summers and mild winters, with a significant influence from the Atlantic Ocean, contributing to the distinctive characteristics of its wines.
geo:es:jerez.style: Fortified wines, primarily Sherry, with diverse styles from dry to sweet

geo:es:jerez-xeres-sherry.name: Jerez-Xérès-Sherry
geo:es:jerez-xeres-sherry.description: Jerez-Xérès-Sherry is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the province of Cádiz, Andalusia, Spain. It is for its production of fortified wines, primarily made from the Palomino, Pedro Ximénez, and Muscat grape varieties. The region's unique climate, characterized by hot summers and mild winters, along with its chalky soil, contributes to the distinctive characteristics of its wines. The appellation encompasses several towns, including Jerez de la Frontera, Sanlúcar de Barrameda, and El Puerto de Santa María, each contributing to the diversity of styles within the category.
geo:es:jerez-xeres-sherry.style: Fortified wines with varying sweetness and aging profiles

geo:de:johannisberg.name: Johannisberg
geo:de:johannisberg.description: Johannisberg is an appellation located in the Rheingau region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is known primarily for its production of white wines, particularly those made from the Riesling grape. The area has a long history of viticulture, with records of wine production dating back to the 8th century. Johannisberg's vineyards benefit from a favorable microclimate and diverse soil types, contributing to the distinctive character of its wines.
geo:de:johannisberg.style: White wines, with a focus on Riesling

geo:fr:julienas.name: Juliénas
geo:fr:julienas.description: Juliénas is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known primarily for its production of red wines made from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated in the northern part of Beaujolais, characterized by its rolling hills and granite soils. Juliénas is recognized for its distinctive terroir, which contributes to the complexity and aging potential of its wines. The wines often exhibit a combination of fruitiness and floral notes, reflecting the unique microclimate of the area.
geo:fr:julienas.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and floral characteristics

geo:es:jumilla-do.name: Jumilla
geo:es:jumilla-do.description: Jumilla is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Murcia region of Spain, recognized for its production of primarily red wines. The appellation is characterized by its arid climate and diverse soil types, which contribute to the unique expression of its wines. The legal boundaries of Jumilla encompass several municipalities, with a significant focus on the Monastrell grape variety, which thrives in the region's conditions. Jumilla has a historical significance in Spanish winemaking, with roots tracing back to Roman times.
geo:es:jumilla-do.style: Predominantly red wines, often full-bodied and robust

geo:fr:jurancon.name: Jurançon
geo:fr:jurancon.description: Jurançon is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its white wines produced primarily from the Petit Manseng and Gros Manseng grape varieties. The appellation is situated on the southern slopes of the Pyrenees, benefiting from a unique microclimate that allows for the production of both dry and sweet styles of wine. The legal boundaries of Jurançon encompass several communes, with a focus on the town of Jurançon itself. The wines are characterized by their aromatic complexity and balance of sweetness and acidity.
geo:fr:jurancon.style: Aromatic white wines, including both dry and sweet styles

geo:de:kaiserstuhl.name: Kaiserstuhl
geo:de:kaiserstuhl.description: Kaiserstuhl is an appellation located in the Baden region of Germany, characterized by its volcanic soil and warm climate. The area is known for its diverse microclimates, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. It is particularly recognized for its white wines, although red varieties are also cultivated. The appellation is part of the larger Bereich classification within Baden.
geo:de:kaiserstuhl.style: White wines with notable acidity and fruit character

geo:at:kamptal-dac.name: Kamptal
geo:at:kamptal-dac.description: Named for the Kamp River, this DAC region in Lower Austria produces some of the country's finest Grüner Veltliner and Riesling. The Heiligenstein vineyard, with its ancient desert sandstone soils, rivals any Grand Cru. Bründlmayer and Schloss Gobelsburg set the standard.

geo:us:knights-valley-ava.name: Knights Valley
geo:us:knights-valley-ava.description: Knights Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 2013, it is characterized by its diverse microclimates and volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique terroir. The AVA is situated to the northeast of the city of Santa Rosa and is bordered by the Mayacamas Mountains. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, as well as some white varietals.
geo:us:knights-valley-ava.style: Primarily red wines with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon.

geo:at:kremstal-dac.name: Kremstal
geo:at:kremstal-dac.description: Centered on the medieval town of Krems, this DAC region bridges the styles of neighboring Wachau and Kamptal. Loess and primary rock soils produce both powerful and elegant Grüner Veltliner. The historic Stift Göttweig monastery overlooks vineyards planted for centuries.

geo:de:kreuznach.name: Kreuznach
geo:de:kreuznach.description: Kreuznach is an appellation within the Nahe wine region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by a diverse range of soils and microclimates, contributing to the complexity of its wines. The area is known for its production of high-quality white wines, particularly from the Riesling grape, which thrives in the region's varied terroir.
geo:de:kreuznach.style: White wines with a focus on Riesling

geo:nz:kumeu.name: Kumeu
geo:nz:kumeu.description: Auckland's wine country, where Croatian immigrants established New Zealand's first commercial vineyards. Kumeu River's Burgundian-style Chardonnay proved the region's potential. Humid and challenging, but the best sites produce wines of real distinction.

geo:fr:letoile.name: L'Étoile
geo:fr:letoile.description: L'Étoile is an appellation located in the Jura region of France, known for its distinctive white wines. The appellation is characterized by its unique limestone and marl soils, which contribute to the mineral qualities of the wines. L'Étoile is situated near the village of the same name and is recognized for its traditional vinification methods, including the use of oxidative aging. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass a small area, emphasizing the importance of terroir in the production of its wines.
geo:fr:letoile.style: White wines with mineral and oxidative characteristics

geo:fr:la-clape-aoc.name: La Clape
geo:fr:la-clape-aoc.description: La Clape is an AOC located within the Languedoc region of France, established in 2013. The appellation is situated on a coastal plateau near the Mediterranean Sea, characterized by its limestone and clay soils. It benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The region primarily produces red wines, often blending Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre, reflecting the local terroir.
geo:fr:la-clape-aoc.style: Red wines with rich fruit and herbal notes

geo:es:la-mancha.name: La Mancha
geo:es:la-mancha.description: La Mancha is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is characterized by a vast, flat landscape with a continental climate, which influences the viticulture of the area. The DO encompasses a large area, making it one of the most extensive wine-producing regions in Spain. La Mancha is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on indigenous and international grape varieties.
geo:es:la-mancha.style: Red wines, often fruit-forward with moderate tannins

geo:fr:la-romanee.name: La Romanee
geo:fr:la-romanee.description: La Romanee is a AOC located within the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is known for its small size and exceptional quality, producing exclusively red wines from Pinot Noir grapes. The vineyard is characterized by its unique terroir, which includes a combination of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines. La Romanee is surrounded by other vineyards, enhancing its reputation within the region.
geo:fr:la-romanee.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and depth

geo:fr:la-tache.name: La Tache
geo:fr:la-tache.description: La Tâche is an AOC located within the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing red wines exclusively from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses a single vineyard, which is classified as a Grand Cru, and is characterized by its unique terroir, including clay-limestone soils and a favorable microclimate. The wines of La Tâche are known for their depth, complexity, and aging potential, reflecting the distinct characteristics of the vineyard.
geo:fr:la-tache.style: Red wines with depth, complexity, and aging potential

geo:it:lacrima-di-morro-dalba.name: Lacrima di Morro d'Alba
geo:it:lacrima-di-morro-dalba.description: Lacrima di Morro d'Alba is a DOC appellation located in the Marche region of Italy. It is defined by its legal boundaries around the commune of Morro d'Alba, where the unique climate and soil contribute to the distinct characteristics of the wines. The primary grape variety permitted is Lacrima, which is known for its aromatic profile and is often produced as a red wine. The appellation emphasizes the expression of this indigenous variety, showcasing its floral and fruity notes.
geo:it:lacrima-di-morro-dalba.style: Aromatic red wines with floral and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:ladoix.name: Ladoix
geo:fr:ladoix.description: Ladoix is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune subregion of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines from Pinot Noir, with some white wines made from Chardonnay. The appellation is situated just north of the more famous Aloxe-Corton and is characterized by its clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the wines' structure and complexity. Ladoix wines often exhibit a balance of fruit and earthiness, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:ladoix.style: Red wines with structure and complexity, often earthy and fruity

geo:pt:lafoes.name: Lafoes
geo:pt:lafoes.description: Lafoes is a designated Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located within the Dão region of Portugal. The appellation is characterized by its mountainous terrain and a climate that benefits from both Atlantic influences and continental conditions. The legal boundaries of Lafoes encompass specific areas known for their unique terroir, which contributes to the distinctiveness of the wines produced here.
geo:pt:lafoes.style: Produces white wines with a focus on freshness and acidity

geo:pt:lagoa.name: Lagoa
geo:pt:lagoa.description: Lagoa is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located in the Algarve region of Portugal. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate, which influences the viticulture and grape varieties cultivated in the area. The appellation primarily focuses on white wines, utilizing indigenous grape varieties that thrive in the local terroir.
geo:pt:lagoa.style: White wines with a focus on local grape varieties

geo:pt:lagos.name: Lagos
geo:pt:lagos.description: Lagos is a designated Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located in the Algarve region of Portugal. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which influences the viticulture and grape ripening. It is primarily known for producing red wines, with a focus on local grape varieties. The region's winemaking history is intertwined with both indigenous practices and modern techniques.
geo:pt:lagos.style: Red wines with regional varietal characteristics

geo:fr:lalande-de-pomerol.name: Lalande-de-Pomerol
geo:fr:lalande-de-pomerol.description: Lalande-de-Pomerol is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, specifically adjacent to the more Pomerol appellation. The area is characterized by a mix of clay and gravel soils, which contribute to the cultivation of high-quality red wines. The appellation covers a relatively small area and is known for producing wines that are often softer and more approachable than those from Pomerol, yet still exhibit complexity and depth. The legal boundaries of Lalande-de-Pomerol encompass several communes, including Lalande-de-Pomerol and Neac.
geo:fr:lalande-de-pomerol.style: Red wines, typically Merlot-dominant, with fruit-forward profiles

geo:it:lambrusco.name: Lambrusco
geo:it:lambrusco.description: Lambrusco is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) appellation located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing sparkling red wines made from the Lambrusco grape variety. The appellation encompasses several municipalities, with a focus on the provinces of Modena and Reggio Emilia. The wines are characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and varying levels of sweetness, often enjoyed as a refreshing accompaniment to local cuisine.
geo:it:lambrusco.style: Sparkling red wines, typically fruity and varying in sweetness

geo:it:lambrusco-di-sorbara.name: Lambrusco di Sorbara
geo:it:lambrusco-di-sorbara.description: Lambrusco di Sorbara is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass specific areas known for producing high-quality Lambrusco wines. The primary grape variety permitted in this appellation is Lambrusco di Sorbara, which is characterized by its sparkling style and often produced using the Charmat method. The terroir is marked by clay and sandy soils, contributing to the wine's distinctive freshness and aromatic profile.
geo:it:lambrusco-di-sorbara.style: Sparkling red wine with fresh acidity and floral notes.

geo:it:lambrusco-grasparossa-di-castelve.name: Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro
geo:it:lambrusco-grasparossa-di-castelve.description: Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries around the municipality of Castelvetro di Modena and surrounding areas. It is primarily known for producing sparkling wines made from the Lambrusco Grasparossa grape variety, characterized by their vibrant acidity and fruit-forward profiles. The region's clay and limestone soils, along with a temperate climate, contribute to the unique characteristics of the wines produced here.
geo:it:lambrusco-grasparossa-di-castelve.style: Sparkling red wines with fruity and floral notes.

geo:it:langhe.name: Langhe
geo:it:langhe.description: Langhe is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It encompasses a variety of vineyards situated in the hilly terrain of the Langhe area, known for its unique microclimates and diverse soils. The appellation is recognized for producing high-quality red wines, particularly those made from Nebbiolo, Barbera, and Dolcetto. The wines often reflect the characteristics of the specific communes within the Langhe, contributing to a range of styles and expressions.
geo:it:langhe.style: Red wines with a focus on Nebbiolo and Barbera

geo:it:langhe-doc.name: Langhe
geo:it:langhe-doc.description: Langhe is an Italian wine appellation located in the Piedmont region, recognized for its diverse range of wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, characterized by rolling hills and a continental climate. Langhe is particularly noted for its production of Barolo and Barbaresco, which are made from the Nebbiolo grape. The legal boundaries of the appellation include several municipalities, contributing to a rich tapestry of wine styles and expressions.
geo:it:langhe-doc.style: Diverse wines including reds, whites, and sparkling varieties

geo:au:langhorne-creek-gi.name: Langhorne Creek
geo:au:langhorne-creek-gi.description: Langhorne Creek is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in South Australia, known for its warm climate and fertile soils. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Bremer River, which influences viticulture through irrigation. Langhorne Creek is primarily recognized for its production of red wines, particularly from Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz varieties. The appellation has a history of winemaking that dates back to the mid-19th century, contributing to its established reputation in the Australian wine industry.
geo:au:langhorne-creek-gi.style: Predominantly red wines with rich fruit and soft tannins

geo:fr:latricieres-chambertin.name: Latricieres-Chambertin
geo:fr:latricieres-chambertin.description: Latricières-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is classified as a Grand Cru vineyard and is known exclusively for producing red wines made from Pinot Noir. The appellation is situated between the more famous Chambertin and Clos de Bèze vineyards, benefiting from similar geological and climatic conditions that contribute to its wine quality.
geo:fr:latricieres-chambertin.style: Red wines characterized by elegance and complexity

geo:at:leithaberg.name: Leithaberg
geo:at:leithaberg.description: Leithaberg is a designated Austrian wine appellation (DAC) located within the Burgenland region. It is characterized by its unique geological formations and microclimates, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The appellation primarily focuses on red wine production, with specific regulations governing permitted grape varieties and winemaking practices.
geo:at:leithaberg.style: Predominantly red wines with a focus on Blaufränkisch

geo:it:lessona.name: Lessona
geo:it:lessona.description: Lessona is a red wine appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC). The appellation is situated in the northern part of Piedmont, characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by the nearby Alps. Lessona primarily produces wines from the Nebbiolo grape, often blended with other local varieties, and is noted for its elegant and complex flavor profile.
geo:it:lessona.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:limoux.name: Limoux
geo:fr:limoux.description: Limoux is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, known for its production of white wines. The appellation is situated in the foothills of the Pyrenees, benefiting from a diverse climate that includes both Mediterranean and Atlantic influences. Limoux is historically significant as it is believed to be one of the first regions to produce sparkling wine, with a tradition dating back to the 16th century. The area is characterized by its limestone soils and varied altitudes, which contribute to the complexity of its wines.
geo:fr:limoux.style: White wines, often with a fresh and vibrant character

geo:fr:lirac.name: Lirac
geo:fr:lirac.description: Lirac is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, established in 1947. It is situated on the right bank of the Rhône River, opposite Châteauneuf-du-Pape. The appellation is known for its diverse terroir, which includes clay, limestone, and sandy soils, contributing to the complexity of its wines. Lirac primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters.
geo:fr:lirac.style: Red blends, with fruit-forward and spicy characteristics

geo:pt:lisboa-doc.name: Lisboa
geo:pt:lisboa-doc.description: Lisboa is an appellation located in the Lisbon District of Portugal, encompassing a diverse range of wine styles. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Atlantic Ocean, which influences its climate and viticulture. The legal boundaries of Lisboa include several subregions, each with distinct terroirs and grape varieties. The appellation allows for a variety of permitted grape types, both indigenous and international, contributing to a wide array of wine styles.
geo:pt:lisboa-doc.style: Diverse wines including reds, whites, and rosés

geo:fr:listrac-medoc.name: Listrac-Médoc
geo:fr:listrac-medoc.description: Listrac-Médoc is an AOC located in the Médoc region of France, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is situated on the left bank of the Gironde estuary and is characterized by its gravelly soils, which are conducive to viticulture. Listrac-Médoc produces wines that typically exhibit a balance of fruit and tannin, with aging potential. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, and it is recognized for its distinct terroir that contributes to the complexity of its wines.
geo:fr:listrac-medoc.style: Structured red wines with aging potential and balanced profiles

geo:us:livermore-valley.name: Livermore Valley
geo:us:livermore-valley.description: Livermore Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California's Central Coast region. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate with warm days and cool nights, which supports the ripening of various grape varieties. The valley is known for its diverse topography, including rolling hills and valley floors, which contribute to the distinct terroir. Livermore Valley has a historical significance in California wine production, being one of the state's oldest wine regions, with commercial viticulture dating back to the 1800s.
geo:us:livermore-valley.style: Red wines with notable Cabernet Sauvignon and Petite Sirah

geo:it:loazzolo.name: Loazzolo
geo:it:loazzolo.description: Loazzolo is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is primarily known for producing dessert wines made from the Moscato Bianco grape. The appellation benefits from a unique microclimate that allows for the development of late-harvest grapes, contributing to the sweetness and aromatic complexity of the wines.
geo:it:loazzolo.style: Sweet dessert wines with aromatic and fruity characteristics

geo:us:lodi-ava.name: Lodi
geo:us:lodi-ava.description: Lodi is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California's Central Valley. It is defined by its flat topography and diverse soil types, which contribute to a wide range of wine styles. The region is known for its warm climate, which allows for the cultivation of both traditional and non-traditional grape varieties. Lodi has a rich viticultural history, with significant plantings dating back to the late 19th century.
geo:us:lodi-ava.style: Fruity, approachable wines with varied styles from red to white.

geo:fr:loupiac.name: Loupiac
geo:fr:loupiac.description: Loupiac is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, known for its sweet dessert wines produced primarily from Sémillon, Sauvignon Blanc, and Muscadelle grapes. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Garonne River, adjacent to the more famous Sauternes region. The climate is characterized by a combination of warm, sunny days and cool nights, which aids in the development of noble rot (Botrytis cinerea) essential for the production of its sweet wines. The vineyards benefit from the river's influence, creating ideal conditions for the late harvest necessary for these wines.
geo:fr:loupiac.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, honeyed flavors

geo:fr:luberon-aoc.name: Luberon
geo:fr:luberon-aoc.description: Luberon is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, established in 1988. The appellation is situated in the foothills of the Luberon mountains, characterized by a Mediterranean climate with diverse soils. It primarily produces red wines, often blends, that reflect the region's varied terroir and climatic influences.
geo:fr:luberon-aoc.style: Red wines, often blends, with fruit-forward characteristics

geo:it:lugana.name: Lugana
geo:it:lugana.description: Lugana is a designated wine appellation located in the Lombardy region of Italy, classified as a DOC. It is situated near the southern shores of Lake Garda, straddling the provinces of Brescia and Verona. The appellation is primarily known for its white wines, which are predominantly made from the Turbiana grape variety. The climate is characterized by mild temperatures and a significant influence from the lake, contributing to the unique terroir of the region.
geo:it:lugana.style: Crisp and aromatic white wines with mineral notes

geo:ar:lujan-de-cuyo.name: Luján de Cuyo
geo:ar:lujan-de-cuyo.description: Luján de Cuyo is a designated appellation of origin (DOC) located within the Mendoza region of Argentina. It is recognized for its high-altitude vineyards and diverse microclimates, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. The area is particularly noted for its Malbec, which has become emblematic of Argentine wine. The soils are primarily alluvial, providing good drainage and mineral content, which supports the growth of premium grape varieties.
geo:ar:lujan-de-cuyo.style: Red wines, known for depth and fruit concentration

geo:fr:lussac-saint-emilion.name: Lussac-Saint-Émilion
geo:fr:lussac-saint-emilion.description: Lussac-Saint-Émilion is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, specifically within the Right Bank's Saint-Émilion area. The appellation is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a temperate maritime climate, which contribute to the cultivation of high-quality red wines. The legal boundaries of Lussac-Saint-Émilion encompass approximately 1,200 hectares of vineyards, and the wines produced here are typically blends dominated by Merlot and Cabernet Franc. The appellation is known for producing wines that exhibit ripe fruit flavors and a smooth texture, often with aging potential.
geo:fr:lussac-saint-emilion.style: Red wines with ripe fruit flavors and smooth textures

geo:fr:macvin-du-jura.name: Macvin du Jura
geo:fr:macvin-du-jura.description: Macvin du Jura is a fortified wine produced in the Jura region of France, classified as an AOC since 1991. It is made by blending grape must with marc (distilled grape pomace) and then aging the mixture in oak barrels. The wine is typically sweet and has a unique character derived from the local grape varieties and the specific production method.
geo:fr:macvin-du-jura.style: Fortified wine with a sweet profile and complex flavors

geo:pt:madeira.name: Madeira
geo:pt:madeira.description: The immortal wine, from a volcanic Atlantic island. Intentional oxidation and heat create wines that can live for centuries. Sercial is dry and bracing; Malmsey is lusciously sweet. A glass of vintage Madeira is a sip of history — bottles from the 1800s still drink beautifully.

geo:pt:madeira-doc.name: Madeira
geo:pt:madeira-doc.description: Madeira is a fortified wine appellation located on the Madeira archipelago, which is part of Portugal. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass the entire island, with specific regulations governing production methods and aging requirements. The unique climate, characterized by volcanic soils and a subtropical climate, contributes to the distinctive character of the wines. Madeira wines are known for their longevity and complexity, often aged for extended periods. The appellation is recognized for its historical significance, having been a key trade item since the 15th century.
geo:pt:madeira-doc.style: Fortified wines with varied sweetness and complex flavors

geo:fr:madiran.name: Madiran
geo:fr:madiran.description: Madiran is an AOC located in the South West France region, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Atlantic Ocean and the Pyrenees mountains. The dominant grape variety is Tannat, which is often blended with other local varieties. The wines typically exhibit robust tannins and a deep color, reflecting the region's unique terroir.
geo:fr:madiran.style: Full-bodied red wines with high tannins and dark fruit flavors

geo:de:maindreieck.name: Maindreieck
geo:de:maindreieck.description: Maindreieck is an appellation located within the Franken wine region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by its unique geographical position along the Main River, which influences the local climate and viticulture. The area is known for its production of white wines, particularly from the Silvaner, Müller-Thurgau, and Bacchus grape varieties. The appellation's vineyards benefit from the region's limestone soils and moderate climate, contributing to the distinctive profiles of the wines produced here.
geo:de:maindreieck.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and fruitiness

geo:cl:maipo-valley-do.name: Maipo Valley
geo:cl:maipo-valley-do.description: Chile's most prestigious red wine address. Cabernet Sauvignon from the Alto Maipo — at the foot of the Andes — rivals Napa and Bordeaux. Concha y Toro's Don Melchor and Almaviva set the standard. Warm days, cool Andean nights, and ancient alluvial soils.

geo:ar:maipu.name: Maipú
geo:ar:maipu.description: Maipú is an Indicative Geographic (IG) appellation located within the Mendoza region of Argentina. It is characterized by a diverse range of soils and a semi-arid climate, which contribute to the cultivation of various grape varieties. The area has historical significance in Argentine viticulture, being one of the first regions to establish vineyards in the country. The appellation is known for producing high-quality red wines, particularly from Malbec and Bonarda.
geo:ar:maipu.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:malepere.name: Malepere
geo:fr:malepere.description: Malepère is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 2007. It is situated near the city of Carcassonne and benefits from a diverse terroir influenced by both Mediterranean and Atlantic climates. The appellation is characterized by its rolling hills and varied soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. Malepère primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that highlight the region's unique grape varieties.
geo:fr:malepere.style: Red wines with a blend of local and international grape varieties

geo:it:malvasia-delle-lipari.name: Malvasia delle Lipari
geo:it:malvasia-delle-lipari.description: Malvasia delle Lipari is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Aeolian Islands of Sicily, Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its production of sweet dessert wines made from the Malvasia grape, often blended with other local varieties. The region's volcanic soils and Mediterranean climate contribute to the unique characteristics of the wines.
geo:it:malvasia-delle-lipari.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, aromatic profiles

geo:it:mamertino-di-milazzo.name: Mamertino di Milazzo
geo:it:mamertino-di-milazzo.description: Mamertino di Milazzo is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Sicily, Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries surrounding the town of Milazzo and includes specific regulations regarding grape cultivation and wine production. The wines produced here are primarily red, characterized by their use of local grape varieties. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which influences the ripening of the grapes and the overall style of the wine.
geo:it:mamertino-di-milazzo.style: Red wines with a focus on local grape varieties

geo:es:manchuela.name: Manchuela
geo:es:manchuela.description: Manchuela is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is characterized by a diverse landscape of rolling hills and valleys, with a continental climate that influences the viticulture. The DO is known for its red wines, primarily produced from the Bobal and Tempranillo grape varieties, among others. The region has a historical significance in Spanish winemaking, with evidence of viticulture dating back to ancient times.
geo:es:manchuela.style: Predominantly red wines with fruit-forward profiles

geo:es:manzanilla.name: Manzanilla
geo:es:manzanilla.description: Manzanilla is a fortified wine produced in the coastal town of Sanlúcar de Barrameda, within the DO (Denominación de Origen) of Andalusia, Spain. It is made primarily from the Palomino grape and is characterized by its unique aging process under a layer of flor yeast, which imparts distinctive flavors and aromas. The appellation benefits from a maritime climate, which influences the wine's profile and contributes to its crisp and dry character.
geo:es:manzanilla.style: Dry, fortified wine with a distinctive saline quality

geo:fr:maranges.name: Maranges
geo:fr:maranges.description: Maranges is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It encompasses three villages: Cheilly-lès-Maranges, Dezize-lès-Maranges, and Maranges itself. The appellation is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a continental climate, which contribute to the production of red wines primarily made from Pinot Noir. Maranges is known for its more rustic and robust style compared to other Côte de Beaune wines, often displaying earthy and fruity characteristics.
geo:fr:maranges.style: Red wines with earthy and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:marcillac.name: Marcillac
geo:fr:marcillac.description: Marcillac is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of red wines primarily from the Fer Servadou grape. The appellation is situated in the Aveyron department, characterized by its steep, terraced vineyards along the banks of the Marcillac Valley. The climate is continental, with significant temperature variations between seasons, contributing to the unique terroir. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and acidity, reflecting the region's distinctive geology and microclimate.
geo:fr:marcillac.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and balanced acidity

geo:it:maremma.name: Maremma
geo:it:maremma.description: Maremma is an Italian wine appellation located in the southern part of Tuscany. It encompasses a diverse range of terroirs, influenced by both coastal and inland climates. The region is known for its production of red wines, often utilizing international grape varieties alongside traditional ones. Maremma has gained recognition for its innovative winemaking practices and the establishment of several high-quality wineries.
geo:it:maremma.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit-forward profiles

geo:it:maremma-toscana.name: Maremma Toscana
geo:it:maremma-toscana.description: Maremma Toscana is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Maremma region of Tuscany, Italy. Established in 2011, it encompasses a variety of wine styles primarily produced from Sangiovese and international grape varieties. The appellation benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes and the development of diverse wine profiles.
geo:it:maremma-toscana.style: Red wines with a focus on Sangiovese and blends

geo:au:margaret-river-gi.name: Margaret River
geo:au:margaret-river-gi.description: Western Australia's premium region, where maritime influences create a Bordeaux-like climate. Cabernet-Merlot blends and Chardonnay set the standard, though Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon rivals the best of Graves. Isolation has bred excellence and independence.

geo:fr:margaux.name: Margaux
geo:fr:margaux.description: The Médoc's most elegant appellation, home to Château Margaux and a constellation of classified growths. The deep gravel soils here produce wines of unusual finesse — floral, silky, never heavy. Margaux at its best is Bordeaux at its most feminine, though that term undersells its underlying structure.

geo:de:markgraflerland.name: Markgraflerland
geo:de:markgraflerland.description: Markgraflerland is a designated wine appellation within the Baden region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is located in the southern part of the country, characterized by a temperate climate that is conducive to viticulture. The area is known for its diverse soil types, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. Markgraflerland primarily focuses on white wine production, with Gutedel being the most prominent grape variety, alongside Spätburgunder and Riesling.
geo:de:markgraflerland.style: White wines, notable for freshness and fruit character

geo:nz:marlborough-gi.name: Marlborough
geo:nz:marlborough-gi.description: The appellation that changed Sauvignon Blanc forever. Intense sunlight and cool nights create aromatics of unmatched pungency — passionfruit, grapefruit, cut grass. Cloudy Bay started it; dozens of producers now compete. Love it or not, Marlborough is unmistakable.

geo:it:marsala.name: Marsala
geo:it:marsala.description: Marsala is a fortified wine produced in the Marsala region of Sicily, Italy. The appellation is classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) and encompasses a specific geographical area defined by legal boundaries. Marsala wines are typically made using a blend of indigenous grape varieties, and the production process involves fortifying the wine with distilled grape spirits. The region's warm climate and diverse soils contribute to the unique characteristics of the wines.
geo:it:marsala.style: Fortified wine with a range of sweetness levels and aging potential

geo:fr:marsannay.name: Marsannay
geo:fr:marsannay.description: Marsannay is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits region of Burgundy, France. It is known for producing red wines primarily from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses several villages and is situated at the northern end of the Côte d'Or, adjacent to the city of Dijon. Marsannay is recognized for its diverse terroirs, which contribute to a range of wine styles, from lighter, fruit-forward expressions to more structured and complex versions. The AOC was established in 1987, making it one of the newer appellations in the Burgundy hierarchy.
geo:fr:marsannay.style: Red wines, primarily from Pinot Noir, showcasing varied terroirs

geo:nz:martinborough-gi.name: Martinborough
geo:nz:martinborough-gi.description: New Zealand's first serious Pinot Noir region. The Wairarapa's gravelly terraces and dry autumns — rare in New Zealand — produce wines of Burgundian restraint. Small scale and hands-on viticulture are the norm. Ata Rangi and Dry River established the benchmark.

geo:fr:maury.name: Maury
geo:fr:maury.description: Maury is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, primarily known for its fortified wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Maury, characterized by its slate and schist soils, which contribute to the unique terroir. The climate is Mediterranean, with hot summers and mild winters, ideal for the production of rich, sweet wines. Maury wines are predominantly made using the Grenache grape, and the region is recognized for its vin doux naturel, a style of fortified wine that undergoes mutage to preserve natural sugars.
geo:fr:maury.style: Rich, sweet fortified wines with a focus on Grenache

geo:fr:mazis-chambertin.name: Mazis-Chambertin
geo:fr:mazis-chambertin.description: Mazis-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is known for producing red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation is situated adjacent to the famous Chambertin vineyard, benefiting from similar geological and climatic conditions. The wines of Mazis-Chambertin are characterized by their depth and complexity, often reflecting the unique terroir of the area, which includes limestone and clay soils.
geo:fr:mazis-chambertin.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and complexity

geo:fr:mazoyeres-chambertin.name: Mazoyeres-Chambertin
geo:fr:mazoyeres-chambertin.description: Mazoyeres-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is situated adjacent to the more famous Chambertin appellation, sharing similar geological and climatic conditions. The vineyard area is relatively small, contributing to its exclusivity. The wines produced here are primarily red, made from Pinot Noir, and are known for their complexity and aging potential.
geo:fr:mazoyeres-chambertin.style: Complex red wines with aging potential, primarily from Pinot Noir

geo:au:mclaren-vale-gi.name: McLaren Vale
geo:au:mclaren-vale-gi.description: South Australia's Mediterranean heart, where Gulf St Vincent moderates the warmth. Shiraz and Grenache dominate, often from old bush vines. The d'Arenberg winery pioneered the region; a new generation embraces diversity. Wines of generosity without heaviness.

geo:us:mcminnville.name: McMinnville
geo:us:mcminnville.description: The McMinnville AVA is located within the Willamette Valley in Oregon, United States. Established in 2005, it is characterized by its unique geological features, including marine sedimentary soils and volcanic basalt. The climate is influenced by the Pacific Ocean, resulting in cool temperatures that are conducive to growing high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. The AVA is known for its commitment to sustainable viticulture and has a growing reputation for producing elegant and complex wines.
geo:us:mcminnville.style: Red wines with a focus on Pinot Noir

geo:us:mendocino.name: Mendocino
geo:us:mendocino.description: Mendocino is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Northern California, known for its diverse microclimates and varied topography. The region encompasses a range of elevations and soil types, contributing to the production of a wide array of wine styles. Mendocino has a history of sustainable farming practices and is recognized for its commitment to organic viticulture.
geo:us:mendocino.style: Produces red wines, particularly from Pinot Noir and Zinfandel

geo:ar:mendoza.name: Mendoza
geo:ar:mendoza.description: Argentina's wine capital and Malbec's adopted home. High-altitude vineyards — some above 1,500 meters — combine intense sunlight with cool nights. The Uco Valley's sub-regions (Gualtallary, Altamira) are increasingly prized. Malbec dominates, but diversity grows.

geo:ar:mendoza-do.name: Mendoza
geo:ar:mendoza-do.description: Mendoza is a prominent wine appellation located in Argentina, known for its diverse terroirs and high-altitude vineyards. The region is characterized by a semi-arid climate, with significant temperature variations between day and night, which contributes to the development of grape acidity and flavor complexity. Mendoza is the largest wine-producing region in Argentina, encompassing several subregions, including Luján de Cuyo and the Uco Valley. The appellation is particularly recognized for its Malbec, which has become emblematic of Argentine wine.
geo:ar:mendoza-do.style: Red wines, with a focus on Malbec and blends

geo:fr:menetou-salon.name: Menetou-Salon
geo:fr:menetou-salon.description: Menetou-Salon is an AOC located in the Centre-Loire region of France, known primarily for its production of white wines made from Sauvignon Blanc. The appellation is situated to the east of Sancerre and Pouilly-Fumé, benefiting from a similar terroir characterized by limestone and clay soils. The wines of Menetou-Salon are recognized for their crisp acidity and aromatic profile, often displaying citrus and floral notes.
geo:fr:menetou-salon.style: Crisp, aromatic white wines with citrus and floral notes

geo:it:menfi.name: Menfi
geo:it:menfi.description: Menfi is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located in the western part of Sicily, Italy. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The region is known for its diverse soil types, including clay and limestone, which influence the wines produced. Menfi primarily focuses on red wine production, with a growing reputation for both traditional and modern winemaking techniques.
geo:it:menfi.style: Predominantly red wines with a focus on fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:mercurey.name: Mercurey
geo:fr:mercurey.description: Mercurey is an AOC located in the Côte Chalonnaise region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from Pinot Noir, which thrive in the region's clay and limestone soils. The appellation encompasses several villages and has a reputation for producing wines that are characterized by their structure and aging potential. The legal boundaries of Mercurey are defined to include specific vineyard sites that contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines.
geo:fr:mercurey.style: Structured red wines with aging potential, primarily from Pinot Noir

geo:fr:meursault.name: Meursault
geo:fr:meursault.description: White Burgundy at its most generous. The deeper soils here produce Chardonnay of unusual richness — nutty, honeyed, almost unctuous. While no Grand Crus exist on paper, Premier Crus like Perrières and Charmes produce wines that rival Montrachet. Meursault is Burgundy's golden center.

geo:fr:minervois.name: Minervois
geo:fr:minervois.description: Minervois is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, primarily known for its red wines. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Canal du Midi and is characterized by a diverse landscape of hills and valleys. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of Minervois encompass several villages and vineyards, and it is recognized for its blend of grape varieties, particularly those typical of the southern Rhône. The wines often exhibit a balance of fruit and earthy characteristics.
geo:fr:minervois.style: Full-bodied red wines with fruit and earthy notes

geo:fr:minervois-la-liviniere.name: Minervois-La Livinière
geo:fr:minervois-la-liviniere.description: Minervois-La Livinière is an AOC located within the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its production of red wines. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. It encompasses a defined geographical area with specific regulations governing viticulture and winemaking practices, ensuring quality and authenticity of the wines produced. The terroir includes a mix of clay and limestone soils, which influences the flavor profiles of the wines.
geo:fr:minervois-la-liviniere.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice characteristics

geo:at:mittelburgenland-dac.name: Mittelburgenland
geo:at:mittelburgenland-dac.description: Mittelburgenland is a designated Austrian DAC appellation located in the Burgenland region. It is primarily known for its red wines, particularly those made from the Blaufränkisch grape, which is the signature variety of the area. The appellation is characterized by its diverse soils and favorable climate, which contribute to the production of wines with depth and complexity.
geo:at:mittelburgenland-dac.style: Red wines with depth, primarily from Blaufränkisch

geo:de:mittelhaardt-deutsche-weinstrasse.name: Mittelhaardt-Deutsche Weinstrasse
geo:de:mittelhaardt-deutsche-weinstrasse.description: Mittelhaardt-Deutsche Weinstrasse is a wine appellation located in the Pfalz region of Germany. It is classified as a Bereich, which is a sub-region within the German wine classification system. The area is characterized by its warm climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality white wines, particularly from the Riesling grape. The appellation is known for its vineyards that benefit from the protection of the Haardt mountain range, which influences local weather patterns.
geo:de:mittelhaardt-deutsche-weinstrasse.style: White wines, notable for their acidity and fruit expression

geo:fr:monbazillac.name: Monbazillac
geo:fr:monbazillac.description: Monbazillac is an AOC located in the South West France region, primarily known for its sweet dessert wines.
