The appellation is situated near the Dordogne River, benefiting from a microclimate that promotes the development of noble rot, essential for producing its signature wines. Monbazillac wines are typically made from late-harvest grapes, resulting in rich, concentrated flavors. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, with strict regulations governing grape varieties and production methods.
geo:fr:monbazillac.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, concentrated flavors

geo:es:mondejar.name: Mondéjar
geo:es:mondejar.description: Mondéjar is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is characterized by a diverse climate influenced by both continental and Mediterranean elements, which contributes to the cultivation of various grape varieties. The DO encompasses a range of vineyards that benefit from the region's fertile soils and elevation, leading to the production of predominantly red wines.
geo:es:mondejar.style: Red wines with a focus on Tempranillo and Cabernet Sauvignon

geo:it:monferrato.name: Monferrato
geo:it:monferrato.description: Monferrato is an Italian appellation located in the Piedmont region, characterized by its varied terroir and microclimates. The appellation encompasses a range of communes, primarily in the provinces of Alessandria and Asti. It is known for producing predominantly red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The legal boundaries of Monferrato allow for a diverse range of blends and varietal wines, reflecting the region's viticultural heritage.
geo:it:monferrato.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:montagne-de-reims.name: Montagne de Reims
geo:fr:montagne-de-reims.description: Montagne de Reims is an appellation within the Champagne region of France, known for its production of sparkling wines. It is characterized by its hilly terrain and chalky soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. The appellation is primarily located to the north of the city of Reims and is recognized for its high-quality Pinot Noir grapes, which dominate the vineyard plantings. The climate is continental, with cool winters and warm summers, ideal for the production of Champagne. Montagne de Reims is home to several Champagne houses and cooperatives.

geo:fr:montagne-saint-emilion.name: Montagne-Saint-Émilion
geo:fr:montagne-saint-emilion.description: Montagne-Saint-Émilion is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, specifically within the Right Bank near the town of Saint-Émilion. The appellation is characterized by its clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the production of predominantly red wines. Montagne-Saint-Émilion is known for its Merlot-based blends, often featuring Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon. The region benefits from a temperate maritime climate, which aids in the ripening of grapes. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and structure, reflecting the terroir of the area.
geo:fr:montagne-saint-emilion.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:montagny.name: Montagny
geo:fr:montagny.description: Montagny is an AOC located within the Côte Chalonnaise region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made exclusively from the Chardonnay grape. The appellation encompasses several villages, with the vineyards situated on predominantly limestone and clay soils, contributing to the wines' characteristic minerality and acidity. Montagny wines are typically noted for their freshness and balance, reflecting the cool climate of the region.
geo:fr:montagny.style: Fresh and balanced white wines from Chardonnay

geo:it:montalcino.name: Montalcino
geo:it:montalcino.description: Montalcino is an Italian wine appellation located in Tuscany, for its production of red wines, primarily made from the Sangiovese grape. The region is defined by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Mediterranean and the Apennine Mountains. Montalcino is home to the Brunello di Montalcino, which has gained international acclaim for its quality and aging potential. The appellation's regulations emphasize the use of Sangiovese, with strict guidelines on vineyard practices and aging requirements.
geo:it:montalcino.style: Full-bodied red wines with aging potential

geo:it:montecarlo.name: Montecarlo
geo:it:montecarlo.description: Montecarlo is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Tuscany, Italy. The appellation is situated near the town of Montecarlo, which is known for its favorable climate and diverse soils. Montecarlo primarily produces white wines, with regulations governing the permitted grape varieties and production methods. The region is characterized by its commitment to quality and traditional winemaking practices.
geo:it:montecarlo.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and balance

geo:it:montecucco.name: Montecucco
geo:it:montecucco.description: Montecucco is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in Tuscany, Italy. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly produced from Sangiovese grapes. The appellation is situated in the southern part of Tuscany, benefiting from a diverse range of soils and microclimates that contribute to the complexity of its wines. The region has a historical significance dating back to ancient times, with viticulture practices that have evolved over centuries.
geo:it:montecucco.style: Red wines with a focus on Sangiovese, often structured and age-worthy

geo:it:montefalco-grechetto.name: Montefalco Grechetto
geo:it:montefalco-grechetto.description: Montefalco Grechetto is a white wine appellation located within the Montefalco region of Italy, classified as DOC. The appellation is primarily known for its production of wines made from the Grechetto grape variety. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas within the Montefalco region, where the terroir is characterized by hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate, contributing to the distinctive qualities of the wines produced here.
geo:it:montefalco-grechetto.style: White wines with fresh acidity and herbal notes

geo:it:montefalco-rosso.name: Montefalco Rosso
geo:it:montefalco-rosso.description: Montefalco Rosso is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Umbria region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries which encompass the town of Montefalco and surrounding areas. It primarily produces red wines that are blends, with Sangiovese as the dominant variety, often complemented by other local grapes such as Sagrantino and Merlot. The terroir features a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the wine's structure and aging potential.
geo:it:montefalco-rosso.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with good acidity and tannins

geo:it:montefalco-sagrantino.name: Montefalco Sagrantino
geo:it:montefalco-sagrantino.description: Montefalco Sagrantino is a red wine appellation located in the Umbria region of Italy, recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG). The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the municipality of Montefalco and surrounding areas. It is primarily known for the Sagrantino grape, which is indigenous to the region and is characterized by its high tannin content and deep color. The wines produced here are often noted for their aging potential and complexity, typically exhibiting dark fruit flavors and earthy notes.
geo:it:montefalco-sagrantino.style: Full-bodied red wines with high tannins and aging potential

geo:it:montepulciano.name: Montepulciano
geo:it:montepulciano.description: Montepulciano is an Italian appellation located in the Tuscany region, known for producing red wines primarily from the Sangiovese grape. The area is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that benefits from both Mediterranean influences and altitude. The wines from this appellation often exhibit a balance of acidity and tannin, reflecting the local terroir. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined by specific geographic markers, and it is recognized for its traditional winemaking practices.
geo:it:montepulciano.style: Red wines with balanced acidity and tannins

geo:it:montepulciano-dabruzzo.name: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo
geo:it:montepulciano-dabruzzo.description: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a red wine appellation located in the Abruzzo region of Italy. It is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass the provinces of Teramo, Pescara, L'Aquila, and Chieti. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Montepulciano, which thrives in the region's diverse terroir characterized by hilly landscapes and a Mediterranean climate. The wines are typically noted for their deep color, robust tannins, and rich fruit flavors, often exhibiting notes of dark berries and spice.
geo:it:montepulciano-dabruzzo.style: Full-bodied red wine with dark fruit and earthy characteristics

geo:us:monterey-ava.name: Monterey
geo:us:monterey-ava.description: Monterey is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, known for its diverse microclimates and coastal influences. The region encompasses a variety of topographies, including valleys and hills, which contribute to its unique terroir. Monterey is recognized for producing high-quality white wines, particularly from Chardonnay and Riesling, as well as notable Pinot Noir. The AVA's proximity to the Pacific Ocean moderates temperatures, allowing for extended growing seasons and the development of balanced acidity in the wines.
geo:us:monterey-ava.style: White wines with a focus on Chardonnay and Riesling

geo:es:monterrei-do.name: Monterrei
geo:es:monterrei-do.description: Monterrei is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Galicia region of Spain. It is characterized by its diverse microclimates and varied soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The appellation primarily focuses on white wine production, with a significant emphasis on indigenous grape varieties. Key villages within Monterrei include Verín and Oimbra, which are central to the region's viticultural activities.
geo:es:monterrei-do.style: White wines with fresh acidity and aromatic profiles

geo:fr:monthelie.name: Monthélie
geo:fr:monthelie.description: Monthélie is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation is situated near the village of the same name and is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a continental climate, which contribute to the unique expression of the wines. Monthélie is bordered by the more appellations of Volnay and Auxey-Duresses, influencing its viticultural practices and wine styles.
geo:fr:monthelie.style: Red wines with elegance and moderate tannins

geo:es:montilla-moriles.name: Montilla-Moriles
geo:es:montilla-moriles.description: Montilla-Moriles is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Andalusia region of Spain, known primarily for its production of fortified wines. The appellation encompasses several municipalities, including Montilla and Moriles, characterized by a hot, dry climate and chalky soils that contribute to the unique profile of its wines. The region has a long history of winemaking, with influences dating back to the Roman period, and it is recognized for its distinctive styles of sherry-like wines, primarily produced from the Pedro Ximénez grape.
geo:es:montilla-moriles.style: Fortified wines with rich, sweet profiles and oxidative aging

geo:fr:montlouis-sur-loire.name: Montlouis-sur-Loire
geo:fr:montlouis-sur-loire.description: Montlouis-sur-Loire is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, primarily known for its white wines made from the Chenin Blanc grape. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Loire River, adjacent to Vouvray, and benefits from a similar limestone-rich terroir that influences the character of its wines. The region's climate is characterized by a continental influence, with warm summers and cold winters, which aids in the development of the grapes. Montlouis-sur-Loire wines are often produced in a range of styles, including still, sparkling, and sweet variations, reflecting the versatility of Chenin Blanc.
geo:fr:montlouis-sur-loire.style: White wines, including still and sparkling styles

geo:fr:montrachet.name: Montrachet
geo:fr:montrachet.description: Montrachet is a AOC Grand Cru appellation located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is exclusively dedicated to the production of white wine made from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation is for its exceptional terroir, characterized by limestone soils and a favorable microclimate that contribute to the complexity and depth of its wines.
geo:fr:montrachet.style: Rich, full-bodied white wines with great aging potential

geo:es:montsant-do.name: Montsant
geo:es:montsant-do.description: Montsant is a Denominació d'Origen (DO) located in Catalonia, Spain, established in 2002. It is bordered by the Priorat DO to the south and encompasses a variety of terrains, including hills and valleys. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes and the development of distinctive wine styles.
geo:es:montsant-do.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:us:moon-mountain-district.name: Moon Mountain District
geo:us:moon-mountain-district.description: Moon Mountain District is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 2013, it is situated on the slopes of the Mayacamas Mountains, characterized by its elevation and diverse microclimates. The region benefits from well-drained volcanic soils and a climate that allows for a long growing season, contributing to the development of complex flavors in the grapes. The AVA is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from varieties such as Cabernet Sauvignon and Zinfandel.
geo:us:moon-mountain-district.style: Red wines with depth and complexity

geo:it:morellino-di-scansano.name: Morellino di Scansano
geo:it:morellino-di-scansano.description: Morellino di Scansano is a red wine appellation located in the Maremma region of Tuscany, Italy. It was granted DOCG status in 2007, recognizing its quality and unique characteristics. The appellation is primarily known for wines made from the Sangiovese grape, which must constitute at least 85% of the blend. The region's climate, influenced by its proximity to the Tyrrhenian Sea, contributes to the ripeness and flavor profile of the grapes. The wines are typically characterized by their fruit-forward nature and moderate tannins.
geo:it:morellino-di-scansano.style: Fruit-forward red wines with moderate tannins

geo:fr:morey-saint-denis.name: Morey-Saint-Denis
geo:fr:morey-saint-denis.description: Morey-Saint-Denis is an AOC located within the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is known primarily for its red wines made from Pinot Noir, which benefit from the region's unique terroir and climate. The appellation is situated between Gevrey-Chambertin to the north and Chambolle-Musigny to the south, featuring a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the complexity of its wines. Morey-Saint-Denis includes several premier cru vineyards, enhancing its reputation for high-quality Pinot Noir production.
geo:fr:morey-saint-denis.style: Red wines with elegance and depth, primarily from Pinot Noir

geo:fr:morgon.name: Morgon
geo:fr:morgon.description: Morgon is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known primarily for producing red wines made from the Gamay grape. The appellation is characterized by its granite soils and varied topography, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. Morgon is one of the ten Crus of Beaujolais, recognized for its potential for aging and depth of flavor. The wines typically exhibit a range of fruit and earthy characteristics, reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:morgon.style: Red wines with depth and aging potential, primarily from Gamay

geo:au:mornington-peninsula-gi.name: Mornington Peninsula
geo:au:mornington-peninsula-gi.description: Mornington Peninsula is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia, known for its cool maritime climate and diverse terroir. The region is characterized by its proximity to Port Phillip Bay, which moderates temperatures and contributes to a long growing season. Mornington Peninsula is particularly recognized for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, with a growing reputation for Pinot Gris as well.
geo:au:mornington-peninsula-gi.style: Red wines, notable for elegance and complexity

geo:it:moscato-dasti.name: Moscato d'Asti
geo:it:moscato-dasti.description: Moscato d'Asti is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is characterized by its production of sweet, lightly sparkling wines made primarily from the Muscat grape, known locally as Moscato Bianco. The appellation encompasses several municipalities, with Asti and Alba being notable centers of production. The wines are typically low in alcohol and exhibit aromatic profiles dominated by floral and fruity notes.
geo:it:moscato-dasti.style: Sweet, lightly sparkling white wine with floral and fruity aromas

geo:de:mosel.name: Mosel
geo:de:mosel.description: Germany's most famous wine region, where Riesling grows on impossibly steep slate slopes. The river's twists create south-facing amphitheaters of perfect exposure. Wines range from bone-dry to lusciously sweet, always with electric acidity. Kabinett, Spätlese, Auslese mark ascending ripeness.

geo:de:mosel-qba.name: Mosel
geo:de:mosel-qba.description: The Mosel appellation is located in Germany, characterized by its steep, slate-covered vineyards along the Mosel River. It is for producing high-quality Riesling wines, which benefit from the region's unique microclimate and topography. The legal boundaries of the Mosel appellation encompass several districts, including Bernkastel, Piesport, and Traben-Trarbach, each contributing distinct terroir influences to the wines. The region's cool climate allows for the production of both dry and sweet styles, with a focus on aromatic and mineral-driven profiles.
geo:de:mosel-qba.style: Aromatic white wines, notably Riesling, with varying sweetness levels

geo:fr:moulin-a-vent.name: Moulin-à-Vent
geo:fr:moulin-a-vent.description: Moulin-à-Vent is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known for producing red wines primarily from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated in the northern part of Beaujolais and is characterized by its granite soils, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. Moulin-à-Vent is one of the ten Beaujolais Crus and is recognized for its potential for aging, often exhibiting more complexity and structure than other Beaujolais wines. The climate is continental, with warm summers and cold winters, influencing the ripening of the grapes.
geo:fr:moulin-a-vent.style: Red wines with structure and aging potential

geo:fr:moulis-en-medoc.name: Moulis-en-Médoc
geo:fr:moulis-en-medoc.description: Moulis-en-Médoc is an AOC located in the Médoc region of Bordeaux, France. It is situated between the appellations of Listrac-Médoc to the south and Margaux to the north. The area is characterized by a mix of gravel, clay, and limestone soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the wines produced here. The appellation primarily focuses on red wine production, with a reputation for producing wines that are approachable in their youth yet capable of aging. Moulis-en-Médoc is one of the smaller appellations in the Médoc, with a diverse range of producers.
geo:fr:moulis-en-medoc.style: Red wines known for balance and aging potential

geo:us:mount-veeder-ava.name: Mount Veeder
geo:us:mount-veeder-ava.description: Mount Veeder is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain, with elevations ranging from 400 to 2,600 feet, which contributes to its unique microclimates. The region is known for its volcanic soil and diverse topography, which influence the growing conditions for grapevines. Mount Veeder primarily produces red wines, with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, reflecting the area's suitability for these varieties.
geo:us:mount-veeder-ava.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and complexity

geo:fr:muscadet.name: Muscadet
geo:fr:muscadet.description: The Loire's Atlantic outpost, where Melon de Bourgogne produces bone-dry wines perfect for oysters. 'Sur lie' aging — on the yeast lees — adds texture and depth to what might otherwise be simple wine. The best examples, from old vines in Sèvre-et-Maine, develop surprising complexity.

geo:fr:muscadet-coteaux-de-la-loire.name: Muscadet Coteaux de la Loire
geo:fr:muscadet-coteaux-de-la-loire.description: Muscadet Coteaux de la Loire is an AOC located in the Pays Nantais region of France, primarily known for its production of white wines. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Loire River, characterized by its unique terroir of schist and gneiss soils which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The climate is influenced by the Atlantic Ocean, providing a temperate maritime environment suitable for viticulture. The wines produced here are typically crisp and mineral-driven, reflecting the region's geological diversity.

geo:fr:muscadet-cotes-de-grandlieu.name: Muscadet Cotes de Grandlieu
geo:fr:muscadet-cotes-de-grandlieu.description: Muscadet Cotes de Grandlieu is an AOC located in the Pays Nantais region of France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from the Melon de Bourgogne grape variety. The appellation is situated near the Grandlieu Lake, which influences the local microclimate, contributing to the freshness and minerality of the wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific communes that adhere to defined viticultural practices. The wines typically exhibit a crisp profile, often characterized by their acidity and ability to pair well with seafood.
geo:fr:muscadet-cotes-de-grandlieu.style: Crisp white wines with high acidity and mineral notes

geo:fr:muscadet-sevre-et-maine.name: Muscadet-Sèvre et Maine
geo:fr:muscadet-sevre-et-maine.description: The heart of Muscadet, where the Sèvre and Maine rivers meet. Sur lie aging is mandatory here, giving wines more texture and complexity than basic Muscadet. The best crus — Clisson, Gorges, Le Pallet — are finally getting the recognition they deserve.

geo:fr:muscat-de-beaumes-de-venise.name: Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise
geo:fr:muscat-de-beaumes-de-venise.description: Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, known for its sweet, fortified dessert wines made primarily from the Muscat grape. The appellation is situated near the village of Beaumes-de-Venise, benefiting from a Mediterranean climate that contributes to the ripening of the grapes. The wines are characterized by their aromatic intensity and are produced using a method that involves the partial fermentation of the grape must, followed by the addition of grape spirit to halt fermentation and preserve natural sugars.
geo:fr:muscat-de-beaumes-de-venise.style: Sweet, aromatic dessert wines with rich fruit flavors

geo:fr:muscat-de-frontignan.name: Muscat de Frontignan
geo:fr:muscat-de-frontignan.description: Muscat de Frontignan is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its production of fortified wines primarily made from the Muscat à Petits Grains grape. The appellation is situated near the town of Frontignan, close to the Mediterranean coast, benefiting from a warm climate that aids in the development of the grapes' aromatic qualities. The wines produced here are typically sweet and aromatic, showcasing the distinctive characteristics of the Muscat variety.
geo:fr:muscat-de-frontignan.style: Sweet fortified wine with aromatic Muscat characteristics

geo:fr:muscat-de-lunel.name: Muscat de Lunel
geo:fr:muscat-de-lunel.description: Muscat de Lunel is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its production of fortified wines primarily from the Muscat à Petits Grains grape variety. The appellation is situated near the Mediterranean coast, benefiting from a warm climate that aids in the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are typically sweet and aromatic, reflecting the characteristics of the Muscat grape. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined to ensure the quality and authenticity of the wines.
geo:fr:muscat-de-lunel.style: Fortified sweet wines with aromatic profiles

geo:fr:muscat-de-mireval.name: Muscat de Mireval
geo:fr:muscat-de-mireval.description: Muscat de Mireval is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its fortified wines made primarily from the Muscat à Petits Grains grape variety. The appellation is situated near the Mediterranean coast, benefiting from a warm climate that contributes to the ripening of the grapes. The production of Muscat de Mireval is characterized by the use of a mutage process, where fermentation is halted by the addition of alcohol, resulting in a sweet, aromatic wine. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined to ensure the quality and authenticity of the wines produced under its designation.
geo:fr:muscat-de-mireval.style: Fortified sweet wine with aromatic profiles

geo:fr:muscat-de-rivesaltes.name: Muscat de Rivesaltes
geo:fr:muscat-de-rivesaltes.description: Muscat de Rivesaltes is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, recognized for its fortified wines made primarily from Muscat grapes. The appellation is characterized by its unique terroir, which includes a Mediterranean climate and diverse soil types, contributing to the aromatic intensity of the wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas within the Roussillon, where traditional production methods are employed to create these sweet, fortified wines.
geo:fr:muscat-de-rivesaltes.style: Sweet, fortified wines with aromatic Muscat characteristics

geo:fr:muscat-de-saint-jean-de-minervois.name: Muscat de Saint-Jean-de-Minervois
geo:fr:muscat-de-saint-jean-de-minervois.description: Muscat de Saint-Jean-de-Minervois is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its production of fortified wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Saint-Jean-de-Minervois, characterized by a Mediterranean climate that supports the growth of Muscat A Petits Grains grapes. This AOC was established to highlight the unique qualities of its sweet, aromatic wines, which are typically produced using traditional methods of fortification.
geo:fr:muscat-de-saint-jean-de-minervois.style: Fortified sweet wine with aromatic characteristics

geo:fr:musigny.name: Musigny
geo:fr:musigny.description: Musigny is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation is characterized by its specific terroir, which includes limestone soils and a favorable microclimate that contribute to the complexity and elegance of its wines.
geo:fr:musigny.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:es:malaga.name: Málaga
geo:es:malaga.description: Málaga is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Andalusia region of Spain, known for its production of fortified wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of microclimates and altitudes, contributing to the diversity of its wine styles. It has a historical significance dating back to ancient times, with a strong influence from Phoenician and Roman viticulture. The region is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which is conducive to the cultivation of sweet and fortified wines.
geo:es:malaga.style: Fortified wines, often sweet and rich

geo:fr:macon.name: Mâcon
geo:fr:macon.description: Mâcon is an AOC located in the Mâconnais region of France, primarily known for its production of white wines made from Chardonnay. The appellation encompasses several communes and is characterized by its limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the wines' structure and minerality. Mâcon wines are typically produced in a fresh, fruit-forward style, often with minimal oak influence, showcasing the varietal's natural characteristics.
geo:fr:macon.style: Fresh, fruit-forward white wines primarily from Chardonnay

geo:fr:macon-villages.name: Mâcon-Villages
geo:fr:macon-villages.description: Mâcon-Villages is an AOC located within the Mâconnais region of Burgundy, France. It encompasses a number of villages that are permitted to produce white wine primarily from the Chardonnay grape. The appellation is characterized by its limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the wine's structure and acidity. Mâcon-Villages wines are typically fresh and fruity, reflecting the cooler climate of the region.
geo:fr:macon-villages.style: Fresh and fruity white wines from Chardonnay

geo:fr:medoc-aoc.name: Médoc
geo:fr:medoc-aoc.description: Médoc is an appellation located in the Bordeaux region of France, known for its production of red wines primarily from Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated on the left bank of the Gironde estuary and is characterized by its gravelly soils, which are conducive to the cultivation of these grape varieties. Médoc encompasses several notable communes, including Pauillac, Margaux, and Saint-Estèphe, each contributing to the diversity of styles within the appellation. The wines from Médoc are often structured and age-worthy, reflecting the terroir's unique characteristics.
geo:fr:medoc-aoc.style: Full-bodied red wines with aging potential and complex flavors

geo:es:mentrida.name: Méntrida
geo:es:mentrida.description: Méntrida is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located within the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. It is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes a mix of clay and sandy soils, and a climate that features hot summers and cold winters. The appellation is known for producing predominantly red wines, with a focus on varieties such as Garnacha, Tempranillo, and Syrah. The wines often reflect the region's unique climatic conditions and soil composition.
geo:es:mentrida.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit and structure

geo:de:nahe.name: Nahe
geo:de:nahe.description: A hidden gem among German wine regions, the Nahe offers remarkable geological diversity — volcanic soils, slate, sandstone, and quartzite within a small area. This variety produces Rieslings of many styles, from delicate and floral to powerful and mineral. Helmut Dönnhoff has brought international attention to this underrated region.

geo:de:nahe-qba.name: Nahe
geo:de:nahe-qba.description: Nahe is a wine appellation located in Germany, characterized by its diverse geology and microclimates. The region is situated along the Nahe River, which influences the local climate and viticulture. It is known for producing a range of wine styles, primarily from Riesling, but also includes varieties such as Pinot Noir and Müller-Thurgau. The appellation encompasses several villages and vineyards, each contributing to the overall diversity of the wines produced.
geo:de:nahe-qba.style: Dry and off-dry white wines, with some red and sparkling options

geo:us:napa-valley-ava.name: Napa Valley AVA
geo:us:napa-valley-ava.description: America's most prestigious wine address. Thirty miles long and five miles wide, the valley encompasses a remarkable range of climates and soils. Sub-AVAs like Oakville, Rutherford, and Stags Leap refine the picture. Cabernet Sauvignon is king; prices reflect the reputation.

geo:es:navarra.name: Navarra
geo:es:navarra.description: Long overshadowed by neighboring Rioja, Navarra has forged its own identity. Rosado (rosé) from Garnacha built the reputation; serious reds from Tempranillo and international varieties now command attention. Better value than Rioja, with quality approaching the best.

geo:es:navarra-do.name: Navarra
geo:es:navarra-do.description: Navarra is a designated wine appellation located in northern Spain, primarily known for its diverse range of wines. The appellation encompasses various microclimates and topographies, which contribute to the production of both red and white wines. It is situated adjacent to the Rioja region and has a historical significance in winemaking, with roots tracing back to Roman times. The region is characterized by its use of both indigenous and international grape varieties.
geo:es:navarra-do.style: Diverse wines including reds, whites, and rosés with regional character

geo:it:nebbiolo-dalba.name: Nebbiolo d'Alba
geo:it:nebbiolo-dalba.description: Nebbiolo d'Alba is an Italian DOC appellation located in the Piedmont region, established in 1970. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Nebbiolo grape variety. The appellation encompasses several communes around the city of Alba, which is situated in a hilly area characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the wine's structure and aging potential.
geo:it:nebbiolo-dalba.style: Red wines with high acidity and tannins, often aged for complexity

geo:it:nero-di-troia.name: Nero di Troia
geo:it:nero-di-troia.description: Nero di Troia is a red wine appellation located in the Puglia region of Italy, classified as DOC. The appellation is named after the indigenous grape variety Nero di Troia, which is known for its deep color and tannic structure. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas within Puglia, where the climate and soil conditions favor the cultivation of this variety. Wines produced under this designation typically exhibit a range of flavors and aromas, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:it:nero-di-troia.style: Full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and earthy notes

geo:at:neusiedlersee-dac.name: Neusiedlersee
geo:at:neusiedlersee-dac.description: Neusiedlersee is a designated Austrian wine appellation located in the Burgenland region, recognized for its production of high-quality dessert wines. The appellation benefits from a unique microclimate created by the proximity of Lake Neusiedl, which contributes to the development of noble rot, essential for the production of sweet wines. The legal boundaries of Neusiedlersee encompass areas that are particularly suited for late-harvest and botrytized wines, reflecting the region's historical significance in the Austrian wine industry.
geo:at:neusiedlersee-dac.style: Known for sweet dessert wines, often botrytized

geo:de:nierstein.name: Nierstein
geo:de:nierstein.description: Nierstein is an appellation located within the Rheinhessen region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is situated along the Rhine River, characterized by its steep slopes and diverse soil types, including red slate and loess. The climate is influenced by the river, contributing to the ripening of grapes. Nierstein is known for producing high-quality white wines, particularly from the Riesling and Silvaner grape varieties.
geo:de:nierstein.style: White wines with a focus on Riesling and Silvaner

geo:it:nizza.name: Nizza
geo:it:nizza.description: Nizza is a DOCG appellation located in the Piedmont region of Italy, recognized for its production of high-quality red wines primarily made from the Barbera grape. Established as a distinct appellation in 2014, Nizza encompasses several municipalities within the province of Asti. The region's unique terroir, characterized by clay and limestone soils, contributes to the distinctive flavor profile of its wines, which often exhibit depth and complexity.
geo:it:nizza.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and acidity

geo:nz:north-canterbury-gi.name: North Canterbury
geo:nz:north-canterbury-gi.description: North Canterbury is an appellation located within the Canterbury region of New Zealand. It is defined by its diverse microclimates and varied soils, which contribute to a range of wine styles. The region is known for its cool climate, ideal for producing high-quality Pinot Noir and aromatic white wines. The legal boundaries encompass several key wine-producing areas, including Waipara Valley, which is recognized for its unique terroir.
geo:nz:north-canterbury-gi.style: Notable for balanced Pinot Noir and aromatic whites.

geo:us:north-coast-ava.name: North Coast
geo:us:north-coast-ava.description: The North Coast is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, encompassing several counties including Napa, Sonoma, and Mendocino. Established in 1983, it is known for its diverse microclimates and varied topography, which contribute to a wide range of wine styles. The AVA is characterized by its coastal influences, which moderate temperatures and enhance grape quality.
geo:us:north-coast-ava.style: Diverse wines with a focus on quality and regional expression.

geo:it:noto-doc.name: Noto
geo:it:noto-doc.description: Noto is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located in the southeastern part of Sicily, Italy. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly made from the Nero d'Avola grape. The region's climate is characterized by warm temperatures and a Mediterranean influence, contributing to the ripeness and richness of the wines produced here. Noto's vineyards benefit from the proximity to the coast, which helps moderate temperatures and allows for a longer growing season. The appellation is recognized for its focus on quality and the expression of local terroir.
geo:it:noto-doc.style: Rich red wines with ripe fruit and balanced acidity

geo:fr:nuits-saint-georges.name: Nuits-Saint-Georges
geo:fr:nuits-saint-georges.description: The commercial heart of Burgundy's Côte de Nuits, and home to some of its earthiest wines. No Grand Crus here, but Premier Crus like Les Saint-Georges and Les Vaucrains rival the best. The wines are typically firm and structured when young, developing savory complexity with age.

geo:us:oak-knoll-district-of-napa-valley.name: Oak Knoll District of Napa Valley
geo:us:oak-knoll-district-of-napa-valley.description: Southern valley-floor AVA between Napa city and Yountville, established by the TTB in 2004. Cooled by maritime air drawn from San Pablo Bay, it is among the more temperate stretches of Napa Valley, which lengthens the growing season and preserves acidity. That moderate climate gives the district unusual varietal breadth: Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay anchor the plantings, but Merlot and Riesling also have a long history here. The result is a regional signature of brightness and structure built for ageing rather than early opulence.
geo:us:oak-knoll-district-of-napa-valley.style: Structured, bright, versatile — Cab-anchored but distinctly cooler than upper-valley AVAs

geo:us:oakville-ava.name: Oakville
geo:us:oakville-ava.description: Napa's most sought-after Cabernet address. The benchland running through Oakville — the Rutherford Bench extending south — produces wines of particular depth and dusty tannins. Screaming Eagle, Harlan, and Opus One call it home. This is Napa at its most concentrated.

geo:pt:obidos.name: Obidos
geo:pt:obidos.description: Obidos is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located within the Lisboa region of Portugal. It is characterized by its historical significance in Portuguese viticulture and its proximity to the Atlantic Ocean, which influences its climate. The appellation is known for producing primarily red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties that thrive in the local terroir.
geo:pt:obidos.style: Red wines with a focus on indigenous varieties

geo:it:offida.name: Offida
geo:it:offida.description: Offida is a DOCG appellation located in the Marche region of Italy, established in 2010. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are made predominantly from the Montepulciano and Sangiovese grape varieties. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that benefits from both maritime and continental influences, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced. The legal boundaries of Offida encompass several municipalities, ensuring a focus on quality and traditional winemaking practices.
geo:it:offida.style: Red wines with a focus on structure and fruit expression

geo:it:oltrepo-pavese-doc.name: Oltrepò Pavese
geo:it:oltrepo-pavese-doc.description: Oltrepò Pavese is an Italian appellation located in the Lombardy region, specifically in the province of Pavia. It is characterized by a hilly landscape and a continental climate, which contribute to the production of a variety of wines. The appellation is known for its sparkling wines made primarily from the Pinot Noir grape, as well as still wines from other varieties. The legal boundaries of Oltrepò Pavese encompass several municipalities, and it is recognized for both its red and white wine production.
geo:it:oltrepo-pavese-doc.style: Notable for sparkling and still wines, particularly from Pinot Noir

geo:fr:orleans.name: Orleans
geo:fr:orleans.description: The Orleans appellation is located in the Loire Valley of France and is classified as an AOC (Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée). It is primarily known for producing white wines, with a focus on varietals that thrive in the region's climate and soil conditions. The appellation encompasses specific legal boundaries that define its production standards and practices.
geo:fr:orleans.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and balance

geo:fr:orleans-clery.name: Orleans-Clery
geo:fr:orleans-clery.description: Orleans-Clery is an Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée (AOC) located within the Loire Valley of France. Established in 2011, it is primarily known for producing red wines made from Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated near the town of Orleans, benefiting from a continental climate that influences the ripening of grapes.
geo:fr:orleans-clery.style: Red wines primarily from Cabernet Franc with regional characteristics

geo:de:ortenau.name: Ortenau
geo:de:ortenau.description: Ortenau is a designated Bereich within the Baden wine region of Germany, known for its diverse topography and microclimates. The area features a mix of hilly landscapes and valleys, which contribute to the cultivation of various grape varieties. Ortenau is recognized for producing both red and white wines, with a focus on quality and regional expression.
geo:de:ortenau.style: Red wines, with notable whites, reflecting regional diversity

geo:it:orvieto-doc.name: Orvieto
geo:it:orvieto-doc.description: Orvieto is a white wine appellation located in the Umbria region of Italy, classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC). The appellation is characterized by its volcanic soil and a climate that benefits from both Mediterranean and continental influences. Orvieto wines are typically produced from a blend of grape varieties, with a focus on Trebbiano Toscano and Grechetto. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several municipalities, including Orvieto, which is situated on a hilltop overlooking the Paglia River valley. The wines are known for their crisp acidity and floral notes, often exhibiting a mineral character attributed to the volcanic terroir.
geo:it:orvieto-doc.style: Crisp, floral white wines with mineral notes

geo:it:orvieto-classico.name: Orvieto Classico
geo:it:orvieto-classico.description: Orvieto Classico is a white wine appellation located in the Umbria region of Italy, recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC). The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and volcanic soil, which contribute to the unique terroir. It encompasses the historical town of Orvieto and its surrounding areas, where winemaking has been practiced for centuries. The wines produced here are typically fresh and aromatic, reflecting the local climate and grape varieties.
geo:it:orvieto-classico.style: Crisp and aromatic white wines with moderate acidity

geo:fr:pacherenc-du-vic-bilh.name: Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh
geo:fr:pacherenc-du-vic-bilh.description: Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh is an AOC located in the South West France region, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Vic-Bilh and benefits from a diverse terroir influenced by both Atlantic and Mediterranean climates. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas where the unique climatic and geological conditions contribute to the production of distinctive wines. The wines are typically characterized by their aromatic complexity and balance, often showcasing a range of fruit and floral notes.
geo:fr:pacherenc-du-vic-bilh.style: Aromatic white wines with complexity and balance

geo:es:pago-de-arinzano.name: Pago de Arínzano
geo:es:pago-de-arinzano.description: Pago de Arínzano is a designated wine appellation located within the Navarra region of Spain, classified as a Vino de Pago. This appellation encompasses a single estate, which is recognized for its commitment to quality and the production of premium wines. The legal boundaries of Pago de Arínzano are defined by the estate's geographical limits, and it is known for its distinct terroir that influences the character of its wines.
geo:es:pago-de-arinzano.style: Red wines with a focus on quality and terroir expression

geo:fr:palette-aoc.name: Palette
geo:fr:palette-aoc.description: Palette is an AOC located in the Provence region of France, known for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the town of Aix-en-Provence and is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes clay-limestone soils and a Mediterranean climate. Palette has a long history of viticulture, with records dating back to Roman times, and it is recognized for its unique blend of grape varieties that reflect the region's heritage.
geo:fr:palette-aoc.style: Red wines with complexity and regional character

geo:pt:palmela.name: Palmela
geo:pt:palmela.description: Palmela is a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC) located in the Setúbal region of Portugal. It is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. The appellation is known primarily for its red wines, particularly those made from the Castelão grape variety, which thrives in the local terroir. The region also produces white wines, with Fernão Pires being a notable variety.
geo:pt:palmela.style: Red wines with a focus on Castelão

geo:it:pantelleria.name: Pantelleria
geo:it:pantelleria.description: Pantelleria is an Italian wine appellation located on the eponymous island in the Strait of Sicily. The region is for its production of sweet dessert wines, particularly those made from the Zibibbo grape, also known as Muscat of Alexandria. The unique volcanic soil and Mediterranean climate contribute to the distinctive character of the wines. The appellation is governed by strict regulations regarding grape varieties and production methods, ensuring the preservation of traditional winemaking practices.
geo:it:pantelleria.style: Sweet dessert wines, often with rich, aromatic profiles

geo:us:paso-robles-ava.name: Paso Robles
geo:us:paso-robles-ava.description: Paso Robles is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, known for its diverse climate and soil types. The region spans approximately 614,000 acres, with a significant portion dedicated to vineyards. It is recognized for producing a wide range of wine styles, particularly red wines, due to its warm days and cool nights, which facilitate balanced ripening of grapes.
geo:us:paso-robles-ava.style: Predominantly red wines with varied styles and blends

geo:it:passito-di-pantelleria.name: Passito di Pantelleria
geo:it:passito-di-pantelleria.description: Passito di Pantelleria is a DOC appellation located on the island of Pantelleria, part of Sicily, Italy. The appellation is for its production of sweet dessert wines made primarily from the Zibibbo grape, also known as Muscat of Alexandria. The unique volcanic soil and the island's hot, dry climate contribute to the concentration of sugars in the grapes, which are typically dried on mats before fermentation. This method enhances the wine's rich flavors and aromas, resulting in a distinctive style that reflects the island's terroir.
geo:it:passito-di-pantelleria.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, concentrated flavors

geo:fr:patrimonio-aoc.name: Patrimonio
geo:fr:patrimonio-aoc.description: Patrimonio is an AOC located in the northern part of Corsica, established in 1968. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are produced from indigenous grape varieties. The appellation is characterized by its diverse terroir, including limestone and clay soils, and a Mediterranean climate that influences the growing conditions.
geo:fr:patrimonio-aoc.style: Red wines with structure and depth, often showcasing local grape varieties

geo:fr:pauillac.name: Pauillac
geo:fr:pauillac.description: Home to three of Bordeaux's five First Growths: Lafite, Latour, and Mouton. Pauillac's wines are Cabernet Sauvignon at its most commanding — structured, complex, built to age. The famous gravel banks here produce wines of remarkable depth and longevity. This is Bordeaux at its most serious.

geo:fr:pays-doc-igp.name: Pays d'Oc
geo:fr:pays-doc-igp.description: Pays d'Oc is an Indication Géographique Protégée (IGP) located in the Languedoc-Roussillon region of France. Established in 2009, it encompasses a wide area that allows for a diverse range of grape varieties and wine styles. The IGP designation permits wines to be produced from grapes grown in the broader region, offering flexibility in blending and production methods.
geo:fr:pays-doc-igp.style: Varied styles, including red, white, and rosé wines.

geo:es:penedes-do.name: Penedès
geo:es:penedes-do.description: Catalonia's most versatile wine region, home to both still wines and the vast majority of Cava production. Torres revolutionized Spanish wine here with international varieties; indigenous grapes are making a comeback. From fresh whites to structured reds, Penedès offers range.

geo:fr:pernand-vergelesses.name: Pernand-Vergelesses
geo:fr:pernand-vergelesses.description: Pernand-Vergelesses is an AOC located within the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is situated to the north of the more famous village of Aloxe-Corton and encompasses both red and white wine production. The appellation is characterized by its limestone soils and a climate that is influenced by the nearby hills, which provide a unique terroir for viticulture. The red wines produced here are primarily made from Pinot Noir, showcasing the region's ability to produce elegant and structured wines.
geo:fr:pernand-vergelesses.style: Red wines are typically elegant with good structure

geo:fr:pessac-leognan-aoc.name: Pessac-Léognan
geo:fr:pessac-leognan-aoc.description: Pessac-Léognan is an AOC located within the Graves region of Bordeaux, France. It is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on red blends primarily composed of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The appellation encompasses several communes, including Pessac and Léognan, and is characterized by its gravelly soils, which contribute to the quality of the wines. Pessac-Léognan is notable for being one of the first regions in Bordeaux to receive classification status in 1953, highlighting its historical significance in the production of fine wines.
geo:fr:pessac-leognan-aoc.style: Red wines with structure and aging potential

geo:fr:petit-chablis.name: Petit Chablis
geo:fr:petit-chablis.description: Petit Chablis is the entry-level appellation within the Chablis classification system. It is produced exclusively from Chardonnay grown on higher, flatter plateau sites surrounding the core Chablis vineyards. These sites are typically based on younger Portlandian limestone soils, rather than the Kimmeridgian marl associated with Chablis, Premier Cru, and Grand Cru vineyards. This geological difference results in wines that are generally lighter in body and simpler in structure. Stylistically, Petit Chablis is characterized by high acidity and a fresh, linear profile, with aromas and flavors commonly centered on citrus, green apple, and subtle floral or saline notes. The wines are usually vinified without significant oak influence and are intended for early consumption, typically within one to two years of release. While it occupies the lowest tier of the Chablis hierarchy, Petit Chablis serves as an accessible introduction to the region’s cool-climate Chardonnay style, emphasizing freshness and minerality rather than depth or aging potential.

geo:de:pfalz.name: Pfalz
geo:de:pfalz.description: Germany's second-largest wine region and sunniest, the Pfalz (Palatinate) stretches along the Haardt mountain foothills. Warmer than the Mosel, it produces fuller-bodied Riesling alongside excellent Spätburgunder (Pinot Noir) and white Burgundy varieties. The Mittelhaardt zone around Deidesheim and Forst is considered the heart of quality production.

geo:de:pfalz-qba.name: Pfalz
geo:de:pfalz-qba.description: Pfalz is an appellation located in the southwestern part of Germany, known for its warm climate and diverse soil types. It is characterized by its extensive vineyards that stretch along the eastern edge of the Rhine River. The region is recognized for producing a wide range of wines, particularly white varieties, and has a history of viticulture that dates back to Roman times. The Pfalz region is also notable for its large number of cooperative wineries and its emphasis on sustainable viticulture practices.
geo:de:pfalz-qba.style: Dry white wines, with notable reds and sparkling options

geo:fr:pic-saint-loup-aoc.name: Pic Saint-Loup
geo:fr:pic-saint-loup-aoc.description: Pic Saint-Loup is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its red wines. The appellation is situated to the north of Montpellier, characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes limestone and clay soils. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, contributing to the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are typically blends, showcasing the region's unique characteristics.
geo:fr:pic-saint-loup-aoc.style: Full-bodied red wines with fruit-forward profiles and herbal notes

geo:fr:picpoul-de-pinet.name: Picpoul de Pinet
geo:fr:picpoul-de-pinet.description: Picpoul de Pinet is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, primarily known for producing white wines from the Piquepoul Blanc grape variety. The appellation is situated near the Étang de Thau, benefiting from a Mediterranean climate that contributes to the freshness and acidity of the wines. The legal boundaries of Picpoul de Pinet encompass specific communes, ensuring the preservation of its unique terroir, characterized by limestone and clay soils. The wines are typically dry and crisp, often exhibiting citrus and mineral notes.
geo:fr:picpoul-de-pinet.style: Crisp, dry white wines with citrus and mineral characteristics

geo:es:pla-de-bages.name: Pla de Bages
geo:es:pla-de-bages.description: Pla de Bages is a designated wine appellation (DO) located in Catalonia, Spain. It is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes a mix of clay and limestone soils, and a Mediterranean climate that influences the growing conditions. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The appellation's boundaries encompass several municipalities, contributing to its unique identity within Catalonia.
geo:es:pla-de-bages.style: Red wines with a focus on indigenous varieties

geo:fr:pomerol.name: Pomerol
geo:fr:pomerol.description: Bordeaux's smallest great appellation, and home to its most expensive wine. Pétrus and its neighbors grow Merlot in clay soils that produce wines of remarkable richness and complexity. No classification exists here — reputation alone determines hierarchy. Pomerol is Bordeaux's most exclusive address.

geo:it:pomino.name: Pomino
geo:it:pomino.description: Pomino is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Tuscany, Italy. The appellation is situated in the foothills of the Apennines, near the town of Rufina, and is characterized by its diverse microclimates and altitudes. The region is known for producing primarily white wines, with a focus on Chardonnay and Pinot Bianco, as well as red wines from Sangiovese and Pinot Nero. The DOC regulations allow for specific grape varieties and emphasize quality production methods.
geo:it:pomino.style: White wines with a focus on Chardonnay and Pinot Bianco

geo:fr:pommard.name: Pommard
geo:fr:pommard.description: Volnay's muscular neighbor. The clay-rich soils here produce Burgundy's sturdiest reds — wines that demand time to soften but reward patience with depth and structure. Rugiens and Épenots, the top Premier Crus, can age for decades. Pommard is Burgundy at its most serious.

geo:pt:porto.name: Porto
geo:pt:porto.description: Porto is a fortified wine produced in the Douro Valley of Portugal, recognized as a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC). The appellation encompasses the city of Porto and its surrounding areas, where wines are typically aged in barrels before bottling. The production process involves the addition of grape spirit to halt fermentation, resulting in a higher alcohol content and residual sugar.
geo:pt:porto.style: Rich, sweet fortified wines with varying levels of aging

geo:fr:pouilly-fuisse.name: Pouilly-Fuissé
geo:fr:pouilly-fuisse.description: Pouilly-Fuissé is an AOC located in the Mâconnais region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing white wines made from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation encompasses several villages, including Fuissé, Solutré-Pouilly, and Vergisson, and is characterized by its limestone-rich soils and varied microclimates, which contribute to the complexity of the wines. The wines of Pouilly-Fuissé often exhibit a range of styles, from fresh and mineral to rich and oaky, depending on the winemaking techniques employed.
geo:fr:pouilly-fuisse.style: White wines, often rich with mineral and fruity characteristics

geo:fr:pouilly-fume.name: Pouilly-Fumé
geo:fr:pouilly-fume.description: Pouilly-Fumé is an AOC located in the Centre-Loire region of France, primarily known for its white wines made from Sauvignon Blanc. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Loire River, characterized by its flinty soils, which contribute to the distinct mineral quality of the wines. The legal boundaries of Pouilly-Fumé encompass several communes, with the town of Pouilly-sur-Loire being central to its production. The wines are typically noted for their aromatic complexity and crisp acidity, reflecting the region's unique terroir.
geo:fr:pouilly-fume.style: Crisp, mineral-driven white wines with aromatic complexity

geo:fr:pouilly-sur-loire.name: Pouilly-sur-Loire
geo:fr:pouilly-sur-loire.description: Pouilly-sur-Loire is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is situated along the Loire River, characterized by a continental climate that influences the grape growing conditions. The region is recognized for its unique terroir, which includes limestone and clay soils that contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. Pouilly-sur-Loire is adjacent to the more famous Pouilly-Fumé, yet it maintains its own identity with specific regulations governing production.
geo:fr:pouilly-sur-loire.style: White wines with mineral and fresh characteristics

geo:fr:premieres-cotes-de-bordeaux.name: Premieres Cotes de Bordeaux
geo:fr:premieres-cotes-de-bordeaux.description: Premieres Cotes de Bordeaux is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Garonne River, adjacent to the more famous regions of Graves and Sauternes. The climate is characterized by a maritime influence, which contributes to the ripening of grapes and the development of aromatic profiles. The wines produced here often exhibit a balance of freshness and richness, reflecting the terroir of the area.
geo:fr:premieres-cotes-de-bordeaux.style: White wines with a balance of freshness and richness

geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria.name: Primitivo di Manduria
geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria.description: Primitivo di Manduria is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Puglia region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its production of red wines made from the Primitivo grape variety. The legal boundaries of the DOC encompass several municipalities, with a focus on the area around Manduria, which is characterized by its warm climate and fertile soils that contribute to the richness of the wines. The wines typically exhibit high alcohol content and pronounced fruit flavors, reflecting the unique terroir of the region.
geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria.style: Rich red wines with high alcohol and pronounced fruit flavors

geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria-dolce-natur.name: Primitivo di Manduria Dolce Naturale
geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria-dolce-natur.description: Primitivo di Manduria Dolce Naturale is a DOCG appellation located in the Puglia region of Italy, specifically known for its production of sweet dessert wines. The appellation is characterized by its use of the Primitivo grape, which is harvested late to achieve higher sugar levels. The wines are typically rich and concentrated, reflecting the warm climate and fertile soils of the area. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific municipalities within the province of Taranto, ensuring adherence to quality standards and production methods.
geo:it:primitivo-di-manduria-dolce-natur.style: Sweet dessert wine made from late-harvest Primitivo grapes

geo:es:priorat-doca.name: Priorat
geo:es:priorat-doca.description: Steep llicorella (slate) slopes produce some of Spain's most concentrated wines. Old-vine Garnacha and Cariñena, once used for bulk blending, now command serious attention. The terrain is brutal; yields are tiny; the wines are intense. Spain's cult region.

geo:it:prosecco.name: Prosecco
geo:it:prosecco.description: Prosecco is a sparkling wine appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy, classified as DOC. The legal boundaries for Prosecco encompass several provinces, primarily Treviso and Vicenza, with the majority of production centered around the Conegliano-Valdobbiadene area. The primary grape variety used in Prosecco production is Glera, which must constitute at least 85% of the blend. The wine is typically produced using the Charmat method, which emphasizes freshness and fruitiness.
geo:it:prosecco.style: Light, fruity sparkling wine with floral and citrus notes

geo:it:prosecco-di-conegliano-valdobbiad.name: Prosecco di Conegliano Valdobbiadene
geo:it:prosecco-di-conegliano-valdobbiad.description: Prosecco di Conegliano Valdobbiadene is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Veneto region of Italy. It encompasses the hilly areas between the towns of Conegliano and Valdobbiadene, recognized for their unique microclimates and soil composition that contribute to the quality of the wines produced. The primary grape variety used in this appellation is Glera, which is known for its aromatic qualities and is essential for the production of the region's sparkling wines. The appellation is characterized by its traditional production methods, including the Charmat method, which enhances the fresh and fruity profile of the wines.
geo:it:prosecco-di-conegliano-valdobbiad.style: Sparkling wine with fresh, fruity characteristics

geo:cl:puente-alto.name: Puente Alto
geo:cl:puente-alto.description: Chile's first sub-appellation of origin, carved from the upper Maipo. The vineyards here, at nearly 700 meters, produce Cabernet of exceptional structure. Concha y Toro, Almaviva, and Viñedo Chadwick share this privileged terroir. Chile's Grand Cru.

geo:fr:puisseguin-saint-emilion.name: Puisseguin-Saint-Émilion
geo:fr:puisseguin-saint-emilion.description: Puisseguin-Saint-Émilion is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, specifically in the Right Bank near the town of Saint-Émilion. The appellation is characterized by its limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the production of red wines primarily made from Merlot and Cabernet Franc. Established as an AOC in 1936, Puisseguin-Saint-Émilion is known for its structured and fruit-forward wines that often reflect the terroir of the surrounding Saint-Émilion region.
geo:fr:puisseguin-saint-emilion.style: Full-bodied red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:puligny-montrachet.name: Puligny-Montrachet
geo:fr:puligny-montrachet.description: Home to half of Le Montrachet, the world's most celebrated white wine vineyard. Puligny's Chardonnays are typically more taut and mineral than neighboring Meursault — elegance over richness. Bâtard-Montrachet, Chevalier-Montrachet, and Bienvenues add to the Grand Cru constellation.

geo:fr:pecharmant.name: Pécharmant
geo:fr:pecharmant.description: Pécharmant is an AOC located in the South West France region, specifically near the town of Bergerac. The appellation is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a temperate climate, which contribute to the cultivation of red grape varieties. The legal boundaries of Pécharmant encompass several communes, and it is known for producing structured red wines that often benefit from aging. The region's viticulture has historical roots dating back to the Roman era, with a focus on blending local and international grape varieties.
geo:fr:pecharmant.style: Full-bodied red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:pezenas.name: Pézenas
geo:fr:pezenas.description: Pézenas is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, known primarily for its red wines. The appellation is situated near the town of Pézenas, characterized by a Mediterranean climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The legal boundaries of Pézenas encompass several communes, and it is recognized for producing wines that reflect the local terroir, often with a blend of grape varieties.
geo:fr:pezenas.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:quarts-de-chaume.name: Quarts de Chaume
geo:fr:quarts-de-chaume.description: Quarts de Chaume is an AOC Grand Cru located within the Loire Valley, specifically in the Anjou region. This appellation is for its production of sweet dessert wines primarily made from the Chenin Blanc grape. The legal boundaries of Quarts de Chaume encompass a small area on the south bank of the Loire River, characterized by its unique terroir, which includes schist and volcanic soils that contribute to the complexity of the wines. The climate is influenced by the nearby river, creating conditions favorable for the development of noble rot, essential for the production of these sweet wines.
geo:fr:quarts-de-chaume.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, honeyed flavors

geo:fr:quincy.name: Quincy
geo:fr:quincy.description: Quincy is an AOC located in the Centre-Loire region of France, primarily known for its production of white wines made from Sauvignon Blanc. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Loire River, characterized by its clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the distinctive qualities of the wines. Quincy was granted AOC status in 1936, making it one of the earliest recognized appellations in the Loire Valley. The wines produced here are typically fresh and aromatic, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:quincy.style: Fresh and aromatic white wines, primarily from Sauvignon Blanc

geo:fr:rasteau.name: Rasteau
geo:fr:rasteau.description: Rasteau is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, recognized for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Rasteau, characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes clay-limestone soils and a warm Mediterranean climate. The wines produced here are primarily based on Grenache, often blended with other varieties, and are known for their richness and depth.
geo:fr:rasteau.style: Rich red wines, primarily from Grenache, with depth and complexity

geo:it:recioto-della-valpolicella.name: Recioto della Valpolicella
geo:it:recioto-della-valpolicella.description: Recioto della Valpolicella is a dessert wine appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy. It is classified as a DOCG, indicating a high level of quality and adherence to strict production regulations. The appellation is known for its sweet red wines made from partially dried grapes, primarily from the Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara varieties. The unique production method involves drying the grapes to concentrate their sugars and flavors before fermentation.
geo:it:recioto-della-valpolicella.style: Sweet red dessert wine with rich fruit flavors

geo:it:recioto-di-soave.name: Recioto di Soave
geo:it:recioto-di-soave.description: Recioto di Soave is a Italian dessert wine produced under the DOCG classification in the Veneto region. It is made primarily from the Garganega grape, which is harvested late and often subjected to drying processes to concentrate sugars. The appellation is located within the Soave wine zone, characterized by its volcanic soils and a climate that supports the development of ripe, aromatic grapes. Recioto di Soave is known for its sweet, rich flavors and is typically enjoyed as a dessert wine.
geo:it:recioto-di-soave.style: Sweet dessert wine with rich, concentrated flavors

geo:us:red-mountain-ava.name: Red Mountain
geo:us:red-mountain-ava.description: Red Mountain is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Washington State, known for its distinct terroir characterized by warm days and cool nights. The AVA encompasses approximately 4,000 acres of vineyards, situated on a south-facing slope that benefits from optimal sun exposure. The region is recognized for producing high-quality red wines, particularly from Bordeaux and Rhône grape varieties. Key producers in the area have contributed to its reputation for concentrated and structured wines.
geo:us:red-mountain-ava.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and complexity

geo:de:remstal-stuttgart.name: Remstal-Stuttgart
geo:de:remstal-stuttgart.description: Remstal-Stuttgart is an appellation located within the Württemberg wine region of Germany. It is classified as a Bereich, which denotes a specific area known for its wine production. The region is characterized by its varied topography and favorable climate, which contribute to the cultivation of several grape varieties. The appellation is particularly noted for its red wines, with a focus on local varieties.
geo:de:remstal-stuttgart.style: Red wines with a focus on local varieties

geo:fr:reuilly.name: Reuilly
geo:fr:reuilly.description: Reuilly is an AOC located in the Centre-Loire region of France, recognized for its production of white wines primarily from the Sauvignon Blanc grape. The appellation is situated near the Cher River, benefiting from a continental climate that contributes to the ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of Reuilly encompass several communes, and the wines produced here are characterized by their fresh acidity and aromatic profile.
geo:fr:reuilly.style: Fresh, aromatic white wines primarily from Sauvignon Blanc

geo:de:rheingau.name: Rheingau
geo:de:rheingau.description: One of Germany's most prestigious wine regions, stretching along the Rhine's north bank between Wiesbaden and Lorch. The river's broad sweep creates a natural sun trap, and the Taunus hills shelter vines from cold north winds. Riesling dominates — over 75% of plantings — producing wines that range from bone-dry to lusciously sweet. As a quality wine designation, Rheingau permits the full Prädikat hierarchy from Kabinett through Trockenbeerenauslese.

geo:de:rheingau-qba.name: Rheingau
geo:de:rheingau-qba.description: Rheingau is a prominent wine appellation located in Germany, known for its steep vineyards along the Rhine River. The region is characterized by a continental climate, which contributes to the development of high-quality grapes. It is particularly for its Riesling wines, which exhibit a range of styles from dry to sweet. The Rheingau has a rich viticultural history, with records of wine production dating back to Roman times.
geo:de:rheingau-qba.style: Riesling, with varying levels of sweetness and acidity

geo:de:rheinhessen.name: Rheinhessen
geo:de:rheinhessen.description: Germany's largest wine region by area, long associated with bulk production but undergoing a quality revolution. The Roter Hang (red slope) near Nierstein produces exceptional Riesling from red slate soils. A new generation of growers is proving that Rheinhessen can rival the Rheingau and Pfalz.

geo:de:rheinhessen-qba.name: Rheinhessen
geo:de:rheinhessen-qba.description: Rheinhessen is a prominent wine appellation located in Germany, known for its diverse range of wines. It is situated in the western part of the country, bordered by the Rhine River. The region benefits from a moderate climate and fertile soils, which contribute to the cultivation of various grape varieties. Rheinhessen is recognized for its production of both white and red wines, with a focus on quality and innovation. The appellation encompasses several villages and is characterized by its rolling hills and vineyards.
geo:de:rheinhessen-qba.style: Diverse wines including dry, sweet, and sparkling styles

geo:us:ribbon-ridge.name: Ribbon Ridge
geo:us:ribbon-ridge.description: Ribbon Ridge is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within the Willamette Valley of Oregon. It is characterized by its unique topography, which includes a series of ridges and valleys that influence the microclimates suitable for viticulture. The AVA is primarily known for its production of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, benefiting from the region's cool climate and volcanic soils.
geo:us:ribbon-ridge.style: Red wines with a focus on Pinot Noir

geo:es:ribeira-sacra-do.name: Ribeira Sacra
geo:es:ribeira-sacra-do.description: Ribeira Sacra is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Galicia region of Spain. It is characterized by its steep, terraced vineyards along the Sil and Miño rivers. The region's unique geography and microclimates contribute to the production of high-quality red wines, primarily from the Mencía grape. Ribeira Sacra has a rich historical context, with viticulture dating back to Roman times.
geo:es:ribeira-sacra-do.style: Red wines with a focus on Mencía, showcasing freshness and minerality

geo:es:ribeiro.name: Ribeiro
geo:es:ribeiro.description: Ribeiro is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Galicia region of Spain. It is characterized by its diverse microclimates and varied soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The appellation is primarily known for producing white wines, often showcasing a blend of indigenous grape varieties. Ribeiro has a historical significance dating back to the Roman era, with a long tradition of viticulture.
geo:es:ribeiro.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and aromatic complexity

geo:es:ribera-del-duero-do.name: Ribera del Duero
geo:es:ribera-del-duero-do.description: Spain's most prestigious red wine region after Rioja. Tempranillo (here called Tinto Fino) grows at altitude on the Castilian plateau, producing wines of power and concentration. Vega Sicilia is the legend; Pingus the modern icon. The best wines age magnificently.

geo:es:ribera-del-jucar.name: Ribera del Jucar
geo:es:ribera-del-jucar.description: Ribera del Júcar is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. The appellation is characterized by its diverse terroir, influenced by the Júcar River, which provides a unique microclimate for viticulture. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on both indigenous and international grape varieties. The legal boundaries of the DO encompass several municipalities, promoting a range of wine styles that reflect the local conditions.
geo:es:ribera-del-jucar.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit-forward characteristics

geo:fr:richebourg.name: Richebourg
geo:fr:richebourg.description: Richebourg is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality red wines exclusively from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses a small area, characterized by its limestone-rich soils and favorable microclimate, which contribute to the complexity and depth of its wines.
geo:fr:richebourg.style: Elegant red wines with depth and aging potential

geo:es:rioja.name: Rioja
geo:es:rioja.description: Spain's most famous wine, where Tempranillo meets American oak. Three sub-zones — Rioja Alta, Alavesa, and Baja — offer distinct styles. Traditional Rioja is aged extensively in barrel; modern versions emphasize fruit. Crianza, Reserva, Gran Reserva indicate time in wood and bottle.

geo:es:rioja-doca.name: Rioja
geo:es:rioja-doca.description: Rioja is a designated wine appellation located in northern Spain, established in 1925. It encompasses three primary subregions: Rioja Alta, Rioja Alavesa, and Rioja Oriental. The region is characterized by a diverse climate, influenced by the Ebro River and surrounding mountains, which contributes to a variety of wine styles. Rioja is known for its red wines, primarily produced using Tempranillo, Garnacha, and other varieties, often aged in oak barrels to develop complexity.
geo:es:rioja-doca.style: Red wines with aging potential, often oak-aged

geo:es:rioja-alavesa.name: Rioja Alavesa
geo:es:rioja-alavesa.description: Rioja Alavesa is a subregion of the Rioja wine region in Spain, located in the northern part of the country. It is characterized by its distinct geographical features, including a mix of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the quality of its wines. The climate is influenced by the nearby Cantabrian Mountains, providing a balance of warmth and humidity. Rioja Alavesa is known for producing high-quality red wines, primarily from Tempranillo, with a focus on traditional aging methods in oak barrels.
geo:es:rioja-alavesa.style: Red wines with structure and aging potential, often oak-aged

geo:es:rioja-alta.name: Rioja Alta
geo:es:rioja-alta.description: Rioja Alta is an appellation within the Rioja region of Spain, known for its production of red wines. It is characterized by a diverse range of microclimates and elevations, contributing to the complexity of its wines. The area is situated in the north-central part of Spain, with the Ebro River playing a significant role in its viticulture. Rioja Alta is recognized for its traditional winemaking practices, often involving aging in American oak barrels, which imparts distinctive flavors to the wines. The region's historical significance dates back to the 19th century, when it became known for quality wine production.
geo:es:rioja-alta.style: Red wines with aging potential and oak influence

geo:es:rioja-baja.name: Rioja Baja
geo:es:rioja-baja.description: Rioja Baja is an appellation within the Rioja region of Spain, located in the southern part of the region. It is characterized by a warmer climate compared to its northern counterparts, which influences the ripening of grapes.
