The area is known for its diverse soils, including clay and limestone, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. Rioja Baja primarily focuses on red wine production, with a significant emphasis on traditional winemaking practices.
geo:es:rioja-baja.style: Predominantly red wines with ripe fruit and moderate tannins

geo:fr:rivesaltes.name: Rivesaltes
geo:fr:rivesaltes.description: Rivesaltes is an AOC located in the Roussillon region of France, known for its fortified wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of styles, including natural sweet wines made from grapes that are partially fermented before fortification. Rivesaltes wines are typically produced from Muscat, Grenache, and other regional varieties, benefiting from the region's Mediterranean climate and diverse terroir.
geo:fr:rivesaltes.style: Fortified wines, often sweet and aromatic

geo:it:roero-docg.name: Roero
geo:it:roero-docg.description: Roero is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from the Nebbiolo grape. Roero is characterized by its sandy soils and a climate that benefits from the nearby Tanaro River, which influences the growing conditions. The wines from this region often exhibit a balance of fruit and acidity, with aging potential.
geo:it:roero-docg.style: Red wines, primarily from Nebbiolo, with fruit and acidity balance

geo:it:romagna.name: Romagna
geo:it:romagna.description: Romagna is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. The appellation encompasses a variety of communes and is primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly those made from the Sangiovese grape. The legal boundaries of Romagna are defined by specific geographic areas within the region, allowing for a focus on local terroir and winemaking traditions. The wines produced here often reflect the diverse climatic conditions and soil types found throughout the region.
geo:it:romagna.style: Primarily red wines, often fruit-forward with moderate tannins.

geo:fr:romanee-conti.name: Romanee-Conti
geo:fr:romanee-conti.description: Romanée-Conti is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing some of the most and sought-after Pinot Noir wines in the world. The appellation encompasses a small vineyard area of approximately 1.8 hectares, situated on a gentle slope with a unique terroir characterized by limestone and clay soils. The wines from Romanée-Conti are known for their complexity, depth, and aging potential.
geo:fr:romanee-conti.style: Red wine known for complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:romanee-saint-vivant.name: Romanee-Saint-Vivant
geo:fr:romanee-saint-vivant.description: Romanée-Saint-Vivant is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality red wines exclusively from Pinot Noir grapes. The appellation covers a small area of approximately 8.5 hectares and is situated adjacent to the more famous Romanée-Conti vineyard. The terroir features a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the complexity and elegance of the wines. The wines from this appellation are characterized by their finesse and aromatic depth.
geo:fr:romanee-saint-vivant.style: Elegant red wines with finesse and aromatic complexity

geo:fr:rose-danjou.name: Rose d'Anjou
geo:fr:rose-danjou.description: Rose d'Anjou is an appellation located in the Anjou-Saumur region of France, primarily known for its production of rosé wines. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries that encompass specific vineyard areas within Anjou. It permits a variety of grape types, with a focus on those that contribute to the characteristic light and fruity style of the wines. The terroir features a mix of clay and limestone soils, which influence the wine's profile. Rose d'Anjou is recognized for its off-dry to semi-sweet rosé wines, often made from a blend of Grolleau and Cabernet Franc grapes.
geo:fr:rose-danjou.style: Light, fruity rosé with off-dry to semi-sweet characteristics

geo:fr:rose-de-loire.name: Rose de Loire
geo:fr:rose-de-loire.description: Rose de Loire is an AOC located within the Loire Valley, designated for the production of rosé wines. The appellation encompasses a variety of communes along the Loire River, characterized by its diverse terroir and climate, which influence the style of the wines produced. The regulations governing the appellation stipulate the use of specific grape varieties and production methods to maintain quality and consistency.
geo:fr:rose-de-loire.style: Dry rosé wines with fresh acidity and fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:rosette.name: Rosette
geo:fr:rosette.description: Rosette is an appellation d'origine contrôlée (AOC) located in the South West France region, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is characterized by its production of sweet and semi-sweet wines, often made from a blend of Sémillon, Sauvignon Blanc, and Muscadelle grapes. The terroir benefits from a temperate climate, which aids in the development of the grapes' aromatic qualities and sugar concentration.

geo:it:rosso-conero.name: Rosso Conero
geo:it:rosso-conero.description: Rosso Conero is a red wine appellation located in the Marche region of Italy, classified as a DOC. The appellation is situated on the Adriatic coast, primarily around the Monte Conero area, which provides a unique microclimate influenced by the nearby sea and hills. The legal boundaries of Rosso Conero encompass the municipalities of Ancona, Numana, and Sirolo, among others. The wines produced here are characterized by their robust structure and deep color, often showcasing the region's indigenous grape varieties.
geo:it:rosso-conero.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and moderate tannins

geo:it:rosso-di-montalcino.name: Rosso di Montalcino
geo:it:rosso-di-montalcino.description: Rosso di Montalcino is a red wine appellation located in Tuscany, Italy, classified as a DOC. It is primarily produced from the Sangiovese grape variety, which is the same grape used for the more Brunello di Montalcino. The appellation is situated within the broader Montalcino region, known for its hilly terrain and diverse microclimates that contribute to the wine's character. Rosso di Montalcino wines are typically intended for earlier consumption than their Brunello counterparts, showcasing the fruitiness and vibrancy of Sangiovese.
geo:it:rosso-di-montalcino.style: Fruit-forward red wines with moderate tannins and acidity

geo:it:rosso-di-montepulciano.name: Rosso di Montepulciano
geo:it:rosso-di-montepulciano.description: Rosso di Montepulciano is a red wine appellation located in Tuscany, Italy, classified as a DOC. It is primarily produced from Sangiovese grapes, which thrive in the region's diverse soils and temperate climate. The appellation encompasses the area surrounding the town of Montepulciano, known for its historical significance in wine production dating back to the Etruscans. The wines are typically characterized by their fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins, making them approachable at a young age.
geo:it:rosso-di-montepulciano.style: Fruit-forward red wines with moderate tannins

geo:fr:rose-des-riceys.name: Rosé des Riceys
geo:fr:rose-des-riceys.description: Rosé des Riceys is an AOC located within the Champagne region of France, specifically in the Aube department. This appellation is known for producing rosé wines primarily from the Pinot Noir grape. The legal boundaries of Rosé des Riceys encompass several villages, where the unique terroir contributes to the wine's character. The wines are typically made using traditional methods, with a focus on the saignée technique, which involves bleeding off juice from red wine fermentation to create a rosé.
geo:fr:rose-des-riceys.style: Dry rosé with a focus on fruit and freshness.

geo:fr:roussette-de-savoie.name: Roussette de Savoie
geo:fr:roussette-de-savoie.description: Roussette de Savoie is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, primarily known for producing white wines. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass specific communes within the region. The dominant grape variety permitted for production is Altesse, which is known for its aromatic qualities and ability to express the unique terroir of the area. The wines typically exhibit a balance of acidity and richness, reflecting the alpine climate and diverse soils of Savoie.
geo:fr:roussette-de-savoie.style: White wines with aromatic complexity and balanced acidity

geo:fr:roussette-du-bugey.name: Roussette du Bugey
geo:fr:roussette-du-bugey.description: Roussette du Bugey is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, known for its production of white wines primarily from the Altesse grape. The appellation is situated in the Bugey area, characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate influenced by the nearby Alps. The legal boundaries of Roussette du Bugey encompass specific communes, ensuring a focus on quality and regional identity. The wines produced here are noted for their freshness and aromatic complexity, often reflecting the unique terroir of the region.
geo:fr:roussette-du-bugey.style: Fresh white wines with aromatic complexity

geo:fr:ruchottes-chambertin.name: Ruchottes-Chambertin
geo:fr:ruchottes-chambertin.description: Ruchottes-Chambertin is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is a Grand Cru vineyard known primarily for producing red wines from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation is situated on a slope with a southeast exposure, benefiting from optimal sunlight and drainage. The terroir consists of limestone and clay soils, contributing to the complexity and depth of the wines. Ruchottes-Chambertin is recognized for its elegant structure and aging potential.
geo:fr:ruchottes-chambertin.style: Red wines with elegance and aging potential

geo:it:ruche-di-castagnole-monferrato.name: Ruchè di Castagnole Monferrato
geo:it:ruche-di-castagnole-monferrato.description: Ruchè di Castagnole Monferrato is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Monferrato region of Italy. This appellation is primarily known for its production of red wines made from the Ruchè grape variety. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific communes within the province of Asti, where the unique terroir contributes to the distinctive characteristics of the wines. The climate is characterized by a combination of continental influences and the presence of hills, which provide optimal conditions for grape cultivation. The wines are noted for their aromatic complexity and moderate tannins, reflecting the varietal's unique profile.
geo:it:ruche-di-castagnole-monferrato.style: Aromatic red wines with moderate tannins and floral notes

geo:es:rueda-do.name: Rueda
geo:es:rueda-do.description: Rueda is a designated wine appellation (DO) located in the Castilla y León region of Spain. It is primarily known for its production of white wines, with Verdejo being the dominant grape variety. The appellation is characterized by a continental climate, which contributes to the aromatic profile and acidity of the wines. Rueda's legal boundaries encompass several municipalities, and it has gained recognition for its quality and distinctiveness since its establishment as a DO in 1980.

geo:fr:rully.name: Rully
geo:fr:rully.description: Rully is an AOC located within the Côte Chalonnaise region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for its white wines made from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation is characterized by its limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the minerality and structure of the wines. Rully encompasses several villages and is recognized for both its still and sparkling wines, with a focus on traditional Burgundy winemaking practices.
geo:fr:rully.style: White wines, known for minerality and balance

geo:us:russian-river-valley-ava.name: Russian River Valley
geo:us:russian-river-valley-ava.description: Russian River Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. The region is characterized by its cool climate, influenced by coastal fog and proximity to the Pacific Ocean, which creates ideal conditions for grape growing. The AVA is known primarily for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, with a focus on expressing the unique terroir of the valley. The region encompasses several notable subregions and is recognized for its diverse microclimates and soil types, contributing to a wide range of wine styles.
geo:us:russian-river-valley-ava.style: Red wines, notably Pinot Noir, with some white Chardonnay

geo:us:rutherford-ava.name: Rutherford
geo:us:rutherford-ava.description: Rutherford is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its well-drained alluvial soils and a warm, Mediterranean climate, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. The AVA is known for its distinctive terroir, which includes a mix of gravel, clay, and loam soils, enhancing the complexity of its wines. Rutherford is particularly recognized for Cabernet Sauvignon, which thrives in this region, alongside other varietals such as Merlot and Cabernet Franc.

geo:au:rutherglen-gi.name: Rutherglen
geo:au:rutherglen-gi.description: Rutherglen is a designated Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia. It is for its production of fortified wines, particularly those made from Muscat and Muscadelle grapes. The region's warm climate and fertile soils contribute to the rich, concentrated flavors characteristic of its wines.
geo:au:rutherglen-gi.style: Known for rich fortified wines, especially Muscat varieties

geo:fr:regnie.name: Régnié
geo:fr:regnie.description: Régnié is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known for producing red wines primarily from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated in the northern part of Beaujolais, characterized by its granite soils and a climate that is influenced by the nearby Monts du Beaujolais. Régnié was granted AOC status in 1988 and is one of the ten Beaujolais Crus, which are recognized for their distinct terroirs and higher quality wines. The wines of Régnié typically exhibit fresh fruit flavors and a light to medium body, reflecting the characteristics of the Gamay grape.
geo:fr:regnie.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with fresh fruit flavors

geo:es:rias-baixas-do.name: Rías Baixas
geo:es:rias-baixas-do.description: Albariño's home, on Galicia's Atlantic coast. The granite pergolas and misty climate produce wines of salinity and freshness — perfect with the region's seafood. Modern winemaking has revealed Albariño's complexity. Spain's most distinctive white wine.

geo:fr:saint-amour.name: Saint-Amour
geo:fr:saint-amour.description: Saint-Amour is an AOC located in the Beaujolais region of France, known primarily for producing red wines from the Gamay grape. The appellation is situated to the north of the Beaujolais region, characterized by its rolling hills and granite soils. It is one of the ten crus of Beaujolais, recognized for its ability to produce wines with both fruit-forward characteristics and complexity. The wines of Saint-Amour are often noted for their floral and berry notes, reflecting the terroir of the area.
geo:fr:saint-amour.style: Red wines with floral and berry characteristics

geo:fr:saint-aubin.name: Saint-Aubin
geo:fr:saint-aubin.description: Saint-Aubin is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. The appellation is primarily known for its white wines made from Chardonnay, which are produced in both village and premier cru classifications. The vineyards are situated on the slopes of the Côte d'Or, benefiting from a favorable microclimate and diverse soil types that contribute to the wines' character. The wines of Saint-Aubin often exhibit a balance of freshness and complexity, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:saint-aubin.style: White wines with freshness and complexity

geo:fr:saint-bris.name: Saint-Bris
geo:fr:saint-bris.description: Saint-Bris is an AOC located in the Burgundy region of France, specifically known for producing white wines. The appellation is situated near the town of Chablis and is notable for its unique terroir, which includes limestone and clay soils. Established as an AOC in 2003, Saint-Bris primarily focuses on Sauvignon Blanc and Sauvignon Gris, making it one of the few Burgundy appellations to produce wines from these grape varieties. The climate is continental, contributing to the freshness and acidity of the wines.
geo:fr:saint-bris.style: Fresh, aromatic white wines with high acidity

geo:fr:saint-chinian.name: Saint-Chinian
geo:fr:saint-chinian.description: Saint-Chinian is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 1982. The appellation encompasses a variety of terroirs, including schist, clay, and limestone soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The climate is Mediterranean, characterized by hot summers and mild winters, facilitating the growth of a range of grape varieties. Saint-Chinian primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre.
geo:fr:saint-chinian.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:fr:saint-chinian-berlou.name: Saint-Chinian-Berlou
geo:fr:saint-chinian-berlou.description: Saint-Chinian-Berlou is an AOC located within the Languedoc region of France, known for its production of red wines. The appellation is characterized by its varied terroir, which includes schist and clay soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, allowing for optimal ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass specific areas within the larger Saint-Chinian AOC, focusing on the production of robust red wines primarily from selected grape varieties.
geo:fr:saint-chinian-berlou.style: Full-bodied red wines with depth and structure

geo:fr:saint-chinian-roquebrun.name: Saint-Chinian-Roquebrun
geo:fr:saint-chinian-roquebrun.description: Saint-Chinian-Roquebrun is an AOC located within the Languedoc region of France, recognized for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Roquebrun, characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes schist soils and a Mediterranean climate. The wines produced here often reflect the influence of the nearby Orb River, contributing to the complexity of the wines. The AOC was established in 2011, distinguishing itself from the broader Saint-Chinian appellation.
geo:fr:saint-chinian-roquebrun.style: Red wines with complexity and structure, often featuring dark fruit flavors

geo:fr:saint-estephe.name: Saint-Estèphe
geo:fr:saint-estephe.description: Saint-Estèphe is an AOC located in the Médoc region of Bordeaux, France. It is situated north of Pauillac and is known for its gravelly soils, which contribute to the production of robust red wines. The appellation is characterized by a maritime climate, influenced by its proximity to the Gironde estuary. Saint-Estèphe is recognized for its full-bodied wines that often exhibit aging potential.
geo:fr:saint-estephe.style: Full-bodied red wines with aging potential

geo:fr:saint-georges-saint-emilion.name: Saint-Georges-Saint-Émilion
geo:fr:saint-georges-saint-emilion.description: Saint-Georges-Saint-Émilion is an AOC located within the Bordeaux wine region, specifically in the Right Bank. It is situated just north of the more famous Saint-Émilion appellation. The area is characterized by its clay-limestone soils and a temperate maritime climate, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. The appellation is known for its small size and the production of predominantly Merlot-based blends, often with Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and structure, reflecting the unique terroir of the region.

geo:fr:saint-joseph.name: Saint-Joseph
geo:fr:saint-joseph.description: A long ribbon of granite hillsides producing some of the Northern Rhône's best values. The wines combine Syrah's dark fruit with a peppery freshness that makes them approachable young. Expansion in the 1990s diluted quality, but the best growers — on the original granite slopes — make profound wine.

geo:fr:saint-julien.name: Saint-Julien
geo:fr:saint-julien.description: The Médoc's most consistent appellation. No First Growths here, but an unusual concentration of excellent estates — Léoville Las Cases, Ducru-Beaucaillou, Gruaud Larose. Saint-Julien wines split the difference between Margaux's elegance and Pauillac's power, aging gracefully for decades.

geo:fr:saint-nicolas-de-bourgueil.name: Saint-Nicolas-de-Bourgueil
geo:fr:saint-nicolas-de-bourgueil.description: Saint-Nicolas-de-Bourgueil is an AOC located in the Touraine region of France, primarily known for its red wines made from Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Loire River, characterized by its varied soils, including clay and limestone, which contribute to the wine's complexity. The climate is continental, with warm summers and cold winters, allowing for the development of ripe fruit flavors in the grapes. The AOC was officially recognized in 1937 and is known for producing wines that are approachable in their youth yet capable of aging.
geo:fr:saint-nicolas-de-bourgueil.style: Red wines, primarily from Cabernet Franc, often fruit-forward and approachable

geo:fr:saint-peray.name: Saint-Peray
geo:fr:saint-peray.description: Saint-Peray is an AOC located in the Northern Rhône region of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Rhône River, near the town of Valence. It encompasses a small area characterized by steep slopes and a mix of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the distinctive qualities of its wines. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, facilitating the ripening of grapes. Saint-Peray is recognized for its production of both still and sparkling white wines, with a focus on the Marsanne and Roussanne grape varieties.
geo:fr:saint-peray.style: White wines, both still and sparkling, with a focus on Marsanne and Roussanne

geo:fr:saint-romain.name: Saint-Romain
geo:fr:saint-romain.description: Saint-Romain is an AOC located within the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. The appellation is situated on a hillside overlooking the village of Saint-Romain, benefiting from a favorable southeast exposure. The terroir consists primarily of limestone and clay soils, which contribute to the character of the wines produced here. The region is known for its white wines, predominantly made from Chardonnay, which exhibit a balance of freshness and minerality.
geo:fr:saint-romain.style: White wines with freshness and minerality, primarily from Chardonnay

geo:fr:saint-veran.name: Saint-Véran
geo:fr:saint-veran.description: Saint-Véran is an AOC located in the Mâconnais region of France, primarily known for producing white wines made from Chardonnay grapes. The appellation is situated to the south of the more famous Pouilly-Fuissé and encompasses several communes, including Saint-Véran, Chânes, and Prissé. The vineyards benefit from a continental climate, with a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the character of the wines. Saint-Véran wines are typically characterized by their freshness and minerality, reflecting the unique terroir of the region.
geo:fr:saint-veran.style: Fresh, mineral-driven white wines from Chardonnay

geo:fr:saint-emilion.name: Saint-Émilion
geo:fr:saint-emilion.description: Bordeaux's Right Bank jewel, where Merlot takes center stage. The limestone plateau and clay slopes produce wines of generous fruit and supple texture — more approachable young than Left Bank Cabernet. Cheval Blanc and Ausone lead a classification that's reclassified every decade.

geo:fr:saint-emilion-grand-cru.name: Saint-Émilion Grand Cru
geo:fr:saint-emilion-grand-cru.description: Saint-Émilion Grand Cru is an AOC located within the Bordeaux region of France, known for its high-quality red wines. The appellation encompasses a specific area around the town of Saint-Émilion and is characterized by its limestone plateau, clay, and gravel soils. The wines produced here must meet stricter regulations than those of the broader Saint-Émilion AOC, including lower yields and higher minimum alcohol levels. This designation is recognized for its historical significance, with viticulture in the area dating back to Roman times.
geo:fr:saint-emilion-grand-cru.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and structured tannins

geo:fr:sainte-croix-du-mont.name: Sainte-Croix-du-Mont
geo:fr:sainte-croix-du-mont.description: Sainte-Croix-du-Mont is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, specifically on the right bank of the Garonne River. This appellation is for its sweet dessert wines, primarily produced from grapes affected by noble rot, which enhances their sweetness and complexity. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several communes, with a focus on the unique terroir that includes clay and limestone soils, contributing to the distinctive character of the wines.
geo:fr:sainte-croix-du-mont.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich flavors and high acidity

geo:it:salento.name: Salento
geo:it:salento.description: Salento is an appellation located in the Puglia region of Italy, known primarily for its red wines. The area is characterized by a Mediterranean climate, with hot, dry summers and mild winters, which contributes to the ripening of grapes. Salento encompasses a variety of landscapes, including coastal areas and inland hills, which influence the viticulture practices. The appellation is recognized for its use of indigenous grape varieties, particularly in the production of robust red wines.
geo:it:salento.style: Robust red wines with rich fruit and moderate tannins

geo:it:salice-salentino.name: Salice Salentino
geo:it:salice-salentino.description: Salice Salentino is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Puglia region of Italy. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly made from the Negroamaro grape. The appellation encompasses several municipalities and is characterized by a warm Mediterranean climate, which contributes to the ripening of the grapes and the development of the wine's flavors.
geo:it:salice-salentino.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and moderate tannins

geo:ar:san-rafael.name: San Rafael
geo:ar:san-rafael.description: San Rafael is a designated appellation within the Mendoza region of Argentina, classified as a DOC. It is situated in the southern part of Mendoza, characterized by a diverse topography and a semi-arid climate that influences viticulture. The region is known for its high-altitude vineyards, which contribute to the quality of the grapes produced. San Rafael primarily focuses on red wine production, with a notable emphasis on varieties such as Malbec and Cabernet Sauvignon.
geo:ar:san-rafael.style: Red wines with a focus on Malbec and Cabernet Sauvignon

geo:fr:sancerre.name: Sancerre
geo:fr:sancerre.description: Sauvignon Blanc's French benchmark. The hills overlooking the Loire produce wines of piercing purity — citrus, flint, and herbs in perfect balance. Different soils (silex, limestone, clay) create distinct styles, but all share that unmistakable Sancerre tension. Light reds from Pinot Noir are increasingly notable.

geo:us:santa-barbara-county.name: Santa Barbara County
geo:us:santa-barbara-county.description: Santa Barbara County is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, known for its diverse microclimates and varied topography. The region's proximity to the Pacific Ocean influences its climate, creating conditions suitable for a range of grape varieties. The AVA encompasses several notable subregions, including Santa Maria Valley and Santa Ynez Valley, each contributing distinct characteristics to the wines produced. The county has a growing reputation for high-quality white wines, particularly from Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc.
geo:us:santa-barbara-county.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and acidity

geo:us:santa-lucia-highlands-ava.name: Santa Lucia Highlands
geo:us:santa-lucia-highlands-ava.description: Santa Lucia Highlands is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Monterey County, California. Established in 1991, it is known for its distinct microclimates created by the nearby Pacific Ocean and the Santa Lucia Mountains. The region's elevation and varied topography contribute to its reputation for producing high-quality Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, among other varietals.
geo:us:santa-lucia-highlands-ava.style: Notable for cool-climate varietals, especially Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

geo:us:santa-maria-valley-ava.name: Santa Maria Valley
geo:us:santa-maria-valley-ava.description: Santa Maria Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Santa Barbara County, California. It is characterized by a transverse valley that runs east-west, allowing for cool ocean breezes and fog to influence the climate, which is conducive to viticulture. The region is known for its diverse soil types and microclimates, contributing to the production of high-quality wines, particularly from the Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grape varieties.

geo:us:santa-ynez-valley-ava.name: Santa Ynez Valley
geo:us:santa-ynez-valley-ava.description: Santa Ynez Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Santa Barbara County, California. The region is characterized by its diverse microclimates and varied topography, which contribute to a wide range of wine styles. The valley runs east to west, allowing for significant ocean influence, particularly from the Pacific Ocean. This AVA is known for producing both red and white wines, with a focus on varietals that thrive in the region's climate.
geo:us:santa-ynez-valley-ava.style: Diverse wines with a focus on white and red varietals

geo:fr:santenay.name: Santenay
geo:fr:santenay.description: Santenay is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. It is primarily known for producing red wines from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses several villages and vineyards, characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the wine's structure and complexity. Santenay wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and earthiness, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:fr:santenay.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and earthy undertones

geo:fr:saumur.name: Saumur
geo:fr:saumur.description: Saumur is an AOC located in the Anjou-Saumur region of France, primarily known for its white wines made from the Chenin Blanc grape. The appellation is situated along the Loire River, benefiting from a continental climate that influences its viticulture. Saumur encompasses several communes and is characterized by its limestone soils, which contribute to the minerality of the wines. The region also produces sparkling wines under the Saumur Mousseux designation, showcasing the versatility of its terroir.
geo:fr:saumur.style: White wines with a focus on Chenin Blanc

geo:fr:saumur-aoc.name: Saumur
geo:fr:saumur-aoc.description: Saumur is an appellation located in the Anjou-Saumur region of France, primarily known for its production of sparkling wines made from Chenin Blanc and Cabernet Franc. The appellation is defined by its limestone soils and a temperate climate, which contribute to the unique characteristics of its wines. Saumur is recognized for both its still and sparkling wines, with a focus on fresh acidity and minerality.
geo:fr:saumur-aoc.style: Sparkling wines, with some still varieties

geo:fr:saumur-mousseux.name: Saumur Mousseux
geo:fr:saumur-mousseux.description: Saumur Mousseux is an AOC in the Saumur region of France, known for its production of sparkling wines. The appellation is primarily associated with the traditional method of sparkling wine production, utilizing secondary fermentation in the bottle. It encompasses a variety of terroirs that contribute to the distinct characteristics of the wines produced within its boundaries.
geo:fr:saumur-mousseux.style: Sparkling wines made primarily from Chenin Blanc and Chardonnay

geo:fr:saumur-champigny.name: Saumur-Champigny
geo:fr:saumur-champigny.description: Saumur-Champigny is an AOC located in the Anjou-Saumur region of France, primarily known for its red wines made from Cabernet Franc. The appellation is situated on the right bank of the Loire River, characterized by its limestone soils and a temperate climate that contributes to the ripening of grapes. The legal boundaries of Saumur-Champigny encompass several communes, including Saumur, and the wines produced here are typically noted for their fruit-forward character and moderate tannins. The region has a historical significance dating back to the Roman era, with viticulture being an integral part of its cultural heritage.
geo:fr:saumur-champigny.style: Red wines, primarily from Cabernet Franc, with fruit-forward profiles

geo:fr:saussignac.name: Saussignac
geo:fr:saussignac.description: Saussignac is an AOC located in the South West France region, known for its production of fortified wines. The appellation is situated near the village of Saussignac, characterized by its unique terroir that supports the cultivation of specific grape varieties. The wines produced here are typically sweet and are made using traditional methods of fortification.
geo:fr:saussignac.style: Fortified sweet wines with a focus on Sémillon

geo:fr:sauternes.name: Sauternes
geo:fr:sauternes.description: Sauternes is an AOC located in the Bordeaux region of France, for its sweet dessert wines. The appellation is defined by its unique microclimate, which fosters the development of Botrytis cinerea, or noble rot, on the grapes. This process enhances the concentration of sugars and flavors in the fruit, primarily Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc, leading to the production of rich, complex wines. The legal boundaries of Sauternes encompass several communes, with Barsac being a notable subregion that can also produce wines under its own appellation.
geo:fr:sauternes.style: Sweet dessert wines with rich, complex flavors

geo:fr:savennieres-coulee-de-serrant.name: Savennieres-Coulee de Serrant
geo:fr:savennieres-coulee-de-serrant.description: Savennieres-Coulee de Serrant is an AOC located within the Anjou-Saumur region of France, known for its production of white wines primarily from the Chenin Blanc grape. The appellation encompasses a single vineyard, which is classified as a monopole and is recognized for its unique terroir characterized by schist soils and a microclimate that contributes to the complexity of the wines. The wines produced here are noted for their ability to age and often exhibit a range of flavors from floral to mineral, reflecting the distinct characteristics of the site.
geo:fr:savennieres-coulee-de-serrant.style: White wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:savennieres-roche-aux-moines.name: Savennieres-Roche aux Moines
geo:fr:savennieres-roche-aux-moines.description: Savennieres-Roche aux Moines is an AOC located within the Anjou-Saumur region of France, known primarily for its production of white wines made from the Chenin Blanc grape. The appellation is characterized by its unique terroir, which includes schist and volcanic soils that contribute to the complexity of the wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation are defined, and it is recognized for producing dry, age-worthy wines that exhibit a range of flavors and aromas.
geo:fr:savennieres-roche-aux-moines.style: Dry white wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:savennieres.name: Savennières
geo:fr:savennieres.description: Chenin Blanc at its most austere and age-worthy. The schist slopes above the Loire produce bone-dry wines of remarkable intensity — steely when young, honeyed with decades of age. Coulée de Serrant and Roche aux Moines are the legendary vineyards.

geo:fr:savigny-les-beaune.name: Savigny-lès-Beaune
geo:fr:savigny-les-beaune.description: Savigny-lès-Beaune is an AOC located in the Côte de Beaune region of Burgundy, France. The appellation is primarily known for its red wines made from Pinot Noir, which thrive in the region's clay-limestone soils. The vineyards are situated on the slopes of the Côte d'Or, benefiting from a continental climate that contributes to the complexity of the wines. Savigny-lès-Beaune encompasses several lieux-dits, each offering distinct terroir characteristics that influence the wine's profile.
geo:fr:savigny-les-beaune.style: Red wines with elegance and finesse, showcasing red fruit and earthy notes

geo:de:schloss-bockelheim.name: Schloss Bockelheim
geo:de:schloss-bockelheim.description: Schloss Bockelheim is an appellation located within the Nahe wine region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by its diverse soil types and varied microclimates, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced. The appellation primarily focuses on white wine production, with a particular emphasis on Riesling and Müller-Thurgau. The legal boundaries of Schloss Bockelheim encompass several vineyards known for their distinct terroir and favorable growing conditions.
geo:de:schloss-bockelheim.style: White wines with a focus on Riesling and Müller-Thurgau

geo:pt:setubal-doc.name: Setúbal
geo:pt:setubal-doc.description: Setúbal is a demarcated wine region located on the Setúbal Peninsula in Portugal. It is known for its production of fortified wines, particularly Muscat of Setúbal, which is made primarily from the Muscat grape. The region's climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, contributing to the ripening of grapes and the development of distinctive flavors. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several municipalities, with a focus on the areas around the town of Setúbal. The wines from this appellation are characterized by their rich sweetness and aromatic complexity.
geo:pt:setubal-doc.style: Fortified and sweet wines, primarily from Muscat grapes

geo:fr:seyssel.name: Seyssel
geo:fr:seyssel.description: Seyssel is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, known for its production of white wines. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Rhône River, characterized by its alpine climate and diverse soil types, which contribute to the unique terroir. Seyssel is notable for its use of local grape varieties, particularly Altesse and Molette, and has a history of winemaking that dates back to the Roman era. The wines produced here often exhibit a fresh and mineral character, reflecting the region's geographical features.
geo:fr:seyssel.style: Fresh, mineral white wines with local grape varieties

geo:it:sforzato-di-valtellina.name: Sforzato di Valtellina
geo:it:sforzato-di-valtellina.description: Sforzato di Valtellina is a red wine appellation located in the Valtellina region of Lombardy, Italy. It is classified as a DOCG, the highest designation for Italian wines, and is known for its production of dry red wines primarily made from the Nebbiolo grape. The appellation's wines are characterized by their rich flavors and complexity, resulting from the unique terroir and the appassimento process, where grapes are dried before fermentation.
geo:it:sforzato-di-valtellina.style: Rich, complex red wines made from dried Nebbiolo grapes

geo:it:sicilia.name: Sicilia
geo:it:sicilia.description: Sicilia is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) established in 2012, encompassing the entire island. It permits a wide range of wine styles — red, white, and rosé — reflecting the island's extraordinary grape diversity. Nero d'Avola anchors the reds; Grillo and Catarratto drive the whites. The Mediterranean climate, with hot summers and mild winters, supports cultivation from sea level to Etna's volcanic slopes.
geo:it:sicilia.style: Red, white, and rosé wines with diverse varietal expressions

geo:es:sierras-de-malaga.name: Sierras de Málaga
geo:es:sierras-de-malaga.description: Sierras de Málaga is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Andalusia region of Spain. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain and diverse microclimates, which contribute to the unique viticultural conditions of the area. The appellation is known for producing primarily red wines, with a focus on both traditional and international grape varieties. The region's historical significance is tied to its long-standing winemaking heritage, which has evolved over centuries.
geo:es:sierras-de-malaga.style: Red wines with a focus on local and international varieties

geo:it:soave.name: Soave
geo:it:soave.description: Soave is a white wine appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy, recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC). The appellation is primarily known for its production of wines made from the Garganega grape, which thrives in the region's volcanic soils. Soave wines are characterized by their crisp acidity and floral notes, often exhibiting mineral qualities due to the terroir. The legal boundaries of the Soave DOC encompass several communes, with Soave and Monteforte d'Alpone being the most prominent.
geo:it:soave.style: Crisp white wines with floral and mineral characteristics

geo:it:soave-classico.name: Soave Classico
geo:it:soave-classico.description: Soave Classico is a designated appellation within the Soave DOC in the Veneto region of Italy. It is characterized by its historical production area, which includes the hillside vineyards surrounding the town of Soave. The terroir features volcanic soils and a climate that is influenced by the nearby Lessini Mountains, contributing to the distinctiveness of the wines produced here.
geo:it:soave-classico.style: Crisp white wines with floral and citrus notes

geo:es:somontano-do.name: Somontano
geo:es:somontano-do.description: Somontano is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the province of Huesca, within the autonomous community of Aragón, Spain. Established in 1984, it is characterized by a diverse range of microclimates and soils, which contribute to the production of both red and white wines. The region is situated at the foot of the Pyrenees, benefiting from a continental climate with significant temperature variations between day and night, enhancing the ripening of grapes.
geo:es:somontano-do.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and balanced acidity

geo:us:sonoma-coast.name: Sonoma Coast
geo:us:sonoma-coast.description: A vast appellation that includes everything from warm inland valleys to extreme coastal sites. The 'true' Sonoma Coast — the fog-swept ridges near the ocean — produces Pinot Noir and Chardonnay of startling purity. Littorai and Hirsch are benchmarks.

geo:us:sonoma-coast-ava.name: Sonoma Coast
geo:us:sonoma-coast-ava.description: Sonoma Coast is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. It encompasses a diverse range of microclimates and topographies due to its proximity to the Pacific Ocean. The AVA is known for its cool climate, which is influenced by coastal fog and breezes, making it suitable for growing a variety of grape types. The region is characterized by its rugged coastline, rolling hills, and varied soil types, contributing to the complexity of its wines.
geo:us:sonoma-coast-ava.style: Cool-climate wines with emphasis on Chardonnay and Pinot Noir

geo:us:sonoma-county.name: Sonoma County
geo:us:sonoma-county.description: Sonoma County is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in California, known for its diverse microclimates and varied topography. The region encompasses a range of geographical features, including coastal mountains, valleys, and rivers, contributing to its suitability for a wide array of grape varieties. It is recognized for producing high-quality red wines, particularly from varietals such as Pinot Noir and Zinfandel, as well as notable white wines.
geo:us:sonoma-county.style: Diverse red wines with a focus on Pinot Noir and Zinfandel

geo:us:sonoma-valley-ava.name: Sonoma Valley
geo:us:sonoma-valley-ava.description: Sonoma Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Sonoma County, California. Established in 1981, it encompasses a diverse range of microclimates and soils, contributing to a wide variety of wine styles. The AVA is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on varietals such as Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay, and Zinfandel. The valley is bordered by the Mayacamas Mountains to the east and the Sonoma Mountains to the west, which influence its climate and viticultural practices.
geo:us:sonoma-valley-ava.style: Diverse wines with a focus on both red and white varietals.

geo:us:spring-mountain.name: Spring Mountain
geo:us:spring-mountain.description: Spring Mountain is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain and elevation, which contribute to a unique microclimate that is cooler than the valley floor. The AVA is known for its well-drained volcanic and sedimentary soils, which support the cultivation of high-quality grapes. The region primarily produces red wines, with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, among others.
geo:us:spring-mountain.style: Red wines with a focus on structured and age-worthy profiles

geo:us:spring-mountain-district.name: Spring Mountain District
geo:us:spring-mountain-district.description: Spring Mountain District is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain and elevation, which contribute to a distinct microclimate suitable for viticulture. The AVA is known for its focus on red wine production, particularly from Bordeaux varieties, and encompasses several notable wineries. The region's steep slopes and varied soil types enhance the complexity of the wines produced here.
geo:us:spring-mountain-district.style: Red wines with a focus on Bordeaux varietals

geo:us:st-helena-ava.name: St. Helena
geo:us:st-helena-ava.description: St. Helena is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. The appellation is characterized by its warm climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality red wines. St. Helena is known for its historic vineyards and a concentration of wineries that emphasize Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Zinfandel. The area has a rich viticultural history dating back to the 19th century, making it significant in the development of California wine.
geo:us:st-helena-ava.style: Red wines with a focus on bold, fruit-forward profiles

geo:us:sta-rita-hills-ava.name: Sta. Rita Hills
geo:us:sta-rita-hills-ava.description: One of California's coolest appellations, where Pacific fog funnels through the transverse ranges. Pinot Noir reaches Burgundian elegance here; Chardonnay shows uncommon freshness. Sanford & Benedict Vineyard is the legend; young producers continue to impress.

geo:us:stags-leap-district-ava.name: Stags Leap District
geo:us:stags-leap-district-ava.description: Stags Leap District is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. Established in 1989, it is known for its distinctive terroir, characterized by its volcanic soil and varied microclimates. The region is primarily recognized for producing high-quality Cabernet Sauvignon, which often exhibits a unique balance of fruit and structure. The AVA is bounded by the Stags Leap Palisades to the east and the Napa River to the west, contributing to its distinct growing conditions.
geo:us:stags-leap-district-ava.style: Predominantly red wines with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon

geo:at:steiermark-dac.name: Steiermark
geo:at:steiermark-dac.description: Steiermark is an Austrian wine appellation located in the southeastern part of the country. It is known for its diverse terroir, which includes rolling hills and a mix of limestone and clay soils. The region benefits from a continental climate with warm summers and cold winters, contributing to the development of distinct wine styles. Key grape varieties include Sauvignon Blanc, Welschriesling, and Pinot Blanc, which thrive in this environment. The appellation is recognized for producing high-quality white wines, often characterized by their freshness and aromatic complexity.
geo:at:steiermark-dac.style: Fresh white wines with aromatic profiles

geo:de:steigerwald.name: Steigerwald
geo:de:steigerwald.description: The Steigerwald appellation is located within the Franken region of Germany and is classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by its distinct terroir, which includes limestone and clay soils, contributing to the unique expression of its wines. The climate is continental, with warm summers and cold winters, allowing for the cultivation of specific grape varieties suited to these conditions. Steigerwald is primarily known for its white wines, which reflect the regional viticultural practices.
geo:de:steigerwald.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and minerality

geo:za:stellenbosch.name: Stellenbosch
geo:za:stellenbosch.description: South Africa's Napa, where mountain slopes produce Cabernet of real distinction. The Cape's oldest wine region combines historic estates with modern ambition. Bordeaux blends dominate, but Chenin Blanc and Shiraz impress. The view of Table Mountain doesn't hurt.

geo:za:stellenbosch-wo.name: Stellenbosch
geo:za:stellenbosch-wo.description: Stellenbosch is an appellation located in the Western Cape of South Africa, known for its diverse terroir and significant wine production. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate with warm, dry summers and cool, wet winters, which supports a variety of grape cultivation. The region is home to several prominent wine estates and has a reputation for producing high-quality red and white wines, particularly from Bordeaux and Rhône varietals.
geo:za:stellenbosch-wo.style: Diverse wines with a focus on red blends and quality whites

geo:de:sudliche-weinstrasse.name: Sudliche Weinstrasse
geo:de:sudliche-weinstrasse.description: Sudliche Weinstrasse is an appellation located in the Pfalz region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by its warm climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. The area is known for its picturesque vineyards and is situated along the southern wine route, which enhances its appeal for viticulture. The appellation primarily produces white wines, with a focus on varietals that thrive in the region's conditions.
geo:de:sudliche-weinstrasse.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and fruit expression

geo:it:sulcis.name: Sulcis
geo:it:sulcis.description: Sulcis is a red wine appellation located in the southwestern part of Sardinia, Italy. It encompasses several municipalities and is characterized by a Mediterranean climate with influences from the surrounding sea. The region is known for its diverse soils, including clay and sandy textures, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The appellation is recognized for producing robust red wines, often with a focus on indigenous grape varieties.
geo:it:sulcis.style: Robust red wines with complexity and regional character

geo:it:suvereto.name: Suvereto
geo:it:suvereto.description: Suvereto is a DOCG appellation located in the Tuscany region of Italy, recognized for its production of red wines. The appellation is situated in the province of Livorno, characterized by a hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate that influences the viticulture. Suvereto's regulations mandate the use of specific grape varieties, ensuring the quality and identity of its wines. The area has a historical significance in winemaking, with a focus on both traditional and modern techniques.
geo:it:suvereto.style: Red wines with a focus on structure and complexity

geo:za:swartland-wo.name: Swartland
geo:za:swartland-wo.description: South Africa's wine revolution started here. Old-bush-vine Chenin Blanc, Mediterranean varieties, minimal intervention — the Swartland Independent movement challenged convention. Eben Sadie led the way; dozens of young producers follow. The Cape's most exciting region.

geo:it:taurasi.name: Taurasi
geo:it:taurasi.description: Taurasi is a DOCG appellation located in the Campania region of Italy, specifically in the province of Avellino. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Aglianico grape, which is considered one of Italy's noble varieties. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and volcanic soils, contributing to the complexity and aging potential of its wines. The legal boundaries of Taurasi encompass several municipalities, and the wines must adhere to strict production regulations to qualify for the DOCG designation.
geo:it:taurasi.style: Full-bodied red wines with aging potential

geo:fr:tavel.name: Tavel
geo:fr:tavel.description: Tavel is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, recognized for its production of rosé wines. The appellation is situated on the left bank of the Rhône River, near the town of Tavel, and benefits from a Mediterranean climate characterized by warm summers and mild winters. The terroir consists of a mix of clay, limestone, and rolled pebbles, which contribute to the distinctive character of the wines. Tavel is notable for being one of the few regions in France that exclusively produces rosé, with a focus on full-bodied and robust styles.
geo:fr:tavel.style: Full-bodied rosé with depth and complexity

geo:pt:tavira.name: Tavira
geo:pt:tavira.description: The Tavira appellation is located in the Algarve region of Portugal and is classified as a Denominação de Origem Controlada (DOC). It is known primarily for its red wines, which are produced from indigenous grape varieties. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters, which influences the ripening of the grapes.
geo:pt:tavira.style: Red wines with regional character and indigenous varietals

geo:pt:tejo-doc.name: Tejo
geo:pt:tejo-doc.description: Tejo is an appellation located in central Portugal, defined by the Tejo River. The region benefits from a Mediterranean climate, with hot summers and mild winters, which supports a variety of grape cultivation. The legal boundaries of Tejo encompass several municipalities, and it is known for both red and white wines that reflect the diverse terroir. The region has a history of viticulture dating back to Roman times, contributing to its established wine culture.
geo:pt:tejo-doc.style: Wines range from fresh whites to structured reds, often with good acidity

geo:it:teroldego-rotaliano.name: Teroldego Rotaliano
geo:it:teroldego-rotaliano.description: Teroldego Rotaliano is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Trentino-Alto Adige region of Italy. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass the Rotaliano plain near the town of Mezzolombardo. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Teroldego grape variety, which thrives in the region's alluvial soils and favorable climate. The wines typically exhibit a deep color and a range of fruit-forward characteristics, reflecting the terroir of the area.
geo:it:teroldego-rotaliano.style: Red wines with deep color and fruit-forward characteristics

geo:es:terra-alta-do.name: Terra Alta
geo:es:terra-alta-do.description: Terra Alta is a designated wine appellation located in the province of Tarragona, Catalonia, Spain. It is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes a mix of limestone and clay soils, and a climate that is influenced by both Mediterranean and continental elements. The region is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The appellation's boundaries encompass several municipalities, and it has a history of viticulture dating back to ancient times.
geo:es:terra-alta-do.style: Wines are typically full-bodied with balanced acidity

geo:fr:terrasses-du-larzac-aoc.name: Terrasses du Larzac
geo:fr:terrasses-du-larzac-aoc.description: Terrasses du Larzac is an AOC located in the Languedoc region of France, established in 2014. The appellation encompasses a diverse range of terroirs characterized by limestone and clay soils, situated on the terraces of the Larzac plateau. It is known for producing predominantly red wines, which are often blends of various grape varieties. The climate is Mediterranean, with significant temperature variations between day and night, contributing to the complexity of the wines.
geo:fr:terrasses-du-larzac-aoc.style: Full-bodied red wines with complexity and structure

geo:it:terre-alfieri.name: Terre Alfieri
geo:it:terre-alfieri.description: Terre Alfieri is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Piedmont region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for producing red wines, with Nebbiolo being the dominant grape variety. The region's terroir, characterized by its hilly terrain and diverse soil types, contributes to the complexity of the wines. The DOCG status indicates a high level of quality and adherence to strict production regulations.
geo:it:terre-alfieri.style: Red wines with complexity and structure, primarily from Nebbiolo

geo:it:terre-dei-vestini-doc.name: Terre dei Vestini
geo:it:terre-dei-vestini-doc.description: Terre dei Vestini is an Indicazione Geografica Tipica (IGT) appellation located in the Abruzzo region of Italy. It encompasses a diverse range of vineyards that produce primarily red wines. The legal boundaries of the appellation allow for various grape varieties, with a focus on local cultivars. The terroir is characterized by hilly terrain and a Mediterranean climate, which contribute to the wines' distinctive profiles.
geo:it:terre-dei-vestini-doc.style: Red wines with a focus on Montepulciano and Trebbiano.

geo:it:terre-siciliane.name: Terre Siciliane
geo:it:terre-siciliane.description: Sicily's island-wide IGT designation, the most flexible appellation on the island. Covers red, white, and rosé in every style — from everyday drinking to ambitious single-vineyard bottlings that don't fit DOC rules. Many producers choose Terre Siciliane deliberately, preferring creative freedom over classification prestige. International varieties thrive alongside indigenous grapes.
geo:it:terre-siciliane.style: Diverse styles — red, white, and rosé — reflecting Sicily's full viticultural breadth

geo:at:thermenregion-dac.name: Thermenregion
geo:at:thermenregion-dac.description: Thermenregion is a designated Austrian DAC located within the Niederösterreich region. It is characterized by a unique combination of warm climate and diverse soil types, which contribute to the distinctiveness of its wines. The appellation is particularly noted for its white wines, primarily produced from the Zierfandler and Rotgipfler grape varieties, as well as some red wines from Sankt Laurent. The region's legal boundaries encompass several municipalities, ensuring the preservation of its viticultural heritage.
geo:at:thermenregion-dac.style: White wines with a focus on Zierfandler and Rotgipfler

geo:es:tierra-de-leon.name: Tierra de León
geo:es:tierra-de-leon.description: Tierra de León is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla y León region of Spain. It is characterized by a diverse range of microclimates and soils, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. The appellation primarily produces red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties. The region has gained recognition for its unique expressions of local varietals.
geo:es:tierra-de-leon.style: Red wines with a focus on local grape varieties

geo:hu:tokaj-dhc.name: Tokaji
geo:hu:tokaj-dhc.description: Tokaj is a historic wine appellation located in northeastern Hungary, for its sweet wines made primarily from the Furmint and Hárslevelű grape varieties. The region's unique volcanic soil and microclimate contribute to the production of botrytized wines, particularly the famous Tokaji Aszú. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several villages and vineyards, with a classification system that includes various quality designations based on sugar content and production methods.
geo:hu:tokaj-dhc.style: Sweet wines, notable for botrytis influence

geo:it:torgiano.name: Torgiano
geo:it:torgiano.description: Torgiano is a designated Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Umbria region of Italy. It is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly made from Sangiovese grapes. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Apennine Mountains and the Tiber River, contributing to the quality of the wines produced here.
geo:it:torgiano.style: Red wines with a focus on Sangiovese, often medium-bodied

geo:es:toro-do.name: Toro
geo:es:toro-do.description: Toro is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla y León region of Spain, known primarily for its robust red wines. The appellation is characterized by its hot, continental climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the distinctive character of its wines. The legal boundaries of Toro encompass several municipalities, with a focus on the towns of Toro, Morales de Toro, and San Román de Hornija. The region has a long viticultural history, with evidence of winemaking dating back to Roman times.
geo:es:toro-do.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and tannic structure

geo:pt:torres-vedras.name: Torres Vedras
geo:pt:torres-vedras.description: Torres Vedras is a designated wine appellation (DOC) located within the Lisboa region of Portugal. It is characterized by a Mediterranean climate that influences the viticulture of the area. The appellation is known for producing red wines primarily from indigenous grape varieties, reflecting the local terroir and winemaking traditions.
geo:pt:torres-vedras.style: Red wines with a focus on local grape varieties

geo:it:toscana-igt.name: Toscana IGT
geo:it:toscana-igt.description: The designation that freed Tuscan winemakers from tradition. Super Tuscans — Cabernet, Merlot, and Sangiovese blends outside the DOC rules — use this classification. Sassicaia started it; Tignanello perfected it. IGT Toscana means ambition unconstrained by regulation.

geo:fr:touraine-aoc.name: Touraine
geo:fr:touraine-aoc.description: Touraine is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, encompassing a diverse range of terroirs. It is situated along the banks of the Loire River and is characterized by a continental climate with influences from the Atlantic. The appellation is known for its production of both red and white wines, primarily from varieties such as Sauvignon Blanc, Chenin Blanc, and Cabernet Franc. The legal boundaries of Touraine include several communes, and it is recognized for its fresh and fruity wine styles.
geo:fr:touraine-aoc.style: Fresh, fruity wines with a range of styles from dry to sweet

geo:fr:touraine-noble-joue.name: Touraine Noble-Joue
geo:fr:touraine-noble-joue.description: Touraine Noble-Joue is an AOC located within the Touraine region of France, recognized for its production of rosé wines. The appellation is characterized by its specific legal boundaries that define the area where grapes must be grown to qualify for the designation. The terroir is influenced by the Loire River, contributing to the unique characteristics of the wines produced here. The appellation is noted for its use of traditional grape varieties, which reflect the local viticultural heritage.
geo:fr:touraine-noble-joue.style: Rosé wines with regional character and varietal expression

geo:fr:touraine-amboise.name: Touraine-Amboise
geo:fr:touraine-amboise.description: Touraine-Amboise is an AOC located in the Touraine region of France, recognized for its production of primarily white wines. The appellation is situated along the banks of the Loire River, characterized by a continental climate with influences from the river. It encompasses several communes, with a focus on the city of Amboise, known for its historical significance and proximity to notable châteaux. The terroir consists of clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the distinct characteristics of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:touraine-amboise.style: White wines with a focus on Chenin Blanc

geo:fr:touraine-chenonceaux.name: Touraine-Chenonceaux
geo:fr:touraine-chenonceaux.description: Touraine-Chenonceaux is an AOC located within the Touraine region of France, recognized for its production of white wines. The appellation is situated near the Cher River, benefiting from a climate that is influenced by both the river and the surrounding limestone soils. The legal boundaries of Touraine-Chenonceaux encompass specific areas known for their unique terroir, which contributes to the character of the wines produced here. The appellation primarily focuses on the production of dry white wines, with regulations governing permitted grape varieties and winemaking practices.
geo:fr:touraine-chenonceaux.style: Dry white wines with fresh acidity and fruit character

geo:fr:touraine-mesland.name: Touraine-Mesland
geo:fr:touraine-mesland.description: Touraine-Mesland is an AOC located within the Touraine region of France. It is characterized by its production of red wines primarily from Gamay and Cabernet Franc, as well as white wines from Chenin Blanc. The appellation benefits from a continental climate, with a mix of clay and limestone soils that contribute to the wines' profiles.
geo:fr:touraine-mesland.style: Red wines with fruit-forward characteristics and moderate tannins

geo:fr:touraine-oisly.name: Touraine-Oisly
geo:fr:touraine-oisly.description: Touraine-Oisly is an AOC located within the Touraine region of France, primarily known for its production of white wines. The appellation is situated in the Loire Valley, characterized by its diverse terroir and favorable climate for viticulture. It is specifically recognized for its Sauvignon Blanc, which thrives in the region's chalky and flinty soils. The legal boundaries of Touraine-Oisly encompass several communes, ensuring the protection of its unique wine style.
geo:fr:touraine-oisly.style: Crisp, aromatic white wines primarily from Sauvignon Blanc

geo:at:traisental-dac.name: Traisental
geo:at:traisental-dac.description: Traisental is a designated Austrian wine appellation located in the Niederösterreich region. It is classified as a DAC (Districtus Austriae Controllatus), which indicates adherence to specific quality standards. The appellation is primarily known for its production of white wines, particularly from Grüner Veltliner and Riesling grapes, reflecting the region's unique terroir and climate.
geo:at:traisental-dac.style: Dry white wines with mineral character

geo:it:trebbiano-dabruzzo.name: Trebbiano d'Abruzzo
geo:it:trebbiano-dabruzzo.description: Trebbiano d'Abruzzo is a white wine appellation located in the Abruzzo region of Italy. It is defined by its legal boundaries which encompass the provinces of Teramo, Pescara, and L'Aquila. The appellation primarily permits the Trebbiano grape, known for its versatility and ability to produce wines with varying levels of acidity and body. The terroir features a mix of hilly and coastal areas, contributing to the wine's fresh and crisp profile. The style of Trebbiano d'Abruzzo is typically characterized by its lightness and fruit-forward nature, often exhibiting notes of citrus and green apple.
geo:it:trebbiano-dabruzzo.style: Light, fresh white wines with citrus and green apple notes

geo:it:trentino-doc.name: Trentino
geo:it:trentino-doc.description: Trentino is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Trentino-Alto Adige region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its diverse microclimates and varied altitudes, which contribute to a range of wine styles, primarily focusing on white wines. The region benefits from a mix of Mediterranean and continental climates, allowing for the cultivation of both indigenous and international grape varieties.
geo:it:trentino-doc.style: White wines with fresh acidity and aromatic profiles

geo:it:trento.name: Trento
geo:it:trento.description: Trento is a designated DOC appellation located in the Trentino-Alto Adige region of Italy, primarily known for its production of high-quality sparkling wines. The appellation is recognized for its use of the traditional method (metodo classico) for sparkling wine production, which involves secondary fermentation in the bottle. The geographical features of the area, including the presence of the Adige River and the surrounding mountains, contribute to its unique terroir, characterized by a combination of cool climate and diverse soils.
geo:it:trento.style: Traditional method sparkling wines with complexity and finesse

geo:za:tulbagh.name: Tulbagh
geo:za:tulbagh.description: Tulbagh is a wine appellation located within the Western Cape region of South Africa, classified under the Wine of Origin (WO) system. The area is characterized by its mountainous terrain and diverse microclimates, which contribute to the cultivation of a variety of grape types. Tulbagh is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on Chenin Blanc, Pinotage, and Shiraz. The region has a rich viticultural history, dating back to the 17th century, and has seen a resurgence in quality wine production in recent decades.
geo:za:tulbagh.style: Varied styles, with notable white wines from Chenin Blanc

geo:de:tuniberg.name: Tuniberg
geo:de:tuniberg.description: Tuniberg is an appellation located within the Baden wine region of Germany, classified as a Bereich. It is characterized by its diverse terroir, which includes a mix of limestone and clay soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines produced. The climate is influenced by the nearby Rhine River, providing a warm microclimate suitable for viticulture. Tuniberg is known for its production of both red and white wines, with a focus on varietals that thrive in this environment.
geo:de:tuniberg.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and fruitiness

geo:ar:tupungato-ig.name: Tupungato
geo:ar:tupungato-ig.description: Tupungato is an Indicación Geográfica (IG) located within the Uco Valley in Argentina. It is characterized by high-altitude vineyards that benefit from a continental climate, which contributes to the development of balanced acidity and fruit expression. The region is known for its diverse terroirs, influenced by the Andes Mountains, which provide a range of microclimates suitable for various grape varieties.
geo:ar:tupungato-ig.style: Red wines, notable for fruit intensity and structure

geo:es:ucles.name: Uclés
geo:es:ucles.description: Uclés is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. This appellation is characterized by its varied topography and a continental climate, which influences the viticulture practices in the area. Uclés is known for producing predominantly red wines, with a focus on both traditional and international grape varieties.
geo:es:ucles.style: Red wines with a range of varietal expressions

geo:ar:uco-valley.name: Uco Valley
geo:ar:uco-valley.description: Uco Valley is an Indicación Geográfica (IG) located within the Mendoza region of Argentina. It is characterized by its high-altitude vineyards, which benefit from a diverse microclimate and varied soil types. The valley is known for producing high-quality red wines, particularly from the Malbec grape, which thrives in the region's conditions. The area has gained recognition for its modern winemaking practices and focus on terroir-driven expressions.
geo:ar:uco-valley.style: Red wines, notable for their fruit intensity and structure

geo:ar:uco-valley-ig.name: Uco Valley
geo:ar:uco-valley-ig.description: Uco Valley is an appellation located within the Mendoza region of Argentina, known for its high-altitude vineyards. The area benefits from a diverse climate, with significant temperature variations between day and night, which aids in the development of grape acidity and flavor complexity. The valley is characterized by alluvial soils and a backdrop of the Andes Mountains, which influence viticulture practices. Uco Valley is recognized for producing high-quality Malbec, as well as other varieties such as Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay.
geo:ar:uco-valley-ig.style: Red wines with notable fruit concentration and acidity

geo:es:utiel-requena.name: Utiel-Requena
geo:es:utiel-requena.description: Utiel-Requena is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Valencia region of Spain. The appellation is situated inland, characterized by a continental climate with hot summers and cold winters, which influences the ripening of grapes. The region is known for its diverse soils, including clay and limestone, which contribute to the complexity of its wines. Red wines dominate production, with a focus on both indigenous and international grape varieties.
geo:es:utiel-requena.style: Red wines with depth and structure

geo:fr:vacqueyras.name: Vacqueyras
geo:fr:vacqueyras.description: Vacqueyras is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, recognized for its red wines. The appellation is situated near the village of the same name, characterized by a mix of clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines. The climate is Mediterranean, with warm summers and mild winters, ideal for grape cultivation. Vacqueyras wines are typically blends dominated by Grenache and Syrah, reflecting the region's terroir.
geo:fr:vacqueyras.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich fruit and spice characteristics

geo:it:val-dorcia.name: Val d'Orcia
geo:it:val-dorcia.description: Val d'Orcia is an Italian wine appellation located in the Tuscany region, recognized for its picturesque landscape and historical significance. The appellation is situated in the province of Siena and is characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and olive groves. It encompasses several municipalities, including Montalcino and Pienza, and is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site. The region is known for producing high-quality red wines, primarily from Sangiovese grapes, as well as white wines from local varieties.
geo:it:val-dorcia.style: Red wines with a focus on Sangiovese, often with elegance and complexity

geo:fr:val-de-loire.name: Val de Loire
geo:fr:val-de-loire.description: Val de Loire is an Indication Géographique Protégée (IGP) located within the Loire Valley in France. It encompasses a broad area that allows for a diverse range of wine styles, primarily focusing on rosé production. The IGP designation permits a variety of grape types, reflecting the region's versatility in viticulture.
geo:fr:val-de-loire.style: Primarily produces dry and fruity rosé wines.

geo:es:valdeorras-do.name: Valdeorras
geo:es:valdeorras-do.description: Valdeorras is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Galicia region of Spain. It is characterized by its mountainous terrain and a climate influenced by both Atlantic and Mediterranean elements. The appellation is known for its white wines, primarily produced from the Godello grape, which thrives in the region's slate and granite soils.
geo:es:valdeorras-do.style: White wines with a focus on Godello

geo:es:valdepenas.name: Valdepeñas
geo:es:valdepenas.description: Valdepeñas is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. The appellation is characterized by its dry climate and diverse soils, which contribute to the production of primarily red wines. The legal boundaries of Valdepeñas encompass several municipalities, with the city of Valdepeñas serving as its namesake and central hub. The region has a long history of winemaking, dating back to Roman times, and has gained recognition for its quality red wines made predominantly from Tempranillo and other local varieties.
geo:es:valdepenas.style: Red wines with fruit-forward profiles and moderate tannins

geo:it:valdobbiadene.name: Valdobbiadene
geo:it:valdobbiadene.description: Valdobbiadene is an appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy, primarily known for its production of Prosecco. The area is characterized by steep hills and a unique microclimate that contributes to the quality of the grapes. The legal boundaries of the appellation encompass several municipalities, with Valdobbiadene itself being the most prominent. The wines produced here are typically made using the Charmat method, which emphasizes the fresh and fruity characteristics of the Glera grape. The appellation is recognized for its high-quality sparkling wines that often display floral and citrus notes.
geo:it:valdobbiadene.style: Sparkling wine, primarily Prosecco, known for freshness and fruitiness

geo:fr:valencay.name: Valencay
geo:fr:valencay.description: Valencay is an AOC located in the Loire Valley of France, established in 2004. The appellation is situated in the Indre department, characterized by a continental climate with influences from the Atlantic. The vineyards are primarily planted on clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the unique expression of the wines produced here. Valencay is known for its white wines, predominantly made from Sauvignon Blanc, as well as red wines from Gamay, Pinot Noir, and Cot.
geo:fr:valencay.style: White wines with fresh acidity and aromatic profiles

geo:es:valencia-do.name: Valencia
geo:es:valencia-do.description: Valencia is a designated wine appellation located in the eastern part of Spain, within the autonomous community of Valencia. The appellation encompasses a variety of microclimates and diverse soils, contributing to a range of wine styles. It is known for both red and white wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties and a growing reputation for quality production.
geo:es:valencia-do.style: Diverse wines with a focus on indigenous varieties, both red and white

geo:fr:vallee-de-la-marne.name: Vallée de la Marne
geo:fr:vallee-de-la-marne.description: Vallée de la Marne is an appellation within the Champagne region of France, known for its production of sparkling wines. It is characterized by a diverse terroir that includes clay and limestone soils, which contribute to the unique profiles of the wines. The appellation encompasses several villages along the Marne River, where the climate is influenced by the river's proximity. The Vallée de la Marne is particularly noted for its production of Pinot Meunier, which thrives in this area.
geo:fr:vallee-de-la-marne.style: Produces sparkling wines, often featuring Pinot Meunier

geo:it:valpolicella-doc.name: Valpolicella
geo:it:valpolicella-doc.description: Valpolicella is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Veneto region of Italy. The appellation is characterized by its hilly terrain and a temperate climate that supports the cultivation of grapes. Valpolicella is primarily known for producing red wines, which are often blends of indigenous grape varieties. The region's winemaking tradition dates back to ancient times, with a focus on both traditional and modern techniques.
