geo:it:valpolicella-doc.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with fruity and herbal notes

geo:it:valpolicella-classico.name: Valpolicella Classico
geo:it:valpolicella-classico.description: Valpolicella Classico is a designated appellation within the Veneto region of Italy, classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC). It is situated in the historical heart of the Valpolicella region, characterized by its hilly terrain and unique microclimates. The appellation is known for producing red wines primarily from indigenous grape varieties, with a focus on the blend of Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara. The wines exhibit a range of styles, from light and fruity to more complex and structured versions, often influenced by the traditional appassimento method used in some of its sub-styles.
geo:it:valpolicella-classico.style: Red wines, varying from light and fruity to rich and complex

geo:it:valpolicella-ripasso.name: Valpolicella Ripasso
geo:it:valpolicella-ripasso.description: Valpolicella Ripasso is a red wine appellation located in the Veneto region of Italy, classified as DOC. It is produced using a unique method that involves re-passing young Valpolicella wine over the pomace of Amarone or Recioto wines, which enhances its complexity and richness. The appellation is known for its medium to full-bodied wines that exhibit a balance of fruit and structure.
geo:it:valpolicella-ripasso.style: Medium to full-bodied red with enhanced complexity

geo:it:valtellina.name: Valtellina
geo:it:valtellina.description: Valtellina is an Italian wine appellation located in the Lombardy region, known primarily for its red wines made from the Nebbiolo grape, locally referred to as Chiavennasca. The appellation is characterized by steep, terraced vineyards along the Adda River, benefiting from a continental climate with significant temperature variations. Valtellina is divided into several sub-zones, with Valtellina Superiore being the most prestigious, recognized for its stricter production regulations and higher quality standards. The region has a long viticultural history, with evidence of grape cultivation dating back to Roman times.
geo:it:valtellina.style: Red wines with structure, acidity, and aging potential

geo:it:valtellina-rosso.name: Valtellina Rosso
geo:it:valtellina-rosso.description: Valtellina Rosso is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in the Valtellina region of Italy. The appellation is primarily known for its red wines, which are predominantly produced from the Nebbiolo grape, locally referred to as Chiavennasca. The wines are characterized by their structure and aging potential, often reflecting the unique terroir of the steep, terraced vineyards in the region.
geo:it:valtellina-rosso.style: Structured red wines with aging potential, primarily from Nebbiolo

geo:it:valtellina-superiore.name: Valtellina Superiore
geo:it:valtellina-superiore.description: Valtellina Superiore is a DOCG appellation located in the Lombardy region of Italy, specifically in the Valtellina valley. It is primarily known for producing red wines made from the Nebbiolo grape, locally referred to as Chiavennasca. The appellation is characterized by steep terraced vineyards that benefit from a combination of alpine and Mediterranean climates, which contribute to the distinctiveness of the wines. The legal boundaries of Valtellina Superiore encompass several municipalities, and the wines must adhere to strict production standards, including aging requirements.
geo:it:valtellina-superiore.style: Red wines with elegance and structure, showcasing Nebbiolo's characteristics

geo:it:venezia-giulia.name: Venezia Giulia
geo:it:venezia-giulia.description: Venezia Giulia is an Indicazione Geografica Tipica (IGT) appellation located in the Friuli-Venezia Giulia region of Italy. It encompasses a diverse range of white wines primarily produced from indigenous and international grape varieties. The appellation benefits from the region's varied microclimates and soils, which contribute to the complexity and character of the wines.
geo:it:venezia-giulia.style: Primarily white wines with a focus on freshness and aromatic complexity.

geo:it:venezia-giulia-igt.name: Venezia Giulia IGT
geo:it:venezia-giulia-igt.description: Friuli's experimental designation. Orange wines, extended maceration, amphora aging — this IGT covers the region's most innovative producers. Gravner and Radikon pioneered the style. Expect texture, tannin, and wines that challenge convention.

geo:fr:ventoux.name: Ventoux
geo:fr:ventoux.description: The Ventoux AOC is located in the Southern Rhône region of France, established in 1973. It is situated around the Mont Ventoux, characterized by diverse soils and a Mediterranean climate. The appellation primarily produces red wines, with a focus on blends that often include Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. The legal boundaries of the AOC encompass several communes, and the wines are known for their fruit-forward character and moderate tannins.
geo:fr:ventoux.style: Fruit-forward red wines with moderate tannins

geo:it:verdicchio-dei-castelli-di-jesi.name: Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi
geo:it:verdicchio-dei-castelli-di-jesi.description: Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi is a white wine appellation located in the Marche region of Italy. It is classified as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) and is primarily known for wines made from the Verdicchio grape. The appellation encompasses a variety of microclimates and soils, contributing to the diversity of styles produced within its boundaries.
geo:it:verdicchio-dei-castelli-di-jesi.style: Crisp, aromatic white wines with citrus and herbal notes

geo:it:verdicchio-di-matelica.name: Verdicchio di Matelica
geo:it:verdicchio-di-matelica.description: Verdicchio di Matelica is a white wine appellation located in the Marche region of Italy, classified as a DOC. The appellation is defined by its legal boundaries, which encompass the municipality of Matelica and surrounding areas. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Verdicchio, known for its versatility and ability to express the local terroir. The wines typically exhibit a range of characteristics, influenced by the region's unique climate and soil composition.
geo:it:verdicchio-di-matelica.style: Crisp white wines with mineral notes and good aging potential

geo:it:verduno-pelaverga.name: Verduno Pelaverga
geo:it:verduno-pelaverga.description: Verduno Pelaverga is a red wine appellation located within the Langhe region of Italy, classified as a DOC. The appellation is known for its production of wines primarily made from the Pelaverga Piccolo grape variety. The geographical boundaries of Verduno Pelaverga encompass the commune of Verduno, characterized by its hilly terrain and favorable microclimates that contribute to the unique expression of the grape. The wines from this appellation are noted for their aromatic complexity and moderate tannins, reflecting the terroir of the region.
geo:it:verduno-pelaverga.style: Aromatic red wines with moderate tannins and complexity

geo:it:vermentino-di-gallura.name: Vermentino di Gallura
geo:it:vermentino-di-gallura.description: Vermentino di Gallura is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) located in the Gallura region of northern Sardinia. It is exclusively produced from the Vermentino grape variety, which thrives in the region's granite soils and Mediterranean climate. The appellation is characterized by its legal boundaries that encompass specific municipalities, ensuring quality and authenticity in production. The wines are known for their aromatic profile and refreshing acidity, often reflecting the coastal influences of the region.
geo:it:vermentino-di-gallura.style: Crisp white wines with aromatic complexity and mineral notes

geo:it:vermentino-di-sardegna.name: Vermentino di Sardegna
geo:it:vermentino-di-sardegna.description: Vermentino di Sardegna is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) located in Sardinia, Italy. The appellation encompasses the entire island and is primarily known for producing white wines from the Vermentino grape variety. The region's Mediterranean climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters, contributes to the aromatic profile and freshness of the wines.
geo:it:vermentino-di-sardegna.style: Fresh, aromatic white wines with citrus and herbal notes

geo:it:vernaccia-di-san-gimignano.name: Vernaccia di San Gimignano
geo:it:vernaccia-di-san-gimignano.description: Vernaccia di San Gimignano is a white wine appellation located in the Tuscany region of Italy, recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG). The appellation is situated around the medieval town of San Gimignano, known for its distinctive towers and historical significance in wine production. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Vernaccia, which thrives in the local clay and limestone soils. The wines are characterized by their crisp acidity and mineral notes, often exhibiting a range of floral and fruity aromas.
geo:it:vernaccia-di-san-gimignano.style: Crisp white wines with floral and mineral characteristics

geo:it:vesuvio.name: Vesuvio
geo:it:vesuvio.description: Vesuvio is a DOC appellation located in the Campania region of Italy, established to regulate the production of wines in the area surrounding Mount Vesuvius. The appellation is characterized by its volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the wines produced here. Vesuvio primarily produces red wines, with a focus on indigenous grape varieties that reflect the local viticultural heritage.
geo:it:vesuvio.style: Red wines with a focus on indigenous varieties and volcanic terroir

geo:fr:vin-de-corse.name: Vin de Corse
geo:fr:vin-de-corse.description: Vin de Corse is an Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée (AOC) located in Corsica, France. It encompasses the entire island and is known primarily for its red wines, which are produced from a variety of indigenous grape varieties. The AOC was established to promote and protect the unique viticultural heritage of Corsica, reflecting its Mediterranean climate and diverse terroirs.
geo:fr:vin-de-corse.style: Red wines with a focus on fruit and Mediterranean character

geo:fr:vin-de-savoie.name: Vin de Savoie
geo:fr:vin-de-savoie.description: Vin de Savoie is an AOC located in the Savoie region of France, primarily known for its white wines. The appellation encompasses several communes and is characterized by its mountainous terrain and alpine climate, which contribute to the unique terroir. The wines produced here often reflect the region's diverse microclimates and soil types, resulting in a variety of styles within the white wine category.
geo:fr:vin-de-savoie.style: White wines with freshness and minerality

geo:it:vin-santo.name: Vin Santo
geo:it:vin-santo.description: Vin Santo is a traditional Italian dessert wine produced primarily in Tuscany. It is made from white grape varieties, typically Trebbiano and Malvasia, which are dried before fermentation to concentrate their sugars. The wine is often aged in small wooden barrels, resulting in a rich, complex flavor profile. Vin Santo is recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) wine, ensuring adherence to specific production regulations.
geo:it:vin-santo.style: Rich, sweet dessert wine with nutty and dried fruit flavors

geo:pt:vinho-verde.name: Vinho Verde
geo:pt:vinho-verde.description: Portugal's green wine country, named for the lush landscape rather than the wine's color. Light, fresh, often with a gentle sparkle — perfect summer drinking. The best, from Alvarinho in Monção e Melgaço, rival fine Albariño. Most is simple; some is sublime.

geo:it:vino-nobile-di-montepulciano.name: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano
geo:it:vino-nobile-di-montepulciano.description: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano is a red wine appellation located in Tuscany, Italy, recognized as a Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG). The appellation is situated around the town of Montepulciano and is characterized by its hilly terrain and a climate that is influenced by both the Mediterranean and continental weather patterns. The primary grape variety permitted for production is Sangiovese, which is locally known as Prugnolo Gentile. The wines are typically aged for a minimum of two years before release, with specific aging requirements for riserva wines.
geo:it:vino-nobile-di-montepulciano.style: Structured red wines with moderate tannins and acidity

geo:fr:vinsobres.name: Vinsobres
geo:fr:vinsobres.description: Vinsobres is an AOC located in the Southern Rhône region of France, recognized for its red wines. The appellation is situated on a plateau with a mix of clay and limestone soils, contributing to the character of its wines. Established as an AOC in 2006, Vinsobres is known for its blend of Grenache and Syrah, which thrive in its Mediterranean climate. The wines typically exhibit a balance of fruit and structure, reflecting the unique terroir of the area.
geo:fr:vinsobres.style: Red wines with a balance of fruit and structure

geo:fr:vire-clesse.name: Viré-Clessé
geo:fr:vire-clesse.description: Viré-Clessé is an AOC located in the Mâconnais region of France, recognized for its production of white wines primarily made from Chardonnay. The appellation encompasses the villages of Viré and Clessé, which are situated on the slopes of the Mâconnais hills, benefiting from a continental climate that contributes to the ripening of grapes. The wines produced here are characterized by their freshness and aromatic complexity, often reflecting the limestone and clay soils of the area.
geo:fr:vire-clesse.style: Fresh, aromatic white wines primarily from Chardonnay

geo:it:vittoria.name: Vittoria
geo:it:vittoria.description: Vittoria is an appellation located in Sicily, Italy, recognized for its production of red wines. It is situated within the larger Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG, which is the only Sicilian wine to hold the DOCG status. The region's climate is characterized by warm temperatures and a Mediterranean influence, contributing to the ripening of grapes.
geo:it:vittoria.style: Red wines with a focus on fruitiness and moderate tannins

geo:fr:volnay.name: Volnay
geo:fr:volnay.description: The most graceful red wines of the Côte de Beaune. Volnay's thin limestone soils and elevated vineyards produce Pinot Noir of exceptional finesse — less powerful than Pommard next door, but often more perfumed and silky. Premier Cru vineyards like Caillerets and Champans are among Burgundy's most prized.

geo:fr:vosne-romanee.name: Vosne-Romanée
geo:fr:vosne-romanee.description: Vosne-Romanée is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. It is for producing high-quality red wines primarily from the Pinot Noir grape. The appellation encompasses several vineyards, including La Romanée and Richebourg, which contribute to its reputation. The terroir is characterized by clay-limestone soils and a favorable microclimate, which enhance the complexity and elegance of the wines.
geo:fr:vosne-romanee.style: Elegant red wines with complexity and aging potential

geo:fr:vougeot.name: Vougeot
geo:fr:vougeot.description: Vougeot is an AOC located in the Côte de Nuits subregion of Burgundy, France. The appellation is known primarily for its red wines produced from Pinot Noir. It encompasses the village of Vougeot and includes the Clos de Vougeot vineyard, which is a historical site with a long tradition of winemaking. The terroir is characterized by clay-limestone soils, which contribute to the wines' structure and complexity.
geo:fr:vougeot.style: Red wines with depth, elegance, and aging potential

geo:fr:vouvray.name: Vouvray
geo:fr:vouvray.description: Chenin Blanc's greatest expression, in styles from bone-dry to lusciously sweet. The tuffeau chalk cellars carved into hillsides store wines that can age for a century. Dry Vouvray offers honeyed depth; sweet versions (moelleux, liquoreux) are among France's most undervalued dessert wines.

geo:at:vulkanland-steiermark.name: Vulkanland Steiermark
geo:at:vulkanland-steiermark.description: Vulkanland Steiermark is a designated Austrian wine appellation (DAC) located within the larger region of Steiermark. The appellation is characterized by its volcanic soils, which contribute to the unique terroir of the area. It is primarily known for the production of white wines, with a focus on varieties that thrive in the region's climate and soil conditions.
geo:at:vulkanland-steiermark.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and minerality

geo:at:sudsteiermark.name: Südsteiermark
geo:at:sudsteiermark.description: Southern Styria's rolling Opok hills — dense marl-limestone soils that force vine roots deep and sharpen aromatic whites to laser-cut precision. Sauvignon Blanc here is Austria's most export-celebrated wine style; Welschriesling and Gelber Muskateller add breadth. Tement at Zieregg and Grassnitzberg set the benchmark for single-vineyard expression.

geo:at:sudsteiermark-dac.name: Südsteiermark
geo:at:sudsteiermark-dac.description: Austria's benchmark DAC for aromatic whites. Opok (marl-limestone) soils on steep south-facing slopes in the foothills bordering Slovenia. Sauvignon Blanc leads — site-expressive, mineral, and long-lived from top estates like Tement — alongside Welschriesling, Morillon (Chardonnay), and Gelber Muskateller. DAC rules formalised in 2018.
geo:at:sudsteiermark-dac.style: Mineral, aromatic whites; site-expressive Sauvignon Blanc

geo:at:weststeiermark.name: Weststeiermark
geo:at:weststeiermark.description: Steep schist and phyllite slopes west of Graz, centred on Deutschlandsberg and Stainz. Defined almost entirely by the indigenous Blauer Wildbacher grape, which produces Schilcher — a sharp, cranberry-tart rosé unlike anything else in Austria. A niche region with a fiercely local identity.

geo:at:weststeiermark-dac.name: Weststeiermark
geo:at:weststeiermark-dac.description: DAC of the Schilcher rosé, made exclusively from Blauer Wildbacher on steep schist-phyllite slopes. Cranberry-sharp, high-acid, and bracingly dry; the antithesis of soft Provençal rosé. Rarely exported, fiercely local — a living example of Austrian viticultural identity.

geo:at:wachau.name: Wachau
geo:at:wachau.description: Austria's Danube jewel. Steep terraces above the river produce Grüner Veltliner and Riesling of crystalline intensity. The local classification — Steinfeder, Federspiel, Smaragd — indicates ascending weight. Smaragd wines can rival the best of Alsace and Germany.

geo:at:wachau-dac.name: Wachau
geo:at:wachau-dac.description: Wachau is an Austrian wine appellation located along the Danube River, characterized by its steep terraced vineyards. The region is known for its distinctive microclimate, which is influenced by the river and the surrounding hills. Wachau is part of the larger Niederösterreich region and is recognized for its strict quality regulations, including the classification system of Steinfeder, Federspiel, and Smaragd. The wines produced here are primarily white, showcasing the unique terroir of the area.
geo:at:wachau-dac.style: Dry white wines with mineral character and high acidity

geo:at:wagram-dac.name: Wagram
geo:at:wagram-dac.description: Wagram is a designated Austrian wine appellation (DAC) located in the Niederösterreich region. It is characterized by its loess soil, which contributes to the distinctiveness of its wines. The appellation primarily focuses on white wine production, with a significant emphasis on the Grüner Veltliner and Roter Veltliner grape varieties. The climate is influenced by the Danube River, providing a favorable environment for viticulture.
geo:at:wagram-dac.style: White wines with a focus on Grüner Veltliner

geo:us:walla-walla-valley-ava.name: Walla Walla Valley
geo:us:walla-walla-valley-ava.description: Walla Walla Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in southeastern Washington State. It is characterized by a diverse range of soils and a climate that is influenced by the nearby Blue Mountains and the Columbia River. The AVA is known for its production of high-quality red wines, particularly from Bordeaux and Rhône varietals. The region has gained recognition since its establishment as an AVA in 1984, with a growing number of wineries contributing to its reputation.
geo:us:walla-walla-valley-ava.style: Red wines with a focus on Bordeaux and Rhône varietals

geo:de:wehlen.name: Wehlen
geo:de:wehlen.description: Wehlen is an Einzellage located within the Mosel region of Germany. It is primarily known for producing high-quality white wines, predominantly from the Riesling grape. The appellation benefits from steep, south-facing slopes along the Mosel River, which enhance sun exposure and contribute to the development of ripe fruit flavors. The terroir is characterized by slate soils, which impart minerality to the wines. Wehlen wines are often noted for their balance of sweetness and acidity, reflecting the cool climate of the region.
geo:de:wehlen.style: White wines, typically off-dry to sweet with high acidity.

geo:at:weinviertel.name: Weinviertel
geo:at:weinviertel.description: Weinviertel is a designated Austrian DAC located in the Niederösterreich region. It is characterized by its predominantly flat terrain and a continental climate, which contributes to the production of high-quality white wines. The appellation is known for its strict regulations governing grape varieties and winemaking practices, ensuring a consistent style and quality across its wines.
geo:at:weinviertel.style: Fresh and aromatic white wines with mineral notes

geo:us:willamette-valley.name: Willamette Valley
geo:us:willamette-valley.description: Willamette Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Oregon, recognized for its diverse microclimates and rich volcanic soils. The region is primarily known for its production of Pinot Noir, which thrives in the cooler climate influenced by the Pacific Ocean. Established as an AVA in 1984, it has become a significant area for premium wine production in the United States.
geo:us:willamette-valley.style: Red wines, notably Pinot Noir, with varied expressions

geo:us:willamette-valley-ava.name: Willamette Valley
geo:us:willamette-valley-ava.description: Willamette Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Oregon, recognized for its diverse microclimates and soils. The region is bounded by the Cascade Range to the east and the Coast Range to the west, providing a range of elevations and exposures. It encompasses several notable subregions, including Dundee Hills, Yamhill-Carlton, and Eola-Amity Hills. The AVA is primarily known for its production of Pinot Noir, which thrives in the cool, maritime climate. The valley's winemaking history began in the 1960s, and it has since gained recognition for its quality wines.
geo:us:willamette-valley-ava.style: Known for elegant Pinot Noir and diverse white wines

geo:de:wonnegau.name: Wonnegau
geo:de:wonnegau.description: Wonnegau is a designated Bereich within the Rheinhessen region of Germany, known for its predominantly white wine production. The area features a mix of limestone and clay soils, contributing to the character of its wines. The climate is characterized by a continental influence, with warm summers and moderate rainfall, which aids in the ripening of grapes. The appellation includes several villages and is recognized for its commitment to quality wine production.
geo:de:wonnegau.style: White wines with a focus on freshness and fruit expression

geo:de:wurttemberg.name: Württemberg
geo:de:wurttemberg.description: Germany's fourth-largest region and unique for its focus on red wines — over 70% of production. Trollinger, a light red consumed locally, dominates, but serious Lemberger (Blaufränkisch) and Spätburgunder are emerging. Most wine is consumed within the region around Stuttgart.

geo:de:wurttemberg-qba.name: Württemberg
geo:de:wurttemberg-qba.description: Württemberg is an appellation located in southwestern Germany, primarily known for its production of red wines, particularly from the Trollinger and Lemberger grape varieties. The region benefits from a varied topography that includes vineyards on slopes and valleys, which contribute to the diversity of its wine styles. The climate is characterized by a continental influence, with warm summers and cold winters, allowing for the cultivation of both red and white grape varieties. The appellation is part of the larger Württemberg wine region, which has a long history of viticulture dating back to Roman times.
geo:de:wurttemberg-qba.style: Red wines with some notable whites

geo:us:yakima-valley.name: Yakima Valley
geo:us:yakima-valley.description: Yakima Valley is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located in Washington State. Established in 1983, it is one of the oldest AVAs in the state and is characterized by a diverse range of microclimates and soils. The valley's geography includes rolling hills and river valleys, which contribute to its varied terroir and suitability for multiple grape varieties.
geo:us:yakima-valley.style: Red wines, with notable whites from Riesling and Chardonnay

geo:au:yarra-valley-gi.name: Yarra Valley
geo:au:yarra-valley-gi.description: Yarra Valley is a Geographical Indication (GI) located in Victoria, Australia, known for its cool climate and diverse terroir. The region is characterized by rolling hills, fertile valleys, and a mix of volcanic and sedimentary soils. It is recognized for producing high-quality wines, particularly from the Pinot Noir grape, which thrives in the cooler temperatures. The Yarra Valley is home to several notable wineries and has a reputation for both traditional and innovative winemaking practices.
geo:au:yarra-valley-gi.style: Red wines, with a focus on Pinot Noir

geo:es:yecla-do.name: Yecla
geo:es:yecla-do.description: Yecla is a Denominación de Origen (DO) located in the Murcia region of Spain, established in 1975. The appellation is characterized by its Mediterranean climate, with hot summers and mild winters, and is situated on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 600 meters. The region is known for its diverse soils, including clay and limestone, which contribute to the production of robust red wines. Yecla primarily focuses on red wine production, with a significant emphasis on the Monastrell grape variety.
geo:es:yecla-do.style: Full-bodied red wines with ripe fruit and spice notes

geo:us:yountville-ava.name: Yountville
geo:us:yountville-ava.description: Yountville is an American Viticultural Area (AVA) located within Napa Valley, California. The appellation is characterized by its diverse microclimates and soils, which contribute to the production of high-quality wines. It is known for its focus on Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Chardonnay, among other varieties. The region's proximity to the Napa River and the surrounding hills influences its climate, providing a range of growing conditions that support various wine styles.
geo:us:yountville-ava.style: Primarily red wines with a focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot.

dim:acidity.name: Acidity
dim:acidity.description: Acidity is the sharp, tart sensation that makes your mouth water — like biting into a green apple or squeezing lemon juice. In wine, it comes primarily from tartaric, malic, and citric acids naturally present in grapes. High acidity makes a wine taste fresh, vibrant, and lively; low acidity can make it feel flat or flabby. Cool-climate wines (Riesling, Chablis, Champagne) tend toward higher acidity, while warm-climate wines (Viognier, Grenache from the Southern Rhône) tend lower. Acidity is the backbone of a wine's freshness and one of the key factors in its aging potential.
dim:acidity.style: Focus on whether your mouth waters after tasting — that's acidity at work.

var:agiorgitiko.name: Agiorgitiko
var:agiorgitiko.description: Agiorgitiko is a red grape variety native to Greece, primarily associated with the Nemea region. It is known for producing medium to full-bodied wines with moderate acidity and tannins. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often characterized by its fruit-forward profile and aging potential.
var:agiorgitiko.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with fruit-forward characteristics.

var:aglianico.name: Aglianico
var:aglianico.description: Aglianico is a red grape variety known for producing full-bodied, tannic wines. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors, along with earthy and spicy notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often associated with long aging potential due to its robust structure.
var:aglianico.style: Full-bodied and tannic red wines with dark fruit and earthy flavors.

var:albarino.name: Albariño
var:albarino.description: Albariño is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and aromatic profile. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines that exhibit crispness and freshness. The grape thrives in cooler climates, often resulting in wines that are vibrant and fruit-forward.
var:albarino.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

dim:alcohol.name: Alcohol
dim:alcohol.description: Alcohol contributes to body, warmth, and texture. You perceive it as a warming sensation in the back of your throat and chest — sometimes described as 'heat.' Wines range from under 8% ABV (Moscato d'Asti) to over 15% (Amarone, fortified wines up to 22%). Alcohol level depends on grape ripeness at harvest: riper grapes have more sugar, which converts to more alcohol. A well-integrated wine balances alcohol with acidity, tannin, and fruit so the heat doesn't dominate. When alcohol feels hot or burning, it's out of balance.
dim:alcohol.style: Swallow and notice the warmth in your throat — that's alcohol.

var:aligote.name: Aligoté
var:aligote.description: Aligoté is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with high acidity. It typically exhibits crisp flavors and is often used in blends or as a base for sparkling wines. The grape thrives in cooler climates, where it can retain its acidity while developing fresh fruit characteristics.
var:aligote.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

var:altesse.name: Altesse
var:altesse.description: Altesse is a white grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with a distinctive aromatic profile. It typically exhibits floral and stone fruit characteristics, along with a notable acidity that contributes to its freshness. The grape is primarily cultivated in the Savoie region of France, where it thrives in the cool climate and diverse soils.
var:altesse.style: Medium-bodied white wines with floral and stone fruit notes.

geo:fr:ambonnay.name: Ambonnay
geo:fr:ambonnay.description: Ambonnay is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated on south-facing slopes, which contribute to the ripening of grapes. The commune is particularly noted for its powerful and full-bodied Pinot Noir, which thrives in the local terroir. Notable producers in Ambonnay include Egly-Ouriet and Krug, both of which source grapes from this commune.
geo:fr:ambonnay.style: Known for powerful, full-bodied Pinot Noir and elegant Chardonnay.

var:arinto.name: Arinto
var:arinto.description: Arinto is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and crisp character. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines that exhibit citrus and mineral notes. The grape thrives in various Portuguese regions, often contributing to both varietal and blended wines.
var:arinto.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp with high acidity.

var:arneis.name: Arneis
var:arneis.description: Arneis is a white grape variety originating from the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and floral, fruity characteristics. The grape thrives in well-drained soils and is often used in blends, although it can also be vinified as a single varietal.
var:arneis.style: Medium-bodied white wine with floral and fruity notes.

dim:aroma.name: Aroma
dim:aroma.description: Aroma character captures where a wine sits on the spectrum from savory to fruity. A savory wine leans toward earth, herbs, leather, smoke, or mushroom — think aged Barolo or Northern Rhône Syrah. A fruity wine foregrounds fresh or ripe fruit — citrus, stone fruit, berries, tropical notes. Most wines blend both: a Burgundy Pinot Noir might show red cherry fruit alongside forest floor and truffle. This axis captures your overall impression of the aromatic profile, not its intensity.
keyGrapes: Pinot Noir (mid-spectrum — fruit with earthy undertones)
dim:aroma.style: Ask yourself: does this wine smell more like a garden or a fruit bowl?

dim:aroma-intensity.name: Aroma Intensity
dim:aroma-intensity.description: Aroma intensity is how pronounced the wine's smell is — not what it smells like, but how strongly it projects. Swirl the glass and bring it to your nose: does the aroma leap out (pronounced), or do you need to search for it (light)? Aromatic grape varieties like Gewürztraminer, Muscat, and Torrontés are naturally intense on the nose. Neutral varieties like Melon de Bourgogne (Muscadet) are more restrained. Intensity also depends on winemaking: oak aging, malolactic fermentation, and lees contact all affect how powerfully a wine smells.
dim:aroma-intensity.style: Before swirling, note what reaches you — that's the baseline intensity.

var:assyrtiko.name: Assyrtiko
var:assyrtiko.description: Assyrtiko is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and mineral character. It typically produces medium-bodied wines that exhibit citrus and stone fruit flavors. The grape thrives in warm climates, particularly in volcanic soils, which contribute to its distinctive profile.
var:assyrtiko.style: Medium-bodied, high acidity with citrus and mineral notes.

geo:fr:avenay-val-dor.name: Avenay-Val-d'Or
geo:fr:avenay-val-dor.description: Avenay-Val-d'Or is a Premier Cru commune located at the junction of the Vallée de la Marne and Montagne de Reims. It is classified as a Premier Cru with a rating of 93%. The commune is known for its production of Champagne, primarily from the grape varieties Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. The terroir is characterized by a mix of clay and chalk soils, contributing to the complexity of the wines.
geo:fr:avenay-val-dor.style: Champagnes with complexity and balance from Premier Cru vineyards.

geo:fr:avize.name: Avize
geo:fr:avize.description: Avize is a Grand Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is recognized as a spiritual center for Blanc de Blancs Champagne, predominantly producing high-quality Chardonnay. The commune features deep chalk subsoils that contribute to the mineral character and age-worthiness of its wines. Avize is also home to the Comité Champagne's viticultural school, emphasizing its importance in the region's wine education and production.
geo:fr:avize.style: Mineral-driven, age-worthy Blanc de Blancs Champagne.

geo:fr:ay.name: Aÿ
geo:fr:ay.description: Aÿ, officially named Aÿ-Champagne, is a Grand Cru commune in the Vallée de la Marne, recognized for its historical significance in Champagne production. The commune is characterized by its south-facing slopes, which are particularly conducive to the cultivation of Pinot Noir, contributing to the production of powerful and structured wines. Aÿ is home to several prominent Champagne houses, including Bollinger, Deutz, and Ayala.
geo:fr:ay.style: Powerful and structured Champagnes, primarily from Pinot Noir.

var:bacchus.name: Bacchus
var:bacchus.description: Bacchus is a white grape variety resulting from a cross between Silvaner, Riesling, and Müller-Thurgau. It is primarily cultivated in Germany, particularly in the regions of Franken and Rheinhessen, as well as in England and cool-climate areas of Japan, such as Hokkaido. The variety is known for its aromatic profile, often exhibiting characteristics reminiscent of Muscat.
var:bacchus.style: Aromatic white wine with floral and fruity notes.

var:baga.name: Baga
var:baga.description: Baga is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Bairrada region of Portugal. It is known for producing full-bodied wines with high acidity and tannins. The grape is often used in blends but can also be made into varietal wines that exhibit depth and complexity.
var:baga.style: Full-bodied, tannic red wines with high acidity.

var:barbera.name: Barbera
var:barbera.description: Barbera is a red grape variety known for its high acidity and moderate to full body. It typically produces wines that are fruit-forward with flavors of cherry, plum, and blackberry. The grape is versatile, often used in both single-varietal wines and blends.
var:barbera.style: Medium to full-bodied, high acid, fruit-forward red wines.

geo:fr:beaumont-sur-vesle.name: Beaumont-sur-Vesle
geo:fr:beaumont-sur-vesle.description: Beaumont-sur-Vesle is a Grand Cru commune located on the northern flank of the Montagne de Reims, near Verzenay. It is recognized for its high-quality vineyards, primarily producing Champagne. The commune's terroir is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable microclimate, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:beaumont-sur-vesle.style: Champagnes with depth and structure, often featuring both varietals.

geo:fr:bergeres-les-vertus.name: Bergères-lès-Vertus
geo:fr:bergeres-les-vertus.description: Bergères-lès-Vertus is a Premier Cru commune located at the southern end of the Côte des Blancs in the Champagne region of France. The commune is primarily known for its production of Chardonnay, which thrives in the local chalky soils and contributes to the region's reputation for high-quality sparkling wines. With a cru rating of 95%, Bergères-lès-Vertus is recognized for its potential to produce wines of notable finesse and complexity.

geo:fr:billy-le-grand.name: Billy-le-Grand
geo:fr:billy-le-grand.description: Billy-le-Grand is a Premier Cru commune located in the eastern part of the Montagne de Reims region. It is classified with a rating of 95% on the Échelle des Crus, indicating its high quality within the Champagne classification system. The commune is primarily known for its production of Champagne, utilizing the region's unique terroir to enhance the characteristics of its wines.
geo:fr:billy-le-grand.style: Champagne with a focus on balance and complexity.

geo:fr:bisseuil.name: Bisseuil
geo:fr:bisseuil.description: Bisseuil is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, near Tours-sur-Marne. It is recognized for its production of high-quality Champagne, primarily utilizing Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grapes. The commune benefits from a favorable microclimate and diverse terroir, contributing to the complexity of its wines.
geo:fr:bisseuil.style: Champagne with a focus on fruit-forward and structured profiles.

var:blaufrankisch.name: Blaufränkisch
var:blaufrankisch.description: Blaufränkisch is a red grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with a spicy character. It typically exhibits moderate acidity and tannins, contributing to its food-friendly nature. The grape thrives in various climates, often resulting in wines that display dark fruit flavors alongside herbal and peppery notes.
var:blaufrankisch.style: Medium-bodied red with spicy and dark fruit characteristics.

var:bobal.name: Bobal
var:bobal.description: Bobal is a red grape variety native to Spain, primarily grown in the Utiel-Requena region. It is known for producing full-bodied wines with deep color and robust tannins. The grape is often used in blends but can also be vinified as a varietal wine, showcasing its rich fruit flavors and earthy undertones.
var:bobal.style: Full-bodied red wines with deep color and robust tannins.

dim:body.name: Body
dim:body.description: Body is the weight and texture of wine in your mouth — how it feels, not how it tastes. Think of the difference between skim milk (light), whole milk (medium), and cream (full). Body comes from alcohol, residual sugar, tannin, and extract working together. A light-bodied Muscadet feels nimble and refreshing; a full-bodied Barossa Shiraz fills your palate with density and warmth. Body affects food pairing more than almost any other dimension — heavy food wants full body, delicate food wants light.
dim:body.style: Imagine the wine's viscosity — closer to water or to cream?

var:bonarda.name: Bonarda
var:bonarda.description: Bonarda is a red grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and tannins. It typically exhibits fruit-forward characteristics, often featuring flavors of dark berries and plum. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it can achieve optimal ripeness.
var:bonarda.style: Medium-bodied red wine with fruit-forward characteristics.

geo:fr:bouzy.name: Bouzy
geo:fr:bouzy.description: Bouzy is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is renowned for its south-facing slopes, which contribute to the production of rich and full-bodied Pinot Noir. Bouzy is notable for both its sparkling wines and still red wines, particularly Bouzy Rouge, which is classified under the Coteaux Champenois AOC. The commune's vineyards benefit from a favorable microclimate, enhancing the quality of the grapes cultivated here.
geo:fr:bouzy.style: Rich, full-bodied wines with a focus on Pinot Noir.

var:brachetto.name: Brachetto
var:brachetto.description: Brachetto is a red grape variety primarily known for producing light-bodied, aromatic, and sweet wines. It is characterized by its floral and fruity aromas, often exhibiting notes of strawberries and rose petals. The grape thrives in warm climates and is typically vinified as a sparkling or semi-sparkling wine.
var:brachetto.style: Light-bodied, aromatic, and sweet red wines.

geo:fr:bezannes.name: Bézannes
geo:fr:bezannes.description: Bézannes is a Premier Cru commune located on the outskirts of Reims in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne. It is classified with a rating of 90% on the Échelle des Crus, indicating its high-quality vineyard potential. The terroir is characterized by chalky soils, which are conducive to the cultivation of high-quality grapes. The commune primarily produces wines that showcase the characteristics of its dominant grape variety.
geo:fr:bezannes.style: Wines are typically structured with depth and complexity.

var:cabernet-franc.name: Cabernet Franc
var:cabernet-franc.description: Cabernet Franc is a red grape variety known for its medium-bodied wines with herbaceous and floral characteristics. It typically exhibits lower tannins compared to its more famous counterpart, Cabernet Sauvignon, and is often used in blends but can also produce varietal wines. The grape thrives in a variety of climates, producing wines that can range from light and aromatic to more structured and age-worthy.
var:cabernet-franc.style: Medium-bodied with herbaceous and floral notes.

var:cabernet-sauvignon.name: Cabernet Sauvignon
var:cabernet-sauvignon.description: Cabernet Sauvignon is a red grape variety known for its full-bodied structure and high tannin content. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and can develop complex secondary notes with aging. The grape thrives in a variety of climates, particularly in regions with warm days and cool nights, allowing for optimal ripening.
var:cabernet-sauvignon.style: Full-bodied and tannic, often with dark fruit and herbal notes.

var:canaiolo.name: Canaiolo
var:canaiolo.description: Canaiolo is a red grape variety primarily associated with the production of Chianti wines. It is known for its light-bodied structure and is often used as a blending grape to add softness and fruitiness to wine. The grape typically produces wines with moderate acidity and tannins.
var:canaiolo.style: Light-bodied red, commonly used in blends.

var:carignan.name: Carignan
var:carignan.description: Carignan is a red grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with high acidity and tannins. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and herbal notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends to enhance structure and complexity.
var:carignan.style: Full-bodied, tannic red wine with dark fruit and herbal characteristics.

var:carmenere.name: Carménère
var:carmenere.description: Carménère is a red grape variety known for its medium to full-bodied wines with herbaceous characteristics. It is often characterized by its deep color and soft tannins, making it approachable in youth. The grape typically exhibits flavors of dark fruits, green bell pepper, and spices.
var:carmenere.style: Medium to full-bodied with herbaceous notes.

var:carricante.name: Carricante
var:carricante.description: Carricante is a white grape variety primarily grown in Sicily, Italy. It is known for producing wines that exhibit high acidity and mineral characteristics, often with citrus and herbal notes. The variety thrives in volcanic soils, contributing to its distinctive profile.
var:carricante.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp with high acidity and mineral notes.

var:castelao.name: Castelão
var:castelao.description: Castelão (historically known as Periquita, after the famous wine from Quinta da Bacalhôa) is one of Portugal's most planted red grapes. Found across southern Portugal — Setúbal, Tejo, Alentejo — it produces medium-bodied wines with red fruit, gentle tannins, and earthy undertones. At its best in sandy soils near the coast, it offers approachable, food-friendly wines with a distinctly Portuguese character. José Maria da Fonseca's Periquita bottling made this grape internationally recognized.
var:castelao.style: Medium-bodied, fruity with red fruit, gentle tannins, and earthy character.

var:catarratto.name: Catarratto
var:catarratto.description: Catarratto is a white grape variety primarily grown in Sicily, Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity. The grape is often used in both varietal wines and blends, contributing floral and citrus characteristics.
var:catarratto.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with floral and citrus notes.

var:cesanese.name: Cesanese
var:cesanese.description: Cesanese is a red grape variety indigenous to Italy, primarily cultivated in the Lazio region. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and tannins. The grape typically exhibits fruity and floral characteristics, making it suitable for both young and aged wine styles.
var:cesanese.style: Medium-bodied red with moderate acidity and tannins.

geo:fr:chamery.name: Chamery
geo:fr:chamery.description: Chamery is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims subregion of Champagne. It is situated on the western slopes of the Montagne de Reims, characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate for viticulture. The commune is known for producing high-quality Champagne, primarily from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. Chamery has a Cru rating of 90%, indicating its significant standing within the Champagne classification system.
geo:fr:chamery.style: Champagnes with depth and complexity, often fruit-forward.

geo:fr:champillon.name: Champillon
geo:fr:champillon.description: Champillon is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, overlooking the Marne River near Épernay. It is recognized for its production of high-quality Champagne, primarily from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. The commune is notable for its proximity to the Royal Champagne hotel, which adds to its significance in the region.
geo:fr:champillon.style: Champagnes with depth and fruitiness, often from Pinot varieties.

var:chardonnay.name: Chardonnay
var:chardonnay.description: Chardonnay is a white grape variety known for its versatility and adaptability to various climates. It typically produces full-bodied wines with a range of flavors, from citrus and green apple to tropical fruits and stone fruits. The grape is often aged in oak, which can impart additional complexity and richness.
var:chardonnay.style: Full-bodied white wine with diverse flavor profiles.

var:chasselas.name: Chasselas
var:chasselas.description: Chasselas is a white grape variety known for its light-bodied and neutral character. It is primarily cultivated in Switzerland, where it is often used to produce dry wines that emphasize freshness and minerality. The grape is also grown in other regions, including France and Germany, where it may be known by different names.
var:chasselas.style: Light-bodied, neutral white wines with fresh and mineral qualities.

var:chenin-blanc.name: Chenin Blanc
var:chenin-blanc.description: Chenin Blanc is a white grape variety known for its versatility and ability to produce a range of wine styles, from dry to sweet. It typically exhibits high acidity and can develop complex flavors with age. The grape thrives in various climates, contributing to its adaptability in different wine regions.
var:chenin-blanc.style: Light to full-bodied, versatile with high acidity and diverse flavor profiles.

geo:fr:chigny-les-roses.name: Chigny-les-Roses
geo:fr:chigny-les-roses.description: Chigny-les-Roses is a Premier Cru commune located on the northern slopes of the Montagne de Reims in the Champagne region of France. It is classified with a cru rating of 94%, indicating its high quality within the Champagne hierarchy. The commune is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable microclimate, which contribute to the production of high-quality sparkling wines.
geo:fr:chigny-les-roses.style: Produces structured and fruit-forward Champagne with complexity.

geo:fr:chouilly.name: Chouilly
geo:fr:chouilly.description: Chouilly is a Grand Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs, known for its predominantly Chardonnay production. It is situated as the northernmost village in the Côte des Blancs, near Épernay, and is recognized for its high-quality vineyards. The commune's terroir contributes to the finesse and elegance of its wines, primarily sparkling. Chouilly also allows for the production of Premier Cru wines from Pinot Noir, reflecting the diversity of its viticultural landscape.
geo:fr:chouilly.style: Primarily elegant sparkling wines with finesse and complexity.

var:cinsault.name: Cinsault
var:cinsault.description: Cinsault is a red grape variety known for its light-bodied and fruity characteristics. It typically exhibits flavors of red fruits and floral notes, with moderate acidity and soft tannins. The grape is often used in blends but can also be vinified as a varietal wine.
var:cinsault.style: Light-bodied red with fruity flavors and moderate acidity.

var:clairette.name: Clairette
var:clairette.description: Clairette is a white grape variety known for its light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits floral and fruity characteristics, with moderate acidity. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends, particularly in the southern Rhône Valley.
var:clairette.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with floral and fruity notes.

dim:clarity.name: Clarity
dim:clarity.description: Clarity describes whether the wine is clear, hazy, or somewhere between. Most commercial wines are filtered to brilliant clarity. Haze can indicate a fault (bacterial spoilage) or a deliberate winemaking choice — natural wines and unfiltered wines are often slightly hazy, which is perfectly acceptable. Sediment in aged reds is normal: tannin and colour pigments polymerize and fall out of solution over time. A wine that should be clear but isn't may signal a problem worth noting.
dim:clarity.style: Hold the glass to light — is it star-bright, slightly hazy, or cloudy?

geo:fr:coligny.name: Coligny
geo:fr:coligny.description: Coligny is a Premier Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of Chardonnay, which thrives in the region's chalky soils and favorable climate. The commune contributes to the overall quality and diversity of Champagne through its unique terroir and vineyard practices.

var:colombard.name: Colombard
var:colombard.description: Colombard is a white grape variety primarily used for producing dry and aromatic wines. It is known for its high acidity and light body, often resulting in crisp and refreshing styles. The grape thrives in warm climates and is commonly used in blends, particularly in the production of white wines.
var:colombard.style: Light-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

var:colorino.name: Colorino
var:colorino.description: Colorino is a red grape variety primarily used in blending, known for its deep color and tannic structure. It contributes to the complexity and aging potential of wines, particularly in Tuscan blends. The grape is often used alongside Sangiovese to enhance the overall profile of the wine.
var:colorino.style: Typically used in blends for color and tannin structure.

dim:color-intensity.name: Colour Intensity
dim:color-intensity.description: Colour intensity is how deep or pale a wine appears when you look through it. Tilt your glass over a white surface and observe: can you see through the wine easily (pale), or is it opaque and concentrated (deep)? Intensity depends on grape variety, winemaking, and age. Thick-skinned grapes like Syrah and Malbec produce deeply coloured wines; thin-skinned Pinot Noir and Nebbiolo are naturally paler. Young reds are vibrant; aged reds fade toward garnet and tawny. White wines gain colour with age, moving from pale lemon toward gold and amber.
keyGrapes: Aged Riesling (deep gold vs. pale youth)
dim:color-intensity.style: Tilt the glass over white paper — can you read through it?

geo:fr:cormontreuil.name: Cormontreuil
geo:fr:cormontreuil.description: Cormontreuil is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims subregion of Champagne. It is situated in the suburban periphery of Reims, contributing to the overall diversity of the Champagne terroir. The commune is rated at 94% on the Échelle des Crus, indicating its high quality within the classification system. The primary grape variety cultivated in Cormontreuil is Pinot Noir, which thrives in the region's chalky soils and favorable climate.
geo:fr:cormontreuil.style: Champagnes with a focus on structure and depth from Pinot Noir.

var:cortese.name: Cortese
var:cortese.description: Cortese is a white grape variety primarily grown in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with high acidity and crispness. The grape typically exhibits citrus and green apple flavors, making it suitable for early consumption.
var:cortese.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

var:corvina.name: Corvina
var:corvina.description: Corvina is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Veneto region of Italy. It is known for its role in the production of Valpolicella and Amarone wines. Corvina typically exhibits moderate acidity and tannins, contributing to light to medium-bodied wine styles.
var:corvina.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with moderate acidity and tannins.

geo:fr:coulommes-la-montagne.name: Coulommes-la-Montagne
geo:fr:coulommes-la-montagne.description: Coulommes-la-Montagne is classified as a Premier Cru commune located in the western part of the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne. It is characterized by its predominantly chalky soils, which contribute to the quality of the wines produced here. The commune is known for its production of both still and sparkling wines, primarily from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. The climate is continental, with influences from the nearby Montagne de Reims, which helps to create a suitable environment for viticulture.
geo:fr:coulommes-la-montagne.style: Produces primarily Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier-based Champagnes.

geo:fr:cramant.name: Cramant
geo:fr:cramant.description: Cramant is a Grand Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is renowned for its production of high-quality Blanc de Blancs, primarily made from Chardonnay grapes. The commune's distinctive terroir, characterized by chalky soils, imparts a mineral and citrus character to the wines. Cramant's vineyards benefit from a favorable microclimate that enhances the ripeness and acidity of the grapes, contributing to the complexity of the wines.
geo:fr:cramant.style: Mineral and citrus-driven Blanc de Blancs Champagne.

geo:fr:cuis.name: Cuis
geo:fr:cuis.description: Cuis is a Premier Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of Chardonnay, which is rated at 95%, and Pinot Noir, rated at 90%. The terroir of Cuis contributes to the distinct character of the wines produced here, with notable producers including Pierre Gimonnet & Fils.
geo:fr:cuis.style: Primarily produces high-quality Champagne with a focus on Chardonnay.

geo:fr:cumieres.name: Cumières
geo:fr:cumieres.description: Cumières is a Premier Cru commune located on the right bank of the Marne in the Vallée de la Marne. It is recognized for its production of high-quality Champagne, primarily from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. The terroir is characterized by a mix of clay and chalk soils, which contribute to the ripe, fruit-forward profile of the wines produced in this area. The commune has a Cru rating of 93%, indicating its esteemed status within the Champagne classification system.
geo:fr:cumieres.style: Ripe, fruit-forward Champagne with a focus on Pinot Noir.

var:delaware.name: Delaware
var:delaware.description: Delaware is a white grape variety known for its light, fruity, and sweet characteristics. It is primarily used in the production of aromatic wines, often exhibiting floral and fruity notes. The grape thrives in cooler climates and is particularly noted for its ability to produce wines with a refreshing acidity.
var:delaware.style: Light, fruity, and sweet white wines.

dim:development.name: Development
dim:development.description: Development describes where a wine sits in its aromatic evolution. Youthful wines show primary aromas — fresh fruit, floral notes directly from the grape. Developing wines gain secondary aromas from winemaking — toast, vanilla, butter from oak and malolactic fermentation. Fully developed wines display tertiary aromas from bottle aging — dried fruit, leather, mushroom, tobacco, honey, petrol. Most wines are drunk young; recognizing development helps you understand when a wine is at its peak and whether cellaring would add complexity.
dim:development.style: Is this wine showing fresh fruit, winemaking influence, or bottle-age character?

geo:fr:dizy.name: Dizy
geo:fr:dizy.description: Dizy is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, directly across the river from Épernay. It is recognized for its production of high-quality Champagne, particularly from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. The terroir of Dizy contributes to the elegance and finesse of its wines, with notable producers such as Jacquesson enhancing its reputation.
geo:fr:dizy.style: Champagnes characterized by elegance and finesse, primarily from Pinot Noir.

var:dolcetto.name: Dolcetto
var:dolcetto.description: Dolcetto is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with low acidity and soft tannins. The grape typically yields wines that are fruit-forward, often featuring flavors of black cherry and plum.
var:dolcetto.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with low acidity and soft tannins.

var:durella.name: Durella
var:durella.description: Durella is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and light body. It is primarily used in the production of sparkling wines, particularly in the Veneto region of Italy. The grape is characterized by its ability to retain freshness and vibrancy, making it suitable for both traditional and modern winemaking techniques.

var:encruzado.name: Encruzado
var:encruzado.description: Encruzado is a white grape variety primarily grown in Portugal, known for producing medium to full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a balanced acidity and can display a range of flavors, including citrus, stone fruit, and floral notes. The grape thrives in regions with a warm climate and well-drained soils, contributing to its complexity and aging potential.
var:encruzado.style: Medium to full-bodied white wines with balanced acidity.

var:erbaluce.name: Erbaluce
var:erbaluce.description: Erbaluce is a white grape variety primarily grown in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with a crisp acidity. The grape thrives in cooler climates, which helps to preserve its freshness and aromatic qualities.
var:erbaluce.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with refreshing acidity.

var:falanghina.name: Falanghina
var:falanghina.description: Falanghina is a white grape variety originating from Italy, primarily grown in the Campania region. It is known for producing wines that are typically light to medium-bodied, with a crisp acidity and floral notes. The grape thrives in warm climates, benefiting from well-drained soils, which contribute to its aromatic profile.
var:falanghina.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with crisp acidity and floral notes.

var:fer-servadou.name: Fer Servadou
var:fer-servadou.description: Fer Servadou is a red grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with rustic characteristics and notable tannins. It is primarily cultivated in the southwest of France, particularly in the regions of Gaillac and Marcillac. The grape is recognized for its ability to express the terroir and often features dark fruit flavors along with herbal and spicy notes.
var:fer-servadou.style: Medium-bodied, rustic, and tannic red wines.

var:fernao-pires.name: Fernão Pires
var:fernao-pires.description: Fernão Pires (known as Maria Gomes in Bairrada) is Portugal's most widely planted white grape. It produces light-bodied, aromatic wines with floral notes, citrus, and sometimes tropical fruit. Best drunk young, it delivers immediate freshness and approachability. Found throughout Portugal but particularly prominent in the Tejo and Bairrada regions, it is also used in sparkling wines.
var:fernao-pires.style: Light-bodied, aromatic with floral and citrus notes. Best young.

var:fiano.name: Fiano
var:fiano.description: Fiano is a white grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with a distinct aromatic profile. It typically exhibits floral and fruity notes, often with a hint of nuttiness. The grape thrives in warm climates and is characterized by moderate acidity.
var:fiano.style: Medium-bodied white wine with floral and fruity characteristics.

dim:finish.name: Finish
dim:finish.description: Finish is how long the wine's flavour persists after you swallow (or spit). A short finish fades in seconds; a long finish lingers for 30 seconds or more, evolving as it goes. Great wines often have finishes that reveal new flavours — a Riesling Spätlese might finish with honeyed minerality, while a Grand Cru Burgundy can linger with smoky, earthy persistence. Finish length generally correlates with wine quality: the longer and more complex the finish, the more interesting the wine.
dim:finish.style: After swallowing, count the seconds the flavour stays — quality wines keep talking.

dim:flavour-intensity.name: Flavour Intensity
dim:flavour-intensity.description: Flavour intensity is how concentrated the wine's taste is on your palate — the difference between a whisper and a shout. A light-intensity wine has delicate, subtle flavours that require attention; a pronounced wine fills your mouth with bold, concentrated taste. Intensity depends on grape variety, vineyard yield, climate, and winemaking: low-yield old vines in warm climates produce more concentrated flavours. Intensity is not the same as quality — a delicately flavoured Mosel Riesling can be as complex and rewarding as a powerfully intense Barossa Shiraz.
keyGrapes: Mosel Riesling (light to medium — delicate precision)
dim:flavour-intensity.style: Does the flavour whisper or shout? Both can be beautiful.

var:frappato.name: Frappato
var:frappato.description: Frappato is a red grape variety known for producing light-bodied wines with pronounced fruit characteristics. It typically exhibits bright acidity and low tannins, making it approachable and refreshing. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it develops its signature aromatic profile.
var:frappato.style: Light-bodied red wine with fruity notes and bright acidity.

var:freisa.name: Freisa
var:freisa.description: Freisa is a red grape variety originating from the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and tannins. The grape typically exhibits flavors of red fruits and floral notes, often with a hint of spice.
var:freisa.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with red fruit and floral characteristics.

var:friulano.name: Friulano
var:friulano.description: Friulano is a white grape variety known for producing medium-bodied wines with a balanced acidity. It typically exhibits floral and herbal notes, along with stone fruit characteristics. The grape thrives in regions with a moderate climate and well-drained soils.
var:friulano.style: Medium-bodied white with floral and herbal notes.

var:furmint.name: Furmint
var:furmint.description: Furmint is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and full-bodied character. It is primarily used in the production of dry and sweet wines, particularly in Hungary. The grape is notable for its ability to produce wines with complex flavors and a long aging potential.
var:furmint.style: Full-bodied white wine with high acidity.

var:gaglioppo.name: Gaglioppo
var:gaglioppo.description: Gaglioppo is a red grape variety primarily grown in southern Italy, particularly in Calabria. It is known for producing medium to full-bodied wines with notable tannins and acidity. The grape typically yields wines that exhibit dark fruit flavors and earthy notes, contributing to its distinctive character.
var:gaglioppo.style: Medium to full-bodied, tannic red wines with dark fruit and earthy notes.

var:gamay.name: Gamay
var:gamay.description: Gamay is a red grape variety known for producing light-bodied, fruity wines. It typically exhibits bright acidity and low tannins, making it approachable in youth. The grape thrives in cooler climates, where it develops its characteristic red fruit flavors.
var:gamay.style: Light-bodied red wines with fruity characteristics.

var:garganega.name: Garganega
var:garganega.description: Garganega is a white grape variety primarily grown in Italy, known for its role in producing Soave wines. It typically exhibits a light to medium body and can display floral and citrus characteristics. The grape thrives in regions with a warm climate, benefiting from well-drained volcanic soils.
var:garganega.style: Light to medium-bodied white with floral and citrus notes.

var:gewurztraminer.name: Gewürztraminer
var:gewurztraminer.description: Gewürztraminer is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and full-bodied character. It typically exhibits pronounced floral and spice notes, often accompanied by flavors of lychee, rose petal, and tropical fruits. The grape thrives in cooler climates, where it can develop high levels of sugar and acidity.
var:gewurztraminer.style: Full-bodied and aromatic with floral and spicy characteristics.

var:glera.name: Glera
var:glera.description: Glera is a white grape variety primarily known for its role in producing Prosecco. It typically exhibits a light-bodied structure and is characterized by its high acidity and floral, fruity aromas. Glera thrives in regions with a temperate climate, contributing to its popularity in sparkling wine production.
var:glera.style: Light-bodied, sparkling wines with floral and fruity notes.

var:godello.name: Godello
var:godello.description: Godello (known as Gouveio in Portugal) is a white grape variety native to the Iberian Peninsula. In Spain's Valdeorras and Bierzo, it produces medium-bodied wines with vibrant acidity, stone fruit, and mineral character. In Portugal's Douro Valley, as Gouveio, it contributes freshness and aromatic lift to white Douro blends. The grape was nearly extinct by the 1970s but has been revived to become one of Iberia's most exciting white varieties.
var:godello.style: Medium-bodied, aromatic with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral notes.

var:graciano.name: Graciano
var:graciano.description: Graciano is a red grape variety known for its full-bodied, aromatic wines. It typically exhibits high acidity and tannin levels, contributing to its aging potential. The grape is often used in blends, particularly in Spain, where it enhances the complexity and structure of red wines.
var:graciano.style: Full-bodied, aromatic red wine with high acidity and tannins.

geo:fr:grauves.name: Grauves
geo:fr:grauves.description: Grauves is a Premier Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is recognized for its high-quality production of sparkling wines, particularly from the Chardonnay and Pinot Noir grape varieties. The commune has a Cru rating of 95% for Chardonnay and 90% for Pinot Noir, indicating the quality of its terroir and the wines produced here.
geo:fr:grauves.style: Champagne with a focus on Chardonnay and Pinot Noir varietals.

var:grechetto.name: Grechetto
var:grechetto.description: Grechetto is a white grape variety primarily grown in central Italy, particularly in Umbria and Lazio. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines that exhibit freshness and a balanced acidity. The grape is often used in blends but can also be vinified as a varietal wine.
var:grechetto.style: Medium-bodied, fresh white wines with balanced acidity.

var:greco.name: Greco
var:greco.description: Greco is a white grape variety primarily grown in southern Italy, particularly in the Campania region. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with a balance of acidity and fruitiness. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used to create both dry and sweet styles.
var:greco.style: Medium-bodied white wines with balanced acidity and fruitiness.

var:grenache.name: Grenache
var:grenache.description: Grenache is a red grape variety known for producing medium to full-bodied wines with a fruity character. It typically exhibits flavors of red fruits, such as strawberry and raspberry, along with spice and herbal notes. Grenache thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends, contributing to the overall complexity of the wine.
var:grenache.style: Medium to full-bodied, fruity red wines.

var:grenache-blanc.name: Grenache Blanc
var:grenache-blanc.description: Grenache Blanc is a white grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with a rich texture. It typically exhibits flavors of stone fruits, citrus, and herbal notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends, particularly in regions known for producing Rhône-style wines.
var:grenache-blanc.style: Full-bodied white wine with rich texture and stone fruit flavors.

var:grignolino.name: Grignolino
var:grignolino.description: Grignolino is a red grape variety known for producing light-bodied wines with high acidity and moderate tannins. It typically exhibits red fruit characteristics and herbal notes. The grape thrives in regions with a temperate climate, where it can achieve optimal ripeness while maintaining its acidity.
var:grignolino.style: Light-bodied red wine with high acidity and moderate tannins.

var:grillo.name: Grillo
var:grillo.description: Grillo is a white grape variety originating from Sicily, Italy. It is primarily used in the production of dry white wines and is known for its ability to retain acidity in warm climates. The grape typically produces light to medium-bodied wines with a fresh and aromatic profile.
var:grillo.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with fresh acidity.

var:gros-manseng.name: Gros Manseng
var:gros-manseng.description: Gros Manseng is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and full-bodied character. It is primarily cultivated in the southwest of France, particularly in the regions of Jurançon and Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used to produce both dry and sweet wines.

var:gruner-veltliner.name: Grüner Veltliner
var:gruner-veltliner.description: Grüner Veltliner is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and versatility. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines that exhibit crispness and freshness. The grape is characterized by its ability to express terroir, often showcasing mineral notes alongside fruit flavors.
var:gruner-veltliner.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

geo:fr:hautvillers.name: Hautvillers
geo:fr:hautvillers.description: Hautvillers is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, renowned for its historical significance as the home of Dom Pérignon at the Abbey of Hautvillers. The commune is situated on a hilltop, providing a commanding view of the Marne valley and the surrounding vineyards. Its terroir is characterized by a mix of chalky soils and a favorable microclimate, contributing to the quality of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:hautvillers.style: Champagne with a focus on complexity and elegance.

var:hondarrabi-zuri.name: Hondarrabi Zuri
var:hondarrabi-zuri.description: Hondarrabi Zuri is a white grape variety primarily cultivated in the Basque Country of Spain and parts of France. It is known for its high acidity and crisp character, making it suitable for producing refreshing white wines. The grape is often used in the production of Txakoli, a lightly sparkling wine that is typically consumed young.

dim:hue.name: Hue
dim:hue.description: Hue is the actual colour of the wine — not how deep it is, but what colour it is. Red wines range from purple (young) through ruby (prime) to garnet and tawny (aged). White wines move from pale lemon-green (young, cool climate) through gold (richer or aged) to amber (very old or oxidative). Rosé spans from pale salmon to deep cherry-pink. Hue tells you about the wine's age and style before you even smell it: a brick-orange rim on a red suggests maturity; a green tinge on a white suggests youth and cool origin.
dim:hue.style: Look at the rim where wine meets glass — that's where hue is most revealing.

var:harslevelu.name: Hárslevelű
var:harslevelu.description: Hárslevelű is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and medium to full-bodied structure. It typically exhibits floral and fruity notes, contributing to complex white wines. The grape thrives in regions with a warm climate and well-drained soils.
var:harslevelu.style: Medium to full-bodied, aromatic white wines.

var:inzolia.name: Inzolia
var:inzolia.description: Inzolia is a white grape variety primarily grown in Sicily, Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity. The grape typically exhibits floral and citrus notes, contributing to its fresh and approachable character.
var:inzolia.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with floral and citrus notes.

var:jacquere.name: Jacquère
var:jacquere.description: Jacquère is a white grape variety primarily grown in the Savoie region of France. It is known for producing light-bodied, crisp wines with high acidity. The grape thrives in cool climates and is often used to create refreshing, mineral-driven white wines.
var:jacquere.style: Light-bodied and crisp with high acidity.

geo:fr:jouy-les-reims.name: Jouy-lès-Reims
geo:fr:jouy-les-reims.description: Jouy-lès-Reims is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of France. It is situated southwest of Reims and is recognized for its production of high-quality Champagne. The terroir is characterized by a mix of chalk and clay soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced in the area.
geo:fr:jouy-les-reims.style: Champagnes with depth and fruitiness, primarily from Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier.

var:kadarka.name: Kadarka
var:kadarka.description: Kadarka is a red grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits bright acidity and moderate tannins, making it suitable for early consumption. The grape is characterized by its fruity and floral notes, often with a hint of spice.
var:kadarka.style: Light to medium-bodied red with bright acidity and moderate tannins.

var:kerner.name: Kerner
var:kerner.description: Kerner is a white grape variety that originated in Germany, a cross between Riesling and Trollinger. It is known for its aromatic qualities and can produce wines that range from dry to sweet. The variety typically exhibits a light to medium body and is characterized by its crisp acidity.
var:kerner.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with aromatic qualities.

var:koshu.name: Koshu
var:koshu.description: Koshu is a white grape variety indigenous to Japan, primarily cultivated in the Yamanashi Prefecture. It is known for producing light and delicate wines with a focus on freshness and subtlety. The grape typically exhibits citrus and floral notes, contributing to its refreshing character.
var:koshu.style: Light, delicate wines with citrus and floral notes.

var:lagrein.name: Lagrein
var:lagrein.description: Lagrein is a red grape variety originating from the Alto Adige region of northern Italy. It is known for producing medium to full-bodied wines with a deep color and moderate acidity. The grape typically exhibits flavors of dark fruits, such as blackberry and plum, along with earthy and spicy notes.
var:lagrein.style: Medium to full-bodied red wine with dark fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:lambrusco.name: Lambrusco
var:lambrusco.description: Lambrusco is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy. It is known for producing light, fizzy wines that can range from dry to sweet. The grape is characterized by its vibrant acidity and fruit-forward profile.
var:lambrusco.style: Light, sparkling red wine with fruity and floral notes.

geo:fr:le-mesnil-sur-oger.name: Le Mesnil-sur-Oger
geo:fr:le-mesnil-sur-oger.description: Le Mesnil-sur-Oger is a Grand Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is renowned for its exceptional Chardonnay production, benefiting from deep chalk soils that contribute to the minerality and longevity of its wines. The commune is home to prestigious producers such as Salon and Krug, both known for their high-quality, single-vintage, single-village, and single-grape offerings. The wines from Le Mesnil-sur-Oger are characterized by their steely structure and are often regarded as some of the finest examples of Blanc de Blancs Champagne.
geo:fr:le-mesnil-sur-oger.style: Steely, long-lived Blanc de Blancs Champagne.

geo:fr:les-mesneux.name: Les Mesneux
geo:fr:les-mesneux.description: Les Mesneux is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated near the city of Reims and is known primarily for its production of high-quality Champagne. The terroir of Les Mesneux is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate, which contribute to the development of the grapes used in sparkling wine production.
