geo:fr:les-mesneux.style: Champagne with a focus on Pinot Noir-based blends.

var:loureiro.name: Loureiro
var:loureiro.description: Loureiro is a white grape variety primarily grown in Portugal, particularly in the Vinho Verde region. It is known for its aromatic qualities and is often used in blends, contributing floral and citrus notes. The grape typically produces light-bodied wines with a refreshing acidity.
var:loureiro.style: Light-bodied, aromatic white wines with floral and citrus characteristics.

geo:fr:louvois.name: Louvois
geo:fr:louvois.description: Louvois is a Grand Cru commune located in the southern part of the Montagne de Reims, near Bouzy. It is characterized by its predominantly Pinot Noir production, contributing to the region's reputation for high-quality Champagne. The small production volume is indicative of the commune's focus on quality over quantity.

geo:fr:ludes.name: Ludes
geo:fr:ludes.description: Ludes is classified as a Premier Cru commune located in the northern part of the Montagne de Reims, near Mailly-Champagne. The commune is known for its production of both Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, which thrive in the local terroir. Ludes has a cru rating of 94%, indicating its high-quality vineyard sites and favorable conditions for grape cultivation.
geo:fr:ludes.style: Produces high-quality Champagne with a focus on structure and finesse.

var:macabeo.name: Macabeo
var:macabeo.description: Macabeo is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits fresh acidity and moderate alcohol levels, making it suitable for various wine styles, including still and sparkling. The grape is often characterized by its floral and citrus notes.
var:macabeo.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with fresh acidity.

geo:fr:mailly-champagne.name: Mailly-Champagne
geo:fr:mailly-champagne.description: Mailly-Champagne is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is notable for its high-quality Pinot Noir production, which thrives in the area's chalky soils and favorable climate. The commune is home to the cooperative Mailly Grand Cru, recognized for its commitment to producing wines exclusively from this single village. Mailly-Champagne is characterized by its steep slopes and south-facing vineyards, which contribute to the ripening of grapes.
geo:fr:mailly-champagne.style: Rich, full-bodied Champagne with a focus on Pinot Noir.

var:malagousia.name: Malagousia
var:malagousia.description: Malagousia is a white grape variety indigenous to Greece, known for its aromatic qualities and medium body. It typically exhibits floral and fruity notes, contributing to a refreshing profile. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends or as a varietal wine.
var:malagousia.style: Medium-bodied, aromatic white wine with floral and fruity characteristics.

var:malbec.name: Malbec
var:malbec.description: Malbec is a red grape variety known for its deep color and full-bodied structure. It typically exhibits fruity characteristics, often featuring dark fruit flavors such as blackberry and plum. The grape thrives in regions with warm climates and well-drained soils, allowing for optimal ripening.
var:malbec.style: Full-bodied red with fruity flavors and a rich mouthfeel.

var:malvasia.name: Malvasia
var:malvasia.description: Malvasia is a white grape variety known for its aromatic and rich characteristics. It is often used in the production of both dry and sweet wines, exhibiting a range of floral and fruity notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is cultivated in various regions around the world.
var:malvasia.style: Aromatic and rich white wines, often with floral and fruity notes.

var:mammolo.name: Mammolo
var:mammolo.description: Mammolo is a red grape variety primarily used in blending, known for its aromatic qualities. It typically contributes floral and fruity notes to wine, enhancing the complexity of blends. The grape is characterized by moderate acidity and tannin levels, making it suitable for various wine styles.
var:mammolo.style: Aromatic blending grape with floral and fruity characteristics.

geo:fr:mareuil-sur-ay.name: Mareuil-sur-Aÿ
geo:fr:mareuil-sur-ay.description: Mareuil-sur-Aÿ is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne region of Champagne, France. It is situated adjacent to the Grand Cru commune of Aÿ, benefiting from similar terroir characteristics. The commune is noted for its production of high-quality Champagne, particularly from the Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grape varieties. Notably, it is home to the renowned Clos des Goisses vineyard, owned by the Philipponnat Champagne house.
geo:fr:mareuil-sur-ay.style: Champagnes with complexity and depth, often showcasing rich fruit and minerality.

var:marsanne.name: Marsanne
var:marsanne.description: Marsanne is a white grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with a rich texture. It typically exhibits flavors of stone fruits, honey, and floral notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often blended with other varieties to enhance complexity.
var:marsanne.style: Full-bodied white wines with rich texture and stone fruit flavors.

var:marselan.name: Marselan
var:marselan.description: Marselan is a red grape variety created in 1961 by crossing Cabernet Sauvignon and Grenache at the INRA research station in Marseillan, France. Originally developed for disease resistance in Languedoc, it has found particular success in China, where it produces deeply colored, full-bodied wines with plum and spice character. It is now considered one of the signature varieties of Chinese wine regions, especially Ningxia and Xinjiang.
var:marselan.style: Full-bodied red with deep color, plum, and spice notes

var:mavrud.name: Mavrud
var:mavrud.description: Mavrud is a red grape variety indigenous to Bulgaria, known for producing full-bodied wines with deep color and robust tannins. It typically exhibits flavors of dark fruits, spices, and earthy notes. Mavrud thrives in warmer climates and is often associated with the Thracian Valley region.
var:mavrud.style: Full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:melon-de-bourgogne.name: Melon de Bourgogne
var:melon-de-bourgogne.description: Melon de Bourgogne is a white grape variety known for its light body and crisp acidity. It is primarily used to produce dry white wines, particularly in the Muscadet region of France. The grape thrives in cooler climates and is often associated with maritime influences.
var:melon-de-bourgogne.style: Light-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

var:mencia.name: Mencía
var:mencia.description: Mencía is a red grape variety primarily grown in northwest Spain, particularly in the regions of Bierzo, Ribeira Sacra, and Valdeorras. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and soft tannins. The grape typically exhibits red fruit flavors, floral notes, and mineral characteristics, often reflecting the terroir of its growing regions.
var:mencia.style: Medium-bodied red with red fruit and floral notes.

var:merlot.name: Merlot
var:merlot.description: Merlot is a red grape variety known for its soft tannins and approachable style. It typically produces medium to full-bodied wines with a smooth texture and fruit-forward character. The grape thrives in a variety of climates and is often blended with other varieties, particularly Cabernet Sauvignon.
var:merlot.style: Medium to full-bodied, soft with fruit-forward characteristics.

var:molinara.name: Molinara
var:molinara.description: Molinara is a red grape variety primarily used as a blending component in Italian wines. It is known for its light color and moderate acidity, contributing freshness and floral notes to blends.
var:molinara.style: Primarily used in blends, adds acidity and floral characteristics.

geo:fr:montbre.name: Montbré
geo:fr:montbre.description: Montbré is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims subregion of Champagne, France. It is situated near Reims and is recognized for its high-quality Pinot Noir production. The commune has a Cru rating of 94%, indicating its esteemed status within the Champagne classification system. The terroir is characterized by chalky soils and a favorable climate for grape cultivation, contributing to the complexity of its wines.
geo:fr:montbre.style: Primarily produces structured, fruit-forward Pinot Noir-based Champagnes.

var:montepulciano.name: Montepulciano
var:montepulciano.description: Montepulciano is a red grape variety known for producing medium to full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a deep color and moderate acidity, with flavors of dark fruit and earthy undertones. The grape thrives in a variety of climates, often resulting in wines that are approachable when young but can also age well.
var:montepulciano.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and earthy notes.

var:moschofilero.name: Moschofilero
var:moschofilero.description: Moschofilero is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and light body. It typically exhibits floral and citrus notes, contributing to its refreshing character. The grape thrives in warmer climates and is often used to produce dry, crisp wines.
var:moschofilero.style: Light-bodied, aromatic white wine with floral and citrus notes.

var:mourvedre.name: Mourvèdre
var:mourvedre.description: Mourvèdre is a red grape variety known for producing full-bodied, tannic wines. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and earthy notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in blends, particularly in regions such as the Southern Rhône and Spain.
var:mourvedre.style: Full-bodied and tannic with dark fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:mtsvane.name: Mtsvane
var:mtsvane.description: Mtsvane is a white grape variety originating from Georgia, known for its aromatic qualities. It typically produces medium-bodied wines with a range of floral and fruity notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often used in both traditional and modern winemaking techniques.
var:mtsvane.style: Medium-bodied, aromatic white wine with floral and fruity characteristics.

var:muscat.name: Muscat
var:muscat.description: Muscat is a family of grape varieties known for their distinctive floral and fruity aromas. It is typically light to medium-bodied and can be produced in a range of styles, from dry to sweet. Muscat grapes thrive in warm climates and are often used in both still and sparkling wines.
var:muscat.style: Light to medium-bodied, aromatic with floral and fruity notes.

var:muscat-bailey-a.name: Muscat Bailey A
var:muscat-bailey-a.description: Muscat Bailey A is a red grape variety developed in Japan, known for its light-bodied and fruity wines. It is a hybrid of the Muscat of Alexandria and the Bailey grape, resulting in a unique flavor profile that often includes notes of strawberry. The variety is primarily cultivated in Japan, where it is appreciated for its early ripening and adaptability to various climates.
var:muscat-bailey-a.style: Light-bodied, fruity wines with strawberry notes.

geo:fr:mutigny.name: Mutigny
geo:fr:mutigny.description: Mutigny is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, situated between the villages of Aÿ and Hautvillers. It is recognized for its high-quality vineyards and is rated at 93% on the Échelle des Crus. The commune benefits from a favorable microclimate that supports the cultivation of key grape varieties.
geo:fr:mutigny.style: Produces predominantly high-quality Champagne with depth and complexity.

var:muller-thurgau.name: Müller-Thurgau
var:muller-thurgau.description: Müller-Thurgau is a white grape variety resulting from a cross between Riesling and Madeleine Royale. It is known for its early ripening and high yields, often producing light-bodied wines with moderate acidity. The grape is cultivated in various regions, primarily in Germany and Switzerland, where it is appreciated for its versatility and adaptability.
var:muller-thurgau.style: Light-bodied white wines with moderate acidity and floral notes.

var:nascetta.name: Nascetta
var:nascetta.description: Nascetta is a white grape variety indigenous to the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with a balanced acidity and aromatic complexity. The grape typically exhibits floral and fruity notes, contributing to its distinctive character.
var:nascetta.style: Medium-bodied white wine with floral and fruity aromas.

var:nebbiolo.name: Nebbiolo
var:nebbiolo.description: Nebbiolo is a red grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with high tannins and acidity. It is characterized by its complex aromas and flavors, often including notes of cherry, rose, tar, and earth. The grape typically thrives in cooler climates, which help preserve its acidity and aromatic profile.
var:nebbiolo.style: Full-bodied, tannic, high acid red wines.

var:negroamaro.name: Negroamaro
var:negroamaro.description: Negroamaro is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Puglia region of Italy. It is known for producing full-bodied wines with rich flavors and a deep color. The grape thrives in warm climates, often resulting in wines with high alcohol content and moderate acidity.
var:negroamaro.style: Full-bodied red wines with rich flavors and deep color.

var:nerello-mascalese.name: Nerello Mascalese
var:nerello-mascalese.description: Nerello Mascalese is a red grape variety indigenous to Sicily, primarily grown on the slopes of Mount Etna. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with good acidity and complexity. The variety often exhibits aromas of red fruits, floral notes, and earthy undertones, contributing to its elegant profile.
var:nerello-mascalese.style: Medium-bodied, elegant red wines with good acidity and complexity.

var:nero-d-avola.name: Nero d'Avola
var:nero-d-avola.description: Nero d'Avola is a red grape variety originating from Sicily, known for producing full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and a robust structure, making it suitable for aging. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often characterized by its high acidity and tannin levels.
var:nero-d-avola.style: Full-bodied red wine with dark fruit flavors and firm tannins.

var:nero-di-troia.name: Nero di Troia
var:nero-di-troia.description: Nero di Troia is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Puglia region of Italy. It is known for producing full-bodied wines with significant tannins and acidity, which contribute to its aging potential. The grape typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and earthy undertones.
var:nero-di-troia.style: Full-bodied, tannic red wines with dark fruit and earthy notes.

var:niagara.name: Niagara
var:niagara.description: Niagara is an American hybrid grape variety, a cross of Vitis labrusca, developed in Niagara County, New York. It is primarily a white grape used for producing sweet and semi-sweet wines. The grape is characterized by its pronounced grapey and foxy aroma, making it distinctive among other varieties.
var:niagara.style: Primarily used for sweet and semi-sweet white wines.

var:nosiola.name: Nosiola
var:nosiola.description: Nosiola is a white grape variety primarily grown in northern Italy. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity. The grape thrives in cooler climates, contributing to its crisp and refreshing character.
var:nosiola.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with moderate acidity.

var:negrette.name: Négrette
var:negrette.description: Négrette is a red grape variety primarily grown in the southwest of France. It is known for producing medium-bodied wines with fruity characteristics and floral notes, particularly of violet. The grape typically exhibits a soft tannin structure and is often blended with other varieties to enhance complexity.
var:negrette.style: Medium-bodied, fruity with floral notes, especially violet.

geo:fr:oger.name: Oger
geo:fr:oger.description: Oger is a Grand Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of Chardonnay, which thrives in the chalky soils characteristic of the area. The commune is adjacent to Le Mesnil-sur-Oger and is recognized for producing elegant Champagnes that often exhibit floral and mineral notes. Oger holds a Cru rating of 100%, indicating its esteemed status within the Champagne classification system.
geo:fr:oger.style: Elegant Champagnes with floral and mineral characteristics.

geo:fr:oiry.name: Oiry
geo:fr:oiry.description: Oiry is a Grand Cru commune located at the northern end of the Côte des Blancs in the Champagne region of France. It is situated near Chouilly and is characterized by its chalky soils, which are conducive to the cultivation of high-quality Chardonnay grapes. The commune is recognized for its exceptional terroir, contributing to the production of elegant and refined Champagne.
geo:fr:oiry.style: Produces elegant and refined Chardonnay-based Champagnes.

var:palomino.name: Palomino
var:palomino.description: Palomino is a white grape variety primarily used in the production of Sherry. It is characterized by its light body and neutral flavor profile, making it suitable for fortification and blending. The grape thrives in warm climates and is known for its ability to produce wines with high acidity.
var:palomino.style: Light-bodied and neutral, often used for Sherry production.

var:parellada.name: Parellada
var:parellada.description: Parellada is a white grape variety primarily used in the production of sparkling wines, particularly Cava. It is known for its high acidity and ability to retain freshness, contributing to light-bodied wine styles. The grape typically exhibits floral and citrus characteristics.
var:parellada.style: Light-bodied white wine with high acidity and citrus notes.

geo:fr:pargny-les-reims.name: Pargny-lès-Reims
geo:fr:pargny-les-reims.description: Pargny-lès-Reims is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated to the west of Reims and is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate for viticulture. The commune primarily produces sparkling wines, utilizing traditional Champagne methods.
geo:fr:pargny-les-reims.style: Champagne with a focus on Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier.

var:pecorino.name: Pecorino
var:pecorino.description: Pecorino is a white grape variety originating from Italy, primarily known for its use in the production of dry white wines. It typically exhibits a medium body and balanced acidity, making it suitable for various food pairings. The grape thrives in regions with warm climates and well-drained soils.
var:pecorino.style: Medium-bodied white wine with balanced acidity.

var:pedro-ximenez.name: Pedro Ximénez
var:pedro-ximenez.description: Pedro Ximénez is a white grape variety primarily used for producing sweet wines. It is known for its high sugar content and is often dried in the sun to concentrate flavors, resulting in rich, full-bodied wines.
var:pedro-ximenez.style: Full-bodied, sweet wines with intense flavors and high residual sugar.

var:pelaverga.name: Pelaverga
var:pelaverga.description: Pelaverga is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Piedmont region of Italy. It is known for producing light-bodied wines with a distinctive aromatic profile. The grape typically exhibits bright acidity and moderate tannins, making it suitable for early consumption.
var:pelaverga.style: Light-bodied red wine with bright acidity and aromatic complexity.

var:petit-manseng.name: Petit Manseng
var:petit-manseng.description: Petit Manseng is a white grape variety known for its high sugar concentration and acidity. It is primarily used to produce rich, sweet wines, often through late harvesting or botrytization. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it can achieve optimal ripeness.
var:petit-manseng.style: Rich, sweet wines with high acidity and complex flavors.

var:petit-verdot.name: Petit Verdot
var:petit-verdot.description: Petit Verdot is a red grape variety known for its deep color and high tannin content. It typically produces full-bodied wines with a robust structure and is often used as a blending component in Bordeaux-style blends. The grape thrives in warm climates and is valued for its ability to add depth and complexity to wine.
var:petit-verdot.style: Full-bodied, tannic red wine with deep color and robust structure.

var:petite-sirah.name: Petite Sirah
var:petite-sirah.description: Petite Sirah is a red grape variety known for its deep color and robust tannins. It typically produces full-bodied wines with a rich flavor profile that may include dark fruit, pepper, and sometimes floral notes. The grape thrives in warmer climates and is often blended with other varieties to enhance structure and complexity.
var:petite-sirah.style: Full-bodied, very tannic red wine with dark fruit flavors.

var:picpoul.name: Picpoul
var:picpoul.description: Picpoul is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and refreshing qualities. It typically produces light-bodied wines that are crisp and zesty, making it suitable for warm climates. The grape thrives in regions with ample sunshine, contributing to its vibrant character.
var:picpoul.style: Light-bodied, crisp white wine with high acidity.

geo:fr:pierry.name: Pierry
geo:fr:pierry.description: Pierry is a Premier Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, adjacent to Épernay. It serves as a transitional area between the Vallée de la Marne and the Côte des Blancs, characterized by its diverse terroir. The commune is known for producing high-quality Champagne, leveraging both Chardonnay and Pinot Noir grapes.
geo:fr:pierry.style: Champagne with a balance of freshness and richness.

var:pinot-blanc.name: Pinot Blanc
var:pinot-blanc.description: Pinot Blanc is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits flavors of apple, pear, and floral notes, with moderate acidity. The grape thrives in cooler climates and is often used in both still and sparkling wine production.
var:pinot-blanc.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with fruit and floral characteristics.

var:pinot-grigio.name: Pinot Grigio
var:pinot-grigio.description: Pinot Grigio is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a crisp acidity and can display flavors ranging from citrus to stone fruits. The grape is a mutation of Pinot Noir and is widely cultivated in various regions around the world.
var:pinot-grigio.style: Light to medium-bodied with crisp acidity and fruit-forward flavors.

var:pinot-meunier.name: Pinot Meunier
var:pinot-meunier.description: Pinot Meunier is a red grape variety known for its light-bodied, fruity wines. It is a key component in the production of Champagne, contributing to the blend's complexity and fruitiness. The grape typically exhibits a lower acidity compared to other varieties used in sparkling wine production.
var:pinot-meunier.style: Light-bodied, fruity wines often used in sparkling wine blends.

var:pinot-noir.name: Pinot Noir
var:pinot-noir.description: Pinot Noir is a red grape variety known for its light to medium-bodied wines, characterized by elegance and complexity. It typically exhibits red fruit flavors, floral notes, and earthy undertones. The grape is sensitive to climate and terroir, which can significantly influence its expression.
var:pinot-noir.style: Light to medium-bodied, elegant with red fruit and earthy notes.

var:pinotage.name: Pinotage
var:pinotage.description: Pinotage is a red grape variety that is a cross between Pinot Noir and Cinsault. It is known for its medium to full-bodied structure and can exhibit a range of flavors including dark fruit, smoke, and earthy notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is primarily associated with South African wine production.
var:pinotage.style: Medium to full-bodied red with dark fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:plavac-mali.name: Plavac Mali
var:plavac-mali.description: Plavac Mali is a red grape variety primarily grown in Croatia, known for producing full-bodied wines. It is a descendant of the Zinfandel grape and is characterized by its robust tannins and high acidity. The grape typically yields wines with dark fruit flavors and a notable structure, making it suitable for aging.
var:plavac-mali.style: Full-bodied red wines with dark fruit flavors and strong tannins.

geo:fr:puisieulx.name: Puisieulx
geo:fr:puisieulx.description: Puisieulx is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is a small village situated near Sillery, known for its high-quality vineyards. The commune is recognized for its exceptional terroir, which contributes to the production of premium Champagne wines.
geo:fr:puisieulx.style: Produces high-quality Champagne with complexity and depth.

var:refosco.name: Refosco
var:refosco.description: Refosco is a red grape variety known for producing medium to full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a deep color and moderate acidity, often characterized by dark fruit flavors and earthy notes. The grape thrives in various climates, particularly in regions with warm summers and cool nights, allowing for balanced ripening.
var:refosco.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and earthy flavors.

var:ribolla-gialla.name: Ribolla Gialla
var:ribolla-gialla.description: Ribolla Gialla is a white grape variety known for its light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a crisp acidity and can display floral and citrus characteristics. The grape is primarily cultivated in northeastern Italy and has a history dating back to ancient times.
var:ribolla-gialla.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp with floral and citrus notes.

var:riesling.name: Riesling
var:riesling.description: Riesling is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and aromatic profile. It is often produced in a range of styles from dry to sweet, and is characterized by floral and fruity notes. The grape thrives in cooler climates, allowing it to retain acidity while developing complex flavors.
var:riesling.style: Light-bodied, high acid, versatile in sweetness levels.

geo:fr:rilly-la-montagne.name: Rilly-la-Montagne
geo:fr:rilly-la-montagne.description: Rilly-la-Montagne is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated on the northern slopes of the Montagne de Reims, benefiting from a favorable microclimate for viticulture. The commune is known for the cultivation of all three primary Champagne grape varieties: Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot Meunier. The terroir is characterized by chalky soils, which contribute to the complexity of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:rilly-la-montagne.style: Champagne with a balance of fruit and acidity.

var:rkatsiteli.name: Rkatsiteli
var:rkatsiteli.description: Rkatsiteli is a white grape variety known for its medium body and crisp acidity. It typically exhibits flavors of green apple, pear, and citrus, often with a distinct herbal or floral note. This grape thrives in various climates, particularly in regions with warm days and cool nights.
var:rkatsiteli.style: Medium-bodied white wine with crisp acidity and herbal notes.

var:roditis.name: Roditis
var:roditis.description: Roditis is a white grape variety primarily grown in Greece. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity. The grape typically exhibits floral and citrus characteristics, making it suitable for both still and sparkling wine production.
var:roditis.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with floral and citrus notes.

var:romorantin.name: Romorantin
var:romorantin.description: Romorantin is a white grape variety indigenous to the Loire Valley in France, primarily associated with the Cheverny and Cour-Cheverny appellations. It is known for its medium-bodied wines that often exhibit a mineral character. The grape has a long history, dating back to the 16th century, and is recognized for its ability to produce age-worthy white wines.

var:rondinella.name: Rondinella
var:rondinella.description: Rondinella is a red grape variety primarily used in the production of blended wines, particularly in the Veneto region of Italy. It is known for its ability to contribute color and fruitiness to wine blends, often complementing other varieties such as Corvina and Molinara. Rondinella typically exhibits moderate acidity and tannins, making it suitable for various wine styles.
var:rondinella.style: Primarily used in blends, adds color and fruitiness.

var:roussanne.name: Roussanne
var:roussanne.description: Roussanne is a white grape variety known for its full-bodied, aromatic wines. It typically exhibits floral and stone fruit characteristics, often with a rich mouthfeel and moderate acidity. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often blended with other varieties, particularly in Rhône-style wines.
var:roussanne.style: Full-bodied, aromatic white wines with floral and stone fruit notes.

var:ruche.name: Ruché
var:ruche.description: Ruché is a red grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and medium-bodied structure. It typically exhibits floral and fruity notes, contributing to its distinctive profile. The grape thrives in specific terroirs that allow for the expression of its unique characteristics.
var:ruche.style: Medium-bodied, aromatic red wine with floral and fruity notes.

geo:fr:sacy.name: Sacy
geo:fr:sacy.description: Sacy is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims subregion of Champagne, France. It is primarily recognized for its production of sparkling wines, particularly those made from Pinot Meunier and Pinot Noir grapes. The commune's vineyards benefit from the region's chalky soils and favorable climate, contributing to the distinct character of its wines.
geo:fr:sacy.style: Notable for producing structured and fruit-forward Champagnes.

var:sagrantino.name: Sagrantino
var:sagrantino.description: Sagrantino is a red grape variety known for its high tannin content and full-bodied character. It is primarily cultivated in the Umbria region of Italy, where it is often used to produce robust red wines that can age well. The grape is notable for its deep color and potential for complex flavor development.
var:sagrantino.style: Full-bodied, very tannic red wine with aging potential.

var:sangiovese.name: Sangiovese
var:sangiovese.description: Sangiovese is a red grape variety known for its high acidity and moderate to full body. It typically produces wines with flavors of cherry, plum, and earthy notes. The grape is highly adaptable, thriving in various climates and soils.
var:sangiovese.style: Medium to full-bodied with high acidity.

var:saperavi.name: Saperavi
var:saperavi.description: Saperavi is a red grape variety known for its deep color and high acidity. It is characterized by its full-bodied structure and pronounced tannins, making it suitable for aging. The grape is primarily cultivated in Georgia, where it is a key component of traditional winemaking.
var:saperavi.style: Full-bodied and tannic red wine with deep color.

var:sauvignon-blanc.name: Sauvignon Blanc
var:sauvignon-blanc.description: Sauvignon Blanc is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and distinctive aromatic profile. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines that are crisp and refreshing. The grape thrives in a variety of climates, often exhibiting green and herbaceous notes, alongside citrus and tropical fruit characteristics.
var:sauvignon-blanc.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp with high acidity.

var:savagnin.name: Savagnin
var:savagnin.description: Savagnin is a white grape variety known for its full-bodied character and oxidative style. It is often associated with the Jura region of France, where it is used to produce both still and vin jaune wines. The grape is characterized by its high acidity and ability to age, leading to complex flavors and aromas.

var:scheurebe.name: Scheurebe
var:scheurebe.description: Scheurebe is a white grape variety that is a cross between Riesling and a variety of unknown parentage. It is known for its aromatic qualities and can produce wines that range from dry to sweet. The grape typically exhibits a good balance of acidity and fruitiness, making it versatile in various wine styles.
var:scheurebe.style: Light to medium-bodied, aromatic white wines.

var:schioppettino.name: Schioppettino
var:schioppettino.description: Schioppettino is a red grape variety known for its medium-bodied wines with a distinctive peppery character. It typically exhibits moderate acidity and tannins, making it approachable yet complex. The grape thrives in specific terroirs that enhance its unique flavor profile.
var:schioppettino.style: Medium-bodied red with peppery notes and moderate acidity.

var:sercial.name: Sercial
var:sercial.description: Sercial is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and dry character. It is primarily used in the production of Madeira wine, contributing to the style's distinctive freshness and longevity. The grape typically produces wines that are crisp and can exhibit a range of flavors depending on the aging process.
var:sercial.style: High-acid, dry white wine with crisp character.

geo:fr:sermiers.name: Sermiers
geo:fr:sermiers.description: Sermiers is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is recognized for its production of high-quality sparkling wines, primarily from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. The commune's vineyards benefit from the region's chalky soils and favorable climate, contributing to the distinctive character of its wines.
geo:fr:sermiers.style: Champagne with a focus on Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier.

geo:fr:sillery.name: Sillery
geo:fr:sillery.description: Sillery is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated on the plain near Reims and has historical significance as one of the most esteemed wine names during the 17th and 18th centuries. The commune is recognized for its high-quality production of Champagne, particularly from its dominant grape varieties.
geo:fr:sillery.style: Champagnes with complexity and depth, reflecting the terroir of Sillery.

var:silvaner.name: Silvaner
var:silvaner.description: Silvaner is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a crisp acidity and can display flavors ranging from green apple to floral notes. The grape thrives in cooler climates and is often associated with regions that emphasize freshness and minerality in their wine profiles.
var:silvaner.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with crisp acidity.

var:st-laurent.name: St. Laurent
var:st-laurent.description: St. Laurent is a red grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It is characterized by its moderate acidity and tannin levels, often exhibiting flavors of dark fruits and spices. The grape thrives in cooler climates, where it can develop complexity and depth.
var:st-laurent.style: Light to medium-bodied red wines with dark fruit and spice notes.

var:steuben.name: Steuben
var:steuben.description: Steuben is an American hybrid grape variety, a cross of Vitis labrusca, developed at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, New York. It is characterized by its sweet, fruity profile and is particularly popular in Japan, especially in Aomori Prefecture. The grape exhibits a pronounced foxy character, typical of many labrusca varieties.
var:steuben.style: Sweet, fruity red wines with a pronounced foxy character.

dim:sweetness.name: Sweetness
dim:sweetness.description: Sweetness in wine comes from residual sugar — grape sugar that wasn't converted to alcohol during fermentation. Dry wines have little to no residual sugar (under 4 g/L); off-dry wines have a hint of sweetness that balances acidity; sweet wines like Sauternes or Tokaji can have over 100 g/L. Perception of sweetness is affected by acidity and tannin: high acidity makes a wine taste drier than its sugar level suggests. Many people confuse fruitiness with sweetness — a wine can taste of ripe fruit while being technically dry.
dim:sweetness.style: Focus on the tip of your tongue — sweetness registers there first.

var:syrah.name: Syrah
var:syrah.description: Syrah is a red grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with a rich flavor profile. It typically exhibits spicy notes, along with dark fruit characteristics such as blackberry and plum. The grape thrives in various climates, often benefiting from warm conditions that enhance its ripeness and complexity.
var:syrah.style: Full-bodied red with spicy and dark fruit flavors.

var:semillon.name: Sémillon
var:semillon.description: Sémillon is a white grape variety known for producing full-bodied and rich wines. It is characterized by its ability to age well and develop complex flavors, often exhibiting notes of stone fruit, honey, and wax. The grape thrives in warm climates and is particularly notable for its use in both dry and sweet wine production.
var:semillon.style: Full-bodied, rich white wines with aging potential.

geo:fr:taissy.name: Taissy
geo:fr:taissy.description: Taissy is a Premier Cru commune located on the eastern periphery of Reims in the Montagne de Reims region. It holds a Cru rating of 94%, indicating its high quality within the Champagne classification system. The commune is primarily known for its production of Pinot Noir, which thrives in its terroir characterized by chalky soils and a favorable climate for grape cultivation.
geo:fr:taissy.style: Champagnes primarily featuring Pinot Noir with structured profiles.

var:tannat.name: Tannat
var:tannat.description: Tannat is a red grape variety known for its high tannin content and full-bodied structure. It typically exhibits dark fruit flavors and can develop complex secondary notes with age. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it achieves optimal ripeness.

dim:tannin.name: Tannin
dim:tannin.description: Tannin is the drying, gripping sensation you feel on your gums and the inside of your cheeks — like over-steeped black tea or biting an unripe grape skin. It comes from grape skins, seeds, stems, and oak barrels. Red wines have more tannin because the juice ferments in contact with skins. High tannin provides structure and aging potential; it softens over time as tannin molecules polymerize. Young Nebbiolo or Cabernet Sauvignon can be intensely tannic, while Pinot Noir and Gamay are naturally lower.
dim:tannin.style: Run your tongue along your gums — the drying grip is tannin.

geo:fr:tauxieres-mutry.name: Tauxières-Mutry
geo:fr:tauxieres-mutry.description: Tauxières-Mutry is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims subregion of Champagne, France. It is recognized for its high-quality production of Champagne, with a cru rating of 99%, indicating its proximity to Grand Cru status. The commune benefits from a favorable terroir that supports the cultivation of both Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes, contributing to the complexity and character of its wines.
geo:fr:tauxieres-mutry.style: Champagnes with complexity and finesse, primarily from Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

var:tempranillo.name: Tempranillo
var:tempranillo.description: Tempranillo is a red grape variety known for producing medium to full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits flavors of cherry, plum, and dried fig, often accompanied by earthy and herbal notes. The grape is characterized by moderate acidity and tannin levels, contributing to its aging potential.
var:tempranillo.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:tempranillo-blanco.name: Tempranillo Blanco
var:tempranillo-blanco.description: Tempranillo Blanco is a natural white mutation of Tempranillo, discovered in 1988 in a single vineyard in Rioja, Spain. It was officially authorized for Rioja DOCa wines in 2007. The grape produces aromatic, medium-bodied white wines with notes of tropical fruit, herbs, and good acidity. It is almost exclusively planted in Rioja Alta.
var:tempranillo-blanco.style: Aromatic, medium-bodied white with tropical fruit and herbal notes.

var:teroldego.name: Teroldego
var:teroldego.description: Teroldego is a red grape variety known for producing medium to full-bodied wines. It typically exhibits a deep color and offers flavors of dark fruits, such as blackberry and plum, along with earthy and spicy notes. The grape thrives in regions with warm climates and well-drained soils.
var:teroldego.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with dark fruit and earthy characteristics.

var:timorasso.name: Timorasso
var:timorasso.description: Timorasso is a white grape variety known for producing full-bodied wines with good acidity and complexity. It is characterized by its rich texture and potential for aging, often exhibiting flavors of stone fruits, citrus, and floral notes. The grape thrives in specific terroirs that allow for optimal ripening and expression.
var:timorasso.style: Full-bodied white wine with rich texture and good acidity.

var:tinta-negra.name: Tinta Negra
var:tinta-negra.description: Tinta Negra is a red grape variety primarily grown in the Madeira region of Portugal. It is known for its adaptability to various growing conditions and is often used in the production of both dry and sweet wines. The grape typically produces medium-bodied wines with a range of flavor profiles.

var:torrontes.name: Torrontés
var:torrontes.description: Torrontés is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and floral notes. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines with a refreshing acidity. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it can develop its characteristic aromas and flavors.
var:torrontes.style: Light to medium-bodied, aromatic white wines with floral notes.

var:touriga-franca.name: Touriga Franca
var:touriga-franca.description: Touriga Franca is a red grape variety known for its role in the production of Port and other red wines. It typically exhibits a medium to full-bodied structure, with a balance of acidity and tannins. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often blended with other varieties to enhance complexity.
var:touriga-franca.style: Medium to full-bodied red wines with balanced acidity and tannins.

var:touriga-nacional.name: Touriga Nacional
var:touriga-nacional.description: Touriga Nacional is a red grape variety known for its full-bodied structure and aromatic complexity. It is characterized by high tannins and acidity, which contribute to its aging potential. The grape is often used in blends but can also produce varietal wines with rich flavors.
var:touriga-nacional.style: Full-bodied and aromatic with high tannins and acidity.

geo:fr:tours-sur-marne.name: Tours-sur-Marne
geo:fr:tours-sur-marne.description: Tours-sur-Marne is a Grand Cru commune located in the Vallée de la Marne, where it meets the Côte des Blancs. It is recognized for its significant production of Champagne, particularly from the Pinot Noir grape, which holds a Grand Cru status with a rating of 100%. Chardonnay from this commune is classified as Premier Cru, with a rating of 90%. The terroir is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate for grape cultivation.
geo:fr:tours-sur-marne.style: Champagnes with depth and complexity, primarily from Pinot Noir.

var:trebbiano.name: Trebbiano
var:trebbiano.description: Trebbiano is a white grape variety known for its light-bodied and neutral character. It is widely cultivated in various regions, often used for producing dry white wines and as a blending component. The grape typically exhibits high acidity and is capable of reflecting the terroir of its growing environment.
var:trebbiano.style: Light-bodied, neutral white wines with high acidity.

var:trincadeira.name: Trincadeira
var:trincadeira.description: Trincadeira (known as Tinta Amarela in the Douro) is a red grape variety widely grown across southern Portugal, especially in the Alentejo. It produces medium to full-bodied wines with spicy, herbal character and notes of dark plum, blackberry, and garrigue. It can be challenging to grow — prone to rot in humid conditions — but in the right hands produces wines of considerable depth and complexity. A key blending partner in Alentejo reds.
var:trincadeira.style: Medium to full-bodied, spicy with dark fruit and herbal notes.

geo:fr:trois-puits.name: Trois-Puits
geo:fr:trois-puits.description: Trois-Puits is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated near the city of Reims and is recognized for its high-quality Pinot Noir production. The commune is classified with a rating of 94% on the Échelle des Crus, indicating its esteemed status within the Champagne hierarchy.
geo:fr:trois-puits.style: Primarily produces structured, fruit-forward Pinot Noir-based Champagnes.

var:trollinger.name: Trollinger
var:trollinger.description: Trollinger is a red grape variety primarily grown in Germany, particularly in the Württemberg region. It is known for producing light-bodied, fruity wines that are often enjoyed young. The grape is also referred to as Schiava in Italy, where it is cultivated in the Alto Adige region.
var:trollinger.style: Light-bodied red wines with fruity characteristics.

geo:fr:trepail.name: Trépail
geo:fr:trepail.description: Trépail is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is notable for its production of both Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, which is somewhat atypical for this area, where Pinot Noir is more dominant. The commune has a cru rating of 95%, indicating its high quality within the Champagne hierarchy.
geo:fr:trepail.style: Champagnes with a balance of freshness and richness.

geo:fr:vaudemange.name: Vaudemange
geo:fr:vaudemange.description: Vaudemange is a Premier Cru commune located on the eastern slopes of the Montagne de Reims. It is classified with a rating of 95% on the Échelle des Crus, indicating its high quality within the Champagne region. The commune is primarily known for its production of Champagne, utilizing the traditional method of secondary fermentation in the bottle.
geo:fr:vaudemange.style: Champagnes with finesse and complexity, often with floral and fruit notes.

var:verdejo.name: Verdejo
var:verdejo.description: Verdejo is a white grape variety native to the Rueda region of Spain. It is known for producing light to medium-bodied wines with a fresh and aromatic profile. The grape typically exhibits high acidity and can display notes of citrus, stone fruits, and herbal characteristics.
var:verdejo.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with high acidity.

var:verdelho.name: Verdelho
var:verdelho.description: Verdelho is a white grape variety known for its medium-bodied wines, often characterized by tropical fruit flavors and vibrant acidity. It is primarily cultivated in regions with warm climates, where it can achieve optimal ripeness while retaining freshness.
var:verdelho.style: Medium-bodied white with tropical fruit characteristics.

var:verdicchio.name: Verdicchio
var:verdicchio.description: Verdicchio is a white grape variety known for its high acidity and crisp character. It typically produces light to medium-bodied wines with a refreshing profile. The grape is primarily cultivated in Italy, particularly in the Marche region.
var:verdicchio.style: Light to medium-bodied, crisp white wines with high acidity.

var:vermentino.name: Vermentino
var:vermentino.description: Vermentino is a white grape variety known for producing light to medium-bodied wines. It typically exhibits bright acidity and a range of flavors including citrus, green apple, and herbal notes. The grape thrives in warm climates and is often associated with coastal regions.
var:vermentino.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with bright acidity.

var:vernaccia.name: Vernaccia
var:vernaccia.description: Vernaccia is a white grape variety known for its crisp acidity and mineral character. It typically produces wines that are refreshing and can exhibit a range of flavors from citrus to stone fruit. The grape thrives in various climates, often showcasing a distinctive terroir-driven profile.

geo:fr:vertus.name: Vertus
geo:fr:vertus.description: Vertus is a Premier Cru commune located in the southern part of the Côte des Blancs in Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of high-quality Chardonnay. The commune is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable microclimate, which contribute to the distinctive style of its wines. Notable producers from Vertus include Duval-Leroy and Larmandier-Bernier, both recognized for their commitment to quality and terroir expression.

geo:fr:verzenay.name: Verzenay
geo:fr:verzenay.description: Verzenay is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir, which thrives on the north-facing slopes that provide a cooler climate. The terroir of Verzenay contributes to the production of structured and age-worthy wines. The commune is also notable for the Phare de Verzenay lighthouse museum, which adds cultural significance to the area. Several prominent growers in Verzenay supply grapes to top Champagne houses.
geo:fr:verzenay.style: Structured, age-worthy wines primarily from Pinot Noir.

geo:fr:verzy.name: Verzy
geo:fr:verzy.description: Verzy is a Grand Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is adjacent to Verzenay and is notable for its unique natural feature, the Faux de Verzy, which consists of twisted beech trees in the nearby forest. The terroir of Verzy is characterized by its chalky soils, which contribute to the production of structured wines, primarily from Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes.
geo:fr:verzy.style: Produces structured wines, primarily from Pinot Noir.

geo:fr:villedommange.name: Villedommange
geo:fr:villedommange.description: Villedommange is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated on the western slopes of the Montagne de Reims, which is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate for grape cultivation. The commune is primarily known for its production of Champagne, with a focus on the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. Villedommange's terroir contributes to the complexity and structure of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:villedommange.style: Champagne with a focus on structure and complexity.

geo:fr:villeneuve-renneville.name: Villeneuve-Renneville
geo:fr:villeneuve-renneville.description: Villeneuve-Renneville is a Premier Cru commune located in the southern part of the Côte des Blancs in Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of high-quality Chardonnay. The commune is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable microclimate, which contribute to the development of the grape's distinctive qualities.
geo:fr:villeneuve-renneville.style: Primarily produces Chardonnay-based Champagne with finesse and elegance.

geo:fr:villers-allerand.name: Villers-Allerand
geo:fr:villers-allerand.description: Villers-Allerand is a Premier Cru commune located in the northern Montagne de Reims, adjacent to Rilly-la-Montagne. The commune is primarily known for its production of Champagne, with a focus on the Pinot Noir grape variety. The terroir is characterized by chalky soils and a climate conducive to the cultivation of high-quality grapes for sparkling wine production.
geo:fr:villers-allerand.style: Champagnes with a focus on structure and depth from Pinot Noir.

geo:fr:villers-aux-noeuds.name: Villers-aux-Nœuds
geo:fr:villers-aux-noeuds.description: Villers-aux-Nœuds is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of high-quality Pinot Noir. The commune benefits from the unique terroir of the Montagne de Reims, which features a combination of chalky soils and a favorable microclimate for grape cultivation.
geo:fr:villers-aux-noeuds.style: Produces robust and structured Champagnes primarily from Pinot Noir.

geo:fr:villers-marmery.name: Villers-Marmery
geo:fr:villers-marmery.description: Villers-Marmery is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is notable for its focus on Chardonnay, contrasting with the predominant Pinot Noir of the surrounding area. The commune is classified with a high Échelle des Crus rating of 95%, indicating its significant quality within the Champagne hierarchy.
geo:fr:villers-marmery.style: Primarily produces Chardonnay-based Champagne with elegance and finesse.

var:viognier.name: Viognier
var:viognier.description: Viognier is a white grape variety known for its aromatic qualities and full-bodied character. It typically exhibits floral and stone fruit aromas, with a rich mouthfeel and moderate acidity. The grape thrives in warm climates, where it can achieve optimal ripeness.
var:viognier.style: Full-bodied and aromatic white wine with floral and stone fruit notes.

var:vital.name: Vital
var:vital.description: Vital is a Portuguese white grape variety found mainly in the Tejo and Lisboa regions. It produces light to medium-bodied wines with moderate acidity and subtle floral and green apple character. Often used in blends, it contributes freshness without dominating. A workhorse variety in central Portugal, it is gaining attention as producers explore its varietal potential.
var:vital.style: Light to medium-bodied with moderate acidity and green apple notes.

geo:fr:voipreux.name: Voipreux
geo:fr:voipreux.description: Voipreux is a Premier Cru commune located in the Côte des Blancs region of Champagne, France. It is primarily known for its production of high-quality Chardonnay, which thrives in the area's chalky soils and favorable climate. The commune holds a significant Échelle des Crus rating of 95%, indicating its esteemed position within the Champagne hierarchy. The terroir of Voipreux contributes to the elegance and finesse characteristic of the wines produced here.
geo:fr:voipreux.style: Champagnes with elegance and finesse, primarily from Chardonnay.

geo:fr:vrigny.name: Vrigny
geo:fr:vrigny.description: Vrigny is a Premier Cru commune located on the western edge of the Montagne de Reims in the Champagne region of France. It is characterized by its chalky soils and a climate conducive to the cultivation of high-quality grapes. The commune primarily produces sparkling wines, which benefit from the unique terroir and microclimate of the area.
geo:fr:vrigny.style: Champagne with a focus on fruit-forward profiles and balanced acidity.

var:welschriesling.name: Welschriesling
var:welschriesling.description: Welschriesling is a white grape variety known for producing light-bodied wines with high acidity. It is often characterized by floral and fruity aromas, with a crisp and refreshing palate. The grape thrives in cooler climates and is primarily cultivated in Central and Eastern Europe.
var:welschriesling.style: Light-bodied white wines with high acidity and floral, fruity notes.

var:xarel-lo.name: Xarel·lo
var:xarel-lo.description: Xarel·lo is a white grape variety primarily grown in Catalonia, Spain. It is known for its high acidity and ability to produce wines that are light to medium-bodied. The grape is often used in the production of Cava, Spain's traditional sparkling wine, where it contributes to the wine's structure and freshness.
var:xarel-lo.style: Light to medium-bodied white wines with high acidity.

var:xinomavro.name: Xinomavro
var:xinomavro.description: Xinomavro is a red grape variety known for its full-bodied character, high acidity, and pronounced tannins. It is often compared to Nebbiolo due to its complex flavor profile and aging potential. The grape typically exhibits dark fruit flavors, along with herbal and earthy notes.
var:xinomavro.style: Full-bodied, tannic, and high in acidity.

var:yama-sauvignon.name: Yama Sauvignon
var:yama-sauvignon.description: Yama Sauvignon is a red grape variety known for its medium-bodied structure and herbaceous characteristics. It typically exhibits a range of flavors that may include green bell pepper, tomato leaf, and various herbal notes. The grape thrives in regions with a moderate climate, allowing for balanced acidity and flavor development.

var:zinfandel.name: Zinfandel
var:zinfandel.description: Zinfandel is a red grape variety known for its high sugar levels and versatility in winemaking. It typically produces full-bodied wines that are fruit-forward, often exhibiting flavors of blackberry, raspberry, and plum. The grape is primarily grown in warm climates, where it achieves optimal ripeness.

var:zweigelt.name: Zweigelt
var:zweigelt.description: Zweigelt is a red grape variety that originated in Austria, a cross between Blaufränkisch and St. Laurent. It typically produces wines that are light to medium-bodied with moderate acidity and soft tannins. The grape is known for its fruit-forward profile, often displaying cherry and berry notes.
var:zweigelt.style: Light to medium-bodied red with fruit-forward characteristics.

geo:fr:ecueil.name: Écueil
geo:fr:ecueil.description: Écueil is a Premier Cru commune located in the Montagne de Reims region of Champagne, France. It is situated on the western slopes of the Montagne de Reims, which is characterized by its chalky soils and favorable climate for viticulture. The commune is primarily known for its production of high-quality Champagne, particularly from the Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier grape varieties. Écueil benefits from a terroir that imparts distinct characteristics to the wines produced here.
geo:fr:ecueil.style: Champagnes with a focus on fruitiness and structure.

geo:fr:etrechy.name: Étréchy
geo:fr:etrechy.description: Étréchy is a Premier Cru commune located at the southern end of the Côte des Blancs, transitioning towards the Côte de Sézanne. It is primarily known for its production of Champagne, particularly from the Chardonnay grape. The terroir features chalky soils that contribute to the finesse and elegance of the wines produced in this area.
geo:fr:etrechy.style: Champagnes characterized by finesse and elegance.

geo:jp:yamagata.name: Yamagata
geo:jp:yamagata.description: Yamagata is a wine-producing prefecture in northeastern Honshu, Japan, centered on the Takahata area in the Okitama basin. The region received GI status in 2021. Its continental climate with heavy snowfall and warm summers supports grape cultivation, particularly Delaware (used for both table consumption and winemaking) and Muscat Bailey A. Yamagata is also known for its fruit agriculture, and local wineries often emphasize the connection between wine and the region's broader agricultural heritage.
geo:jp:yamagata.style: Light reds and off-dry whites from indigenous hybrid varieties

geo:jp:osaka.name: Osaka
geo:jp:osaka.description: Osaka is a wine-producing prefecture in the Kansai region of Japan, with a winemaking history dating to the Meiji era. The Kashiwara area in eastern Osaka, on the slopes of the Ikoma mountain range, is the main vineyard zone. Katashimo Winery, established in 1914, is one of Japan's oldest operating wineries and the anchor of the region's wine identity. Osaka received GI status in 2022. The warm humid subtropical climate favors Delaware and Muscat Bailey A.
geo:jp:osaka.style: Light, fruit-forward reds and off-dry whites

geo:jp:yamanashi-gi.name: Yamanashi
geo:jp:yamanashi-gi.description: GI Yamanashi, granted in 2013, was Japan's first geographical indication for wine. It covers the entire Yamanashi Prefecture, with the historic Katsunuma district in Koshu city as its viticultural heart. Koshu, an indigenous Vitis vinifera variety with over 1,000 years of cultivation history, is the signature grape, producing delicate, citrus-inflected whites. Muscat Bailey A, a Japanese hybrid developed in the 1920s, is the principal red variety. The region lies in a basin surrounded by mountains including Mount Fuji, creating a continental microclimate with marked diurnal temperature variation.
geo:jp:yamanashi-gi.style: Delicate whites from Koshu; light, fruity reds from Muscat Bailey A

geo:jp:hokkaido-gi.name: Hokkaido
geo:jp:hokkaido-gi.description: GI Hokkaido, granted in 2018, covers wine production across Japan's northernmost main island. The cool continental and maritime climate, with long daylight hours in summer and cold winters, favors aromatic white varieties. Kerner and Müller-Thurgau thrive alongside Pinot Noir, Zweigelt, and other cool-climate grapes. The Yoichi district on the western coast and the Sorachi area have emerged as the most recognized sub-zones, with producers like Domaine Takahiko bringing international attention.
geo:jp:hokkaido-gi.style: Aromatic cool-climate whites; elegant, light-bodied reds

geo:jp:nagano-gi.name: Nagano
geo:jp:nagano-gi.description: GI Nagano, granted in 2021, encompasses wine production in Nagano Prefecture in Japan's mountainous central highlands. Vineyards at 600-800 meters elevation benefit from significant diurnal temperature variation and lower humidity than coastal regions. Merlot has emerged as the leading red variety, producing structured wines that have drawn comparisons to cool-climate Bordeaux styles. The Kikyogahara plateau and Chikumagawa river valley are notable sub-areas.
geo:jp:nagano-gi.style: Structured Bordeaux-style reds; crisp high-altitude whites

geo:jp:yamagata-gi.name: Yamagata
geo:jp:yamagata-gi.description: GI Yamagata, granted in 2021, covers wine production in Yamagata Prefecture in northeastern Honshu. The Takahata area in the Okitama basin is the primary wine zone. Delaware, an American hybrid grape, is the most widely planted variety, used for both table consumption and winemaking, producing light, off-dry whites. Muscat Bailey A is the principal red variety. The continental climate with heavy snowfall provides good winter dormancy and moderate summers.
geo:jp:yamagata-gi.style: Light, off-dry whites from Delaware; fruity reds from Muscat Bailey A

geo:jp:osaka-gi.name: Osaka
geo:jp:osaka-gi.description: GI Osaka, granted in 2022, recognizes the winemaking tradition of Osaka Prefecture dating to the Meiji era. The Kashiwara area on the western slopes of the Ikoma mountains is the main vineyard zone. Katashimo Winery, established in 1914, is the historic anchor producer. Delaware and Muscat Bailey A are the dominant varieties, reflecting a warm humid climate. The GI has helped distinguish Osaka's wine identity from its broader reputation as a food and sake capital.
geo:jp:osaka-gi.style: Approachable, fruit-forward wines in a warm-climate style

var:muscadelle.name: Muscadelle
var:muscadelle.description: Muscadelle is a white grape variety found primarily in Bordeaux and Bergerac, where it plays a supporting role alongside Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc in both dry and sweet wines. Despite its name, Muscadelle is not related to the Muscat family, though it does share some floral, musky aromatics. It is particularly valued in Saussignac and Monbazillac for adding perfumed lift to botrytized dessert blends. In Australia (as 'Tokay'), it was historically important in Rutherglen.
var:muscadelle.style: Floral, musky, blending grape for Bordeaux whites and sweet wines

var:grolleau.name: Grolleau
var:grolleau.description: Grolleau (also known as Groslot) is a red grape variety native to the Loire Valley, where it is the principal grape in Rosé d'Anjou and Rosé de Loire. It produces light-colored, fruity wines with moderate alcohol and gentle acidity. While rarely vinified as a varietal, Grolleau is valued for producing easy-drinking rosés with red fruit character. Some producers are exploring more serious expressions.

var:mauzac.name: Mauzac
var:mauzac.description: Mauzac is a white grape variety central to the sparkling wines of Limoux in southwestern France, including Blanquette de Limoux — one of the world's oldest sparkling wines, predating Champagne. The grape has a distinctive apple-skin aroma and good natural acidity that suits méthode ancestrale and traditional method production. It is also used in still wines in Gaillac.
var:mauzac.style: Apple-scented sparkling wine base, historic Limoux grape

var:pineau-daunis.name: Pineau d'Aunis
var:pineau-daunis.description: Pineau d'Aunis is an ancient red grape variety of the Loire Valley, once the dominant grape of the region before Cabernet Franc. It produces distinctively peppery, light-bodied reds and rosés with white pepper and spice aromatics. Found primarily in Coteaux du Loir and Coteaux du Vendômois, it is experiencing a revival among natural wine producers drawn to its unique character.

var:folle-blanche.name: Folle Blanche
var:folle-blanche.description: Folle Blanche is a white grape variety historically important as the base for Cognac and Armagnac distillation. In the Loire Valley, it survives as Gros Plant du Pays Nantais, producing bracingly acidic, lean white wines near the Atlantic coast. The grape's high acidity and neutral flavor make it ideal for distillation but challenging as a table wine, though Loire examples can be refreshing.
var:folle-blanche.style: High-acid, lean, historically a distillation grape

var:arrufiac.name: Arrufiac
var:arrufiac.description: Arrufiac (or Arrufiat) is a rare white grape variety found almost exclusively in the Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh appellation of southwestern France. It contributes floral aromatics and roundness to blends alongside Petit Manseng and Petit Courbu. The grape is quite rare, with only a handful of hectares remaining, making it one of France's most endangered indigenous varieties.
var:arrufiac.style: Rare aromatic SW France white, nearly extinct

var:courbu.name: Courbu
var:courbu.description: Courbu is a white grape variety of southwestern France, found in Jurançon and Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh. It adds body and richness to blends dominated by Petit Manseng and Gros Manseng. Courbu can produce full-bodied wines with good structure when vinified alone, though this is rare. It contributes tropical fruit and honeyed notes to sweet wine blends.
var:courbu.style: Rich, full-bodied SW France blending white

var:mondeuse.name: Mondeuse
var:mondeuse.description: Mondeuse Noire is a red grape variety native to Savoie in the French Alps, producing deeply colored, peppery, tannic wines with distinctive character. DNA analysis has shown it to be a parent of Syrah, which helps explain some shared aromatic traits. The grape thrives at altitude, producing structured wines with black fruit, pepper, and earthy notes. It is enjoying a renaissance as Savoie wines gain wider recognition.
var:mondeuse.style: Peppery, tannic Savoie Alpine red — parent of Syrah

var:persan.name: Persan
var:persan.description: Persan is an extremely rare red grape variety from the Alpine valleys of Savoie, France. Nearly extinct by the late 20th century, it has been rescued by dedicated vignerons who recognized its quality potential. Persan produces dark, structured wines with black fruit and spice. It is genetically related to several other Alpine varieties and represents an important piece of Savoyard viticultural heritage.
var:persan.style: Rare, dark Alpine Savoie red, recently rescued from extinction

var:poulsard.name: Poulsard
var:poulsard.description: Poulsard (also spelled Ploussard) is a distinctive red grape variety of the Jura region in eastern France. Despite being a red grape, it produces remarkably pale wines — often barely darker than rosé — with delicate flavors of red fruits, flowers, and earth. The thin-skinned grape is susceptible to oxidative winemaking, which is traditional in the Jura. It is one of the region's signature varieties alongside Trousseau and Savagnin.
var:poulsard.style: Pale, delicate Jura red with ethereal transparency

var:trousseau.name: Trousseau
var:trousseau.description: Trousseau is a red grape variety of the Jura region in eastern France (known as Bastardo in Portugal). In the Jura, it produces darker, more structured wines than Poulsard, with earthy complexity, red and dark fruit, and moderate tannins. It requires the warmest vineyard sites in the continental Jura climate. The grape is experiencing renewed interest as Jura wines gain international acclaim.
var:trousseau.style: Dark, earthy Jura red, more structured than Poulsard

var:piedirosso.name: Piedirosso
var:piedirosso.description: Piedirosso (also called Per' e Palummo, meaning 'pigeon's foot' for the shape of its stalks) is a red grape variety native to Campania, southern Italy. It is an essential blending partner with Aglianico in many Campanian DOCs including Vesuvio, Costa d'Amalfi, and Falerno del Massico. The grape produces medium-bodied wines with red cherry fruit and herbal notes, adding freshness and drinkability to blends.
var:piedirosso.style: Medium-bodied Campanian blending red, pigeon's foot grape

var:biancolella.name: Biancolella
var:biancolella.description: Biancolella is a white grape variety grown on the islands and coastal slopes of Campania, particularly Ischia and the Amalfi Coast. It produces aromatic, mineral-driven white wines with citrus and Mediterranean herb notes, reflecting the volcanic and maritime terroir. The grape is well-adapted to the steep, terraced vineyards of these areas and is increasingly recognized for its quality.

var:sciascinoso.name: Sciascinoso
var:sciascinoso.description: Sciascinoso (also known as Olivella) is a red grape variety native to Campania. It plays a minor blending role in several Campanian DOCs, including Vesuvio, where it adds lightness and aromatic complexity to Piedirosso and Aglianico blends. The grape produces light-colored wines and is declining in planted area.

var:coda-di-volpe.name: Coda di Volpe
var:coda-di-volpe.description: Coda di Volpe ('fox's tail,' named for the shape of its clusters) is a white grape variety from Campania, Italy. It produces crisp, neutral white wines that serve as versatile blending partners in many Campanian DOCs. When carefully vinified, it can produce characterful wines with almond and green apple notes. The grape is an important part of the traditional Campanian viticultural heritage.
var:coda-di-volpe.style: Crisp Campanian white, fox-tail grape cluster

var:forastera.name: Forastera
var:forastera.description: Forastera is a white grape variety found almost exclusively on the island of Ischia in the Bay of Naples. Its name means 'foreigner,' suggesting the grape was introduced from elsewhere, though it has been cultivated on Ischia for centuries. It produces fresh, mineral white wines with citrus and floral notes, well-suited to the island's volcanic terroir.
var:forastera.style: Fresh, mineral Ischian white, volcanic island grape

var:nocera.name: Nocera
var:nocera.description: Nocera is a rare red grape variety found in northeastern Sicily, particularly associated with the Mamertino di Milazzo DOC. It produces deeply colored, tannic wines with dark fruit character. The grape is quite rare, with limited plantings, and is typically used in blends. It represents one of Sicily's many indigenous varieties that are being rediscovered.

var:perricone.name: Perricone
var:perricone.description: Perricone (also known as Pignatello) is a red grape variety native to western Sicily. It produces deeply colored, full-bodied wines with dark fruit, spice, and robust tannins. Once widely planted, Perricone declined dramatically in the late 20th century but is being revived by quality-focused Sicilian producers. It is used in some DOC blends and increasingly as a varietal.

var:lacrima.name: Lacrima
var:lacrima.description: Lacrima (as in Lacrima di Morro d'Alba) is a red grape variety from the Marche region of central Italy. It is remarkable for its intense, unmistakable floral and aromatic character — reminiscent of roses, violets, and wild strawberries. The name means 'tear,' referring to how the ripe grape's juice weeps through the skin. It produces medium-bodied wines best enjoyed young.
var:lacrima.style: Intensely floral, aromatic Marche red — roses and violets

var:croatina.name: Croatina
var:croatina.description: Croatina is a red grape variety important in the Oltrepò Pavese area of Lombardy and in Emilia-Romagna's Colli Piacentini. It produces fruity, deeply colored wines with cherry and plum notes and moderate tannins. Croatina is a key component of Gutturnio and Bonarda dell'Oltrepò Pavese (where it is confusingly labeled as Bonarda, though genetically distinct from Piedmontese Bonarda).
var:croatina.style: Fruity, deeply colored Lombardy/Emilia blending red

var:pignola-valtellinese.name: Pignola Valtellinese
var:pignola-valtellinese.description: Pignola Valtellinese is a rare red grape variety found in the Valtellina district of Lombardy, northern Italy. It serves as a minor blending partner alongside Nebbiolo (locally called Chiavennasca) in Valtellina Superiore wines. The grape is one of only three permitted in Valtellina reds. Very small plantings remain.
var:pignola-valtellinese.style: Rare Valtellina blending red, Nebbiolo partner

var:bombino-nero.name: Bombino Nero
var:bombino-nero.description: Bombino Nero is a red grape variety native to Puglia in southern Italy, most associated with the Castel del Monte appellation. It produces delicate, light-colored rosé wines with floral and cherry notes. Bombino Nero rosato from Castel del Monte has gained recognition as one of southern Italy's finest rosé styles.

var:pignoletto.name: Pignoletto
var:pignoletto.description: Pignoletto (genetically identical to Grechetto di Todi from Umbria) is a white grape variety associated with the hills south of Bologna in Emilia-Romagna. It produces fresh, lightly aromatic white wines and is increasingly popular as a frizzante and spumante. The Colli Bolognesi Pignoletto DOCG recognizes it as a quality varietal.

var:bosco.name: Bosco
var:bosco.description: Bosco is a white grape variety grown in the coastal vineyards of Liguria, northwestern Italy. It is the primary grape in Cinque Terre wines, grown on dramatic steep terraces above the sea. Bosco produces crisp, mineral wines with citrus and saline notes reflecting the maritime terroir. The grape also contributes to the rare sweet Sciacchetrà dessert wine.
var:bosco.style: Ligurian Cinque Terre grape, maritime and mineral

var:albana.name: Albana
var:albana.description: Albana is a white grape variety native to Romagna in Emilia-Romagna. In 1987, Albana di Romagna became the first Italian white wine to receive DOCG status. The grape is versatile, used for dry, sweet, and passito-style wines. At its best, Albana produces full-bodied whites with stone fruit, honey, and almond notes. The passito versions can be exceptional.
var:albana.style: Full-bodied Romagnol white, Italy's first white DOCG

var:pelaverga-piccolo.name: Pelaverga Piccolo
var:pelaverga-piccolo.description: Pelaverga Piccolo is a red grape variety grown almost exclusively in Verduno, a small commune in the Langhe hills of Piedmont. Despite being surrounded by Barolo vineyards, Verduno Pelaverga DOC produces a distinctly different wine: light-bodied, aromatic, with distinctive white pepper and spice notes. It is one of Piedmont's most distinctive indigenous varieties.
var:pelaverga-piccolo.style: Peppery, aromatic Verduno red, spice-laden Piedmont specialty

var:tai-rosso.name: Tai Rosso
var:tai-rosso.description: Tai Rosso is a red grape variety found in the Veneto, particularly associated with the Colli Berici DOC south of Vicenza. Though it shares a local name with the Grenache family, genetic studies suggest it may be distinct. It produces medium-bodied red wines with cherry and spice notes. Tai Rosso is one of several local varieties being championed by quality-minded Veneto producers.
var:tai-rosso.style: Medium-bodied Veneto red, Colli Berici specialty

var:vespaiola.name: Vespaiola
var:vespaiola.description: Vespaiola is a white grape variety native to the Breganze area in the Veneto. Its name derives from 'vespa' (wasp), as the grape's high sugar content at ripeness attracts wasps. Vespaiola is essential for making Torcolato, a prized passito-style dessert wine. As a dry wine, it produces crisp, aromatic whites with floral and stone fruit notes.
var:vespaiola.style: Aromatic Breganze white, essential for Torcolato dessert wine

var:listan-negro.name: Listán Negro
var:listan-negro.description: Listán Negro is the most widely planted red grape variety in the Canary Islands, where it thrives on volcanic soils at altitude. The grape produces light to medium-bodied red wines with bright acidity, red fruit, and volcanic mineral notes. Pre-phylloxera vines survive on the islands' sandy volcanic soils, making Canary Island Listán Negro wines a living piece of viticultural history.
var:listan-negro.style: Volcanic Canary Islands red, pre-phylloxera living history

var:negramoll.name: Negramoll
var:negramoll.description: Negramoll is a red grape variety found in the Canary Islands, where it serves as a blending partner with Listán Negro. It produces soft, fruity wines with gentle tannins. The grape is related to Tinta Negra of Madeira but adapted to Canary Island conditions. It contributes body and roundness to blends.

var:airen.name: Airén
var:airen.description: Airén is the most widely planted white grape variety in Spain and, by vineyard area, one of the most planted grapes in the world. Found predominantly in La Mancha and central Spain, it produces neutral, light-bodied white wines. Historically used for bulk production and brandy distillation, modern winemaking has improved quality. Some old-vine Airén from arid bush-vine vineyards can produce surprisingly characterful wines.
var:airen.style: Spain's most planted white, neutral and prolific

var:manto-negro.name: Manto Negro
var:manto-negro.description: Manto Negro is a red grape variety native to Mallorca, where it is the primary grape in Binissalem DO wines. It produces soft, medium-bodied reds with red fruit and herbal notes. The grape is well-adapted to the hot, dry Mallorcan climate and is central to the island's wine identity alongside Callet.

var:callet.name: Callet
var:callet.description: Callet is a red grape variety indigenous to Mallorca. Once at risk of extinction, it has been revived by quality-conscious producers who recognized its potential. Callet produces aromatic, medium-bodied wines with floral and herbal notes, good acidity, and refined tannins. It is increasingly bottled as a premium varietal and is central to Mallorca's wine renaissance.
var:callet.style: Revived Mallorcan red, island renaissance grape

var:prensal-blanc.name: Prensal Blanc
var:prensal-blanc.description: Prensal Blanc (also known as Moll) is the principal white grape variety of Mallorca, featured in Binissalem and Pla i Llevant DOs. It produces fresh, light-bodied white wines with citrus and floral notes. Though sometimes compared to Parellada, Prensal Blanc is genetically distinct and uniquely adapted to Mallorcan terroir.

var:malvar.name: Malvar
var:malvar.description: Malvar is a white grape variety grown primarily in the Madrid wine region (Vinos de Madrid DO). It produces light, fresh white wines with subtle floral and green apple notes. Though not widely known outside Spain, Malvar is an important component of Madrid's local viticultural identity.

var:juan-garcia.name: Juan García
